Business justification: termination has security_deposit_amount, security_deposit_return_amount, and security_deposit_forfeited_amount — denormalized fields that duplicate data from the security_deposit record. Linking termi']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant party whose lease is being terminated. Used for downstream AR settlement and security deposit return processing.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific leasable unit or space being surrendered upon termination. Drives space availability updates.']
  ar_settlement_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all outstanding AR balances related to the terminated lease have been settled, written off, or transferred to collections.']
  asc842_derecognition_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ASC 842 / IFRS 16 derecognition journal entries for the ROU asset and lease liability have been posted in the general ledger upon termination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the termination record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  cure_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days granted to the defaulting party to remedy a lease breach before the termination becomes final. Typically specified in the lease agreement.']
  cure_period_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the cure period expires. Calculated as notice_date plus cure_period_days. Determines when default-driven termination becomes irrevocable.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the DocuSign CLM envelope containing the executed termination agreement or mutual surrender document. Enables traceability to the signed legal instrument.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the lease termination becomes legally effective. Used as the derecognition date for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 right-of-use asset and lease liability.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fee payable by the tenant (or landlord) as consideration for early or negotiated termination of the lease. Triggers AR invoice generation in Yardi Voyager.']
  fee_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the termination fee was waived as part of a negotiated mutual surrender or other agreement. Affects AR settlement processing.']
  gain_loss_on_termination decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net gain or loss recognized in the income statement upon lease termination, calculated as the difference between the derecognized lease liability and ROU asset, adjusted for termination fees and other consideration.']
  holdover_rent_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly rent rate per square foot (PSF) charged during the holdover period, typically a premium above the base lease rate as stipulated in the lease agreement.']
  holdover_status_at_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant was in holdover status (occupying the premises beyond the lease expiry without a new agreement) at the time the termination was processed.']
  initiating_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party initiated the termination — landlord, tenant, mutual agreement, or court order. Affects legal rights, fee obligations, and accounting treatment.']
  keys_returned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant has returned all physical access credentials (keys, access cards, fobs) for the leased premises upon surrender.']
  lease_classification varchar [note: 'ASC 842 / IFRS 16 lease classification at the time of termination — finance lease or operating lease. Determines the derecognition accounting entries and disclosure requirements.']
  lease_liability_derecognition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining lease liability balance derecognized from the balance sheet upon lease termination per ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Used to generate the derecognition journal entry.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date on which the termination notice was formally issued by either the landlord or tenant. Triggers the notice period and cure period calculations.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Contractually required number of days of advance notice required before the termination effective date, as stipulated in the lease agreement.']
  original_lease_expiry_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled lease expiration date before any termination event. Used to calculate early termination penalties and remaining lease term at termination.']
  outstanding_ar_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total unpaid accounts receivable balance owed by the tenant at the time of termination, including rent arrears, CAM charges, and other lease obligations. Drives AR settlement workflow.']
  possession_return_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tenant physically vacated and returned possession of the leased premises to the landlord. May differ from the effective termination date.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the termination (e.g., non-payment, lease breach, relocation, business closure, redevelopment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_payment|lease_breach|relocation|business_closure|redevelopment|condemnation|mutual_agreement|natural_expiry — promote to reference product]']
  reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context or explanation for the termination reason, supplementing the standardized reason code.']
  restoration_cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to restore the surrendered premises to the required condition for re-leasing. Informs CAPEX planning and security deposit deduction calculations.']
  restoration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant is contractually obligated to restore the premises to original condition upon surrender, as specified in the lease agreement.']
  rou_asset_derecognition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net book value of the Right-of-Use (ROU) asset derecognized from the balance sheet upon lease termination per ASC 842 / IFRS 16 accounting requirements.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total security deposit held by the landlord at the time of termination. Drives security deposit return or forfeiture processing upon lease close-out.']
  security_deposit_forfeited_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the security deposit retained by the landlord to cover damages, unpaid rent, or other lease obligations not fulfilled by the tenant at termination.']
  security_deposit_processed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security deposit return or forfeiture has been fully processed and disbursed through Yardi Voyager Accounts Payable.']
  security_deposit_return_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the security deposit to be returned to the tenant after deductions for damages, unpaid rent, or other charges. Triggers AP disbursement in Yardi Voyager.']
  space_condition_at_surrender varchar [note: 'Assessed physical condition of the leased premises at the time of tenant surrender. Determines security deposit deductions and informs re-leasing capital expenditure planning.']
  termination_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number assigned to the termination event, used in correspondence with tenants, legal counsel, and DocuSign CLM workflows.']
  termination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the termination event, from initial notice through final possession return and accounting close-out.']
  termination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the termination event. Drives downstream accounting treatment, ASC 842 derecognition methodology, and AR settlement workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_termination|mutual_surrender|lease_expiration|default_termination|holdover_resolution|condemnation — promote to reference product]']
  unamortized_ti_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unamortized balance of Tenant Improvement (TI) allowances at the time of termination. Triggers accelerated amortization or write-off entries under ASC 842 derecognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the termination record was last modified. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit compliance in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  yardi_termination_ref varchar [note: 'Source system reference identifier from Yardi Voyager for the termination transaction record. Used for reconciliation and audit trail between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']

  Note: 'Records lease termination events — early terminations, mutual surrenders, lease expirations, default-driven terminations, and holdover resolutions. Captures termination type, effective termination date, termination fee, notice date, cure period, holdover status at termination, final possession return date, and space condition at surrender. Triggers downstream AR settlement, security deposit return processing, and ASC 842 derecognition entries. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM.'
}

Table lease.rent_schedule {
  rent_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rent schedule row. Primary key for the rent_schedule data product in the lease domain.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lease amendment that created or modified this rent schedule row. Null for rent steps established in the original executed lease. Enables full audit trail of rent schedule changes through the lease lifecycle.']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Rent PSF calculations require a validated area unit of measure (BOMA, IPMS, sq ft, sq m). The UOM reference drives rent per square foot/meter computations, lease abstracts, and cross-market portfolio ']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Each rent schedule step maps to a GL account (base rent revenue, straight-line rent adjustment, percentage rent) for automated journal entry generation and ASC 842 straight-line rent recognition. Real']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rent schedule steps generate recurring GL postings to specific cost centers for property-level revenue tracking and NOI computation. Finance teams need cost center on rent schedules to automate billin']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Rent schedule amounts (base rent, escalations) must reference a validated currency for multi-currency portfolio cash flow modeling, straight-line rent calculations, and ASC842 lease liability present ']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent lease agreement that this rent schedule row belongs to. One lease agreement may have many rent schedule rows representing each rent step period.']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Rent schedule structure (what charges are included in base rent vs. passed through) is directly determined by lease type. NNN vs. gross lease type drives straight-line rent calculations and ASC842 lea']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Rent schedules in rent-controlled jurisdictions must comply with regulatory obligations specifying allowable increase percentages and escalation caps. Compliance teams must verify each rent step again']
  renewal_option_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.renewal_option. Business justification: Rent schedule steps covering renewal periods should be linked to the specific renewal_option they represent. renewal_option has fixed_renewal_rent_psf, renewal_rent_escalation_type, and renewal_rent_e']
  annual_rent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annualized base rent amount for this rent step period (typically base_rent_amount × 12 for monthly leases). Used in Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling and portfolio-level NOI reporting.']
  base_rent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monthly base rent amount payable by the tenant for this rent step period, expressed in the lease currency. Excludes CAM charges, operating expenses, and other pass-throughs. Core input for ASC 842 straight-line rent calculation.']
  cpi_base_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'The baseline CPI index value at the commencement of the lease or the start of the CPI measurement period. Used to calculate the percentage change for CPI-linked rent escalation. Null for non-CPI escalation types.']
  cpi_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The maximum annual CPI escalation rate permitted under the lease terms, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00% cap). Protects the tenant from excessive CPI-driven rent increases. Null for uncapped CPI or non-CPI escalation types.']
  cpi_floor_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The minimum annual CPI escalation rate guaranteed under the lease terms, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = 2.00% floor). Protects the landlord from zero or negative CPI adjustments. Null for non-CPI escalation types.']
  cpi_index_name varchar [note: 'The name of the CPI index used for rent escalation when escalation_type is \'cpi\' (e.g., \'US CPI-U All Items\', \'UK RPI\'). Null for non-CPI escalation types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rent schedule record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance controls.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The discount rate used to calculate the present value of lease payments for this rent step under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Typically the implicit rate in the lease or the lessee\'s incremental borrowing rate (IBR). Expressed as a decimal annual rate (e.g., 0.0450 = 4.50%).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The last date on which this rent step period is in effect. The next step\'s effective_start_date should immediately follow. Null for open-ended final steps.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this rent step period begins and the corresponding base rent amount becomes billable to the tenant. Aligns with the lease commencement or escalation trigger date.']
  escalation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fixed dollar increment added to the prior step\'s base rent to arrive at this step\'s base rent. Used when escalation_type is \'fixed_step\' with a dollar-denominated increase rather than a percentage. Null for percentage or CPI escalations.']
  escalation_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The escalation rate applied to derive this step\'s rent from the prior step. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.0300 = 3.00%). Applicable when escalation_type is \'fixed_step\' (percentage) or \'cpi\'. Null for fixed-dollar steps or percentage-of-sales.']
  escalation_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which rent increases from one step to the next. \'fixed_step\' = predetermined dollar or percentage increase; \'cpi\' = Consumer Price Index-linked; \'percentage_of_sales\' = percentage rent tied to tenant gross sales; \'market_review\' = reset to fair market value; \'none\' = flat rent with no escalation.']
  free_rent_months varchar [note: 'The number of months of rent abatement granted to the tenant within this step period. Used to quantify the total concession value for ASC 842 straight-line rent amortization and Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days after the payment due date before a late fee is assessed. Defined in the lease agreement and used by Yardi Voyager to trigger arrears events and late charge calculations.']
  is_free_rent_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rent step period is a free-rent (rent abatement) period during which no base rent is payable by the tenant. True = free-rent period; False = normal rent-paying period. Critical for ASC 842 straight-line rent calculation over the full lease term.']
  late_fee_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to the overdue rent balance to calculate the late payment fee after the grace period expires. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5.00%). Null if a flat late fee amount applies instead.']
  lease_classification varchar [note: 'The accounting classification of the lease under ASC 842 (lessee perspective) or IFRS 16. \'operating\' = operating lease with straight-line expense recognition; \'finance\' = finance/capital lease with front-loaded expense recognition. Drives balance sheet treatment and disclosure requirements.']
  lease_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The present value of remaining lease payments attributable to this rent step period under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Represents the lessee\'s financial obligation for this step. Required for balance sheet disclosure and debt covenant compliance reporting.']
  leased_area_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The net leasable area (NLA) in square feet covered by this rent schedule step. Used to derive rent_psf and to validate rent amounts against market benchmarks in CoStar Suite.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for lease administrators to record contextual notes about this rent step, such as negotiated concession rationale, amendment history, or special billing instructions not captured in structured fields.']
  payment_due_day varchar [note: 'The day of the month on which rent payment is due (e.g., 1 = first of the month). Used to generate AR billing schedules in Yardi Voyager and to calculate late payment penalties.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which base rent payments are due from the tenant under this rent step. Drives accounts receivable billing cycle in Yardi Voyager and cash flow timing in Argus Enterprise.']
  percentage_rent_breakpoint decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross sales threshold above which percentage rent becomes payable by the tenant. Applicable when escalation_type is \'percentage_of_sales\'. Typically calculated as base_rent_amount divided by the percentage rent rate. Null for non-percentage-of-sales leases.']
  percentage_rent_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage of tenant gross sales above the breakpoint that is payable as additional rent. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0600 = 6.00%). Common in retail leases. Null for non-percentage-of-sales leases.']
  rent_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base rent expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) annual basis for this rent step period. Calculated as annual_rent_amount divided by the leased area in square feet. Standard CRE benchmarking metric used in CoStar comparable analysis.']
  rou_asset_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The right-of-use (ROU) asset value attributable to this rent step period under ASC 842 / IFRS 16 lessee accounting. Represents the present value of lease payments allocated to this step. Required for balance sheet disclosure and fixed asset register in SAP S/4HANA.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this rent schedule row, as assigned in the source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager or MRI Software). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit tracing.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this rent schedule row. \'active\' indicates the current billing period; \'future\' indicates an upcoming step; \'expired\' indicates a past step; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by an amendment; \'cancelled\' indicates voided.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this rent schedule row was sourced. \'yardi\' = Yardi Voyager; \'mri\' = MRI Software; \'argus\' = Argus Enterprise; \'manual\' = manually entered in the data platform.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier for this rent schedule row in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi charge code + period key). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and incremental load deduplication in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  step_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential integer ordering this rent step within the lease agreement\'s full escalation schedule. Step 1 is the initial rent period; subsequent steps represent each escalation event.']
  straight_line_rent_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the straight-line rent amount and the cash base rent amount for this step period. A positive value indicates deferred rent (cash < straight-line); a negative value indicates prepaid rent. Required for ASC 842 deferred rent liability/asset tracking.']
  straight_line_rent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The ASC 842 / IFRS 16 straight-line rent amount for this step period, representing the equal monthly recognition amount calculated by spreading total lease payments evenly over the lease term. Used for GAAP/IFRS income statement recognition, distinct from cash rent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rent schedule record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental processing, change data capture, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Stores the full rent escalation schedule for a lease agreement, including each rent step period, effective start and end dates, base rent amount, rent PSF, escalation type (fixed step, CPI, percentage of sales), escalation rate or index, and free-rent periods. Enables accurate ASC 842 straight-line rent calculation and cash flow forecasting in Argus Enterprise. One agreement may have many rent schedule rows.'
}

Table lease.cam_schedule {
  cam_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cam_schedule']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the leased premises are located. Used to aggregate CAM reconciliations at the building or campus level.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: CAM reconciliation is performed at the building level — building operating expenses are pooled and allocated to tenants. For multi-building assets, building-level CAM pools and reconciliation reports ']
  cam_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.cam_pool. Business justification: CAM schedules represent annual reconciliations within a specific CAM pool. The cam_pool master table defines pool allocation rules, included/excluded expense categories, billing cycles, and gross-up m']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: CAM reconciliation entries post to specific GL accounts (CAM recovery income, CAM expense). The gl_account_code on cam_schedule is a denormalized reference; a proper FK enables automated GL posting, a']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAM reconciliation charges and credits are posted to cost centers for property operating expense tracking and NOI reporting. Controllers need cost center linkage on CAM reconciliations to route true-u']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: CAM reconciliation amounts (estimated vs. actual, credit/charge) require a validated currency reference for multi-currency property portfolios and accurate tenant billing reconciliation reporting.']
  financial_period_close_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period_close. Business justification: CAM reconciliations are completed as part of the annual financial close process. Linking cam_schedule to financial_period_close enables period-close checklist tracking of CAM reconciliation status — a']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed lease agreement for which this CAM reconciliation is performed. Links the reconciliation to the governing lease instrument.']
  premium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.premium. Business justification: Commercial real estate CAM reconciliations include insurance premiums as a recoverable operating expense billed to tenants. Linking cam_schedule to the specific insurance premium enables precise CAM a']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: CAM reconciliation applicability and methodology (gross-up, exclusions, admin fee) are determined by lease type. NNN leases have full CAM pass-through while gross leases do not. Lease type reference i']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant entity responsible for paying CAM charges under the lease. Supports tenant-level CAM exposure reporting.']
  valuation_tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: CAM Tax Reconciliation process: NNN/modified gross lease CAM reconciliations include actual property tax as a recoverable expense. Property accountants must reference the specific tax_assessment for t']
  actual_cam_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual gross operating expenses incurred at the property level for the reconciliation period, before application of the gross-up factor or tenant pro-rata share. Sourced from the property general ledger via Yardi Voyager.']
  actual_cam_grossed_up decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual CAM operating expenses after application of the gross-up factor. Represents the normalized total expense pool used as the basis for tenant pro-rata share allocation.']
  actual_cam_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual CAM charge allocated to the tenant expressed on a per-square-foot basis (PSF). Calculated as tenant_cam_allocated divided by tenant_nla_sqft. Key metric for portfolio benchmarking and market comparisons.']
  admin_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated dollar amount of the landlord\'s administrative fee included in the tenant\'s CAM allocation for the reconciliation period. Derived from admin_fee_rate applied to the eligible CAM expense pool.']
  admin_fee_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Percentage rate charged by the landlord as an administrative or management fee on top of actual CAM expenses, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Permitted under many NNN leases. Null if no admin fee applies.']
  audit_right_exercised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant has exercised their contractual right to audit the landlord\'s CAM expense records for the reconciliation period. True if an audit has been formally requested or conducted.']
  building_gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total Gross Leasable Area of the building in square feet used as the denominator for pro-rata share calculation. May differ from total building area if certain areas are excluded per lease terms.']
  cam_cap_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAM increase cap was triggered and applied during this reconciliation period. True if the calculated CAM increase exceeded the cap and was limited accordingly.']
  cam_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Maximum annual percentage increase in CAM charges permitted under the lease, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5% cap). Limits the tenant\'s CAM exposure year-over-year. Null if no cap applies.']
  cam_cap_savings decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount by which the tenant\'s CAM charge was reduced due to the application of the CAM cap. Represents the difference between the uncapped allocated amount and the capped amount billed. Zero if cap was not applied.']
  cam_cap_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the CAM increase cap is cumulative (unused cap carries forward to future years) or non-cumulative (cap resets each year). Determines how the cap is applied in multi-year reconciliations.']
  cam_exclusions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of operating expense line items excluded from the CAM pool per lease terms (e.g., capital expenditures, management fees above a threshold, leasing commissions). Reduces the gross CAM expense pool before tenant allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CAM reconciliation record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX and FASB ASC 842.']
  credit_or_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount due to or from the tenant as a result of the CAM reconciliation. A positive value represents an additional charge invoiced to the tenant; a negative value represents a credit applied to the tenant\'s account. Drives the AR billing or credit memo in Yardi Voyager.']
  credit_or_charge_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation outcome results in an additional charge to the tenant, a credit to the tenant, or a zero-balance settlement. Drives downstream AR and billing workflow routing in Yardi Voyager.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant has formally disputed the CAM reconciliation statement. True if a dispute has been raised; False if no dispute exists. Triggers dispute management workflow.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the tenant\'s stated reason for disputing the CAM reconciliation, as recorded by the property manager. Populated only when dispute_flag is True.']
  dispute_resolved_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAM reconciliation dispute was formally resolved between landlord and tenant. Null if no dispute exists or dispute is still open.']
  estimated_cam_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated CAM charges billed to the tenant over the reconciliation year, based on the budgeted CAM rate established at lease commencement or annual budget cycle. Represents the sum of monthly estimated CAM payments collected from the tenant.']
  estimated_cam_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated annual CAM charge expressed on a per-square-foot basis (PSF). Calculated as estimated_cam_annual divided by tenant_nla_sqft. Used for benchmarking and lease comparison analytics.']
  grossup_factor decimal(8,6) [note: 'Multiplier applied to actual CAM expenses to normalize costs to a fully occupied building (typically to 90–100% occupancy). Ensures tenants pay their fair share of variable operating costs regardless of actual vacancy. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.05 for a 5% gross-up).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for property manager or asset manager annotations regarding the CAM reconciliation, including explanations of unusual variances, exclusion justifications, or tenant communication summaries.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the additional CAM charge (if any) must be paid by the tenant, or by which the landlord must issue a credit. Drives AR billing and cash flow forecasting.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant\'s additional CAM payment was received and applied in Yardi Voyager. Null if no additional charge was due or payment has not yet been received.']
  prior_year_estimated_cam decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated CAM charges billed to the tenant in the immediately preceding reconciliation year. Used for year-over-year variance analysis and CAM cap calculations.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this CAM reconciliation record as assigned by Yardi Voyager (e.g., CAMREC-2024-00123). Used in tenant correspondence, audit trails, and billing statements.']
  reconciliation_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the CAM reconciliation period. Defines the boundary of actual operating expenses included in the reconciliation calculation.']
  reconciliation_period_start date [note: 'First day of the CAM reconciliation period. May differ from the calendar year start for leases with non-standard fiscal periods or mid-year commencement dates.']
  reconciliation_sent_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAM reconciliation statement was formally delivered to the tenant. Triggers the tenant\'s review and dispute window as defined in the lease.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the CAM reconciliation record. Tracks progression from initial draft through tenant review, finalization, dispute resolution, and settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|finalized|disputed|settled|voided — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  reconciliation_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the tenant\'s allocated actual CAM charge and the estimated CAM billed during the reconciliation period (tenant_cam_allocated minus estimated_cam_annual). A positive value indicates an underpayment (additional charge due from tenant); a negative value indicates an overpayment (credit due to tenant).']
  reconciliation_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year (four-digit integer, e.g., 2024) for which actual CAM operating expenses are being reconciled against estimated billings. Defines the reconciliation period.']
  tenant_cam_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual CAM expense allocated to this specific tenant, calculated as the grossed-up actual CAM multiplied by the tenant\'s pro-rata share. This is the amount the tenant is contractually obligated to pay for the reconciliation period.']
  tenant_nla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s Net Leasable Area in square feet as defined in the lease and used for pro-rata share calculation. Captures the measurement basis for CAM allocation at the time of reconciliation.']
  tenant_prorata_share decimal(8,6) [note: 'The tenant\'s proportionate share of total building CAM expenses, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.12500 = 12.5%). Typically calculated as the tenant\'s Net Leasable Area (NLA) divided by the total building NLA or Gross Leasable Area (GLA), as defined in the lease.']
  tenant_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the tenant must respond to or dispute the CAM reconciliation statement, as stipulated in the lease agreement. Typically 30–90 days after the reconciliation statement is sent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CAM reconciliation record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks the latest state change for audit and change-data-capture purposes.']
  yardi_cam_batch_code varchar [note: 'Source system batch identifier assigned by Yardi Voyager when the CAM reconciliation was processed or posted. Supports data lineage tracing back to the originating system batch run.']

  Note: 'Annual CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation record comparing estimated CAM charges billed to tenants against actual operating expenses incurred. Captures reconciliation year, estimated CAM per lease, actual CAM allocated, gross-up factor, tenant pro-rata share, reconciliation variance, credit or additional charge due, and reconciliation status. Sourced from Yardi Voyager CAM module. Critical for NNN and modified gross lease portfolios.'
}

Table lease.cam_estimate {
  cam_estimate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the CAM estimate record. Primary key for the cam_estimate data product in the lease domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the leased premises are located. Used to aggregate CAM estimates across all tenants within a building or complex.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: CAM estimates are prepared from building-level operating budgets. Multi-building assets require building-specific CAM pools. Building-level CAM budget-to-actual variance reports and annual estimate pr']
  cam_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.cam_pool. Business justification: CAM estimates are budgeted charges for a specific CAM pool. The estimate calculation requires pool-level configuration including allocation method, expense caps, gross-up thresholds, and included/excl']
  cam_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annual CAM reconciliation record that settled this estimate period. Null until the reconciliation is completed. Links the estimate to its corresponding true-up or reconciliation adjustment.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: CAM estimate billing maps to GL accounts for revenue recognition and expense allocation. A proper FK to chart_of_accounts enables automated billing journal entry generation and financial statement lin']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAM estimates are budgeted at the cost center level for property operating expense forecasting and budget-to-actual variance reporting. The gl_account_code and charge_code on cam_estimate are denormal']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: CAM estimate amounts (annual, monthly, PSF) require a validated currency reference for multi-currency property portfolios, tenant billing, and budget-to-actual variance reporting.']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property operating budget from which the estimated total operating expenses were sourced when estimate_basis is \'current_year_budget\'. Links the CAM estimate to the finance.budget product for traceability.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed lease agreement for which this CAM estimate is prepared. Links the estimate to the governing lease instrument.']
  premium_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.premium. Business justification: CAM estimates for the upcoming year incorporate projected insurance premiums as a recoverable expense. Linking cam_estimate to the insurance premium invoice enables property accountants to reconcile e']
  prior_year_estimate_cam_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAM estimate record from the prior year for the same lease. Enables year-over-year comparison, cap calculations, and audit trail continuity.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant entity responsible for paying the estimated CAM charges. Supports tenant-level CAM billing and reconciliation workflows.']
  valuation_tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: CAM Tax Budgeting process: annual CAM estimates for NNN leases use the prior year tax assessment as the basis for projecting the tax component of estimated CAM charges. Property managers reference the']
  admin_fee_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The landlord\'s administrative or management fee percentage added to the CAM expense pool, as permitted by the lease (e.g., 15.0000 = 15% of controllable expenses). Represents the property management overhead charge passed through to tenants.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the property manager or asset manager who approved this CAM estimate for issuance. Supports internal controls and SOX compliance audit trails.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the CAM estimate was formally approved by the authorized property manager or asset manager. Required for SOX internal control documentation.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which estimated CAM charges are billed to the tenant as specified in the lease. Determines how the annual estimate is divided into installment amounts for AR billing.']
  cam_psf_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The estimated CAM charge expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) annual basis. Calculated as estimated_annual_cam divided by tenant_nla_sqft. Used for benchmarking, lease negotiations, and market comparisons.']
  cap_base_year varchar [note: 'The reference year against which CAM cap calculations are measured when cap_type is \'base_year\'. The base year\'s CAM amount serves as the floor for cap computations in subsequent years.']
  cap_rate_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The maximum annual percentage increase in controllable CAM charges permitted under the lease cap provision (e.g., 5.0000 = 5%). Null if cap_type is \'none\'. Used to calculate the capped CAM amount when actual expenses exceed the cap threshold.']
  cap_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a CAM expense cap applies to this tenant\'s estimate and the type of cap structure. Cumulative caps allow unused cap room to carry forward; non-cumulative caps reset annually; base-year caps limit increases relative to a base year. Drives cap calculation logic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CAM estimate record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  estimate_basis varchar [note: 'The methodology used to derive the estimated total operating expenses. Indicates whether the estimate was built from prior year actuals, the current year approved budget, a blended approach, or a management projection. Critical for audit and reconciliation transparency.']
  estimate_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to this CAM estimate record, typically generated by Yardi Voyager. Used in tenant correspondence, billing statements, and audit trails.']
  estimate_period_end date [note: 'The last date of the period covered by this CAM estimate. Defines the boundary for the budgeted operating expense pool and monthly billing cycle.']
  estimate_period_start date [note: 'The first date of the period covered by this CAM estimate. May differ from the calendar year start if the lease commenced mid-year or if a partial-year estimate is issued.']
  estimate_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the CAM estimate record. Controls whether the estimate is eligible for billing. Transitions: draft → pending_approval → approved → issued. Superseded when a revised estimate replaces it.']
  estimate_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year for which this CAM estimate applies (e.g., 2025). Determines the billing period and is used to match estimates against annual reconciliation actuals.']
  estimated_annual_cam decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s estimated annual CAM charge for the estimate period, calculated as estimated_total_opex multiplied by pro_rata_share_pct, adjusted for any caps, exclusions, or gross-up provisions. This is the total annual amount the tenant is expected to pay.']
  estimated_monthly_cam decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monthly CAM installment amount billed to the tenant, derived by dividing the estimated annual CAM charge by the number of billing months in the estimate period. This is the amount posted to the tenant\'s AR ledger each month prior to annual reconciliation.']
  estimated_total_opex decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total budgeted operating expenses for the property or CAM pool for the estimate period, before applying tenant pro-rata share. Represents the gross expense pool from which each tenant\'s share is derived. Expressed in the property\'s functional currency.']
  excluded_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total dollar amount of operating expenses excluded from the CAM pool for this tenant\'s estimate per lease exclusion provisions. Provides transparency into the gross-to-net expense pool adjustment.']
  exclusions_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lease-specific CAM exclusions (e.g., capital expenditures, management fees, ground lease payments, depreciation) have been applied to reduce the expense pool for this tenant\'s estimate. True = exclusions were applied; False = no exclusions.']
  first_billing_date date [note: 'The date on which the first CAM installment under this estimate is scheduled to be billed to the tenant. Marks the start of the billing cycle for this estimate period.']
  gross_up_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The occupancy gross-up percentage applied to variable operating expenses when the building is not fully occupied (e.g., 95.0000 = 95% occupancy gross-up). Normalizes CAM expenses to a fully-occupied basis to prevent tenants from benefiting from vacancy. Null if no gross-up applies.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the annual CAM reconciliation for this estimate period has been completed. True = reconciliation finalized; False = reconciliation pending. Used to filter open estimates in billing and reporting workflows.']
  issued_date date [note: 'The date on which the approved CAM estimate was formally issued or communicated to the tenant. Triggers the start of monthly CAM billing in Yardi Voyager.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for property manager or asset manager annotations regarding this CAM estimate, such as special assumptions, tenant negotiations, or unusual expense items included in the pool.']
  pool_total_gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the CAM expense pool (building or defined pool) used as the denominator in the pro-rata share calculation. Captures the denominator at the time the estimate is prepared, which may change year-over-year.']
  prior_year_actual_cam decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s actual CAM charge from the immediately preceding year, as determined by the annual reconciliation. Used as the baseline when estimate_basis is \'prior_year_actuals\' and for year-over-year variance analysis.']
  pro_rata_share_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'The tenant\'s proportionate share of the CAM expense pool, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.125000 = 12.5%). Typically calculated as the tenant\'s Net Leasable Area (NLA) divided by the total leasable area of the building or CAM pool. Sourced from the lease agreement.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision counter for this CAM estimate within the same estimate year and lease. Version 0 is the original estimate; subsequent revisions increment this counter. Enables tracking of mid-year estimate adjustments.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why this estimate was revised (e.g., \'Lease amendment changed NLA\', \'Budget reforecast Q2\', \'Corrected pro-rata share\'). Populated only when revision_number > 0.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this CAM estimate record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer. Typically \'yardi_voyager\' for CAM estimates.']
  tenant_nla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The net leasable area (NLA) of the tenant\'s demised premises in square feet as used in the pro-rata share calculation for this estimate period. May differ from the lease NLA if a lease amendment has changed the premises size.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CAM estimate record was last modified. Updated on every revision, status change, or data correction. Supports audit trail and change detection in the Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Annual or periodic CAM estimate record establishing the budgeted CAM charge to be billed to a tenant for the upcoming period. Stores estimate year, estimated total operating expenses, tenant pro-rata share percentage, estimated monthly CAM charge, and the basis for the estimate (prior year actuals, budget). Sourced from Yardi Voyager budgeting module. Feeds monthly CAM billing prior to annual reconciliation.'
}

Table lease.ti_allowance {
  ti_allowance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ti_allowance']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.amendment. Business justification: TI allowances are frequently revised or introduced via lease amendments (e.g., expansion amendments granting additional TI). amendment has ti_allowance_amount and ti_allowance_psf fields confirming th']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the tenant improvement work is being performed. Enables property-level TI spend reporting.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPEX project record in the maintenance domain tracking the TI construction project. Links TI allowance to capital project cost management.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: TI allowances post to specific GL accounts (lease incentive asset, leasehold improvement capex). The gl_account_code on ti_allowance is a denormalized reference; a proper FK enables automated GL posti']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Tenant improvement allowance disbursements are contingent on building permits and certificates of occupancy. Linking TI allowance to the compliance permit enables milestone-based disbursement tracking']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: TI allowance disbursements are tracked against cost centers for capex budgeting, NOI impact analysis, and ASC 842 lease incentive accounting. The cost_center_code on ti_allowance is a denormalized ref']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: TI allowance budgets, disbursements, and loan structures are denominated in a specific currency. Multi-currency portfolios require validated currency reference for CAPEX tracking, ASC842 lease incenti']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: TI allowance commitments are tracked against capex budgets for property-level capital expenditure planning and budget variance reporting. Real estate asset managers require TI allowance-to-budget link']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed lease agreement to which this TI allowance is attached. Links the TI record to its governing lease instrument.']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: TI allowances fund improvements to a specific physical space. Space-level TI tracking, construction scope management, and capex reporting by space require ti_allowance→space. ti_allowance has asset_id']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant entity receiving the TI allowance. Supports tenant-level TI exposure and portfolio analytics.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: TI allowance milestone-based disbursement process: each disbursement milestone is triggered by completion of specific construction work orders. Property managers link TI allowance records to the work ']
  allowance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TI allowance record. Tracks progression from negotiation through full disbursement or expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|active|disbursing|completed|expired|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the TI allowance structure: landlord_work (landlord constructs improvements), tenant_work (tenant constructs with reimbursement), turnkey (landlord delivers finished space), cash_allowance (cash paid to tenant), loan_structured (TI structured as amortizing loan). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: landlord_work|tenant_work|turnkey|cash_allowance|loan_structured — promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the landlord representative (Asset Manager or Property Manager) who approved the TI allowance and construction plans.']
  approved_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount of TI expenditure formally approved by the landlord against submitted tenant work scopes and contractor bids. May be less than or equal to total_budget_amount.']
  asc842_lease_incentive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TI allowance is classified as a lease incentive under FASB ASC 842, requiring recognition as a reduction to the right-of-use asset on the lessee\'s balance sheet.']
  construction_completion_date date [note: 'Actual or projected date on which TI construction work was or is expected to be substantially completed. Triggers final disbursement eligibility.']
  construction_start_date date [note: 'Actual or scheduled date on which TI construction work commenced in the leased premises. Sourced from Procore project schedule.']
  coo_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) was issued by the relevant authority for the TI-improved premises. Often a prerequisite for final TI disbursement.']
  disbursed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount actually paid out by the landlord to the tenant or contractor as TI reimbursement. Represents cash outflow against the TI commitment.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which TI funds are released to the tenant or contractor: lump_sum (single payment), milestone_based (tied to construction milestones), invoice_reimbursement (per submitted invoices), rent_credit (applied as rent offset), loan_advance (structured as a loan draw). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lump_sum|milestone_based|invoice_reimbursement|rent_credit|loan_advance — promote to reference product]']
  disbursement_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of disbursement milestones defined in the TI disbursement schedule. Applicable when disbursement_method is milestone_based.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the TI allowance becomes binding and eligible for disbursement, typically aligned with lease commencement or a negotiated milestone date.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date by which the tenant must complete TI work and submit all disbursement requests. Unused allowance after this date is typically forfeited per lease terms.']
  ifrs16_lease_incentive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TI allowance is classified as a lease incentive under IFRS 16, requiring adjustment to the lease liability measurement for international reporting entities.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative dollar amount of TI-related invoices submitted by the tenant or contractor to the landlord for reimbursement. Sourced from Procore and Yardi AP module.']
  is_loan_structured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the TI allowance is structured as an amortizing loan to the tenant rather than a grant. When true, amortization terms apply and the TI is treated as a lease incentive receivable under ASC 842.']
  landlord_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the landlord formally approved the tenant\'s TI construction plans and budget. Required before disbursement can commence.']
  landlord_work_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the improvements to be constructed or funded by the landlord as part of the TI package. Sourced from the lease work letter exhibit.']
  leasable_area_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net leasable area of the demised premises in square feet used as the basis for calculating the total TI budget from the PSF rate. Sourced from the lease exhibit.']
  lien_waiver_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required lien waivers have been received from contractors and subcontractors prior to the most recent TI disbursement.']
  lien_waiver_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lien waivers from contractors and subcontractors are required as a condition of each TI disbursement. Standard risk management practice in commercial real estate construction.']
  loan_amortization_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the TI loan is amortized through rent payments or separate repayment schedule. Applicable only when is_loan_structured is true.']
  loan_commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the TI loan amortization begins. Typically aligned with lease commencement or first disbursement date. Applicable only when is_loan_structured is true.']
  loan_interest_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the TI loan balance when the allowance is structured as an amortizing loan. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.065 = 6.5%). Applicable only when is_loan_structured is true.']
  milestones_completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of disbursement milestones that have been completed and verified to date. Used to track TI disbursement progress against the agreed schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or exceptions related to the TI allowance not captured in structured fields. Sourced from Yardi Voyager or Procore project notes.']
  procore_project_code varchar [note: 'External project identifier in Procore Construction Management system tracking the TI construction project associated with this allowance. Enables cross-system reconciliation of cost data.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TI allowance record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TI allowance record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Supports change tracking and incremental processing.']
  remaining_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining undisbursed TI allowance balance calculated as total_budget_amount minus disbursed_amount. Represents the landlord\'s outstanding TI commitment at any point in time.']
  tenant_work_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the improvements to be constructed by the tenant using the TI allowance. Defines the boundary between landlord and tenant construction responsibilities.']
  ti_allowance_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the TI allowance record, used in lease exhibits, Procore project references, and Yardi Voyager TI tracking. Format: TIA- followed by alphanumeric characters.']
  ti_amount_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'TI allowance expressed as a dollar amount per square foot (PSF) of the leased premises. Standard industry metric used for benchmarking TI generosity across deals and markets.']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total negotiated TI allowance budget in the lease currency. Represents the maximum landlord commitment for tenant improvement expenditure as agreed in the lease exhibit.']
  work_letter_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or exhibit identifier for the work letter attached to the lease that defines the TI scope, budget, and construction obligations. Sourced from DocuSign CLM or lease document management.']
  yardi_ti_code varchar [note: 'TI allowance tracking code assigned in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration module. Used for reconciliation between the Silver layer and the Yardi source system.']

  Note: 'Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance record tracking the negotiated TI budget, disbursement schedule, approved spend, invoiced amounts, and remaining balance for a lease. Captures TI amount PSF, total TI budget, disbursement milestones, landlord work vs. tenant work scope, TI expiry date, and amortization terms if TI is structured as a loan. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and Procore project cost data.'
}

Table lease.snda_agreement {
  snda_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SNDA agreement record. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property encumbered by the lender\'s security interest and covered by this SNDA agreement.']
  debt_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.debt_facility. Business justification: SNDA agreements are executed specifically in connection with a debt facility — the lender named in the SNDA is the lender on the specific loan. Lenders require SNDA tracking per facility for loan serv']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Business justification: SNDAs are executed in connection with specific loan/debt instruments. Linking snda_agreement to debt_instrument enables lender consent tracking for loan covenant compliance, refinancing workflows, and']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: SNDA agreements are legal instruments with specific document type classifications governing retention periods, notarization requirements, recording obligations, and workflow templates. reference.docum']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed lease agreement to which this SNDA is attached. Links the SNDA to its governing lease record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lender or superior interest holder (mortgagee, deed-of-trust beneficiary, or mezzanine lender) who is a party to this SNDA.']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner. Business justification: SNDAs are tripartite instruments executed directly by the owner/landlord entity alongside tenant and lender. Real estate legal and compliance teams track SNDA execution status per owner entity for len']
  prior_snda_snda_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predecessor SNDA agreement that this record supersedes, enabling version chain tracking for amended or restated SNDAs.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant entity that is a signatory to this SNDA agreement and whose lease protections are governed by it.']
  transaction_financing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.financing. Business justification: SNDAs are executed specifically in connection with a financing instrument — the lender requires subordination of the lease to their mortgage. Linking snda_agreement to the financing record enables len']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SNDA agreement. \'draft\' indicates preparation stage; \'pending_execution\' indicates awaiting signatures; \'executed\' indicates fully signed and binding; \'expired\' indicates past the expiration date; \'released\' indicates lender has released the encumbrance; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer SNDA.']
  attornment_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific conditions and obligations governing the tenant\'s attornment to a successor landlord, including any limitations on the successor\'s liability for prior landlord obligations.']
  attornment_obligated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant is obligated to attorn to (recognize and accept) a new landlord following a foreclosure or deed-in-lieu event, continuing to perform lease obligations under the successor owner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SNDA agreement record was first created in the system, sourced from DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier assigned by DocuSign CLM for the electronic execution of this SNDA agreement. Used to retrieve the signed document and audit trail from the DocuSign platform.']
  docusign_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the DocuSign envelope for this SNDA agreement. \'sent\' indicates dispatched to signatories; \'delivered\' indicates viewed by at least one signatory; \'completed\' indicates all parties have signed; \'declined\' indicates a signatory declined to sign; \'voided\' indicates the envelope was cancelled.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the SNDA agreement\'s protections and obligations become operative. May differ from execution date if the agreement specifies a future effective date.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties (landlord, tenant, and lender) fully executed the SNDA agreement, making it legally binding. This is the principal real-world event date for this agreement.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the SNDA agreement expires or is released, typically coinciding with loan maturity, payoff, or refinancing. Null if the SNDA remains in force for the life of the loan.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent jurisdiction) whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this SNDA agreement, as specified in the agreement\'s governing law clause.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal asset management or legal team notes regarding negotiation history, special conditions, or follow-up actions related to this SNDA agreement. Not shared externally.']
  landlord_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the landlord (property owner or authorized representative) executed their signature on the SNDA agreement.']
  lease_modification_restriction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SNDA agreement restricts the landlord and tenant from amending, modifying, or terminating the lease without the lender\'s prior written consent.']
  lender_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certain lease actions (e.g., assignment, subletting, early termination, rent abatement) require the lender\'s prior written consent under the terms of this SNDA.']
  lender_cure_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days granted to the lender to cure a landlord default under the lease before the tenant may exercise any remedies, as specified in the SNDA agreement.']
  lender_liability_carveouts varchar [note: 'Description of specific landlord obligations that the lender or successor landlord is not required to assume upon foreclosure, such as prior TI allowances owed, security deposit obligations, or pre-foreclosure defaults.']
  lender_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the lender (or authorized lender representative) executed their signature on the SNDA agreement.']
  loan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying loan or debt instrument secured by the property. Determines the applicable SNDA requirements and servicer protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conventional|cmbs|mezzanine|construction|bridge|life_company|agency|preferred_equity — promote to reference product]']
  non_disturbance_conditions varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any conditions or qualifications attached to the lender\'s non-disturbance covenant, such as tenant not being in default, lease being in full force, or specific carve-outs negotiated by the lender.']
  non_disturbance_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lender has granted the tenant a non-disturbance covenant, ensuring the tenant\'s right to quiet enjoyment and continued occupancy will not be disturbed in the event of a foreclosure, provided the tenant is not in default.']
  notarization_date date [note: 'Date on which the SNDA agreement was notarized. Null if notarization is not required or has not yet occurred.']
  notarization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SNDA agreement requires notarization of one or more party signatures as a condition of enforceability under applicable state law.']
  notice_to_lender_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant is required to send copies of default notices and other material lease communications directly to the lender under the terms of this SNDA.']
  recording_date date [note: 'Date on which the SNDA agreement was recorded with the applicable county recorder or register of deeds. Null if recording is not required or has not yet occurred.']
  recording_instrument_number varchar [note: 'The instrument number, document number, or book-and-page reference assigned by the county recorder upon recording of the SNDA agreement in the public land records.']
  recording_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SNDA agreement must be recorded in the applicable county land records to provide constructive notice to third parties.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the lender formally released the SNDA agreement, typically upon loan payoff, refinancing, or property disposition. Null if the agreement has not been released.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the release of the SNDA agreement. \'loan_payoff\' indicates the underlying loan was fully repaid; \'refinancing\' indicates the property was refinanced with a new lender; \'property_sale\' indicates the property was sold; \'lease_termination\' indicates the underlying lease was terminated; \'lender_waiver\' indicates the lender voluntarily waived the SNDA.']
  security_deposit_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SNDA agreement includes a provision requiring the lender or successor landlord to honor the tenant\'s security deposit held by the prior landlord.']
  snda_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this SNDA agreement, used for cross-referencing with DocuSign CLM, lender records, and loan closing documents.']
  snda_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SNDA agreement by form or structure. \'standard\' is a balanced landlord form; \'lender_form\' is the lender\'s prescribed form; \'tenant_negotiated\' reflects material tenant modifications; \'cmbs\' is specific to Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities loan structures; \'mezzanine\' applies to mezzanine debt structures.']
  snda_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this SNDA agreement, used to track amendments or restatements. For example, \'v1.0\' for the original, \'v2.0\' for a restated version following loan modification.']
  subordination_acknowledged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant has formally acknowledged that their lease interest is subordinate to the lender\'s mortgage or deed of trust. True when the subordination clause has been agreed to by the tenant.']
  tenant_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant (or authorized tenant representative) executed their signature on the SNDA agreement.']
  ti_allowance_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SNDA agreement includes a provision protecting the tenant\'s right to receive any outstanding Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance from a successor landlord following foreclosure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SNDA agreement record was last modified in the system. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Subordination, Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement record linking a lease to a specific lender or superior interest holder. Captures SNDA execution date, lender identity, loan reference number, non-disturbance protections granted, subordination acknowledgment, attornment obligations, SNDA expiration or release date, and DocuSign execution status. Required for institutional lenders, CMBS-financed properties, and mezzanine debt structures. Sourced from DocuSign CLM.'
}

Table lease.clause {
  clause_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clause']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.amendment. Business justification: clause.amendment_reference is a denormalized STRING field referencing an amendment. Replacing it with a proper FK amendment_id → lease.amendment.amendment_id normalizes this relationship. Lease clause']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent executed lease agreement from which this clause is extracted. Links the clause to its governing lease instrument.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Lease clauses (exclusivity, co-tenancy, termination options, ADA accommodations) are often required by or must comply with specific regulatory obligations. Lease abstraction teams link clauses to gove']
  related_clause_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this clause to a related or superseding clause within the same lease (e.g., an amendment clause that replaces an original clause, or a co-tenancy clause linked to an exclusivity clause). Null if no related clause.']
  renewal_option_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.renewal_option. Business justification: Lease clauses of type \'renewal_option\' correspond to specific renewal_option records. Linking clause.renewal_option_id → lease.renewal_option.renewal_option_id connects the granular clause text (claus']
  termination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.termination. Business justification: Lease clauses of type \'termination_option\' or \'early_termination\' correspond to termination events when exercised. Linking clause.termination_id → lease.termination.termination_id connects the clause ']
  abstract_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this clause was abstracted or sourced (DocuSign CLM, Yardi Voyager lease abstract, MRI Software, manual entry, or other).']
  abstracted_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the individual or team responsible for abstracting this clause from the source lease document. Supports accountability and data quality review.']
  abstracted_date date [note: 'Date on which this clause was abstracted from the source lease document and entered into the data system. Supports data quality auditing and abstraction workflow tracking.']
  area_threshold_sqf decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum or maximum square footage threshold relevant to this clause (e.g., co-tenancy anchor must occupy at least 50,000 SQF, expansion option covers up to 5,000 SQF of adjacent space). Expressed in square feet (SQF).']
  asc842_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this clause has a material impact on ASC 842 lease accounting classification, measurement, or disclosure (e.g., a purchase option, termination option, or variable rent clause that affects the right-of-use asset or lease liability calculation). True = material ASC 842 impact.']
  clause_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clause indicating whether it is currently in force, has been triggered, waived by mutual agreement, expired by its own terms, or superseded by an amendment.']
  clause_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the lease clause by its legal and operational function. Common types include exclusivity, co-tenancy, kick-out, ROFO (Right of First Offer), ROFR (Right of First Refusal), force majeure, assignment, subletting, audit rights, termination option, expansion option, and holdover. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exclusivity|co_tenancy|kick_out|rofo|rofr|force_majeure|assignment|subletting|audit_rights|termination_option|expansion_option|holdover|relocation|demolition|condemnation|insurance|indemnification|estoppel|snda|other — promote to reference product]']
  co_tenancy_anchor_tenant varchar [note: 'For co-tenancy clauses, the name of the anchor or key tenant whose continued occupancy is a condition of the co-tenancy provision. Vacancy or departure of this tenant may trigger rent abatement or termination rights.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this clause record was first created in the silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and ASC 842 / IFRS 16 disclosure tracking.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM corresponding to the executed lease document containing this clause. Enables direct traceability to the digitally signed source document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this clause becomes operative and enforceable. May differ from the lease commencement date if the clause has a deferred or conditional effective date.']
  exclusivity_use_category varchar [note: 'For exclusivity clauses, specifies the protected retail or business use category that the tenant holds exclusive rights to operate within the property or defined trade area (e.g., \'Coffee and Espresso Beverages\', \'Full-Service Pharmacy\'). Null for non-exclusivity clause types.']
  exercise_date date [note: 'Date on which the clause was formally exercised by the entitled party (e.g., tenant exercised ROFR, landlord exercised kick-out). Null if not yet exercised.']
  exercising_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party holds the right to exercise or invoke this clause — landlord, tenant, either party, or a specified third party (e.g., guarantor).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this clause ceases to be operative by its own terms. Null if the clause runs for the full remaining lease term or is perpetual in nature.']
  ifrs16_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this clause has a material impact on IFRS 16 lease accounting classification, measurement, or disclosure. True = material IFRS 16 impact. Supports dual-standard reporting for international portfolios.']
  is_mutual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rights or obligations under this clause apply mutually to both landlord and tenant (True) or are unilateral in favor of one party (False). Relevant for indemnification, audit rights, and assignment clauses.']
  is_negotiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this clause was specifically negotiated and customized from the landlord\'s standard lease form (True) or is a standard boilerplate provision (False). Supports risk profiling and lease negotiation benchmarking.']
  is_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clause has been triggered or activated based on a qualifying event (e.g., co-tenancy failure, kick-out threshold breach, force majeure event). True = currently triggered; False = not triggered.']
  lease_section_number varchar [note: 'Section, article, or exhibit number within the lease document where this clause is located (e.g., \'Article 14\', \'Section 22.3\', \'Exhibit D\'). Enables direct cross-reference to the source legal document.']
  monetary_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount threshold that governs the activation or measurement of this clause (e.g., minimum sales volume for co-tenancy, kick-out sales threshold PSF, TI allowance cap for assignment). Expressed in the lease currency.']
  monetary_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the monetary threshold value (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, legal counsel observations, or operational notes regarding this clause that are not captured in structured fields. Used by property managers (PM) and asset managers (AM) for context.']
  notice_deadline_date date [note: 'Calculated or contractually specified deadline by which notice must be delivered to exercise or respond to this clause. Supports proactive portfolio-wide deadline monitoring.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days\' advance written notice required to exercise, invoke, or respond to this clause. Critical for kick-out, ROFO, ROFR, and termination option clauses.']
  priority_order varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of this clause relative to other clauses of the same type on the same lease, used when multiple clauses of the same type exist (e.g., multiple ROFR provisions with different priority rankings). Lower number = higher priority.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code or section reference identifying this clause within the lease document (e.g., Section 12.4, Exhibit B-3). Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager lease abstract.']
  rent_abatement_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction in base rent applicable when this clause is triggered (e.g., co-tenancy failure entitles tenant to 50% rent abatement). Expressed as a percentage of base rent.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Portfolio risk classification assigned to this clause based on its potential financial or operational impact if triggered. Used by asset managers (AM) for portfolio-wide clause risk screening and prioritization.']
  termination_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount payable as a termination fee or penalty if this clause is exercised (e.g., kick-out fee, early termination payment). Expressed in the lease currency.']
  text_summary varchar [note: 'Abstracted plain-language summary of the clause\'s key provisions, obligations, and rights. Captured during lease abstraction from DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager. Not the full legal text but sufficient for portfolio screening and risk management.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable short title or heading of the clause as it appears in the lease document (e.g., \'Tenant Exclusivity Provision\', \'Co-Tenancy Requirement\'). Used for portfolio-wide clause screening and reporting.']
