  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: A sublease covers a specific physical space, often a portion of the head lease space. Space-level sublease tracking for occupancy reporting, availability management, and ASC 842 sublease classificatio']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Sublease rent structure (NNN, gross, modified gross) determines ASC842 sublease classification (operating vs. sales-type) and profit/loss on sublease calculations. Domain experts expect lease type ref']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Sublease arrangements must comply with regulatory obligations governing subletting rights, assignment restrictions, and tenant protections under local law. Compliance teams track regulatory constraint']
  rent_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.rent_schedule. Business justification: Subleases have their own rent escalation schedules (sublease_rent_monthly, rent_escalation_type, rent_escalation_rate on sublease). Linking sublease.rent_schedule_id → lease.rent_schedule.rent_schedul']
  sublease_subtenant_profile_tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subtenant entity occupying the subleased premises. The subtenant has a direct contractual relationship with the sublessor, not the landlord.']
  sublease_tenant_id varchar [note: 'FK to tenant.tenant']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific leasable unit or suite being sublet. May represent a partial unit if only a portion of the demised premises is subleased.']
  abstracted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the lease abstraction analyst or team who extracted and entered the sublease terms into the system. Supports data quality auditing and abstraction accountability.']
  abstracted_date date [note: 'Date on which the sublease terms were abstracted and entered into the system. Used for data quality tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  asc842_sublease_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the sublease under FASB ASC 842 from the sublessor\'s perspective as an intermediate lessor. \'operating\' means the sublessor recognizes lease income on a straight-line basis; \'finance\' means the sublessor derecognizes the underlying asset and recognizes a net investment in the sublease.']
  cam_amount_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges payable by the subtenant. Relevant for NNN and modified gross sublease structures. Used for total occupancy cost analysis.']
  cam_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges are applicable to the subtenant under the sublease terms. When true, CAM reconciliation obligations flow through from the head lease.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'The date on which the sublease term begins and the subtenant takes possession of the subleased premises. Used as the effective start date for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 lessor accounting measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sublease record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'The interest rate used to discount future sublease payments for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 net investment measurement. Typically the rate implicit in the sublease or the sublessor\'s incremental borrowing rate.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the DocuSign CLM envelope containing the executed sublease agreement and all associated documents. Enables direct retrieval of the signed sublease document from the contract management system.']
  early_termination_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublease contains an early termination clause allowing either party to terminate the sublease before the scheduled expiration date. Affects ASC 842 lease term determination and WALE calculations.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the sublease agreement was fully executed (signed by all parties) via DocuSign CLM or physical signature. Marks the legally binding commencement of the sublease contract.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which the sublease term ends. Must not extend beyond the head lease expiration date. Critical for WALT/WALE calculations and portfolio expiry scheduling.']
  free_rent_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of rent abatement granted to the subtenant at the commencement of the sublease. Affects straight-line rent recognition under ASC 842 operating sublease accounting.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent jurisdiction) whose laws govern the sublease agreement. Relevant for dispute resolution, regulatory compliance, and legal enforceability analysis.']
  head_lease_rent_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'The sublessor\'s rent obligation under the head lease expressed on a PSF basis for the subleased area. Used to calculate profit or loss on sublease relative to the head lease rent obligation.']
  ifrs16_sublease_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the sublease under IFRS 16 from the intermediate lessor\'s perspective. Classification is based on the right-of-use (ROU) asset arising from the head lease, not the underlying asset. \'finance\' requires recognition of a net investment in the sublease.']
  landlord_consent_date date [note: 'The date on which the landlord formally granted written consent to the sublease arrangement. Required for sublease validity where landlord consent is mandated by the head lease.']
  landlord_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the head lease requires the landlord\'s written consent before the sublease can be executed. Derived from the head lease assignment and subletting clause.']
  landlord_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the landlord\'s consent process for the sublease. \'not_required\' indicates the head lease permits subletting without consent; \'conditional\' indicates consent granted subject to specific conditions.']
  net_investment_in_sublease decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of future sublease payments plus any unguaranteed residual value, recognized on the balance sheet for finance subleases under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Applicable only when asc842_sublease_classification or ifrs16_sublease_classification is \'finance\'.']
  profit_loss_on_sublease decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net financial position of the sublease relative to the head lease rent obligation for the subleased area. Positive value indicates sublease rent exceeds head lease cost (profit); negative value indicates a loss position. Calculated as sublease rent minus allocated head lease rent for the sublet area.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the sublease agreement as recorded in Yardi Voyager or DocuSign CLM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and document retrieval.']
  renewal_option_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sublease includes a renewal option allowing the subtenant to extend the sublease term. Relevant for WALT/WALE calculations and ASC 842 lease term reassessment.']
  rent_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual base rent payable by the subtenant to the sublessor under the sublease agreement. Used for NOI analysis, profit/loss on sublease calculations, and ASC 842 lessor income recognition.']
  rent_escalation_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate applied to base rent escalation where escalation type is \'fixed\' or \'stepped\'. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 = 3%). Used for cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Method by which sublease base rent increases over the sublease term. \'fixed\' applies a fixed percentage; \'cpi\' ties escalation to the Consumer Price Index; \'fmv\' resets to fair market value; \'stepped\' uses pre-agreed step-up amounts; \'none\' indicates flat rent.']
  rent_monthly decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monthly base rent payable by the subtenant. Derived from the annual rent schedule but stored explicitly for billing and cash flow forecasting in Yardi Voyager AR.']
  rent_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Annual base rent expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis for the subleased area. Key metric for market benchmarking against CoStar comparable lease data and head lease PSF comparison.']
  rou_asset_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the head lease Right-of-Use (ROU) asset allocated to the subleased area for ASC 842 / IFRS 16 intermediate lessor accounting. Used to determine the carrying amount of the net investment in a finance sublease or the ROU asset derecognition amount.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash security deposit collected from the subtenant at sublease commencement. Held by the sublessor as collateral against subtenant default. Tracked separately from head lease security deposit obligations.']
  sublease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sublease agreement. \'draft\' indicates the sublease is being negotiated; \'pending_consent\' indicates awaiting landlord approval; \'active\' indicates the sublease is in force; \'expired\' indicates the term has ended; \'terminated\' indicates early termination; \'holdover\' indicates the subtenant remains in possession beyond the sublease expiry.']
  sublease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sublease arrangement. \'full_assignment\' transfers all rights and obligations of the head lease; \'partial_sublease\' sublets only a portion of the demised premises; \'master_sublease\' covers an entire building or multi-unit portfolio; \'short_term_sublease\' is a temporary arrangement typically under 12 months.']
  sublet_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'The net leasable area (NLA) in square feet being subleased to the subtenant. May be less than the total demised premises area under the head lease for partial subleases. Used for PSF rent calculations and GLA reporting.']
  term_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the sublease in months, calculated from commencement to expiration. Used for WALT/WALE analysis and ASC 842 lease term determination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sublease record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  yardi_sublease_ref varchar [note: 'Internal reference code assigned to the sublease record in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the operational system of record.']

  Note: 'Records sublease arrangements where a primary tenant sublets all or part of their demised premises to a subtenant. Captures sublease commencement, expiration, sublet area SQF, sublease rent, subtenant identity, landlord consent date and status, sublease type (full assignment, partial sublease), profit/loss on sublease relative to head lease rent, and ASC 842 sublease classification (operating vs. finance for lessor accounting). Sourced from Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM. Distinct from the head lease agreement; linked to parent agreement via FK.'
}

Table lease.ground_lease {
  ground_lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the ground lease record. Primary key for the ground_lease data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the land parcel or property subject to the ground lease arrangement.']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Ground leases impose development obligations requiring building permits and certificates of occupancy. Linking the ground lease to the compliance permit governing development completion is essential f']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Ground lease rent expense is tracked against cost centers for property-level NOI reporting and budget variance analysis. Real estate controllers need cost center linkage on ground leases to route rent']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Ground lease annual rent, escalations, and ROU asset/liability values require a validated currency reference for multi-currency portfolio reporting and ASC842/IFRS16 lease liability present value calc']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Ground leases are executed by specific legal entities (the lessee/developer entity). Required for ASC 842/IFRS 16 entity-level ROU asset and lease liability reporting, entity-level balance sheet prese']
  lessor_owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landowner (ground lessor) who retains fee simple ownership of the land and leases it to the lessee.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: A ground lease is fundamentally a lease of land encumbering a specific parcel. Ground lease rent roll by parcel, title encumbrance tracking, and development rights analysis require ground_lease→parcel']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.policy. Business justification: Ground leases contractually require the ground lessee to maintain comprehensive property insurance covering leasehold improvements throughout the lease term. Asset managers and lenders must verify the']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Ground leases are fundamental encumbrances affecting portfolio asset leasehold valuation, NAV calculation, financing eligibility, and exit strategy. Investment teams must identify which portfolio asse']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Ground leases have specific lease type classifications (ground lease, leasehold) that drive ASC842/IFRS16 classification, ROU asset measurement, and leasehold financing eligibility. Domain experts exp']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Ground leases are subject to land use regulations, environmental obligations, and development requirements. Compliance teams must link ground leases to governing regulatory obligations for development']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the developer or tenant (ground lessee) who holds the leasehold interest and constructs or operates improvements on the land.']
  tenure_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.tenure_type. Business justification: Ground leases create leasehold interests with specific tenure types affecting financing eligibility, CMBS qualification, and title insurance requirements. reference.tenure_type has ground_lease_applic']
  abstracted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the lease administrator or analyst who performed the lease abstraction. Supports audit trail and quality control for lease data governance.']
  abstracted_date date [note: 'Date on which the ground lease was formally abstracted by the lease administration team, capturing key economic and legal terms into the data system.']
  annual_ground_rent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual base ground rent payable by the lessee to the lessor under the current rent period. Expressed in the lease currency. Foundational input for NOI, DCF, and leasehold valuation models.']
  asc842_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Lease classification under FASB ASC 842 (finance lease or operating lease) as determined by the lessee\'s accounting team. Ground leases with ultra-long terms typically qualify as finance leases. Drives ROU asset and lease liability recognition.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the ground lease term begins and the lessee takes possession of the land. Establishes the start of the lease term for rent, escalation, and ASC 842/IFRS 16 measurement purposes.']
  cpi_base_index decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base CPI index value at lease commencement or last rent reset, used to calculate CPI-linked rent escalations. Applicable when rent_escalation_type is \'cpi\' or \'hybrid\'.']
  cpi_cap_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Maximum percentage cap on CPI-linked rent escalation per review period, protecting the lessee from excessive rent increases. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0500 = 5%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ground lease record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance.']
  development_completion_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the lessee must complete construction of improvements on the leased land, where a development obligation exists. Null if no development obligation is specified.']
  development_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ground lease imposes a contractual obligation on the lessee to construct improvements on the land within a specified timeframe. Failure to develop may trigger default provisions.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Discount rate used to calculate the present value of future ground rent payments for ASC 842/IFRS 16 lease liability measurement. Typically the lessee\'s incremental borrowing rate (IBR) or the rate implicit in the lease. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0450 = 4.50%).']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the DocuSign CLM envelope containing the executed ground lease agreement and all amendments. Enables direct retrieval of the signed document from the contract management system.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the ground lease agreement was fully executed (signed by all parties). Establishes the binding contract date, which may differ from the commencement date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which the ground lease term ends and the land (along with all improvements) reverts to the lessor, absent renewal. Drives WALT/WALE calculations and reversion planning.']
  fmv_reset_frequency_years varchar [note: 'Frequency in years at which ground rent is reset to fair market value, applicable when rent_escalation_type is \'fmv_reset\'. Typically every 20–25 years in long-term ground leases.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or jurisdiction identifier) whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the ground lease agreement.']
  ifrs16_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Lease classification under IASB IFRS 16 for entities reporting under international accounting standards. Determines lessee balance sheet treatment of the right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability.']
  improvement_ownership varchar [note: 'Specifies who owns the improvements (buildings and structures) constructed on the leased land during the term and at reversion. Typically improvements revert to the lessor at lease expiry, which is a key valuation and financing consideration.']
  land_area_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total area of the leased land parcel in square feet (SQF). Used for rent PSF calculations, FAR analysis, and valuation benchmarking.']
  lease_liability_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of future ground rent obligations recognized as a lease liability on the lessee\'s balance sheet under ASC 842 or IFRS 16 at the measurement date.']
  lease_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the ground lease agreement, as recorded in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM. Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ground lease agreement, tracking progression from drafting through execution, active operation, and eventual expiry or termination.']
  leasehold_financing_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lessee is contractually permitted to encumber the leasehold interest with a mortgage or other financing instrument. Critical for lender underwriting and SNDA structuring.']
  lender_cure_rights boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ground lease grants the lessee\'s leasehold lender the right to cure lessee defaults before the lessor may terminate the lease. Essential for leasehold mortgage bankability.']
  new_lease_on_termination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ground lease contains a provision obligating the lessor to enter into a new ground lease with the leasehold lender upon termination of the original lease due to lessee default. A key bankability criterion for CMBS lenders.']
  next_rent_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date of the next ground rent review or escalation event. Used for proactive lease administration and cash flow forecasting.']
  original_term_years varchar [note: 'Total original contractual term of the ground lease in years, typically ranging from 50 to 99 years. Used for valuation, leasehold financing underwriting, and ASC 842/IFRS 16 lease classification.']
  permitted_use varchar [note: 'Description of the permitted use of the leased land as specified in the ground lease agreement (e.g., commercial office development, mixed-use residential, hotel). Restricts the lessee\'s development and operational activities.']
  remaining_term_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of years remaining on the ground lease as of the record\'s last update date. Critical for leasehold mortgage underwriting, valuation, and WALT/WALE reporting.']
  renewal_option_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years of each renewal option period. Combined with renewal_options_count to determine maximum potential lease term for valuation and ASC 842/IFRS 16 lease term assessment.']
  renewal_options_count varchar [note: 'Total number of renewal option periods available to the lessee under the ground lease. Each option extends the lease term and affects WALT/WALE and ASC 842 reasonably-certain assessments.']
  rent_escalation_interval_years varchar [note: 'Number of years between each ground rent escalation event (e.g., every 5 or 10 years). Used to schedule rent review dates and model future cash flows in Argus Enterprise.']
  rent_escalation_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Fixed percentage rate applied to ground rent escalation when rent_escalation_type is \'fixed\' or \'percentage\'. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 = 3%). Null when escalation is CPI or FMV-based.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which ground rent escalates over the lease term. Options include fixed step-up, CPI-linked, fair market value (FMV) reset, percentage of revenue, hybrid, or no escalation. Drives rent forecasting and DCF modeling.']
  reversion_rights_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the lessor\'s reversion rights at lease expiry, including conditions for improvement transfer, demolition obligations, and any compensation provisions.']
  rou_asset_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Carrying value of the right-of-use asset recognized on the lessee\'s balance sheet under ASC 842 or IFRS 16 at the measurement date. Represents the present value of future ground rent payments plus initial direct costs.']
  snda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Subordination, Non-Disturbance, and Attornment (SNDA) agreement has been executed between the ground lessor, lessee, and leasehold lender. Required for leasehold mortgage bankability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ground lease record in the Silver Layer. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing.']
  yardi_lease_code varchar [note: 'Unique lease code assigned to the ground lease record in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration module. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the system of record.']

  Note: 'Master record for ground lease arrangements where the landowner leases land to a developer or tenant who constructs improvements. Captures ground lease term (often 50–99 years), ground rent, rent escalation schedule, reversion rights, leasehold financing provisions, subordination terms (subordinated vs. unsubordinated), ground lessor/lessee identities, and improvement ownership at reversion. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM. Modeled as a distinct product due to unique legal structure, ultra-long duration, leasehold financing implications, and separate valuation methodology.'
}

Table lease.guaranty {
  guaranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the lease guaranty record. Primary key for the guaranty data product.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.amendment. Business justification: guaranty.amendment_reference is a denormalized STRING field. Replacing it with amendment_id → lease.amendment.amendment_id normalizes the relationship between a guaranty and the amendment that introdu']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property associated with the guaranteed lease. Used for portfolio-level credit risk reporting.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Guaranty amounts (guaranteed amount, burn-down schedule, amount recovered) require a validated currency reference for multi-currency portfolios and credit risk reporting on lease guaranty exposure.']
  guarantor_id varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (EIN/TIN) or government-issued registration number for the guarantor entity. Used for credit verification, legal enforcement, and regulatory compliance. For individuals, this may be a masked SSN reference.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial lease agreement this guaranty supports. Links the guaranty obligation to the underlying lease contract.']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner. Business justification: The owner/landlord is the direct beneficiary of every lease guaranty. Real estate asset managers track guaranty exposure, burn-down schedules, and enforcement actions at the owner entity level for cre']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Guaranty enforceability is governed by jurisdiction-specific regulations (NY COVID-era guaranty moratoriums, state-specific personal guaranty laws). Compliance teams must track which regulatory obliga']
  replaced_guaranty_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on guaranty (replaced_guaranty_id)']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant entity whose lease obligations are being guaranteed. The primary obligor under the lease.']
  abstracted_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the lease abstraction analyst or paralegal who extracted and entered the guaranty terms from the executed document into the system of record.']
  abstracted_date date [note: 'Date on which the guaranty terms were abstracted from the executed document and entered into the data system. Used for data quality auditing and lease administration workflow tracking.']
  amount_recovered decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount actually recovered from the guarantor through enforcement, settlement, or voluntary payment. Used to calculate net credit loss on the lease.']
  burn_down_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guaranty includes a contractual burn-down schedule under which the guaranteed amount reduces over time as the tenant demonstrates satisfactory lease performance.']
  burn_down_interval_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in months at which the guaranteed amount is reduced per the burn-down schedule (e.g., 12 for annual reductions). Null if burn-down is not applicable.']
  burn_down_reduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount by which the guaranteed exposure is reduced at each burn-down interval. Used when the burn-down is amount-based rather than percentage-based.']
  burn_down_reduction_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of the original guaranteed amount by which exposure is reduced at each burn-down interval. Used when the burn-down is percentage-based rather than fixed-amount-based. Stored as a decimal (e.g., 0.25 = 25%).']
  burn_down_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the guaranty burn-down schedule commences. Typically tied to a lease anniversary or a performance milestone such as rent payment history.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guaranty record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  credit_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit risk classification assigned to the guarantor based on financial statement review, credit bureau data, and underwriting analysis. Drives portfolio-level credit risk reporting and reserve calculations.']
  current_guaranteed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present outstanding guaranteed amount after applying any burn-down reductions, partial releases, or enforcement recoveries. Reflects the landlord\'s current credit risk exposure under this guaranty.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the DocuSign CLM envelope containing the executed guaranty instrument. Enables direct retrieval of the signed document from the contract lifecycle management system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the guaranty obligation becomes operative and enforceable. May differ from execution date if the guaranty contains a deferred commencement provision.']
  enforcement_date date [note: 'Date on which the landlord formally initiated enforcement action against the guarantor (e.g., date of demand letter or filing of legal claim).']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any enforcement action taken against the guarantor. \'demand_issued\' indicates a formal demand letter has been sent; \'litigation\' indicates legal proceedings are active; \'recovered\' indicates full or partial recovery has been achieved.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the guaranty instrument was fully executed (all parties signed). This is the principal business event date establishing the guaranty as legally binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the guaranty obligation terminates by its own terms, independent of lease expiry. Null for guaranties that run co-terminus with the lease or until full performance.']
  financial_covenant_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guaranty requires the guarantor to maintain specified financial covenants (e.g., minimum net worth, minimum liquidity ratio, maximum leverage). Critical for ongoing credit risk monitoring.']
  financial_statement_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency at which the guarantor must deliver financial statements to the landlord for covenant compliance monitoring.']
  good_guy_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of advance written notice the tenant must provide to the landlord to trigger the good-guy guaranty release clause. Applicable only when guaranty_type is \'good_guy\'.']
  good_guy_vacate_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tenant vacated the premises and surrendered possession, triggering the good-guy guaranty release. Populated upon confirmed surrender.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent jurisdiction code) specifying the governing law under which the guaranty instrument is interpreted and enforced. Critical for legal enforcement strategy.']
  guaranteed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary exposure covered by the guaranty at inception. For full guaranties, this equals the total lease obligation value; for limited guaranties, this is the capped dollar amount. Expressed in the lease currency.']
  guaranteed_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of lease obligations covered by the guaranty. Used for limited and rolling guaranty types where exposure is defined by a time window rather than a fixed dollar amount.']
  guarantor_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the guarantor as stated in the executed guaranty instrument. For corporate guarantors, this is the registered entity name; for individuals, the full legal name as on government-issued ID.']
  guarantor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guarantor entity. \'individual\' is a natural person; \'corporate_parent\' is the tenant\'s parent company; \'affiliate\' is a related entity; \'third_party_entity\' is an unrelated corporate guarantor; \'personal\' is a principal/owner of the tenant entity providing a personal guarantee.']
  guaranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guaranty obligation. \'active\' indicates the guaranty is in force; \'burned_down\' indicates the guaranteed amount has been reduced per schedule; \'enforced\' indicates a claim has been made against the guarantor; \'released\' indicates the landlord has formally discharged the guarantor.']
  guaranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guaranty structure. \'full\' covers all lease obligations for the full term; \'limited\' caps exposure by amount or period; \'good_guy\' releases guarantor upon tenant vacating and providing notice; \'rolling\' covers a fixed rolling window of rent; \'springing\' activates only upon a defined trigger event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full|limited|good_guy|rolling|springing|burn_down|completion — promote to reference product]']
  last_financial_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently received guarantor financial statement. Used to track compliance with financial reporting covenants and assess current creditworthiness.']
  min_net_worth_covenant decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum net worth the guarantor must maintain throughout the guaranty term as a financial covenant condition. Breach of this threshold may constitute a guaranty default or trigger a springing guaranty.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, enforcement history narrative, or legal counsel observations regarding this guaranty that do not fit structured fields.']
  personal_guarantee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guaranty is a personal guarantee provided by an individual (principal, officer, or owner of the tenant entity) as opposed to a corporate or entity guarantee. Drives different enforcement and credit risk treatment.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the guaranty instrument, typically assigned by DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager at execution. Used for cross-system reconciliation and legal document referencing.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the landlord formally released the guarantor from all obligations under the guaranty instrument. Populated when guaranty_status transitions to \'released\'.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the formal release of the guarantor. Tracks whether release was triggered by lease expiry, early termination, good-guy clause activation, full burn-down, landlord waiver, or lease assignment to a creditworthy assignee.']
  springing_trigger_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the condition(s) that activate a springing guaranty (e.g., tenant credit rating downgrade below investment grade, tenant default on another obligation, change of control). Applicable only when guaranty_type is \'springing\'.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this guaranty record in the Silver layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance.']
  yardi_guaranty_ref varchar [note: 'Source system reference identifier for this guaranty record in Yardi Voyager. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver layer and the operational system of record.']

  Note: 'Tracks lease guaranty and guarantor obligations for commercial lease agreements. Captures guarantor identity (individual, corporate parent, or entity), guaranty type (full, limited, good-guy, rolling), guaranteed amount or period, burn-down schedule, guarantor financial covenants, guaranty expiration triggers, and enforcement status. Critical for landlord credit risk management on commercial leases where tenant creditworthiness depends on a third-party guarantee. Sourced from DocuSign CLM and Yardi Voyager.'
}

Table lease.meter_allocation {
  meter_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the meter allocation record in the lakehouse. Primary key.']
  energy_meter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the energy meter allocated to this lease agreement']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement that has billing responsibility for this meter allocation']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate the meter_allocation_pct for this lease. Direct = 100% allocation to single tenant. Pro-Rata = allocated based on tenant\'s proportionate share. Square Footage = allocated based on leased area. Occupancy = allocated based on headcount or usage patterns. Custom = negotiated allocation per lease terms.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the meter allocation. Active allocations drive utility billing. Inactive allocations are historical. Pending allocations are configured but not yet effective. Disputed allocations are under tenant dispute resolution.']
  billing_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenant\'s billing responsibility for this meter ends under this allocation. Null for active allocations. Used to close out meter allocations when leases expire or when meter configurations change.']
  billing_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenant\'s billing responsibility for this meter begins under this allocation. May differ from lease commencement if meter allocation is established mid-term or if utility billing is delayed.']
  cam_billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether utility consumption from this meter allocation is billable to the tenant as part of Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges or as a direct utility reimbursement. Drives CAM reconciliation and tenant billing workflows. Determined by lease terms (NNN, gross, modified gross) and meter location (common area vs. demised space).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter allocation record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  is_primary_meter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this meter is the primary utility meter for this lease agreement for the given utility type. Used to distinguish main suite meters from supplemental or shared meters. Drives default meter selection in utility billing workflows.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this meter allocation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special allocation terms, tenant disputes, meter configuration changes, or other contextual information relevant to this meter allocation. Used by property managers during CAM reconciliation and tenant billing.']
  pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage of meter consumption allocated to this lease agreement, expressed as a decimal (0.0000 to 1.0000). Used for shared meters serving multiple tenants (e.g., common area HVAC, shared water meters). Sum of allocation percentages across all leases for a given meter should equal 1.0 for fully allocated meters.']
  utility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the utility commodity measured by this meter allocation. Drives cost allocation rules, CAM reconciliation logic, and sustainability reporting. Denormalized from energy_meter.meter_type for query performance in billing workflows.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation relationship between lease agreements and energy meters in sub-metered commercial properties. It captures utility cost allocation, billing responsibility, and CAM reconciliation data. Each record links one lease agreement to one energy meter with allocation percentages, billing periods, and utility type classifications that exist only in the context of this relationship. Supports tenant utility billing, CAM reconciliation, and ESG reporting workflows.. Existence Justification: In sub-metered commercial real estate, one lease agreement can be responsible for multiple utility meters (e.g., electric, gas, water, HVAC serving the leased suite), and one meter can serve multiple lease agreements in shared spaces (e.g., common area HVAC, shared water meters in multi-tenant buildings). Property managers actively manage meter allocations as a distinct business process, configuring allocation percentages, billing periods, and CAM billable flags when leases are executed and updating them during lease modifications or meter reconfigurations.'
}

Table lease.regulatory_applicability {
  regulatory_applicability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lease-regulatory obligation applicability record.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement record to which this regulatory obligation applies.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the regulatory obligation that applies to this lease agreement.']
  applicability_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this regulatory obligation ceased to apply to this lease agreement (e.g., due to lease expiration, property sale, exemption granted, or regulation superseded). Null if currently applicable.']
  applicability_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this regulatory obligation became applicable to this specific lease agreement. May differ from the obligation\'s general effective_date based on lease commencement, property type, or jurisdiction-specific triggers.']
  assigned_compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer responsible for monitoring this specific lease-obligation applicability relationship.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this lease agreement with respect to this specific regulatory obligation. Tracks whether the lease is meeting the requirements of this obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this applicability record was created in the system.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why this lease agreement is exempt from this regulatory obligation, if applicable. Includes legal basis, grandfathering provisions, or jurisdictional exclusions. Null if no exemption applies.']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'The date on which compliance with this obligation was most recently verified for this lease agreement.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The cadence at which compliance with this obligation must be monitored for this specific lease agreement. May differ from the obligation\'s general renewal_frequency based on risk level, property type, or lease terms.']
  next_compliance_check_date date [note: 'The date by which the next compliance verification must be completed for this lease-obligation combination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the applicability of this obligation to this lease, including special circumstances, remediation plans, or audit findings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this applicability record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the applicability relationship between lease agreements and regulatory obligations. It captures which regulatory obligations apply to which lease agreements, when the obligation became applicable, current compliance status, any exemptions granted, and monitoring requirements. Each record links one lease agreement to one regulatory obligation with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific applicability relationship.. Existence Justification: In real estate operations, a single lease agreement is simultaneously subject to multiple regulatory obligations (rent control, fair housing, ADA accessibility, environmental compliance, zoning, fire safety, etc.). Each regulatory obligation applies to many lease agreements across the portfolio. Compliance teams actively manage these applicability relationships, tracking when each obligation became applicable to each lease, monitoring compliance status, documenting exemptions, and scheduling compliance checks at lease-specific frequencies.'
}

Table lease.enrollment {
  enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the lease compliance program enrollment record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the enrollment association.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance program in which the lease agreement is enrolled']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement enrolled in the compliance program']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'The date on which the lease agreement was formally enrolled into the compliance program. Used for program eligibility verification, compliance period calculations, and audit trail purposes.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lease enrollment in the compliance program. Values: active (enrolled and compliant), suspended (temporarily non-compliant), terminated (enrollment ended), pending (enrollment in process), exempt (granted exemption). Drives compliance reporting and remediation workflows.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease agreement has been granted a formal exemption from certain requirements of this compliance program. TRUE indicates exemption granted (with details typically stored in linked exemption documentation). Used for regulatory reporting and compliance scoring adjustments.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance review or audit of this lease agreement under this compliance program was completed. Used for compliance history tracking and review frequency monitoring.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of this lease agreement under this specific compliance program. Used for compliance calendar management and proactive monitoring. Distinct from program-level next_review_date which applies to the program as a whole.']
  program_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing this specific lease agreement\'s compliance posture within this specific compliance program. Calculated based on program-specific requirements, assessment results, and remediation status. Distinct from the program-level overall_compliance_score which aggregates across all enrolled leases.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease agreement\'s enrollment in this compliance program was formally terminated or expired. NULL for active enrollments. Used for historical compliance reporting and program lifecycle tracking.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Categorical or narrative explanation for why the enrollment was terminated (e.g., lease expired, program requirements no longer applicable, voluntary withdrawal, non-compliance). NULL for active enrollments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the enrollment relationship between lease agreements and compliance programs. It captures the operational reality that a single lease agreement can be enrolled in multiple compliance programs simultaneously (e.g., affordable housing, rent stabilization, LIHTC, green lease certification), and each compliance program covers many lease agreements across the portfolio. Each record links one lease agreement to one compliance program with enrollment-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, including enrollment dates, compliance status, program-specific scores, review schedules, and exemption flags tracked by compliance teams for regulatory reporting and program management.. Existence Justification: In real estate compliance operations, lease agreements are routinely enrolled in multiple compliance programs simultaneously—a single affordable housing lease may be enrolled in HUD Fair Housing, local rent stabilization, LIHTC tax credit, and green lease certification programs at the same time. Each compliance program covers hundreds or thousands of lease agreements across the portfolio. Compliance teams actively manage these enrollments as operational records, tracking enrollment dates, program-specific compliance scores, review schedules, exemptions, and status changes for regulatory reporting and audit purposes.'
}

Table lease.cam_charge_allocation {
  cam_charge_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this CAM charge allocation record']
  billable_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific billable maintenance charge being included in this CAM reconciliation']
  cam_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the annual CAM reconciliation record for which this charge is being allocated']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of this charge allocated to this specific tenant reconciliation after applying the pro-rata share and allocation method. Calculated value stored for audit trail and performance.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate this specific charge to the tenant in this reconciliation context. May differ from the general CAM pool allocation method if this is a direct charge or special allocation.']
  charge_inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether this charge is included in the final reconciliation calculation. Tracks workflow state from initial inclusion through tenant dispute resolution.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant has disputed the inclusion or allocation of this specific charge in this reconciliation. Used to track dispute resolution workflow.']
  dispute_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing tenant dispute details, property manager response, and resolution outcome for this specific charge allocation.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'If charge_inclusion_status is Excluded, captures the business reason (e.g., \'Capital improvement per lease exclusion\', \'Tenant-specific charge\', \'Outside reconciliation period\').']
  inclusion_date date [note: 'Date on which this charge was included in the CAM reconciliation calculation. Used for audit trail and to track when charges were added during reconciliation preparation.']
  pro_rata_share_applied decimal(8,6) [note: 'The specific pro-rata share percentage applied to this charge for this reconciliation. May differ from the tenant\'s general pro-rata share if this charge uses a different allocation basis (e.g., HVAC allocated by conditioned space rather than NLA).']
  reconciliation_year varchar [note: 'The reconciliation year context for this allocation. Denormalized from cam_schedule for query performance and to support charges that may be allocated to multiple years (e.g., a December charge split across two reconciliation periods).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation relationship between annual CAM reconciliations and individual billable maintenance charges. It captures how specific maintenance charges are included in tenant CAM reconciliation calculations, including the allocation method applied, the pro-rata share used for this specific charge-to-reconciliation pairing, the reconciliation year context, charge inclusion status, and any dispute flags. Each record links one CAM reconciliation to one billable charge with allocation-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship. Critical for CAM audit trails and tenant dispute resolution.. Existence Justification: In real estate CAM reconciliation operations, one annual CAM reconciliation aggregates many individual billable maintenance charges (HVAC repairs, janitorial services, landscaping, etc.) into the tenant\'s annual expense calculation. Simultaneously, a single billable maintenance charge can be allocated across multiple tenant CAM reconciliations when the charge is part of a shared CAM pool serving multiple tenants in the same building. Property accountants actively manage these allocations, tracking which charges are included in which reconciliations, with what allocation method, pro-rata share, and inclusion status.'
}

Table lease.underwriting_input {
  underwriting_input_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this underwriting input relationship record. Primary key for the association.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement record that serves as an underwriting input for the pro forma. References the authoritative lease record in the lease domain.']
  proforma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the development pro forma record that consumes this lease as an underwriting input. References the financial feasibility model in the development domain.']
  assumption_type varchar [note: 'The pro forma scenario type for which this lease serves as an input. Base case uses executed leases and conservative comps. Upside scenarios may use aggressive comps or speculative pre-leases. Downside scenarios stress-test with lower comps. Sensitivity scenarios model lease-up risk.']
  input_date date [note: 'The date on which this lease was added as an underwriting input to the pro forma. Tracks the evolution of pro forma assumptions as new leases are executed or new comps are identified. Critical for audit trails showing when key assumptions changed.']
  input_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this underwriting input. Active inputs are currently used in pro forma calculations. Superseded inputs were replaced by newer leases or comps but retained for audit history. Excluded inputs were removed due to lease termination, tenant default, or determination that the comp is no longer relevant.']
  lease_role varchar [note: 'The role this lease agreement plays in the pro forma underwriting. Anchor pre-leases drive stabilized NOI projections and lender confidence. Comparable leases establish market rent benchmarks. In-place leases represent existing cash flow for value-add or acquisition pro formas. Shadow anchors are signed LOIs not yet executed. Credit tenant comps validate cap rate assumptions.']
  occupancy_assumption_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The stabilized occupancy percentage assumption informed by this lease. For anchor pre-leases, contributes to committed occupancy. For in-place leases, reflects current occupancy. For comparables, informs market occupancy benchmarks. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% occupancy).']
  rent_assumption_psf decimal(18,4) [note: 'The annual rent per square foot assumption derived from this lease and applied to the pro forma revenue projection. For anchor pre-leases, this is the contracted base_rent_psf_annual. For comparables, this may be adjusted for location, tenant credit, lease term, or TI differences. Expressed in USD per square foot per year.']
  underwriter_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the underwriting team explaining why this lease was selected as an input, any adjustments made to derive assumptions, and any caveats or risks associated with relying on this lease. Critical for investment committee due diligence and lender underwriting packages.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the underwriting input relationship between executed or comparable lease agreements and development pro forma financial models. It captures which leases inform which pro forma scenarios, the role each lease plays in the underwriting (anchor pre-lease, comparable, in-place for value-add), and the specific financial assumptions derived from each lease. Each record links one lease agreement to one pro forma with attributes that exist only in the context of this underwriting relationship. Critical for investment committee due diligence, lender underwriting packages, and audit trails showing how pro forma rent and occupancy assumptions were derived.. Existence Justification: In real estate development underwriting, a single pro forma scenario references multiple lease agreements as inputs (anchor pre-leases that drive NOI projections, comparable leases that establish market rent benchmarks, and in-place leases for value-add acquisitions). Conversely, a single executed or comparable lease can inform multiple pro forma scenarios across different projects, sensitivity cases (base/upside/downside), or pro forma versions as assumptions evolve. Investment committees and lenders require audit trails showing which leases informed which assumptions, making this a managed operational relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table lease.lease_representation {
  lease_representation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lease_representation']
  brokerage_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the broker who provided representation services in this lease transaction.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement that this broker represented a party in.']
  representation_id varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this broker representation record in the lakehouse.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total commission amount earned by this broker for this specific lease representation, calculated based on commission_split_pct and the total lease commission. Contributes to the broker\'s GCI metrics.']
  commission_paid_date date [note: 'The date on which the commission for this representation was paid to the broker. Used for cash flow tracking and commission reconciliation.']
  commission_split_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage of the total lease commission allocated to this specific broker for this representation. Used for commission calculation and GCI tracking. Sum of all commission_split_pct values for a given agreement_id should equal 100% (or the agreed split structure).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this broker\'s representation engagement became effective for this lease transaction. Typically the date the broker engagement letter was signed or the listing agreement was executed.']
  engagement_letter_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal broker engagement letter or representation agreement was signed for this specific lease transaction. Required for compliance and commission dispute resolution.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'The specific role this broker played in the lease transaction. Lead broker is the primary relationship owner, co-broker shares responsibility, junior agent assists under supervision, referral agent provided the lead but did not execute the transaction.']
  side varchar [note: 'Indicates which party the broker represented in this lease transaction. Landlord-side brokers represent the lessor, tenant-side brokers represent the lessee, dual agency represents both (where legally permitted), and co-broker indicates multiple brokers on the same side.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the brokerage representation relationship between a lease agreement and a broker. It captures which brokers represented which parties in a lease transaction, their commission arrangements, and their specific role in the deal. Each record links one lease agreement to one broker with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific representation engagement.. Existence Justification: In real estate brokerage operations, a single lease agreement routinely involves multiple brokers representing different parties (landlord-side broker, tenant-side broker, and potentially co-brokers on either side). Each broker handles many lease agreements throughout their career. The business actively manages these representation relationships as distinct operational records, tracking each broker\'s side, commission split, role, and payment status for every deal.'
}

Table lease.collateral_assignment {
  collateral_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this collateral assignment record. Primary key.']
  debt_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the debt facility for which this lease agreement serves as collateral']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement that is assigned as collateral security for the debt facility']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The date on which the lease agreement was formally assigned as collateral to the lender for this debt facility. Marks the effective date of the assignment of rents.']
  assignment_recorded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment of rents has been formally recorded with the appropriate jurisdiction (county recorder, land registry). Critical for lender perfection of security interest.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collateral assignment. Values: active (assignment in force), released (assignment terminated upon loan payoff or release), suspended (temporarily suspended by agreement), defaulted (borrower in default, lender may exercise assignment rights).']
  collateral_value_allocated decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the lease\'s present value of future rents allocated as collateral coverage for this specific debt facility. Used in LTV and collateral coverage ratio calculations when a lease secures multiple facilities.']
  lender_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lender must provide consent for material lease modifications, tenant releases, or lease terminations under this collateral assignment. Drives lease administration workflow requirements.']
  recording_date date [note: 'The date on which the assignment of rents was recorded with the jurisdiction. Null if assignment_recorded is false.']
  recording_reference varchar [note: 'The official recording reference number or document number assigned by the recording jurisdiction. Used for title and lien searches.']
  release_date date [note: 'The date on which the collateral assignment was released by the lender, typically upon loan payoff, refinancing, or collateral substitution. Null if assignment_status is active.']
  subordination_agreement_date date [note: 'The date of the subordination agreement, if applicable. Null if subordination_status is senior or unsubordinated.']
  subordination_status varchar [note: 'The priority status of this collateral assignment relative to other debt facilities secured by the same lease. Values: senior (first priority), subordinate (junior to another facility), pari_passu (equal priority with another facility), unsubordinated (no subordination agreement in place).']
  tenant_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the tenant was notified of the assignment and instructed to redirect rent payments to the lender. Typically occurs upon borrower default. Null if no notification has been issued.']
  tenant_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant must be notified of the assignment of rents and instructed to pay rent directly to the lender in the event of borrower default. Drives default management workflows.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the collateral assignment relationship between lease agreements and debt facilities in real estate finance. It captures the legal and operational assignment of lease rents as collateral security for debt instruments. Each record links one lease agreement to one debt facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this collateral relationship, including assignment dates, recording status, lender consent requirements, and subordination hierarchy.. Existence Justification: In commercial real estate finance, a single debt facility is routinely collateralized by multiple lease agreements (assignment of rents across all tenants on a property or portfolio), and a single lease agreement can be assigned as collateral to multiple debt facilities simultaneously (e.g., senior mortgage + mezzanine debt, or across refinancing events where subordination agreements preserve junior lender positions). Lenders actively manage these collateral assignments as distinct operational records with legal recording requirements, subordination hierarchies, consent workflows, and collateral coverage calculations.'
}

Table lease.coverage_requirement {
  coverage_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lease-policy coverage requirement record']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the lease agreement that mandates this insurance coverage requirement']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the insurance policy that satisfies this coverage requirement']
  certificate_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current Certificate of Insurance (COI) for this policy has been received and is on file with the landlord, as required by the lease agreement.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of this lease-policy pairing. Indicates whether the policy meets the coverage requirements stipulated in the lease agreement, is pending verification, has expired, or has been waived by the landlord.']
  coverage_requirement_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of insurance coverage required by the lease agreement for this policy pairing. Distinguishes between general liability, property, workers compensation, umbrella, and specialty coverages mandated in the lease terms.']
  landlord_additional_insured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease requires the landlord to be named as an additional insured on this specific policy. Critical for general liability and property policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding this specific lease-policy coverage requirement, including special terms, verification issues, or compliance exceptions.']
  required_coverage_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum coverage limit amount mandated by the lease agreement for this specific coverage type. Used to verify that the policy coverage_limit meets or exceeds the lease requirement.']
  requirement_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific coverage requirement became effective for the lease agreement. May differ from lease commencement if requirements change during lease term or upon renewal.']
  requirement_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this coverage requirement expires or must be renewed. Typically aligns with policy expiration but may differ based on lease amendment or requirement waiver terms.']
  verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease administrator or property manager verified that this policy satisfies the lease coverage requirement. Used for compliance audit trails and renewal tracking.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this coverage requirement was waived by the landlord, if applicable. Captures business justification for non-compliance exceptions.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the insurance coverage requirement relationship between lease agreements and insurance policies. It captures the specific insurance compliance obligations that each lease agreement imposes, and tracks which policies satisfy those requirements. Each record links one lease agreement to one insurance policy with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance relationship, including the type of coverage required by the lease, the minimum coverage limits mandated, compliance status, and verification dates.. Existence Justification: In real estate lease administration, a single lease agreement routinely requires multiple types of insurance coverage (general liability, property, workers compensation, umbrella, and specialty coverages like flood or terrorism), and each of these requirements may be satisfied by a different policy. Conversely, a single blanket insurance policy—particularly master property policies or portfolio-wide general liability policies—commonly covers multiple lease agreements across a property portfolio. Lease administrators actively manage this many-to-many relationship by tracking compliance status, verifying coverage limits against lease requirements, maintaining certificates of insurance, and monitoring expiration dates for each lease-policy pairing.'
}

Table lease.cam_pool {
  cam_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cam_pool']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property to which this CAM pool belongs.']
  parent_cam_pool_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cam_pool (parent_cam_pool_id)']
  administrative_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage administrative fee added to CAM expenses before allocation to tenants.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate CAM expenses to tenants within the pool (pro-rata by area, fixed amount, percentage, or custom formula).']
  area_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for area calculations (square feet or square meters).']
  base_year varchar [note: 'Base year for CAM expense calculations, used in expense stop or base year stop lease structures.']
  base_year_expense_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total CAM expenses incurred in the base year, used as the baseline for calculating tenant pass-through charges.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which CAM charges are billed to tenants.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAM pool record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the CAM pool.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the CAM pool ceases to be effective. Nullable for open-ended pools.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the CAM pool becomes effective and begins accumulating expenses.']
  excluded_expense_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of expense categories explicitly excluded from the CAM pool (e.g., capital improvements, leasing commissions, debt service).']
  expense_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum CAM expense amount per unit area that can be charged to tenants. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  gross_up_method varchar [note: 'Method used to gross-up variable expenses to reflect full occupancy when calculating tenant CAM charges.']
  gross_up_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Occupancy percentage threshold below which gross-up calculations are applied to variable expenses.']
  includes_insurance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property insurance costs are included in the CAM pool expenses.']
  includes_management_fees boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property management fees are included in the CAM pool expenses.']
  includes_property_taxes boolean [note: 'Indicates whether property taxes are included in the CAM pool expenses.']
  includes_repairs_maintenance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether repairs and maintenance costs are included in the CAM pool expenses.']
  includes_utilities boolean [note: 'Indicates whether utility costs are included in the CAM pool expenses.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAM pool record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the CAM pool configuration, exceptions, or special handling instructions.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the CAM pool, used in lease documents and billing systems.']
  pool_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the CAM pool, including scope, coverage, and any special terms or conditions.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the CAM pool, typically reflecting the property or building grouping.']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAM pool.']
  pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAM pool based on the grouping method (building-level, campus-level, floor-level, zone-level, portfolio-level, or custom).']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which CAM expenses are reconciled and adjusted with tenants.']
  total_rentable_area decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total rentable square footage or square meters included in the CAM pool, used as the denominator for pro-rata expense allocation.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cam_pool. Referenced by cam_pool_id.'
}

  deed_recorded_date date [note: 'Date on which the deed was officially recorded with the county recorder or registrar of deeds, establishing the public record of title transfer.']
  deed_type varchar [note: 'Type of deed instrument used to convey title from seller to buyer. Determines the scope of title warranties provided. Common types include warranty, special warranty, quitclaim, grant, and bargain and sale deeds.']
  due_diligence_end_date date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the buyer must complete all due diligence inspections, reviews, and investigations and either proceed with or terminate the transaction.']
  earnest_money_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnest money deposit submitted by the buyer upon execution of the purchase and sale agreement (PSA), held in escrow pending closing.']
  earnest_money_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the earnest money deposit must be received and confirmed in escrow per the terms of the purchase and sale agreement.']
  earnest_money_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the earnest money deposit has been received and confirmed in escrow. True = received; False = not yet received.']
  escrow_company varchar [note: 'Name of the escrow company or closing agent holding funds and documents in trust during the transaction period between contract execution and closing.']
  exchange_deadline_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which the replacement property must be identified and acquired to qualify for 1031 like-kind exchange tax deferral treatment. Applicable only when is_1031_exchange is True.']
  expected_closing_date date [note: 'Projected date on which the transaction is anticipated to close, as tracked in the deal pipeline. Used for cash flow forecasting and portfolio planning in Argus Enterprise.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the financing structure used to fund the acquisition. Includes all-cash, conventional, Adjustable Rate Mortgage (ARM), Fixed Rate Mortgage (FRM), Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities (CMBS), seller financing, loan assumption, or bridge financing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_cash|conventional|arm|frm|cmbs|seller_financing|assumption|bridge — promote to reference product]']
  gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the property in square feet (SQF) at the time of sale, used as the denominator for PSF pricing calculations and CoStar comparable benchmarking.']
  is_1031_exchange boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is structured as a 1031 like-kind exchange under IRS Section 1031, deferring capital gains tax recognition. True = 1031 exchange; False = standard sale.']
  loan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debt financing amount secured for the property acquisition. Used in conjunction with LTV ratio and DSCR calculations for investment underwriting.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(6,4) [note: 'Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio at the time of sale, calculated as the total debt financing divided by the purchase price. Key underwriting metric for lenders and investment committees.']
  mls_number varchar [note: 'The Multiple Listing Service (MLS) identifier assigned to the property listing associated with this sale, enabling cross-referencing with MLS and CoStar comparable sales data.']
  net_sale_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net proceeds from the property sale after deducting seller concessions, credits, prorations, and closing cost adjustments from the gross purchase price.']
  noi_at_sale decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Operating Income (NOI) of the property at the time of sale, used to derive the implied CAP Rate and support investment return analysis in Argus Enterprise.']
  price_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Executed sale price expressed on a Per Square Foot (PSF) basis, calculated as purchase price divided by the property\'s gross leasable area. Key benchmarking metric for CoStar comparable sales analysis.']
  purchase_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross executed purchase price of the property as agreed in the purchase and sale agreement (PSA). Represents the total consideration paid before adjustments, credits, or prorations.']
  recording_number varchar [note: 'Official document recording number assigned by the county recorder upon deed recordation, serving as the public record reference for the title transfer.']
  seller_concessions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of concessions granted by the seller to the buyer, including closing cost credits, repair allowances, and price reductions negotiated post-inspection.']
  title_company varchar [note: 'Name of the title insurance company responsible for conducting the title search, issuing the ALTA title commitment, and facilitating the closing and deed recordation.']
  transaction_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing material deal notes, special conditions, negotiation context, or other qualitative information relevant to the property sale transaction.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the property sale transaction, used across Yardi Voyager, CoStar, and MLS systems for cross-referencing and audit trail purposes.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the property sale transaction. Drives workflow routing in Yardi Voyager and Salesforce CRM deal pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|under_contract|closed|cancelled|on_hold|terminated — promote to reference product]']
  transfer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total real estate transfer tax or documentary stamp tax assessed on the property sale transaction by state and local taxing authorities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the property sale record was last modified, supporting change data capture, audit trail, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  yardi_transaction_ref varchar [note: 'Source system transaction reference identifier from Yardi Voyager, used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the system of record.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every property sale or disposition transaction — acquisition, disposition, REO sale, short sale, portfolio trade, or sale-leaseback — capturing executed purchase price, PSF pricing, earnest money terms, closing date, deal type classification, transaction status, and key deal economics (price, PSF, cap rate). Serves as the SSOT hub to which all transaction detail entities (PSA, closing statement, title, deed, financing, escrow, due diligence, parties, offers, documents) are linked via FK. Sourced from Yardi Voyager and CoStar comparable sales data.'
}

  contract_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final negotiated and agreed purchase price as recorded in the executed PSA, which may differ from the initial offer price following negotiations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this purchase agreement record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'The unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the digitally executed purchase agreement, enabling traceability to the contract lifecycle management system.']
  down_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The equity portion of the purchase price to be paid by the buyer at closing, calculated as contract price minus loan amount.']
