  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax filing associated with this record. Tracks progression from not filed through submission, amendment, and acceptance or rejection by the taxing authority. Supports SOX compliance monitoring and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.']
  firpta_withholding_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable FIRPTA withholding tax rate for foreign owners on disposition of US real property interests. Standard rate is 15% (0.1500) for amounts realized over $1M; reduced rates may apply per IRS withholding certificates. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%).']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interest charges accrued on unpaid or underpaid tax balances as assessed by the taxing authority. Tracked separately from penalties and base tax for accurate OPEX and NOI reporting.']
  is_foreign_owner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the owner is a foreign person or entity subject to FIRPTA (Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act) withholding requirements. Drives withholding tax rate application and IRS Form 8288 filing obligations.']
  jurisdiction_county_district varchar [note: 'County, municipality, or special tax district within the state where the property tax is levied. Relevant for local property tax assessments and special assessment districts.']
  k1_capital_gain_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Partner\'s or investor\'s share of net capital gain allocated via IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065). Includes short-term and long-term capital gains from property dispositions within the partnership. Null for non-partnership structures.']
  k1_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Partner\'s or investor\'s share of depreciation deductions allocated via IRS Schedule K-1. Relevant for REIT FFO (Funds From Operations) and AFFO (Adjusted Funds From Operations) calculations and investor tax planning.']
  k1_ordinary_income_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Partner\'s or investor\'s share of ordinary business income allocated via IRS Schedule K-1 (Form 1065) for partnership entities. Applicable for JV entities, limited partnerships, and REIT operating partnerships. Null for non-partnership ownership structures.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing supplementary information, special circumstances, or administrative notes related to this tax record — such as appeal rationale, installment payment arrangements, treaty election details, or correspondence references.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Owner\'s percentage equity stake in the property or partnership entity as of the tax year, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2500 = 25%). Drives K-1 income allocation calculations and proportional tax obligation attribution.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by the taxing authority for late filing, late payment, or underpayment of taxes. Tracked separately from the base tax obligation for OPEX reporting and NOI impact analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this owner tax record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, SOX IT general controls, and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this owner tax record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, SOX audit controls, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  tax_abatement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of tax reduction granted through formal abatement, exemption, or appeal decision by the taxing authority. Reduces the net tax obligation. Common in development projects with tax incentive agreements or successful assessment appeals.']
  tax_due_date date [note: 'The statutory deadline by which the tax payment must be remitted to the taxing authority to avoid penalties and interest. May reflect installment due dates for jurisdictions with semi-annual or quarterly payment schedules.']
  tax_exempt_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the owner or property qualifies for a full or partial tax exemption under applicable law (e.g., nonprofit ownership, government entity, REIT structural exemption, homestead exemption). Drives tax obligation calculation.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Owner\'s Tax Identification Number (TIN) as assigned by the relevant tax authority — may be an EIN (Employer Identification Number), SSN (Social Security Number), ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number), or foreign equivalent. Used for FIRPTA withholding certificates, K-1 issuance, and 1099 reporting.']
  tax_paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount of tax remitted or paid to the taxing authority for this record period. May differ from assessed amount due to appeals, abatements, installment payments, or partial payments. Supports cash flow reporting and DSCR calculations.']
  tax_payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tax payment was remitted to the taxing authority. Used to determine timeliness of payment, calculate penalty and interest exposure, and support cash flow reporting.']
  tax_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the tax assessment or reporting period covered by this record. For annual property tax, typically December 31 of the tax year. Defines the boundary of the tax obligation period.']
  tax_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the tax assessment or reporting period covered by this record. For annual property tax, typically January 1 of the tax year. For withholding tax, the start of the applicable withholding period.']
  tax_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective tax rate or mill rate applied by the taxing jurisdiction to the assessed property value to compute the property tax bill. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.012500 = 1.25% or 12.5 mills). Also used for income or withholding tax rates where applicable.']
  tax_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known reference number assigned by the taxing jurisdiction or internal tax administration system for this specific tax obligation record. Used for correspondence with tax authorities and audit trails.']
  tax_treaty_article varchar [note: 'Specific article or provision of the applicable bilateral tax treaty under which the owner claims reduced withholding or exemption (e.g., \'Article 6 — Income from Immovable Property\'). Required for IRS Form W-8BEN treaty benefit claims.']
  tax_treaty_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the treaty country under which the owner claims a reduced withholding rate or tax exemption. Null if no treaty election applies. Used in conjunction with IRS Form W-8BEN treaty claims.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax obligation captured in this record. Covers property tax obligations by jurisdiction, income tax, withholding tax for foreign owners (FIRPTA), transfer tax on disposition, capital gains tax, and K-1 partnership income allocations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_tax|income_tax|withholding_tax|transfer_tax|capital_gains_tax|k1_partnership_allocation|excise_tax|franchise_tax — promote to reference product]']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year to which this tax record applies (e.g., 2024). Used for annual tax reporting, K-1 allocations, and SOX compliance period alignment.']
  tin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax identification number provided — EIN (Employer Identification Number) for entities, SSN (Social Security Number) for US individuals, ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number) for non-resident individuals, or foreign TIN for international owners.']
  withholding_certificate_number varchar [note: 'IRS-issued withholding certificate number (from Form 8288-B application) that authorizes a reduced or zero FIRPTA withholding rate for a foreign owner. Null if no certificate has been obtained. Required for FIRPTA compliance tracking.']
  yardi_tax_entity_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference code identifying the corresponding tax entity or property record in Yardi Voyager Property Management and General Ledger modules. Enables reconciliation between the owner tax record and Yardi GL tax entries and AR/AP tax transactions.']

  Note: 'SSOT for owner-perspective tax records — property tax obligations by jurisdiction, tax identification numbers, withholding tax rates for foreign owners (FIRPTA), tax treaty elections, K-1 partnership income allocations, and annual tax reporting status. Distinct from finance domain tax accounting (GL tax entries) and compliance domain regulatory filings; this product captures the owner-specific tax profile and obligations. Captures tax year, jurisdiction, tax type, assessed amount, filing status, and SAP S/4HANA tax cross-reference. Supports SOX compliance and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.'
}

Table owner.entity_hierarchy {
  entity_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each parent-child ownership hierarchy relationship record in the Silver Layer. Primary key for this data product.']
  child_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child owner entity in the hierarchy (e.g., subsidiary, SPV, operating partnership, joint-venture entity). References the owner entity that sits below the parent in the ownership structure.']
  investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.investment_vehicle. Business justification: Entity hierarchy node investment vehicle type (REIT, LP, LLC) determines consolidation method, REIT qualifying income tests, and ERISA look-through rules. Fund administrators require normalized vehicl']
  jurisdiction_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Entity hierarchy jurisdiction country determines consolidation accounting standards (ASC 810 vs IFRS 10), foreign subsidiary reporting requirements, and cross-border ownership disclosure obligations. ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Entity hierarchies span multiple jurisdictions for consolidation and regulatory reporting (GAAP, IFRS, country-by-country reporting). The owner_entity_hierarchy has jurisdiction_country_code and juris']
  primary_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent owner entity in the hierarchy (e.g., holding company, REIT umbrella partnership, fund-of-funds). References the owner entity that sits above the child in the ownership structure.']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Entity hierarchy consolidation is governed by specific accounting/regulatory frameworks (ASC 810, IFRS 10, SEC Reg S-X). Finance teams require this link to determine consolidation methodology, VIE ass']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the parent entity acquired its ownership interest in the child entity. Used for holding period calculations, capital gains tax analysis, and investment performance measurement.']
  agreement_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the governing legal agreement establishing this ownership relationship was formally executed and signed by all parties.']
  aum_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Assets Under Management (AUM) attributable to the parent entity for portfolio-level AUM reporting. May differ from equity ownership percentage due to management fee structures or co-investment arrangements.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting consolidation method applied to this parent-child relationship for financial reporting purposes. Determines how the child entity\'s financials are incorporated into the parent\'s consolidated statements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy relationship record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the parent entity disposed of or transferred its ownership interest in the child entity. Null for currently active relationships. Used for realized gain/loss reporting and portfolio disposition tracking.']
  economic_interest_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of economic interest (profit/loss participation) held by the parent entity in the child entity. Relevant for joint ventures and operating partnerships where economic interest differs from voting rights or equity ownership.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date on which this parent-child ownership hierarchy relationship became legally effective. Used for point-in-time ownership reporting and historical roll-up analysis.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this parent-child ownership hierarchy relationship ceased to be effective. Null for currently active relationships. Supports temporal querying and historical ownership reconstruction.']
  entity_structure_type varchar [note: 'Legal or organizational structure type of the child entity within the hierarchy. Drives applicable regulatory reporting, tax treatment, and consolidation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holding_company|spv|reit|operating_partnership|joint_venture|fund|partnership|subsidiary — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  equity_ownership_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of equity interest held by the parent entity in the child entity, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 100.0000 for wholly owned, 51.0000 for majority controlled). Critical for NAV, GAV, and AUM roll-up calculations.']
  gav_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Gross Asset Value (GAV) attributable to the parent entity for consolidated GAV reporting. Supports portfolio-level investment performance analysis.']
  governing_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the legal agreement (e.g., partnership agreement, JV agreement, shareholder agreement) that establishes and governs this parent-child ownership relationship. Links to DocuSign CLM contract records.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business code uniquely identifying this parent-child hierarchy relationship, used for cross-system reconciliation with Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise portfolio structures.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child entity within the ownership hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents a direct subsidiary of the ultimate parent. Enables roll-up reporting of AUM, NAV, and GAV across multi-tier structures.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from the ultimate root entity to the child entity (e.g., \'/RootHoldCo/SubHoldCo/SPV-001\'). Enables efficient tree traversal and roll-up queries in Databricks without recursive CTEs.']
  hierarchy_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which this hierarchy relationship is defined. A single pair of entities may have multiple hierarchy records serving different purposes (e.g., legal ownership vs. management reporting vs. tax consolidation).']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parent-child hierarchy relationship. Active indicates the relationship is currently in force; dissolved indicates the child entity has been wound down or the relationship terminated.']
  hierarchy_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this hierarchy relationship record, incremented each time the relationship is modified (e.g., ownership percentage change, consolidation method change). Supports temporal versioning and audit trail.']
  is_consolidation_entity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity is included in the parent\'s consolidated financial statements for regulatory and statutory reporting purposes. True if consolidated; False if excluded (e.g., minority interest below consolidation threshold).']
  is_primary_hierarchy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship represents the primary (canonical) parent-child link for the child entity. A child entity may have multiple hierarchy records (e.g., legal vs. management reporting), but only one is designated primary.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 two-letter state or province code of the sub-national jurisdiction governing this ownership relationship. Used for state-level regulatory filings and tax reporting.']
  minority_interest_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship represents a non-controlling (minority) interest position where the parent holds less than 50% equity or voting rights. Drives non-controlling interest disclosure in consolidated financial statements.']
  nav_attribution_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of the child entity\'s Net Asset Value (NAV) attributable to the parent entity for consolidated NAV reporting. Used in REIT and fund-of-funds NAV roll-up calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, qualifications, or commentary about this ownership hierarchy relationship that cannot be expressed in structured fields (e.g., special voting arrangements, earn-out provisions, pending regulatory approvals).']
  reit_qualifying_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity qualifies as a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) or REIT subsidiary for tax and regulatory purposes. Drives REIT compliance testing and SEC reporting obligations.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership relationship between parent and child entities. Drives consolidation treatment and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wholly_owned|majority_controlled|minority_interest|joint_venture|fund_of_funds|operating_partnership — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this hierarchy relationship record was sourced (e.g., Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, Argus Enterprise, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this hierarchy relationship record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi entity tree node ID, MRI entity relationship ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  tax_transparent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity is tax-transparent (pass-through) for income tax purposes (e.g., partnership, LLC treated as disregarded entity). Affects tax consolidation and investor-level tax reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy relationship record was last modified in the Silver Layer data product. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  variable_interest_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child entity qualifies as a Variable Interest Entity (VIE) under FASB ASC 810, requiring consolidation by the primary beneficiary regardless of voting interest percentage.']
  voting_rights_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of voting rights held by the parent entity in the child entity. May differ from equity ownership percentage in structures with preferred equity, non-voting shares, or dual-class share arrangements.']

  Note: 'Models the parent-child organizational hierarchy among owner entities — holding companies, subsidiaries, SPVs, fund-of-funds structures, and REIT umbrella partnerships. Captures parent entity, child entity, hierarchy level, relationship type (wholly owned, majority controlled, minority interest), effective date, and consolidation flag. Enables roll-up reporting of AUM, NAV, and GAV across complex multi-tier ownership structures.'
}

Table owner.accreditation {
  accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each investor accreditation record. Primary key for the accreditation data product within the owner domain.']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Accreditation document type (net worth certification, income verification letter, CPA letter) maps to document_type for retention period enforcement, notarization requirements, and expiry tracking. Co']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Accreditation is validated against specific fund subscription requirements (Reg D 506(b)/(c), QP thresholds). Linking accreditation to the fund enables compliance verification during subscription and ']
  investor_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Investor accreditation in real estate private placements is managed by an internal employee (investor relations rep or compliance officer). Linking to workforce.employee enables accreditation renewal ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Accreditation standards differ by jurisdiction (SEC in US, FCA in UK, ASIC in Australia). Compliance teams need the structured jurisdiction reference to apply correct accreditation criteria, expiry ru']
  kyc_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.kyc_profile. Business justification: Accreditation (investor qualification) is closely tied to the KYC compliance lifecycle. The accreditation record carries inline kyc_status, aml_check_status, and sanctions_check_status fields that mir']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific private real estate offering or investment vehicle for which this accreditation record was obtained or is applicable. Links to the investment offering record in the investment domain.']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property owner or investor entity for whom this accreditation record applies. Links to the owner master record in the owner domain.']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Investor accreditation is governed by specific SEC regulatory frameworks (Reg D Rule 506, Reg S, Reg A+). Compliance officers require this link to track applicable accreditation standards, verificatio']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Investor accreditation is mandated by specific securities regulations (SEC Reg D Rule 506, Reg A+). Compliance teams must link each accreditation record to its mandating regulatory obligation for secu']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this accreditation record, used in investor onboarding documentation, private placement memoranda, and regulatory correspondence.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investor accreditation record. Active indicates valid and in-force; Expired indicates past the expiry date without renewal; Pending Renewal indicates renewal process initiated; Revoked indicates accreditation withdrawn; Under Review indicates verification in progress; Suspended indicates temporarily inactive pending investigation.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investor accreditation status under applicable securities regulations. Accredited Investor per SEC Rule 501(a); Qualified Purchaser per Investment Company Act Section 2(a)(51); Qualified Institutional Buyer (QIB) per SEC Rule 144A; ERISA Plan Investor per ERISA Section 3(3); Knowledgeable Employee per Investment Company Act Rule 3c-5.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Anti-Money Laundering (AML) screening performed as part of investor onboarding and accreditation. Cleared indicates no adverse findings; Flagged indicates potential AML concerns requiring escalation; Pending indicates screening not yet completed; Not Required indicates AML screening exempted for this investor type.']
  annual_income_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Reported individual annual income of the investor in US dollars used to assess accredited investor eligibility under the income test ($200,000 individual or $300,000 joint income for the past two years with expectation of same in current year) per SEC Rule 501(a)(6).']
  assets_under_management_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total assets under management in US dollars for institutional investors (e.g., registered investment advisers, banks, insurance companies) used to determine Qualified Institutional Buyer (QIB) eligibility under SEC Rule 144A ($100M threshold) or Qualified Purchaser status under the Investment Company Act ($25M threshold for entities).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor\'s accreditation record is configured for automatic renewal workflow initiation upon approaching the expiry date. True enables automated renewal reminders and workflow triggers; False requires manual renewal initiation by the compliance team.']
  beneficial_ownership_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether beneficial ownership information has been collected and confirmed for entity investors (e.g., LLCs, partnerships, trusts) as required by FinCEN\'s Customer Due Diligence Rule. True if beneficial ownership of 25% or more equity interest has been identified and verified; False if not yet confirmed.']
  certification_date date [note: 'The date on which the investor\'s accreditation status was formally certified or confirmed by the certifying party. Represents the effective start of the accreditation period. Aligns with SEC Regulation D requirement to verify accredited investor status at the time of sale.']
  certifying_party_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or registration number of the certifying party (e.g., CPA license number, bar number for attorneys, CRD number for broker-dealers or RIAs). Used to validate the credentials of the certifying professional and maintain audit documentation.']
  certifying_party_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual, firm, or institution that certified or verified the investor\'s accreditation status (e.g., CPA firm name, law firm name, registered broker-dealer name, registered investment advisor name). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance under SEC Rule 506(c).']
  certifying_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity or professional who certified the investor\'s accreditation. Determines the regulatory weight and acceptability of the verification under SEC Rule 506(c) requirements for reasonable steps to verify accredited investor status.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record observations, exceptions, escalation notes, or special circumstances related to the investor\'s accreditation verification process. Confidential — restricted to compliance and legal personnel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this accreditation record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping.']
  document_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the supporting verification document (e.g., CPA letter, attorney letter) expires or becomes stale for regulatory purposes. Typically 90 days from issuance for third-party verification letters under SEC Rule 506(c). Distinct from the accreditation expiry date.']
  document_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the primary supporting document used to verify accreditation status (e.g., DocuSign envelope ID, document management system reference, CPA letter reference number). Used to locate the underlying verification document in the document management system.']
  erisa_plan_assets_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total plan assets in US dollars for ERISA-governed pension or benefit plans, used to determine whether the plan investor qualifies as an accredited investor under SEC Rule 501(a)(1) ($5,000,000 threshold) and to assess ERISA fiduciary obligations and plan asset regulations. Null for non-ERISA investors.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the investor\'s accreditation certification expires and must be renewed or re-verified. Typically 90 days from certification date for Regulation D 506(c) offerings. Null for perpetual or open-ended accreditations where no expiry is mandated.']
  investments_owned_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total value of investments owned by the investor in US dollars, used to determine Qualified Purchaser eligibility under the Investment Company Act Section 2(a)(51). Threshold is $5,000,000 for individuals and $25,000,000 for entities. Excludes primary residence and property used in business.']
  investor_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the investor entity type for accreditation and regulatory purposes. Determines applicable accreditation thresholds and regulatory treatment under SEC Regulation D, Investment Company Act, and ERISA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|entity|trust|family_office|pension_fund|endowment|sovereign_wealth|reit|fund_of_funds — promote to reference product]']
  joint_income_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Reported joint annual income of the investor and spouse or spousal equivalent in US dollars, used to assess accredited investor eligibility under the joint income threshold of $300,000 per SEC Rule 501(a)(6). Null for non-joint filings.']
  knowledgeable_employee_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor qualifies as a knowledgeable employee of the fund under Investment Company Act Rule 3c-5, which exempts certain fund employees from the investor count limitations of Sections 3(c)(1) and 3(c)(7). True if the investor is a qualifying knowledgeable employee; False otherwise.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence process for the investor as part of the accreditation and onboarding workflow. Passed indicates all KYC checks cleared; Failed indicates disqualifying findings; Pending indicates not yet initiated; In Progress indicates checks underway; Requires Update indicates previously passed but refresh needed.']
  net_worth_usd decimal(20,2) [note: 'Reported net worth of the investor in US dollars at the time of accreditation, excluding the value of the investor\'s primary residence per SEC Rule 501(a)(5). Used to determine eligibility as an accredited investor under the $1,000,000 net worth threshold. Sensitive financial data.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic re-verification or review of the investor\'s accreditation status. Used by compliance teams to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous eligibility for private real estate offerings.']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the private securities offering type under which the investor\'s accreditation is being assessed. Determines the applicable verification requirements and investor eligibility rules. Reg D 506(b) allows self-certification; 506(c) requires third-party verification.']
  politically_exposed_person_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investor or a beneficial owner has been identified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) — a current or former senior government official, political party official, or their immediate family member or close associate. PEP status triggers enhanced due diligence requirements under AML regulations.']
  professional_certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of professional financial certification or license held by the investor that qualifies them as an accredited investor under the SEC\'s 2020 amendments to Rule 501(a). Includes FINRA Series 7 (General Securities Representative), Series 65 (Investment Adviser Representative), and Series 82 (Private Securities Offerings Representative) licenses. None if accreditation is based on financial thresholds rather than professional credentials.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for rejection or revocation of the investor\'s accreditation status, recorded when accreditation_status is set to \'revoked\' or when a renewal application is denied. Provides audit trail for compliance and regulatory review purposes.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the investor\'s accreditation was most recently renewed or re-verified following a prior expiry or scheduled review cycle. Null if the accreditation has never been renewed.']
  renewal_reminder_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal reminder notification has been sent to the investor or their representative ahead of the accreditation expiry date. Used by compliance teams to track outreach status and ensure timely re-verification before expiry.']
  sanctions_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of OFAC and international sanctions screening performed on the investor as part of accreditation and KYC due diligence. Cleared indicates no sanctions matches; Flagged indicates potential match requiring review; Pending indicates screening not yet completed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this accreditation record originated or was ingested. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Salesforce CRM is the primary source for investor onboarding and accreditation tracking; DocuSign CLM for executed accreditation documents.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this accreditation record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce CRM record ID, MRI Software investor ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this accreditation record was most recently modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver Layer.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify and confirm the investor\'s accreditation status. Third-party verification methods (broker-dealer letter, RIA letter, attorney letter, CPA letter) are required for SEC Rule 506(c) offerings. Self-certification is permissible under Rule 506(b). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: third_party_letter|financial_statement|tax_return|credit_report|self_certification|broker_dealer_letter|registered_investment_advisor_letter|attorney_letter|cpa_letter — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Investor accreditation and qualification records for owners participating in private real estate offerings — SEC Regulation D accredited investor status, qualified purchaser status, qualified institutional buyer (QIB) status, and ERISA plan investor classification. Captures accreditation type, certification date, expiry date, verification method, certifying party, and renewal status. Required for private placement compliance.'
}

Table owner.power_of_attorney {
  power_of_attorney_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the power of attorney record in the owner domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.asset. Business justification: Real estate POAs are routinely granted for specific property transactions — authorizing execution of closing documents, lease agreements, or loan closings on a named asset. Transaction counsel and tit']
  brokerage_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.deal. Business justification: When a POA authorizes execution of a real estate transaction, the deal must reference the authorizing POA for title company review, lender approval, and regulatory compliance. This is a standard closi']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: POAs in development authorize specific actions on a project — signing construction contracts, permit applications, loan closings. Scoping a POA to a specific dev_project is standard practice for JV de']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Power of attorney is a specific legal document type with defined notarization, recording, and retention requirements. Document management teams require this link to enforce POA-specific workflow rules']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: POAs are recorded in and governed by specific jurisdictions with different validity, notarization, and recording requirements. The recording_jurisdiction plain-text column is denormalized; a structure']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the owner entity (grantor) who is granting the power of attorney. Links to the owner master record.']
  recording_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: POA recording jurisdiction determines applicable state recording requirements, instrument numbering, and legal validity for property transactions. Title officers and closing attorneys require normaliz']
  authority_scope varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific powers granted to the grantee. For limited POAs, this details the exact transactions authorized (e.g., \'execute lease agreements for properties in portfolio X\', \'sign loan closing documents for property at address Y\'). Critical for compliance and transaction validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POA record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and GDPR data governance obligations.']
  document_storage_ref varchar [note: 'Reference path or URI to the executed POA document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM, SharePoint, or enterprise DMS). Enables retrieval of the original signed document for legal review.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the electronic execution of the POA document. Enables retrieval of the signed document, audit trail, and signature certificate from the DocuSign platform.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the POA becomes legally binding and the grantee is authorized to act on behalf of the grantor. For springing POAs, this is the date the triggering condition was confirmed.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the POA was formally executed (signed by the grantor). This is the date the document was created and signed, which may differ from the effective date for springing POAs.']
  execution_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the POA was executed. Wet Ink indicates physical signature; Electronic indicates DocuSign or equivalent e-signature; Remote Online Notarization (RON) indicates notarization conducted via video conference per applicable state law.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the POA ceases to be valid. Null for durable or open-ended POAs with no defined termination date. Critical for compliance monitoring and transaction authorization checks.']
  governing_law_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the POA. May differ from the recording state if the grantor is domiciled in a different state.']
  grantee_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the grantee (authorized agent) used for correspondence, DocuSign CLM notifications, and transaction coordination.']
  grantee_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the grantee (authorized agent) for urgent transaction authorization and identity verification purposes.']
  grantee_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the grantee indicating whether the authorized agent is an individual, law firm, corporate officer, trustee, or property manager. Determines applicable professional liability and licensing requirements.']
  grantee_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the grantee (authorized agent or attorney-in-fact) as it appears on the executed POA document. This individual or entity is authorized to act on behalf of the grantor.']
  grantor_entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal entity classification of the grantor. Determines the form of execution required (e.g., corporate resolution for corporations, trustee signature for trusts). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|trust|corporation|partnership|llc|reit|joint_venture — promote to reference product]']
  grantor_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the grantor (owner entity) as it appears on the executed POA document. May be an individual, trust, corporation, REIT, or partnership. Used for legal document matching and title company verification.']
  is_financial_mgmt_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA grants authority to manage financial accounts, execute AP/AR transactions, or make investment decisions on behalf of the owner. Relevant for SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation and GL management.']
  is_lease_execution_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA grants authority to execute, amend, or terminate lease agreements on behalf of the owner. Relevant for Yardi Voyager lease administration and DocuSign CLM lease execution workflows.']
  is_loan_closing_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA grants authority to execute mortgage, deed of trust, or other financing documents at loan closing. Critical for LTV and DSCR compliance checks by lenders and title companies.']
  is_notarized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA document has been formally notarized by a licensed notary public. Notarization is typically required for real estate transactions and recording with county/state authorities.']
  is_property_transaction_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA grants authority to execute property acquisition and disposition transactions (purchase, sale, exchange). Used by transaction teams to validate signing authority before closing.']
  is_recorded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POA has been recorded with the applicable county recorder or register of deeds. Recorded POAs provide constructive notice and are required for certain real estate transactions.']
  is_revoked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grantor has formally revoked the POA. A revoked POA must be communicated to all parties relying on it and, where recorded, a revocation instrument must be filed.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the grantee\'s KYC identity verification was completed. Used to assess whether re-verification is required based on the organization\'s KYC refresh policy.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grantee\'s identity has been verified through the owner onboarding KYC process. Required for institutional owners, trust structures, and REIT compliance with AML regulations.']
  notarization_date date [note: 'Date on which the POA document was notarized by a licensed notary public. Required for recording with county recorder offices and for title company acceptance.']
  notary_commission_number varchar [note: 'Official commission number of the notary public who notarized the POA. Used to verify the notary\'s credentials and commission validity with the issuing state authority.']
  notary_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the notary public who witnessed and notarized the POA execution. Required for legal validity verification and title company review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional legal, operational, or compliance notes related to the POA. May include estate planning context, trust structure details, or special conditions not captured in structured fields.']
  poa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the POA document, typically assigned by the executing attorney, notary, or DocuSign CLM system. Used for cross-referencing with legal and operational systems.']
  poa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POA by scope and durability. General grants broad authority; Limited restricts to specific acts; Durable survives incapacity; Springing activates upon a triggering condition; Healthcare covers medical decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general|limited|durable|springing|healthcare|special — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  power_of_attorney_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the POA record. Active indicates the POA is in force; Revoked indicates the grantor has formally withdrawn authority; Expired indicates the POA has passed its expiry date; Suspended indicates temporary hold; Pending Execution indicates awaiting notarization or signature.']
  recording_date date [note: 'Date on which the POA was officially recorded with the county recorder or register of deeds. Establishes the public notice date for the POA\'s existence and authority.']
  recording_instrument_number varchar [note: 'Official instrument or document number assigned by the county recorder or register of deeds upon recording the POA in public land records. Enables retrieval of the recorded document.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the grantor formally revoked the POA. Null if the POA has not been revoked. Used to prevent unauthorized transactions after revocation.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for revocation of the POA. Grantor Request indicates voluntary revocation; Grantee Death or Grantor Death indicates termination by operation of law; Court Order indicates judicial revocation; Superseded indicates replacement by a new POA.']
  springing_trigger_condition varchar [note: 'For springing POAs only, describes the specific condition or event that activates the POA (e.g., \'incapacity of grantor as certified by two licensed physicians\', \'grantor absence exceeding 90 days\'). Null for non-springing POA types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POA record was last modified in the system. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and compliance audit purposes.']
  witness_count varchar [note: 'Number of witnesses present at the execution of the POA. Some states require one or two witnesses in addition to notarization for the POA to be valid for real estate transactions.']

  Note: 'Power of attorney (POA) and authorized signatory records for owner entities — designating individuals or entities authorized to act on behalf of the owner in property transactions, lease executions, loan closings, and financial matters. Captures grantor (owner FK), grantee (authorized agent), scope of authority (general, limited, durable, springing), effective and expiry dates, notarization status, recording jurisdiction, revocation status, and DocuSign CLM execution reference. Critical for institutional owners, trust structures, and estate planning scenarios.'
}

Table owner.owner_representation {
  owner_representation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for owner_representation']
  brokerage_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the licensed broker or agent providing representation services to the owner']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the property owner or investor entity being represented by the broker']
  commission_override_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Owner-specific commission rate override that supersedes the broker\'s standard commission plan for deals involving this owner. Null if standard commission plan applies.']
  engagement_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the owner-broker relationship was originally established. Used for referral fee tracking and marketing attribution analysis.']
  exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this representation relationship grants the broker exclusive rights to represent the owner for transactions within the defined scope (market, asset class, transaction type). Used for conflict checks and commission dispute resolution.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent documented contact or interaction between the broker and owner in this representation relationship. Used for relationship health monitoring and CRM follow-up workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for broker notes regarding the owner relationship, including preferences, communication style, deal history, and other context relevant to this specific owner-broker pairing.']
  preferred_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market area or MSA code where this owner-broker relationship is primarily focused. Used for deal assignment routing and market conflict checks when multiple brokers represent the same owner in different markets.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the representation relationship was formally terminated or expired. Null for active ongoing relationships.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal representation relationship between the owner and broker was established, typically the effective date of the representation agreement or engagement letter']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the representation relationship. Drives eligibility for deal assignment, commission calculations, and CRM workflows.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the representation arrangement governing the scope and exclusivity of the broker\'s engagement with the owner. Determines commission entitlement, conflict-of-interest rules, and deal assignment eligibility.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal representation relationship between property owners/investors and licensed brokers. It captures the engagement terms, exclusivity arrangements, market focus, and relationship lifecycle for each owner-broker pairing. Each record links one owner to one broker with attributes that exist only in the context of this professional representation relationship, used for conflict-of-interest checks, commission tracking, deal assignment, and CRM workflows.. Existence Justification: In real estate brokerage operations, property owners and investors routinely engage multiple brokers across different markets, asset classes, and transaction types (e.g., one broker for multifamily acquisitions in Dallas, another for office dispositions in NYC). Simultaneously, brokers maintain active representation relationships with multiple owner clients, each with distinct engagement terms, exclusivity arrangements, and market focus areas. The business actively manages these representation relationships as formal engagements with lifecycle tracking, conflict-of-interest checks, commission overrides, and CRM workflows.'
}

Table owner.subscription {
  subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fund subscription record. Primary key for the subscription association.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the real estate investment fund being subscribed to']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the owner/investor entity who is subscribing to the fund']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Investor\'s accreditation or qualified purchaser status as verified at the time of subscription. Determines regulatory compliance requirements, investment limits, and disclosure obligations under SEC regulations (Reg D, 3(c)(1), 3(c)(7)).']
  agreement_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system reference to the executed subscription agreement and related legal documents for this investor-fund relationship.']
  called_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cumulative capital called from this investor\'s commitment to date. Sum of all capital call notices issued to this investor. Cannot exceed commitment_amount.']
  carried_interest_override decimal(8,4) [note: 'Investor-specific carried interest rate that overrides the fund\'s standard carried_interest_rate, typically negotiated for early or anchor investors. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15.00%). Null if standard fund carry applies.']
  commitment_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total capital amount committed by the investor to the fund under this subscription agreement. This is the maximum amount the GP can call from this investor. Denominated in the fund\'s base currency.']
  distributed_capital decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cumulative capital distributed to this investor to date, including return of capital, preferred return, and profit distributions. Used to calculate investor-level IRR and equity multiple (TVPI).']
  investor_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the investor within the fund structure at the time of subscription. Determines fee terms, distribution priority, voting rights, and reporting requirements. Common classes include institutional vs retail, different fee tiers, or co-investment vehicles.']
  management_fee_override decimal(8,4) [note: 'Investor-specific management fee rate that overrides the fund\'s standard management_fee_rate, typically negotiated in a side letter for large institutional commitments. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0150 = 1.50%). Null if standard fund fee applies.']
  side_letter_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription includes a side letter agreement with customized terms, fee arrangements, co-investment rights, information rights, or other investor-specific provisions that deviate from the standard Limited Partnership Agreement.']
  side_letter_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of key side letter provisions for this subscription, including fee discounts, co-investment rights, advisory board seats, information rights, key person provisions, or investment restrictions. Null if side_letter_flag is false.']
  subscription_date date [note: 'Date on which the investor executed the subscription agreement and became a Limited Partner in the fund. Used to determine eligibility for distributions, carried interest waterfall calculations, and investor reporting periods.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription. Active subscriptions are subject to capital calls and distributions. Withdrawn subscriptions were cancelled before final close. Defaulted subscriptions failed to meet capital call obligations. Fully Distributed subscriptions have received all capital back and the fund has terminated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual subscription relationship between an owner/investor and a real estate investment fund. It captures the investor\'s capital commitment, subscription terms, investor classification at the time of subscription, accreditation status verification, and side letter provisions. Each record represents a distinct subscription agreement linking one owner to one fund with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific investment commitment.. Existence Justification: In real estate fund operations, an owner/investor can subscribe to multiple funds (diversification across strategies, vintages, and asset classes), and each fund has multiple Limited Partner investors who have subscribed. Each subscription is a distinct contractual relationship with its own capital commitment, subscription date, investor class, fee terms (potentially customized via side letters), and accreditation verification. Fund administrators actively manage subscriptions as operational entities—tracking capital calls, distributions, investor reporting, K-1 generation, and waterfall calculations at the subscription level.'
}

Table valuation.appraisal {

  appraisal_review_appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal or external review appraiser who performed a USPAP Standard 3 appraisal review of the report. Required for lender compliance and internal quality control processes.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being appraised. Links the appraisal engagement to the master property record in the portfolio.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Appraisal comparable selection, cap rate benchmarks, and market rent conclusions are building-class-specific (Class A vs B vs C).   building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Appraisals are conducted at the building level — the physical inspection, GBA measurement, condition rating, and USPAP scope of work all reference a specific building record. A commercial RE appraiser']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: appraisal stores a concluded cap_rate.   compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit.   compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Appraisal engagements belong to compliance programs (annual portfolio appraisal cycle, lender appraisal panel program, FIRREA compliance program). Linking individual appraisals to the governing compli']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: All appraisal financial outputs (concluded_market_value, engagement_fee, value_range_low/high) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK enables multi-currency portf']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Lenders order appraisals at origination and refinancing; the appraisal must be traceable to the loan it ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Lenders and equity investors pull all appraisals by dev_projec']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Appraisal comparable selection, cap rate benchmarks, and market conditions analysis are submarket-specific. A domain expert would expect every appraisal to reference the geographic hierarchy for subma']

Business justification: Appraisals are jurisdiction-specific: appraiser licensing, USPAP enforcement, and regulatory filing requirements vary by jurisdiction. Compliance teams and lenders require jurisdiction traceability on']

Business justification: Appraisals are commissioned by and delivered to specific legal entities for REIT financial reporting, ASC 820 fair value hierarchy disclosures, and audit purposes. The existing `intended_user` and `cl']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.listing. Business justification: Appraisals are routinely commissioned to validate or support a specific listing price — by sellers, lenders, or buyers during active marketing. Linking appraisal to listing enables listing price valid']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: Appraisers cite market research reports to support market conditions narratives, cap rate selection, and rent assumptions in USPAP-compliant appraisal reports. A real estate appraiser would expect the']

Business justification: Appraisers directly reference market surveys for vacancy rates, asking rents, and absorption data used in the market conditions section of appraisal reports. Business justification: Appraisals must comply with USPAP, IVSC, or jurisdiction-specific standards; the uspap_compliant and ivsc_compliant flags are boolean indicators without a FK to the governing framework.
