  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this comparable lease record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this comparable lease record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the comparable lease includes one or more tenant renewal options. Presence of renewal options affects the economic value of the lease and is noted in market rent analysis.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal option period available to the tenant under the comparable lease. Used to assess the total potential lease commitment and WALT calculations.']
  rent_escalation_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'Annual rent escalation rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.03 for 3%) applicable to fixed or percentage-based escalation structures. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models to project future rent cash flows.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Method by which base rent escalates over the lease term (e.g., fixed annual percentage, CPI-linked, stepped increases, or flat). Used to assess the economic profile of the comparable lease in DCF-based valuation models.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the comparable property is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping and geographic market analysis.']
  ti_allowance_psf decimal(12,4) [note: 'Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance provided by the landlord expressed on a per-square-foot basis. A key concession metric used to normalize comparable leases and assess true economic rent in income approach valuations.']
  time_adjustment_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage adjustment applied to the comparable lease rent to account for market conditions between the lease execution date and the valuation date. Used in the sales comparison and income approaches to normalize historical comps to current market.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the comparable lease transaction data was verified through a reliable source. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology documentation and appraisal report quality control.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the comparable lease data indicating whether the transaction details have been confirmed through a reliable source such as broker confirmation, public record, or direct tenant/landlord contact. Drives reliability weighting in rent analysis.']

  Note: 'Comparable lease transaction record used as market evidence in income capitalization and rent analysis. Stores comp property address, tenant name, lease execution date, lease commencement date, lease term (months), base rent PSF, rent type (NNN, gross, modified gross), TI allowance PSF, free rent period, lease size (SQF/SQM), property class, submarket, data source (CoStar, MLS, direct), and verification status. Supports market rent conclusions in income approach valuations.'
}

Table valuation.approach {
  approach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approach']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent valuation engagement or appraisal report to which this approach record belongs. Links the approach to its overarching valuation assignment.']
  appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified appraiser responsible for preparing and signing off on this valuation approach. Links to the appraiser credential record capturing Appraisal Institute certification, state license, and USPAP compliance status.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being valued under this approach. Ties the valuation approach to the specific real estate asset in the property master record.']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: valuation_approach uses cap_rate in direct capitalization. The applied cap rate should be benchmarked against market data. Adding cap_rate_benchmark_id links the approach model to the market benchmark']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Valuation approach financial outputs (concluded_value, pgi, egi, noi, land_value, replacement_cost_new) are currency-denominated. Normalizing to a FK enables consistent currency handling in multi-curr']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervising appraiser or peer reviewer who reviewed and approved this valuation approach. Supports quality control workflows and USPAP Standard 3 (Appraisal Review) compliance.']
  noi_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: The income approach in valuation_approach uses NOI as its primary input. Linking to the authoritative NOI statement documents the source of the NOI figure used in the income approach, supporting USPAP']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Valuation approach parameters (cap rate, GRM, cost indices, depreciation rates) are property-type-specific. A domain expert would expect the income, sales comparison, and cost approaches to reference ']
  approach_date date [note: 'Effective date as of which the valuation approach analysis is performed. Represents the appraisal effective date for this specific methodology, which may differ from the report date.']
  approach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this valuation approach record within the appraisal workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through peer review, approval, and potential supersession by a revised approach.']
  approach_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the valuation methodology applied in this record. Supported types: direct_cap (Direct Capitalization), DCF (Discounted Cash Flow), cost (Cost Approach), sales_comparison (Sales Comparison Approach), GRM (Gross Rent Multiplier). Drives which set of methodology-specific fields are populated.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this valuation approach was formally approved by the reviewing appraiser or valuation committee. Marks the transition from in_review to approved status in the approach lifecycle.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model associated with this DCF valuation approach. Enables traceability back to the source Argus model for audit, review, and replication purposes. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  cap_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Overall capitalization rate (CAP rate) applied to stabilized NOI to derive the direct capitalization value. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). Derived from market comparable sales analysis and reflects investor return expectations for the asset class and submarket.']
  comparable_count varchar [note: 'Number of comparable sales or lease transactions used to support the approach inputs (e.g., CAP rate derivation for direct cap, market rent for income approaches, adjusted sales for sales comparison). Indicates the depth of market evidence supporting the concluded value.']
  concluded_market_rent_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Appraiser\'s concluded market rent per square foot (PSF) used as the basis for PGI estimation in income capitalization approaches. Derived from comparable lease analysis and market surveys. Expressed in the reporting currency per square foot per year.']
  concluded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The indicated market value produced by this specific valuation approach before reconciliation weighting. Represents the raw output of the methodology (e.g., direct capitalization value, DCF NPV, depreciated cost, or sales comparison adjusted value) in the reporting currency.']
  cost_index_source varchar [note: 'Source of the construction cost index or cost manual used to estimate RCN in the cost approach. Common sources include Marshall & Swift Valuation Service and RSMeans Cost Data. Supports methodology transparency and IVSC IVS 105 compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording when this valuation approach record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls for valuation records used in financial reporting.']
  depreciated_cost_of_improvements decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net value of the improvements after deducting total accrued depreciation (physical, functional, and external obsolescence) from RCN. Added to land value to derive the cost approach indicated value. Populated for cost approach type.']
  direct_cap_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Indicated value derived by dividing stabilized NOI by the overall CAP rate (NOI / CAP Rate). Represents the concluded value under the direct capitalization method. Populated only when approach_type = direct_cap.']
  discount_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Risk-adjusted discount rate (expressed as a decimal) used in the DCF analysis to convert projected future cash flows to present value. Reflects the required rate of return for the investment, incorporating market risk, asset type, and leverage. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  economic_life_years varchar [note: 'Total estimated economic life of the improvements in years — the period over which the building is expected to contribute positively to property value. Used with effective age to calculate the age-life depreciation ratio in the cost approach.']
  effective_age_years varchar [note: 'Appraiser\'s estimate of the effective age of the improvements in years, reflecting the actual condition and utility of the building relative to a new structure of similar type. May differ from chronological age due to renovations or deferred maintenance. Used in cost approach depreciation calculations.']
  egi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Effective Gross Income — PGI adjusted for vacancy, credit loss, and other income (e.g., parking, ancillary revenue). Represents the realistic annual income the property is expected to generate. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  external_obsolescence decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value caused by factors external to the property, such as adverse market conditions, neighborhood decline, or proximity to negative influences. Also known as economic obsolescence. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.']
  functional_obsolescence decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value resulting from deficiencies or superadequacies in the design, layout, or functionality of the improvements relative to current market standards (e.g., outdated floor plans, inadequate HVAC systems). Deducted from RCN in the cost approach.']
  grm decimal(7,4) [note: 'Gross Rent Multiplier — the ratio of property value to gross annual (or monthly) rental income, derived from comparable sales. Applied to the subject property\'s gross rent to estimate value under the GRM approach. Populated when approach_type = GRM.']
  hold_period_years varchar [note: 'Projected investment hold period in years used in the DCF analysis. Defines the number of annual cash flow periods modeled before the terminal reversion value is calculated. Typically ranges from 5 to 15 years for commercial real estate. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  irr decimal(7,6) [note: 'Internal Rate of Return — the discount rate at which the net present value of all projected cash flows (including acquisition cost and terminal reversion) equals zero. Key investment performance metric output from the DCF model. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  ivsc_methodology_code varchar [note: 'IVSC IVS 105 methodology classification code identifying the specific valuation technique applied (e.g., IVS105-IC for Income Capitalization, IVS105-CA for Cost Approach, IVS105-SC for Sales Comparison). Supports regulatory compliance documentation and cross-border reporting.']
  land_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market value of the land component as if vacant and available for its highest and best use. Used in the cost approach as the foundation to which depreciated cost of improvements is added. Populated for cost approach type.']
  methodology_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing appraiser commentary on the rationale, assumptions, limitations, and adjustments specific to this valuation approach. Supports IVSC IVS 105 compliance, USPAP disclosure requirements, and peer review documentation.']
  noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Operating Income — EGI minus total OPEX. The primary income metric used in direct capitalization and DCF valuation. Represents the annual pre-debt, pre-tax cash flow generated by the property. Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value — the sum of all discounted future cash flows minus the initial investment cost, as output by the DCF model. A positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  pgi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Potential Gross Income — the maximum annual rental revenue the property could generate at 100% occupancy at market rent. Used as the starting point for income capitalization approaches (direct cap and DCF). Populated for direct_cap and DCF approach types.']
  physical_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Loss in value attributable to physical deterioration of the improvements, including curable deferred maintenance and incurable structural wear. Deducted from RCN in the cost approach. Populated for cost approach type.']
  rent_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the concluded market rent is expressed. Indicates whether the rent is annual or monthly and the lease structure (NNN — Triple Net, gross, or modified gross) that underpins the income analysis.']
  replacement_cost_new decimal(18,2) [note: 'Replacement Cost New (RCN) — the estimated cost to construct a building of equivalent utility using current materials, standards, and design at current prices. Starting point for the cost approach before depreciation deductions. Populated for cost approach type.']
  sensitivity_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of key sensitivity scenarios tested for this approach, such as the impact of a 25 bps change in CAP rate or discount rate on concluded value. Supports investment decision-making and risk disclosure in appraisal reports.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(7,6) [note: 'Reversion or terminal CAP rate applied to the projected NOI in the final year of the hold period to estimate the property\'s residual (terminal) value in a DCF analysis. Typically higher than the going-in CAP rate to reflect future uncertainty. Populated for DCF approach type.']
  total_opex decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual operating expenses (OPEX) for the subject property, including property taxes, insurance, management fees, maintenance, utilities, and reserves for replacement. Deducted from EGI to derive NOI. Populated for income capitalization approaches.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp recording the most recent modification to this valuation approach record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vacancy_credit_loss_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Estimated rate (decimal fraction) applied to PGI to account for expected vacancy and uncollectible rent. For example, 0.05 represents a 5% vacancy and credit loss allowance. Used in income capitalization to derive EGI from PGI.']
  weight_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weighting factor (expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1) assigned to this approach in the final value reconciliation. For example, 0.60 indicates this approach contributes 60% to the concluded value. All approach weights for a given valuation should sum to 1.0.']

  Note: 'Valuation approach model record capturing the quantitative analysis for income capitalization and cost approach methodologies. For income capitalization: stores PGI, vacancy/credit loss rate, EGI, total OPEX, NOI, CAP rate, direct capitalization value, terminal CAP rate, discount rate, hold period, IRR/NPV outputs, and concluded market rent inputs. For cost approach: stores land value, replacement cost new (RCN), depreciation components (physical, functional, external obsolescence), depreciated cost of improvements, effective age, economic life, and cost index source (Marshall & Swift, RSMeans). Includes approach type discriminator (direct_cap, DCF, cost, sales_comparison, GRM) and approach weight in final reconciliation. Supports IVSC IVS 105 compliance. After merge with cost_approach, this is the unified approach model for all valuation methodologies.'
}

Table valuation.cost_approach {
  cost_approach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the cost approach valuation record. Primary key for this entity.']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent valuation engagement or appraisal report under which this cost approach analysis was performed.']
  appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified appraiser or appraisal firm responsible for performing and certifying the cost approach analysis.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being valued under the cost approach methodology.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: Cost approach valuation requires condition rating, useful life, replacement cost, and depreciation data that resides on the building_asset record. Appraisers performing USPAP-compliant cost approaches']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Cost approach replacement cost benchmarks, finish quality grades, and depreciation rates are building-class-specific. The quality_rating and condition_rating in cost_approach are class-adjacent; linki']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: The cost approach directly uses building physical data: GBA, construction_type, year_built, structural_system, and condition_rating. cost_approach.gross_building_area_sqf is denormalized from building']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Cost approach valuations for properties under construction directly depend on building permits — approved scope of work, permitted floor area, and permit conditions affect replacement cost calculation']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Cost approach replacement cost per SF, depreciation method, economic life, and insurance replacement value are all construction-type-specific (wood frame vs steel vs concrete). A domain expert would e']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Cost approach financial outputs (land_value, rcn_total, indicated_value, insurance_replacement_value) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK supports multi-curren']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: The cost approach valuation for development properties uses RCN inputs derived from the active construction project. Appraisers reference the development project\'s hard cost budget and specifications ']
  income_approach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.valuation_approach. Business justification: cost_approach is the detailed cost approach model record. valuation_approach with approach_type = \'COST\' stores the concluded value and weight within the appraisal reconciliation. Adding income_approa']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: The cost approach requires land value as a separate component (land_value field). The parcel record holds assessed_land_value, lot_size_acres, zoning, and FAR — all direct inputs to the land value con']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Cost approach replacement cost benchmarks, depreciation rates, economic life estimates, and cost index sources are all property-type-specific. The plain property_type column is denormalized; normalizi']
  appraiser_designation varchar [note: 'Professional designation held by the appraiser, such as MAI (Member Appraisal Institute) or SRA, confirming qualification level for the type of property being appraised. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MAI|SRA|AI-GRS|AI-RRS|FRICS|ASA|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appraiser_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued license or certification number of the appraiser performing the cost approach valuation, required for regulatory compliance and credential verification per Appraisal Institute and USPAP standards.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file name of the Argus Enterprise valuation model associated with this cost approach record, enabling traceability to the source cash flow and valuation model used in the broader appraisal engagement.']
  chronological_age_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'The actual age of the improvements in years from the date of original construction to the valuation date. Provides context for comparing effective age and assessing the degree of deferred maintenance or renovation.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Appraiser\'s qualitative rating of the physical condition of the improvements at the time of inspection, informing the observed condition depreciation analysis and supporting the effective age estimate.']
  cost_index_multiplier decimal(10,6) [note: 'Geographic and temporal adjustment multiplier applied to the base cost index to reflect local construction cost conditions at the subject property location and valuation date.']
  cost_index_source varchar [note: 'The cost estimating publication or data source used to derive replacement cost new (RCN) figures, such as Marshall & Swift Valuation Service or RSMeans Construction Cost Data. Drives reproducibility and auditability of the cost estimate.']
  cost_index_year varchar [note: 'The publication year of the cost index edition (e.g., Marshall & Swift or RSMeans) used to establish the replacement cost new, ensuring temporal alignment with the valuation date.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cost basis used is replacement cost new (RCN — cost of a substitute improvement of equivalent utility using current materials and standards) or reproduction cost new (cost of an exact replica using original materials and design). Replacement cost is standard for most appraisals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost approach valuation record was first created in the system, providing an audit trail for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  depreciated_cost_of_improvements decimal(18,2) [note: 'The depreciated cost of improvements (DCI), calculated as replacement cost new less total accrued depreciation. Represents the contributory value of the improvements to the overall property value under the cost approach.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to estimate total accrued depreciation, such as the age-life method, modified age-life method, observed condition method, or the breakdown method which separately quantifies each depreciation component.']
  economic_life_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total estimated economic life of the improvements in years, representing the period over which the improvements contribute positively to property value. Used as the denominator in the age-life depreciation ratio.']
  effective_age_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'The appraiser\'s estimate of the age of the improvements in years based on observed condition and utility, which may differ from chronological age. Used in the age-life depreciation method to compute physical deterioration.']
  entrepreneurial_profit_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The market-derived profit percentage added to direct and indirect costs to reflect the entrepreneurial incentive and compensation for risk undertaken in developing the improvements, expressed as a percentage of total development cost.']
  external_obsolescence_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of depreciation attributable to external (economic) obsolescence, reflecting loss in value caused by factors external to the property such as adverse market conditions, locational disadvantages, or environmental influences.']
  functional_obsolescence_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of depreciation attributable to functional obsolescence, reflecting loss in value due to deficiencies or superadequacies in the design, layout, or utility of the improvements relative to current market standards.']
  indicated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The concluded property value as indicated by the cost approach, calculated as land value plus depreciated cost of improvements plus site improvements value. This is the primary output of the cost approach model.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the appraiser physically inspected the subject property to assess condition, quality, and improvements for the cost approach analysis.']
  insurance_replacement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated cost to replace the insurable improvements (excluding land) at current prices without deduction for depreciation, used specifically for property insurance coverage determination. May differ from the cost approach indicated value.']
  land_to_value_ratio decimal(7,4) [note: 'The ratio of land value to total indicated value via cost approach, expressed as a decimal. Used in portfolio analytics, tax assessment allocation, and insurance underwriting to assess the relative contribution of land versus improvements.']
  land_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market value of the land component of the subject property as if vacant and available for its highest and best use, derived from comparable land sales analysis. A foundational input to the cost approach concluded value.']
  land_value_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to estimate the land value component, such as sales comparison of vacant land, allocation from improved sales, or extraction. Documents the analytical basis for the land value conclusion.']
  methodology_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the appraiser\'s methodology, assumptions, limiting conditions, and any deviations from standard cost approach procedures. Supports IVSC-compliant methodology metadata and USPAP disclosure requirements.']
  physical_deterioration_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of depreciation attributable to physical deterioration of the improvements, including curable items (deferred maintenance) and incurable items (short-lived and long-lived components), derived from age-life or observed condition analysis.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Appraiser\'s qualitative rating of the construction quality of the improvements, used to select the appropriate cost class and quality multiplier from the cost index (e.g., Marshall & Swift quality class). Drives the RCN per square foot selection.']
  rcn_per_sqf decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to construct a replica or substitute improvement of equivalent utility at current prices, expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis, derived from the selected cost index and adjusted for location and quality.']
  rcn_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total replacement cost new (RCN) of all improvements, representing the estimated cost to construct a substitute improvement of equivalent utility at current prices. Computed as RCN PSF multiplied by gross building area, inclusive of direct and indirect costs.']
  remaining_economic_life_years decimal(6,2) [note: 'The estimated remaining economic life of the improvements in years, calculated as total economic life less effective age. Indicates the remaining period of positive contribution to value and is used in investment underwriting and insurance assessments.']
  report_date date [note: 'The date on which the appraisal report containing the cost approach analysis was signed and issued by the certified appraiser.']
  report_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known appraisal report number or engagement reference code assigned by the appraiser or appraisal firm, used for cross-referencing with Argus Enterprise and source appraisal documents.']
  site_improvements_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated depreciated value of site improvements such as paving, landscaping, utilities, and fencing that are separate from the primary building improvements and land value.']
  total_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total accrued depreciation applied to the replacement cost new, representing the sum of physical deterioration, functional obsolescence, and external obsolescence. Subtracted from RCN to derive the depreciated cost of improvements.']
  total_depreciation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Total accrued depreciation expressed as a percentage of replacement cost new, providing a normalized measure of overall improvement condition and obsolescence for benchmarking and trend analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost approach valuation record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking for financial reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The effective date as of which the cost approach value conclusion is applicable, representing the point-in-time market conditions used in the analysis. Distinct from the report date.']
  valuation_purpose varchar [note: 'The intended use and purpose of the cost approach valuation, which governs the applicable standard of value and methodology. Common purposes include insurance replacement cost, financial reporting under ASC 820/IFRS 13, and acquisition underwriting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insurance|financial_reporting|acquisition|disposition|financing|tax_assessment|litigation|internal_review — promote to reference product]']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost approach valuation record, tracking progression from initial draft through certified completion or supersession by a revised analysis.']

  Note: 'Cost approach valuation model record capturing land value, replacement cost new (RCN), depreciation components (physical deterioration, functional obsolescence, external obsolescence), depreciated cost of improvements, and concluded value via cost approach. Stores cost estimator reference, Marshall & Swift or RSMeans cost index used, effective age, economic life, remaining economic life, and land-to-value ratio. Used primarily for special-use properties, insurance valuations, and new construction assessments.'
}

Table valuation.dcf_model {
  dcf_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the DCF model record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the valuation_dcf_model product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being underwritten or valued through this DCF model.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: DCF models are reviewed during audit engagements for fair value testing of investment properties. External auditors test DCF model inputs, discount rates, and terminal cap rates as part of investment ']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: DCF models in commercial RE are built at the building level — rent roll, lease expirations, capex_reserve_psf, and NLA are all building-specific inputs. Argus Enterprise models are structured around b']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: valuation_dcf_model uses going_in_cap_rate and terminal_cap_rate assumptions. These should be benchmarked against market cap rate data. Adding cap_rate_benchmark_id links the DCF model to the market b']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: DCF model financial outputs (acquisition_price, equity_investment, debt_amount, npv, reversion_value) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK is required for multi']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.debt_instrument. Business justification: Leveraged DCF models incorporate actual loan terms (interest rate, amortization, LTV) from specific debt instruments. The model has `debt_amount` and `ltv_ratio` as manual inputs; linking to the actua']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: DCF model assumptions (rent growth rate, vacancy rate, exit cap rate) are submarket-specific. Linking to geographic_hierarchy enables submarket-level DCF assumption validation, market-driven sensitivi']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: DCF models use market-derived rent growth rates, vacancy assumptions, and terminal cap rates sourced from market research. Investment committees require DCF models to cite the market research supporti']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: DCF model assumptions (discount rate, terminal cap rate, hold period, leverage) are calibrated to investment strategy. The market_segment defines target IRR ranges, LTV limits, and hold periods that v']
  primary_dcf_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment analyst or appraiser who built and owns this DCF model.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: DCF models used for REIT reporting, fund NAV, or SEC filings must comply with specific regulatory obligations (ASC 820 fair value hierarchy, SEC Regulation S-X, INREV guidelines). Compliance teams tra']
  acquisition_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assumed purchase price of the property at the start of the hold period, used as the basis for LTV, equity multiple, and IRR calculations.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this DCF model was formally approved by the designated reviewer or investment committee. Required for SOX audit trail and governance compliance.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Native model identifier assigned by Argus Enterprise to the source cash flow model file. Used to trace back to the originating Argus Enterprise model for audit and reconciliation.']
  argus_model_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the Argus Enterprise model file (e.g., v1.0, v2.3-final) to distinguish iterative revisions of the same model.']
  avg_dscr decimal(8,4) [note: 'Average annual Debt Service Coverage Ratio across the hold period, calculated as NOI divided by annual debt service. Lenders typically require a minimum DSCR of 1.20x–1.25x.']
  capex_reserve_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual capital expenditure reserve amount per square foot budgeted for building improvements, tenant improvements (TI), and leasing commissions over the hold period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCF model record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  debt_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debt (mortgage or loan) assumed at acquisition used to finance the property purchase. Drives LTV and DSCR calculations within the model.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The target Internal Rate of Return (IRR) used as the discount rate to compute Net Present Value (NPV) of projected cash flows. Reflects the required return on investment for this asset.']
  equity_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total equity capital contributed at acquisition, representing the difference between acquisition price and debt financing. Used to compute levered IRR and equity multiple.']
  equity_multiple decimal(8,4) [note: 'Total equity distributions (cash flows plus net reversion proceeds) divided by total equity invested. An equity multiple of 2.0x means the investor doubles their equity over the hold period.']
  expense_growth_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assumed annual percentage increase in operating expenses (OPEX) applied across the projection period. Typically benchmarked to CPI or market inflation indices.']
  going_in_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The capitalization rate implied by the acquisition price and Year 1 NOI. Used to benchmark the entry pricing against market comparables and assess acquisition risk.']
  hold_period_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the modeled investment hold period ends, representing the assumed disposition or reversion date.']
  hold_period_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the modeled investment hold period commences, typically the assumed acquisition or ownership transfer date.']
  hold_period_years varchar [note: 'Total duration of the modeled investment hold period expressed in whole years (e.g., 10 for a 10-year hold). Drives the number of annual cash flow projection periods.']
  is_levered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DCF model incorporates debt financing (levered analysis). True = levered model with debt service; False = unlevered all-equity model.']
  levered_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return computed on levered (equity) cash flows after debt service. Reflects the actual return to equity investors accounting for financial leverage.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of the debt amount to the acquisition price at the time of underwriting, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 for 65% LTV). Key metric for lender and investor risk assessment.']
  methodology_standard varchar [note: 'The governing valuation methodology standard applied in constructing this DCF model. Ensures compliance with applicable appraisal and financial reporting frameworks.']
  min_dscr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The lowest annual DSCR observed across all projection periods in the hold period. Identifies the most stressed year for debt coverage, critical for lender covenant compliance.']
  model_assumptions_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the key assumptions, market conditions, data sources, and qualifications underlying this DCF model. Required for IVSC-compliant methodology disclosure and investment committee review.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for this DCF model record, formatted as DCF-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in investment committee presentations, audit trails, and cross-system references.']
  model_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which this DCF model was constructed. Determines the applicable methodology standards and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition_underwriting|disposition_analysis|refinancing|annual_valuation|portfolio_reporting|impairment_testing|loan_origination — promote to reference product]']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DCF model record. Controls which version is authoritative for investment decisions and financial reporting.']
  net_reversion_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross reversion value less estimated selling costs, representing the net cash inflow to the investor upon disposition at the end of the hold period.']
  npv_at_discount_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value of all projected cash flows (including reversion) discounted at the model\'s target discount rate. Positive NPV indicates value creation above the required return threshold.']
  rent_growth_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Assumed annual percentage increase in market rents applied across the projection period. Drives revenue escalation in the DCF cash flow schedule.']
  reversion_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated gross sale proceeds at the end of the hold period, calculated by applying the terminal cap rate to the projected NOI in the reversion year. Represents the largest single cash inflow in the DCF model.']
  selling_costs_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage of reversion value deducted for estimated disposition costs (brokerage commissions, transfer taxes, legal fees) to arrive at net reversion proceeds.']
  sensitivity_irr_high decimal(8,4) [note: 'Levered IRR under the upside/bull case sensitivity scenario, reflecting optimistic assumptions (lower vacancy, higher rent growth, lower cap rate). Bounds the investment return range.']
  sensitivity_irr_low decimal(8,4) [note: 'Levered IRR under the downside/bear case sensitivity scenario, reflecting stressed assumptions (higher vacancy, lower rent growth, higher cap rate). Used in investment committee risk analysis.']
  stabilized_noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected NOI at stabilized occupancy, representing the normalized income potential of the property once lease-up or repositioning is complete. Used for terminal cap rate application.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The capitalization rate applied to the final year Net Operating Income (NOI) to derive the reversion/terminal value at the end of the hold period. Critical assumption in DCF reversion calculation.']
  unlevered_irr decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return computed on unlevered (all-equity, pre-financing) cash flows. Measures the intrinsic return of the asset independent of capital structure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCF model record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale models and enforce model refresh policies.']
  vacancy_rate_assumption decimal(8,4) [note: 'Blended vacancy and credit loss rate applied to Potential Gross Income (PGI) to derive Effective Gross Income (EGI) in the model. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The effective date as of which the DCF model value conclusion is stated. This is the principal real-world event date for the model, distinct from the model creation or approval date.']
  year1_noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected Net Operating Income for the first year of the hold period, representing Effective Gross Income (EGI) less operating expenses. Foundational input for going-in cap rate and debt sizing.']

  Note: 'Discounted Cash Flow model record linked to an Argus Enterprise model file. Stores Argus model ID, model version, hold period start and end dates, annual cash flow projections (year 1–10+), reversion/terminal value, discount rate (IRR target), NPV at various discount rates, levered vs. unlevered IRR, equity multiple, DSCR per period, LTV at acquisition, and model assumptions narrative. Serves as the quantitative backbone for investment-grade valuations and acquisition underwriting.'
}

Table valuation.cap_rate_benchmark {
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CAP rate benchmark record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Benchmark governance: the internal analyst responsible for maintaining and publishing cap rate benchmarks must be tracked for data governance, benchmark review workflows, and investment committee repo']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by building class (Class A, B, C) alongside property type; the asset_class plain column is a denormalized building class reference. Normalizing to a FK enables struct']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are country-specific for international real estate portfolios; the plain country_code column is a denormalized country reference. Normalizing to a FK enables country-level benchmar']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Cap rate benchmark NOI and value PSF figures (noi_psf_low/high, implied_value_psf_low/high) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK enables cross-currency benchmar']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are defined by submarket/MSA; the submarket_name, msa_name, msa_code, and state_code plain columns are all denormalized geographic hierarchy fields. Normalizing to a FK enables str']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are derived from and corroborated by market research reports. Valuation teams link benchmarks to the research that supports them for audit trails and IVSC/USPAP compliance document']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are segmented by investment strategy (core, value-add, opportunistic); institutional-grade core assets trade at different cap rates than value-add. Linking to market_segment enable']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Cap rate benchmarks are primarily organized by property type; this is the core classification axis for benchmark lookup in appraisals and DCF models. The plain property_type column is denormalized; no']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for valuation analyst commentary on this benchmark record, including observations about market conditions, anomalies in the survey data, adjustments made during data ingestion, or guidance for appraisers on appropriate application of this benchmark.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier or file path of the Argus Enterprise valuation model that incorporates or was calibrated against this benchmark record. Enables traceability between market benchmark data and specific DCF/cash flow models used in investment analysis and portfolio reporting.']
  benchmark_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this benchmark record, used for cross-system referencing and CoStar/Argus Enterprise integration (e.g., CRB-2024Q3-OFF-CLA-NYC). Serves as the business identifier for this benchmark.']
  benchmark_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this benchmark record indicating whether it is currently active for use in valuations, has been superseded by a newer survey, is in draft pending publication, archived for historical reference, or under review for accuracy.']
  cap_rate_high decimal(8,4) [note: 'The upper bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0650 = 6.50%). Represents the most conservative (highest) CAP rate observed, typically associated with lower-quality assets or less competitive submarkets.']
  cap_rate_low decimal(8,4) [note: 'The lower bound of the surveyed CAP rate range for this property type, asset class, and submarket combination, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0450 = 4.50%). Represents the most aggressive (lowest) CAP rate observed in the market, typically associated with the highest-quality assets or most competitive submarkets.']
  cap_rate_mid decimal(8,4) [note: 'The midpoint or median of the surveyed CAP rate range, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0550 = 5.50%). This is the primary benchmark value used by appraisers for CAP rate selection validation and by asset managers for NAV/GAV calculations in Argus Enterprise models.']
  cap_rate_prior_mid decimal(8,4) [note: 'The midpoint CAP rate from the immediately preceding survey period for the same property type, asset class, and submarket. Used to calculate period-over-period movement and validate the trend_direction field. Supports time-series analysis in investment committee presentations.']
  costar_market_reference varchar [note: 'The CoStar Suite internal market identifier for the submarket or market area covered by this benchmark. Enables direct linkage to CoStar market analytics, comparable transaction data, and property analytics for benchmark validation and enrichment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benchmark record was first created and loaded into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record management in compliance with SOX financial controls and GDPR data governance requirements.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the assessed quality and reliability of this benchmark record based on sample size, source credibility, methodology transparency, and recency. Used by the data governance team to flag low-confidence benchmarks and by appraisers to weight multiple sources.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this benchmark record is considered valid and applicable for use in appraisals, NAV/GAV calculations, and investment committee presentations. May differ from survey_date if there is a publication lag.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this benchmark record is no longer considered current and should not be used for new valuations without review. Typically set to the next expected survey publication date.']
  implied_value_psf_high decimal(10,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Used in conjunction with implied_value_psf_low to establish a market value range for portfolio-level reporting.']
  implied_value_psf_low decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the implied market value per square foot derived from the CAP rate range and prevailing NOI levels for this segment. Provides a sanity-check metric for appraiser CAP rate selections and supports portfolio-level GAV/NAV estimation.']
  investor_sentiment varchar [note: 'The prevailing investor sentiment toward this property type and market segment as reported in the source survey (bullish, neutral, bearish). Provides qualitative market context for investment committee presentations and portfolio strategy discussions.']
  is_institutional_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benchmark applies specifically to institutional-grade investment properties meeting NCREIF/REIT portfolio standards (True) or to the broader market including non-institutional assets (False). Relevant for REIT portfolio NAV/GAV calculations and SEC reporting.']
  is_primary_benchmark boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is designated as the primary (preferred) benchmark for the given property type, asset class, and submarket combination when multiple sources exist for the same period. True = primary benchmark to be used in appraisal validation; False = secondary/supplemental benchmark.']
  market_liquidity_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the transaction liquidity in this market segment at the time of the survey (high, moderate, low, illiquid). Informs the reliability weighting of the benchmark and the appropriate discount for illiquidity in investment advisory and REIT portfolio reporting.']
  ncreif_property_index_code varchar [note: 'The NCREIF (National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries) Property Index segment code corresponding to this benchmark\'s property type and geographic classification. Enables alignment with NCREIF NPI return benchmarks for institutional investment performance reporting.']
  noi_psf_high decimal(10,2) [note: 'The upper bound of the surveyed Net Operating Income (NOI) per square foot (PSF) range for this market segment. Used alongside noi_psf_low to bracket NOI assumptions in Argus Enterprise DCF models and appraisal income approach analyses.']
  noi_psf_low decimal(10,2) [note: 'The lower bound of the surveyed Net Operating Income (NOI) per square foot (PSF) range for this market segment, expressed in the currency per square foot. Used as an input cross-check for DCF models in Argus Enterprise and for validating NOI assumptions in appraisal reports.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of transactions, properties, or investor respondents included in the source survey for this specific property type, asset class, and submarket segment. A key indicator of statistical reliability; smaller samples warrant greater caution in appraisal application.']
  source_organization varchar [note: 'The name of the industry research organization or brokerage firm that published the survey from which this benchmark data was sourced (e.g., CoStar, CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield, NCREIF, PwC/Korpacz). Governs the credibility weighting applied during appraiser CAP rate selection validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CoStar|CBRE|JLL|Cushman & Wakefield|NCREIF|PwC/Korpacz|Marcus & Millichap|Colliers|Avison Young|Green Street — promote to reference product]']
  source_report_name varchar [note: 'The formal name of the survey report or publication from which this benchmark data was extracted (e.g., \'CBRE Cap Rate Survey H1 2024\', \'PwC Real Estate Investor Survey Q3 2024\', \'NCREIF Property Index Q2 2024\'). Enables traceability back to the original source document for audit and IVSC compliance.']
  source_report_url varchar [note: 'The URL or digital reference link to the original published survey report. Supports audit trail requirements and allows appraisers to access the primary source document when validating CAP rate selections.']
  survey_date date [note: 'The date on which the market survey or data collection was conducted or published by the source organization. This is the principal real-world event date for this benchmark record, representing when the CAP rate data was captured from the market.']
  survey_frequency varchar [note: 'The publication cadence of the source survey (quarterly, semi-annual, annual, monthly). Used to determine the expected expiry_date of each benchmark record and to schedule automated refresh workflows in the data pipeline.']
  survey_methodology varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the data collection and analysis methodology used by the source organization to derive the CAP rate benchmarks (e.g., transaction-based, investor survey, broker opinion, hedonic regression). Required for IVSC-compliant methodology metadata and appraiser disclosure in appraisal reports.']
  total_transaction_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'The aggregate dollar value of all transactions included in the benchmark dataset for this segment and period, expressed in the currency indicated by currency_code. Provides context for the depth and liquidity of the market segment being benchmarked.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'The number of actual closed sale transactions underlying the CAP rate benchmark data for this segment and period, where the source provides transaction-based data. Distinct from sample_size which may include survey respondents. Supports statistical confidence assessment.']
  trend_basis_points varchar [note: 'The period-over-period change in the midpoint CAP rate expressed in basis points (bps), where 1 bps = 0.01%. Negative values indicate compression; positive values indicate expansion. Provides a precise quantitative measure of market movement to complement the qualitative trend_direction field.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Directional trend of CAP rates in this market segment relative to the prior survey period. Compressing indicates CAP rates are decreasing (values rising); stable indicates no material change; expanding indicates CAP rates are increasing (values falling). Critical context for investment committee presentations and portfolio NAV/GAV assessments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this benchmark record was most recently modified, including updates to CAP rate values, status changes, or analyst note revisions. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and SOX audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Market CAP rate benchmark record by property type, submarket, and asset class sourced from industry surveys. Stores survey date, property type (office, retail, industrial, multifamily, hotel, data center), submarket/MSA, asset class (Class A/B/C), CAP rate range (low, mid, high), source (CoStar, CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield, NCREIF, PwC/Korpacz), survey methodology, sample size, and trend direction (compressing, stable, expanding). Used to validate appraiser CAP rate selections, support portfolio-level NAV/GAV calculations, and provide market context for investment committee presentations.'
}

Table valuation.nav_calculation {
  nav_calculation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for nav_calculation']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: NAV calculations for real estate funds are subject to external audit engagements (annual fund audits, SEC examinations). Fund managers and auditors link each NAV calculation to the audit engagement th']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: NAV calculation processes for real estate funds are governed by compliance programs (fund valuation policy program, SEC compliance program, AIFMD compliance program). Linking NAV calculations to the g']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: NAV calculation financial outputs (gross_asset_value, net_asset_value, nav_per_unit, ffo, affo, aum) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK is required for multi-']
  financial_period_close_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period_close. Business justification: Fund NAV calculations are produced as part of the period-end close process and must be finalized before the period can be signed off. Real estate fund accountants expect NAV to be tied to the specific']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific real estate fund (e.g., REIT, private equity fund) for which this NAV calculation is prepared. Supports multi-fund reporting and SEC REIT disclosure requirements.']
  insurance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.insurance_program. Business justification: Real estate fund NAV calculations must incorporate total insurance program premiums and TIV as operating cost inputs affecting net asset value. Fund accountants and auditors require the NAV calculatio']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: NAV calculations use weighted_avg_cap_rate and market condition assumptions sourced from market research. Fund administrators and auditors require NAV calculations to reference the market research sup']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: NAV calculations are strategy-specific; core, value-add, and opportunistic funds use different valuation methodologies, discount rates, and benchmark comparisons. Linking to market_segment enables str']
  noi_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: NAV calculations use period NOI as a primary input for cap rate-based property valuation. Linking to the authoritative NOI statement enables fund accountants to trace the NOI figure used in NAV to the']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio or fund for which this NAV/GAV calculation is performed. Links to the portfolio or fund master record in MRI Software or Argus Enterprise.']
  primary_nav_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the analyst, asset manager, or finance professional who prepared this NAV/GAV calculation. Supports audit trail and SOX control requirements.']
  prior_calculation_nav_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding NAV calculation record for this portfolio/fund. Enables calculation chain traceability and period-over-period reconciliation.']
  reference_investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.investment_vehicle. Business justification: NAV calculation methodology, distribution requirements, and reporting standards differ by investment vehicle type (REIT requires 90% distribution, open-end fund uses INREV NAV, CMBS uses DSCR-based va']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: NAV calculations must comply with ASC 820 fair value hierarchy, INREV guidelines, or SEC reporting requirements; the fair_value_hierarchy_level and valuation_methodology fields lack a FK to the govern']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: NAV calculations for real estate funds are governed by specific regulatory obligations (ASC 820 fair value hierarchy, SEC Regulation S-X, AIFMD). Compliance teams must trace which regulatory obligatio']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the senior analyst, asset manager, or controller who reviewed and approved this NAV/GAV calculation. Required for SOX internal control compliance and investor reporting governance.']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: NAV calculations for publicly traded REITs are subject to SOX controls over financial reporting. The NAV calculation process (inputs, methodology, approval chain) is a key control area; SOX testing te']
  affo decimal(20,2) [note: 'Adjusted Funds From Operations (AFFO) for the reporting period, representing FFO adjusted for recurring capital expenditures, straight-line rent adjustments, and other non-cash items. Considered a more accurate proxy for distributable cash flow than FFO.']
  appraisal_coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the portfolio GAV supported by independent third-party appraisals in this calculation cycle (e.g., 75.00 means 75% of GAV is appraiser-supported). Relevant for FASB ASC 820 Level 3 disclosure and investor transparency.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this NAV/GAV calculation was formally approved by the designated reviewer or investment committee. Supports SOX control documentation and audit trail.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the Argus Enterprise cash flow model(s) used as the source for property valuations feeding into this NAV/GAV calculation. Supports audit traceability back to the originating valuation model.']
  aum decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total Assets Under Management (AUM) for the fund or portfolio as of the calculation date, which may differ from GAV by including non-real-estate assets, cash, and receivables. Used in investment advisory fee calculations and investor reporting.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The as-of date for which the NAV and GAV are calculated. This is the principal business event date — the valuation reference point used in investor reports, REIT filings, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.']
  calculation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this NAV/GAV calculation run (e.g., NAV-2024-000123). Used in investor reports, SEC filings, and audit trails to uniquely reference a specific calculation event.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NAV/GAV calculation record. Controls workflow from initial preparation through review, approval, and publication to investors or regulators. \'Superseded\' indicates a revised calculation replaced this one.']
  capex_reserve decimal(20,2) [note: 'Estimated capital expenditure (CAPEX) reserve set aside for future property improvements, TI (Tenant Improvements), and major maintenance across the portfolio. Deducted in AFFO calculation and considered in NAV adjustments.']
  cash_and_equivalents decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total cash and cash equivalent balances held at the portfolio or fund level as of the calculation date. Included in the NAV bridge as a non-real-estate asset component.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NAV calculation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, SOX control documentation, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The discount rate (required rate of return) applied in DCF-based property valuations feeding into this NAV calculation. Reflects risk-adjusted cost of capital for the portfolio. Applicable when valuation_methodology is \'dcf\' or \'blended\'.']
  fair_value_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'ASC 820 / IFRS 13 fair value hierarchy classification for the inputs used in this NAV calculation. Level 1 = quoted market prices; Level 2 = observable inputs; Level 3 = unobservable inputs (most common for real estate). Required for SEC and financial statement disclosures.']
  ffo decimal(20,2) [note: 'Funds From Operations (FFO) for the reporting period, calculated per NAREIT definition as net income plus real estate depreciation and amortization, excluding gains/losses on property sales. Standard REIT performance metric reported to investors and the SEC.']
  gross_asset_value decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total Gross Asset Value (GAV) of the portfolio as of the calculation date, representing the sum of all property market values before deducting debt and liabilities. Core output of the NAV model used in REIT reporting, investor communications, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.']
  is_audited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NAV/GAV calculation has been subject to external audit review (True) or is an unaudited management estimate (False). Relevant for annual REIT financial statement audits and SEC filings.']
  is_published boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NAV calculation has been formally published and communicated to investors or regulators (True) or remains an internal working estimate (False). Controls investor disclosure workflow.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this NAV calculation record. Supports change tracking, SOX audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'Portfolio-level Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio, calculated as total debt outstanding divided by GAV. Key leverage metric monitored by lenders, investors, and rating agencies for CMBS and REIT credit analysis.']
  methodology_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing key assumptions, methodology choices, significant judgments, and any departures from standard methodology applied in this NAV/GAV calculation. Required for FASB ASC 820 Level 3 disclosure and investor transparency.']
  nav_change decimal(20,2) [note: 'Absolute change in NAV from the prior period to the current calculation date (current NAV minus prior NAV). Supports period-over-period performance reporting to investors and the board.']
  nav_per_unit decimal(20,6) [note: 'NAV divided by total units or shares outstanding, representing the per-unit intrinsic value of the fund or REIT. Key metric for investor reporting, unit pricing, and performance benchmarking.']
  net_asset_value decimal(20,2) [note: 'Net Asset Value (NAV) of the portfolio or fund, calculated as GAV minus total debt outstanding minus other liabilities. The primary output metric used in REIT investor reporting, unit pricing, and SEC fair value disclosures.']
  noi_total decimal(20,2) [note: 'Aggregate Net Operating Income (NOI) across all properties included in this NAV calculation, representing total effective gross income minus operating expenses before debt service. Primary input to direct capitalization and DCF valuation methodologies.']
  other_liabilities decimal(20,2) [note: 'Non-debt liabilities attributable to the portfolio (e.g., deferred revenue, security deposits payable, accrued expenses, lease incentive obligations) deducted from GAV in the NAV calculation. Supports completeness of the NAV bridge.']
  prior_nav decimal(20,2) [note: 'NAV from the immediately preceding calculation period, used to compute period-over-period NAV change and growth rate for investor reporting and performance attribution.']
  property_count varchar [note: 'Total count of properties included in this NAV/GAV calculation. Provides a completeness check and supports reconciliation against the portfolio property register in Argus Enterprise or MRI Software.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The date on which this NAV calculation was formally published and made available to investors or filed with regulators. Null if not yet published.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The financial reporting period this NAV calculation corresponds to (e.g., Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Annual, Monthly). Aligns the calculation with the fund\'s financial reporting calendar for investor and SEC reporting.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal or calendar year associated with this NAV calculation (e.g., 2024). Used in conjunction with reporting_period to uniquely identify the reporting cycle.']
  straight_line_rent_adjustment decimal(20,2) [note: 'Non-cash straight-line rent adjustment applied to AFFO, representing the difference between contractual rent escalations recognized on a straight-line basis under ASC 842 and actual cash rent received. Standard REIT AFFO adjustment.']
  third_party_appraiser varchar [note: 'Name of the external appraisal firm or certified appraiser (MAI designation, Appraisal Institute member) engaged to provide independent property valuations used in this NAV calculation. Required when valuation_methodology includes third-party appraisal.']
  total_debt_outstanding decimal(20,2) [note: 'Aggregate outstanding debt balance (mortgages, credit facilities, CMBS, and other borrowings) secured against or attributable to the portfolio as of the calculation date. Deducted from GAV to arrive at NAV.']
  units_outstanding decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total number of fund units or REIT shares outstanding as of the calculation date. Used as the denominator to compute NAV per unit/share for investor reporting and unit pricing.']
  valuation_methodology varchar [note: 'The primary methodology used to determine property market values feeding into the GAV calculation. Options include direct capitalization (applying a CAP rate to NOI), Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), third-party appraisal, a blended approach, or a proprietary NAV model. Aligns with IVSC and FASB ASC 820 fair value hierarchy.']
  weighted_avg_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Portfolio-level weighted average CAP rate (Capitalization Rate) applied across all properties in the NAV calculation, weighted by property value. Used to validate the implied GAV against market benchmarks and support investor reporting.']

  Note: 'Net Asset Value (NAV) and Gross Asset Value (GAV) calculation record at the portfolio or fund level. Stores calculation date, portfolio/fund reference, total GAV (sum of market values), total debt outstanding, NAV (GAV minus debt), NAV per unit/share, AFFO adjustment, FFO, methodology (direct capitalization, DCF, third-party appraisal blend), number of properties included, and calculation preparer. Supports REIT financial reporting (SEC), investor reporting, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.'
}

Table valuation.appraisal_review {
  appraisal_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the appraisal review record. Primary key for the appraisal_review data product in the valuation domain.']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original appraisal report that is the subject of this review. Links to the parent appraisal record in the valuation domain.']
  appraisal_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.appraisal_report. Business justification: An appraisal review is a formal desk or field review of a specific appraisal report deliverable. While appraisal_review already has appraisal_id linking to the engagement, the review is conducted on t']
  appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified appraiser or qualified reviewer who performed the appraisal review. Must hold appropriate credentials per Appraisal Institute and USPAP Standard 3 requirements.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject property being appraised and reviewed. Enables linkage to property master data for portfolio reporting and investment analysis.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Appraisal reviews are commissioned as part of or in response to audit engagements — external auditors request desk or field reviews during financial statement audits. Linking appraisal_review to audit']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Appraisal review programs (lender appraisal review programs, FIRREA compliance programs, OCC appraisal review programs) are compliance programs. Individual appraisal reviews belong to these programs; ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Construction lenders order appraisal reviews at each draw milestone to confirm value supports the outstanding loan balance. Linking appraisal_review to dev_project allows lenders and owners to retriev']
  engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overarching valuation engagement or client assignment under which this appraisal review was ordered. Supports billing, project tracking, and Argus Enterprise model linkage.']
  ordering_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Appraisal review commissioning: the internal employee (chief appraiser, risk officer, asset manager) who orders an appraisal review for quality control or dispute resolution must be tracked. \'requesti']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Appraisal review criteria, comparable adequacy standards, and methodology checks differ by property type. The plain property_type column is denormalized; normalizing to a FK enables property-type-leve']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Appraisal reviews must comply with USPAP Standard 3 and IVSC review standards; the uspap_standard3_compliant and ivsc_methodology_compliant flags lack a FK to the governing framework. Linking to regul']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Appraisal reviews are conducted to satisfy specific regulatory obligations (USPAP Standard 3, FIRREA review requirements, OCC appraisal review guidelines for banks). Compliance teams track which regul']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file name of the Argus Enterprise DCF model associated with the original appraisal being reviewed. Enables direct linkage to the cash flow model for technical review of DCF assumptions, IRR, and NPV outputs.']
  cap_rate_reviewed decimal(8,6) [note: 'The overall capitalization rate (CAP rate) applied in the original appraisal\'s income approach, as reviewed and assessed by the reviewer. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.065 for 6.5%). Reviewer assesses market support for the selected CAP rate against CoStar and market comparables.']
  comparable_adequacy_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the reviewer determined that the comparable sales or lease data used in the original appraisal were adequate in number, recency, and geographic proximity. False triggers a deficiency notation and may require revision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appraisal review record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary values in this review record, including original appraised value and reviewer value opinion. Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting for international real estate investments.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct deficiencies, errors, or omissions identified in the original appraisal report during the review. A count of zero indicates no deficiencies found. Used for quality scoring and vendor performance management.']
  deficiency_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the key deficiencies, errors, or omissions identified in the original appraisal report. Includes methodology issues, comparable selection problems, adjustment errors, and USPAP compliance failures. Supports revision requests and vendor feedback.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the completed appraisal review report was delivered to the client or requesting party. Used for SLA compliance tracking and lender submission deadlines.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'The final administrative outcome of the appraisal review process, indicating whether the original appraisal is accepted for use, rejected, requires revision, or is conditionally accepted pending corrections. Drives lender approval workflows and CMBS securitization eligibility.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'The stated purpose for which the appraisal review is being performed. Drives the applicable standards, required scope of work, and report format. CMBS underwriting and mortgage lending are the most common intended uses requiring USPAP Standard 3 compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mortgage_lending|cmbs_underwriting|internal_qc|portfolio_reporting|litigation|regulatory_compliance|investment_decision — promote to reference product]']
  irr_reviewed decimal(8,6) [note: 'The Internal Rate of Return (IRR) output from the DCF analysis in the original appraisal, as reviewed by the appraiser reviewer. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.085 for 8.5%). Reviewer assesses reasonableness against market investor return expectations.']
  is_rush_order boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the appraisal review was ordered on an expedited or rush basis, typically commanding a premium fee and reduced turnaround SLA. Used for capacity planning and fee schedule application.']
  ivsc_methodology_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the original appraisal and the review process comply with IVSC International Valuation Standards. Relevant for cross-border investment portfolios, institutional investors, and REIT financial reporting under IFRS.']
  market_conditions_adequate boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the reviewer assessed the original appraisal\'s market conditions analysis (supply, demand, absorption, trends) as adequate and current. False indicates the market analysis section requires revision or supplementation.']
  noi_reviewed decimal(18,2) [note: 'The stabilized Net Operating Income (NOI) figure used in the original appraisal\'s income approach, as reviewed by the appraiser reviewer. Reviewer assesses reasonableness of income and expense assumptions against market data. Expressed in currency_code.']
  ordered_date date [note: 'Date on which the appraisal review was formally ordered or commissioned by the client, lender, or internal department. Used for SLA tracking and turnaround time measurement.']
