  original_appraisal_date date [note: 'The effective date of the original appraisal report being reviewed. Critical for assessing whether the appraisal reflects current market conditions and for CMBS underwriting compliance.']
  original_appraisal_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the appraisal firm or company that produced the original appraisal report being reviewed. Used for vendor performance tracking and CMBS lender panel management.']
  original_appraised_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The market value opinion stated in the original appraisal report being reviewed. Expressed in the currency indicated by currency_code. Used as the baseline for reviewer agreement or disagreement analysis.']
  original_appraiser_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the appraiser who prepared the original appraisal report under review. Retained as a denormalized field for audit trail and USPAP compliance documentation, as the original appraiser may not be in the internal system.']
  requester_reference varchar [note: 'The client\'s or lender\'s own reference number or loan number associated with this appraisal review order. Enables cross-referencing with the requesting party\'s loan origination or CMBS deal management systems.']
  review_conclusion varchar [note: 'The reviewer\'s overall conclusion regarding the original appraisal report. \'Agree\' indicates the reviewer concurs with the original value and methodology; \'Disagree\' indicates material differences; \'Conditional\' indicates agreement subject to specific corrections or conditions being met.']
  review_date date [note: 'The effective date on which the appraisal review was performed and the reviewer\'s opinion was formed. This is the principal business event date, distinct from the order date or report delivery date.']
  review_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'The fee charged or paid for the appraisal review engagement, expressed in currency_code. Used for accounts receivable, vendor invoice reconciliation, and engagement profitability analysis.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the appraisal review engagement, used for cross-referencing with lenders, CMBS underwriters, and internal quality control systems. Typically assigned by the review firm or internal QC department.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the appraisal review workflow, from initial order through completion or cancellation. Drives workflow routing in quality control and lender compliance processes.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appraisal review methodology performed. Desk review involves analysis of the written report only; field review includes a property inspection; technical review focuses on methodology and calculations; administrative review checks completeness and compliance.']
  reviewer_designation varchar [note: 'Professional designation held by the reviewer as awarded by the Appraisal Institute. MAI (Member Appraisal Institute) and AI-GRS (General Review Specialist) are most common for commercial appraisal reviews. Validates reviewer qualifications for CMBS and institutional lender requirements.']
  reviewer_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued appraiser license or certification number of the reviewer. Required for USPAP compliance documentation and lender panel eligibility verification. Must be a Certified General Appraiser license for commercial property reviews.']
  reviewer_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the jurisdiction in which the reviewer holds their appraiser license or certification. Validates geographic competency for the subject property location per USPAP competency rule.']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the reviewer\'s additional observations, qualifications, assumptions, or limiting conditions not captured in structured fields. Supplements the formal review report narrative.']
  reviewer_value_opinion decimal(18,2) [note: 'The reviewer\'s independent market value opinion for the subject property, applicable when a field review is performed and the reviewer develops their own value conclusion. Null for desk reviews where no independent value is developed. Required for CMBS underwriting when the reviewer disagrees with the original appraised value.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope of work performed in the appraisal review, including what was reviewed, the extent of the review, and any limitations. Required by USPAP Standard 3 to be disclosed in the review report.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appraisal review record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  uspap_standard3_compliant boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the appraisal review was conducted in full compliance with USPAP Standard 3 (Appraisal Review). Required for lender compliance, CMBS underwriting, and internal quality control certification. True indicates full compliance; False triggers mandatory remediation workflow.']
  valuation_approach varchar [note: 'The primary valuation approach(es) employed in the original appraisal that were subject to review. Income approach (including DCF) is standard for commercial properties; sales comparison for residential. Reviewer must assess the appropriateness of the approach selected.']
  value_variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage difference between the reviewer\'s value opinion and the original appraised value, calculated as (reviewer_value_opinion - original_appraised_value) / original_appraised_value. Null when no reviewer value opinion is developed. Used to flag material discrepancies requiring escalation per lender guidelines.']

  Note: 'Formal appraisal review record capturing the desk review or field review of an appraisal report. Stores review type (desk, field, technical), reviewer credentials, review date, original appraisal reference, review conclusions (agree, disagree, conditional), value opinion of reviewer (if field review), deficiencies identified, USPAP Standard 3 compliance flag, and final disposition (accepted, rejected, revision required). Required for lender compliance, CMBS underwriting, and internal quality control.'
}

Table valuation.value_history {
  value_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for value_history']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.appraisal. Business justification: value_history tracks all concluded values for a property over time. When valuation_source = \'APPRAISAL\', the record should reference the specific appraisal engagement that produced the concluded value']
  appraisal_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.appraisal_report. Business justification: value_history tracks concluded values and the appraisal_report is the formal deliverable containing the concluded market value. Adding appraisal_report_id (nullable) links the historical value record ']
  appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified appraiser, broker, or internal analyst who concluded the valuation. Supports credential tracking per Appraisal Institute requirements.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset being valued. Links this valuation record to the canonical property master record.']
  bpo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.bpo. Business justification: value_history tracks all concluded values including BPO-derived estimates. When valuation_source = \'BPO\', the record should reference the specific BPO engagement. Adding bpo_id (nullable) enables dire']
  cma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cma. Business justification: value_history tracks all concluded values including CMA-derived estimates. When valuation_source = \'CMA\', the record should reference the specific CMA. Adding cma_id (nullable) enables direct traceabi']
  cost_approach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cost_approach. Business justification: value_history tracks concluded values from cost approach models. When valuation_method = \'COST_APPROACH\', the record should reference the specific cost_approach record with its RCN, depreciation, and ']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Value history financial figures (concluded_value, value_range_low/high, gav, nav_contribution) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK enables currency-adjusted hi']
  dcf_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.valuation_dcf_model. Business justification: value_history tracks concluded values derived from DCF models. When valuation_source = \'DCF\' or valuation_method = \'DISCOUNTED_CASH_FLOW\', the record should reference the specific DCF model (with its ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Value history tracks the progression of asset valuations through the development lifecycle — as-is, as-complete, and as-stabilized values are recorded at each phase milestone. Linking value_history to']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal reviewer or review appraiser who approved or reviewed this valuation record. Supports the SOX internal control requirement for segregation of duties in financial reporting valuations.']
  engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent valuation engagement or assignment under which this concluded value was produced. Enables grouping of multiple value conclusions from a single engagement.']
  financial_period_close_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period_close. Business justification: Valuation snapshots in value_history are recorded at period-end for ASC 820 fair value reporting. The existing `financial_period_id(unlinked)` does not match the PK `financial_period_close_id`. Linkin']
  financial_period_financial_period_close_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial reporting period (quarter or year-end) with which this valuation is associated for REIT portfolio reporting, NAV reconciliation, and ASC 820 fair value disclosures.']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Value history trend analysis requires geographic context for submarket-level performance reporting and NCREIF/MSCI benchmark comparison. Linking to geographic_hierarchy enables submarket-level valuati']
  income_approach_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.valuation_approach. Business justification: value_history records concluded values from specific valuation approach models (income capitalization, sales comparison, cost). Adding income_approach_id (nullable, using the PK of valuation_approach)']
  noi_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.noi_statement. Business justification: Historical valuation records capture NOI at the time of valuation. Linking to the NOI statement used enables auditors and analysts to verify the NOI figure in each historical valuation snapshot, suppo']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Value history trend analysis and benchmark comparison require property type context; NCREIF and MSCI performance indices are segmented by property type. A domain expert would expect historical valuati']
  valuation_nav_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.nav_calculation. Business justification: value_history has a nav_contribution field indicating this record contributes to a NAV calculation. Adding valuation_nav_calculation_id (nullable) links the property-level value history record to the ']
  valuation_tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: value_history tracks all concluded values for a property including tax-assessed values. When valuation_source = \'TAX_ASSESSMENT\', the record should reference the specific tax_assessment record. Adding']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'File name or unique identifier of the Argus Enterprise DCF model file used to produce this valuation. Enables direct traceability from the concluded value back to the source cash flow model for audit and reproducibility purposes.']
  argus_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Argus Enterprise model file (e.g., v3.2, 2024-Q4) used to produce this valuation. Supports model governance, change tracking, and audit trail requirements.']
  asc820_level varchar [note: 'FASB ASC 820 fair value hierarchy classification for this valuation. Level 1 = quoted prices in active markets; Level 2 = observable inputs other than Level 1; Level 3 = unobservable inputs (most real estate falls here). Mandatory for REIT financial statement disclosures and SEC reporting.']
  cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The capitalization rate applied in the income capitalization approach, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.065 = 6.5%). Derived from market evidence and used to convert NOI into an indicated value. Key input for investment analysis and portfolio benchmarking.']
  concluded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final concluded value amount for the property as determined by the appraiser or analyst. Represents the point estimate of value (market value, assessed value, etc.) in the stated currency. Core monetary fact for NAV reconciliation and portfolio reporting.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative confidence level assigned to the concluded value by the appraiser or internal reviewer. High = strong market evidence and data quality; Medium = adequate evidence with some uncertainty; Low = limited market data or significant assumptions. Used for risk weighting in portfolio analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valuation history record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage.']
  discount_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The discount rate applied in the DCF valuation model, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.075 = 7.5%). Reflects the required rate of return for the investment, incorporating risk, market conditions, and asset-specific factors. Stored for model reproducibility and audit.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this valuation is considered stale and should no longer be used for financial reporting or investment decisions without a refresh. Typically 12 months from the valuation effective date for REIT reporting purposes.']
  gav decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross Asset Value of the property as determined in this valuation, representing the total undiluted market value before deducting liabilities. Used in REIT portfolio reporting, NAV calculations, and investor disclosures.']
  gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross Leasable Area of the property in square feet as measured at the time of this valuation. Used as the denominator for PSF value calculations and for tracking changes in measurable area over time.']
  hold_period_years varchar [note: 'The assumed investment hold period in years used in the DCF valuation model. Typically 5 or 10 years for commercial real estate. Stored as a key Argus Enterprise model assumption for reproducibility.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'The stated intended use of this valuation as declared in the engagement letter. Drives the applicable standard of value and methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_reporting|loan_collateral|acquisition|disposition|portfolio_review|insurance|tax_appeal|litigation — promote to reference product]']
  irr decimal(8,6) [note: 'Internal Rate of Return output from the DCF valuation model, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.085 = 8.5%). Represents the discount rate at which the NPV of projected cash flows equals zero. Key metric for investment decision-making and portfolio performance reporting.']
  market_conditions_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing prevailing market conditions at the valuation effective date, including supply/demand dynamics, comparable transaction activity, and macroeconomic factors. Required USPAP disclosure and supports qualitative analysis.']
  nav_contribution decimal(18,2) [note: 'This property\'s contribution to the fund or portfolio Net Asset Value as calculated in this valuation period, after deducting allocated debt and liabilities. Used for investor NAV reconciliation and REIT unit pricing.']
  noi decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Operating Income used as the primary income input in the income capitalization or DCF valuation approach. Represents effective gross income less operating expenses, excluding debt service and capital expenditures. Sourced from Argus Enterprise or Yardi Voyager operating statements.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net Present Value output from the DCF valuation model in the stated currency. Represents the present value of projected future cash flows discounted at the required rate of return. Supports investment advisory and portfolio management decisions.']
  occupancy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Physical occupancy rate of the property at the valuation effective date, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50%). Key income approach input affecting NOI and concluded value. Sourced from Yardi Voyager or MRI Software lease data.']
  prior_concluded_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The concluded value from the immediately preceding valuation for this property. Stored to enable direct period-over-period value change calculation for investor reporting and NAV reconciliation without requiring a self-join.']
  prior_valuation_date date [note: 'The effective date of the immediately preceding valuation for this property. Stored to facilitate period-over-period value change analysis and trend reporting without requiring a self-join.']
  property_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the property as assessed at the time of the valuation inspection. Influences the concluded value and is a required disclosure in USPAP-compliant appraisal reports.']
  report_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the completed valuation report was delivered to the client or internal stakeholder. Distinct from the valuation effective date. Used for SLA tracking and engagement management.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the internal reviewer completed their review and approved or rejected this valuation record. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail for financial reporting valuations.']
  reviewer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the internal reviewer documenting their findings, adjustments requested, or basis for approval. Supports the audit trail and quality control process.']
  terminal_cap_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The reversion or terminal capitalization rate applied at the end of the DCF hold period to estimate the residual/terminal value of the property. Expressed as a decimal. Key Argus Enterprise DCF assumption stored for model audit and sensitivity analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valuation history record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the last change for audit trail, data lineage, and incremental Silver Layer processing.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The principal real-world date as of which the concluded value is stated. This is the appraisal effective date, not the report delivery date. Critical for time-series trend analysis and ASC 820 Level 1/2/3 hierarchy disclosures.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Primary methodology applied to derive the concluded value. Sales Comparison = comparable sales analysis; Income Capitalization = direct cap rate applied to NOI; DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) = multi-period cash flow model (Argus Enterprise); Cost Approach = land plus depreciated replacement cost; AVM = automated model; Hybrid = combination of methods. Required for IVSC methodology metadata and ASC 820 Level classification.']
  valuation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this valuation record, formatted as VAL-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in investor reports, audit trails, and cross-system reconciliation with Argus Enterprise and Yardi Voyager.']
  valuation_source varchar [note: 'Source discriminator identifying how the concluded value was derived. Appraisal = certified third-party appraisal; BPO (Broker Price Opinion) = broker-prepared opinion; CMA (Comparative Market Analysis) = agent-prepared analysis; desk_review = internal analyst review; tax_assessment = government assessed value; AVM (Automated Valuation Model) = algorithmic estimate. Drives audit trail and confidence weighting.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this valuation record. Draft = in preparation; under_review = submitted for internal or external review; approved = accepted for financial reporting use; superseded = replaced by a newer valuation; withdrawn = retracted by the appraiser or analyst. Controls which record is the current authoritative value for a property.']
  value_change_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage change in concluded value from the prior valuation to the current valuation, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 = 3.50% increase). Calculated as (concluded_value - prior_concluded_value) / prior_concluded_value. Stored for reporting efficiency and trend analysis.']
  value_psf decimal(12,2) [note: 'Concluded value expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis, calculated as concluded_value divided by the property\'s gross leasable area. Enables cross-property benchmarking and market comparability analysis.']
  value_range_high decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the concluded value range when the appraiser or analyst expresses value as a range rather than a single point estimate. Used in BPO and CMA contexts and for sensitivity analysis.']
  value_range_low decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the concluded value range when the appraiser or analyst expresses value as a range rather than a single point estimate. Used in BPO and CMA contexts and for sensitivity analysis.']
  value_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the basis of value being reported. Distinguishes market value (IVSC IVS 104), assessed value (tax authority), insurance value (replacement cost), book value (GAAP carrying amount), investment value (investor-specific), and liquidation value. Drives how the concluded value is used in financial reporting and investment analysis.']

  Note: 'Historical valuation record tracking all concluded values for a property over time. Stores property reference, valuation date, concluded value, value type (market value, assessed value, insurance value, book value, investment value), valuation method used, source discriminator (appraisal, BPO, CMA, internal desk review, tax assessment, automated valuation model), appraiser/analyst reference, confidence level, and financial reporting period association. Enables trend analysis, supports ASC 820 fair value hierarchy disclosures (Level 1/2/3), and provides the audit trail for REIT portfolio reporting and investor NAV reconciliation.'
}

Table valuation.tax_assessment {
  tax_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_assessment']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assessed property in the property master data product. Links the tax assessment to the specific real estate asset.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Tax assessments separately value land and improvements (assessed_improvement_value). The improvement value is building-specific. Assessors issue building-level improvement assessments. Linking to buil']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Tax assessment financial figures (assessed_land_value, total_assessed_value, gross_annual_tax, net_annual_tax) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK supports mul']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal asset manager (AM) or property manager (PM) responsible for reviewing and managing this tax assessment. Links to the workforce/employee master for accountability and workflow routing.']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Tax assessments are jurisdiction-specific; the taxing_jurisdiction_name and jurisdiction_code plain columns are denormalized geographic hierarchy fields. Normalizing to a FK enables jurisdiction-level']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Tax assessments are issued by specific jurisdictions; appeal deadlines, filing requirements, and penalty authority are all jurisdiction-governed. taxing_jurisdiction_name and jurisdiction_code are den']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Tax assessments are issued by jurisdiction against a specific parcel (APN). tax_assessment.parcel_number is denormalized from parcel. The parcel record is the authoritative source for APN, tax_distric']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Tax assessments are governed by state assessment laws and IAAO standards that define assessment ratios, appeal rights, and methodology requirements. Linking to regulatory_framework enables jurisdictio']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Property tax assessments are governed by specific regulatory obligations (state assessment statutes, appeal procedures, disclosure requirements). Compliance teams track which regulatory obligation gov']
  abatement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of any tax abatement granted by the taxing authority (e.g., development incentive abatement, payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) arrangement). Distinct from exemptions; abatements are typically time-limited incentives tied to development agreements.']
  abatement_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which any tax abatement arrangement expires, after which the full tax liability applies. Critical for long-term cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise and investment underwriting — abatement burn-off materially impacts NOI (Net Operating Income) projections.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which a formal appeal of the assessed value must be filed with the taxing authority. Critical compliance date tracked in Yardi Voyager and DocuSign CLM for deadline management.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal appeal was submitted to the taxing authority or appeals board. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used for tracking appeal timelines and legal deadlines.']
  appeal_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the taxing authority or appeals board issued a final decision on the appeal. Null if appeal is still pending. Triggers revised tax liability calculations.']
  appeal_settled_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final assessed value agreed upon or ordered as a result of the appeal process. Null if appeal is pending or not filed. Replaces the original assessed value for tax liability calculation upon resolution.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any formal appeal filed against this assessment. Tracks the appeal lifecycle from filing through final determination. Informs contingent liability disclosures under FASB ASC 450. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_appealed|appeal_filed|hearing_scheduled|decision_pending|appeal_granted|appeal_denied — promote to reference product]']
  appeal_target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assessed value the property owner is seeking through the appeal process. Represents the owner\'s position on fair assessed value, typically supported by an independent appraisal or BPO (Broker Price Opinion). Used to quantify potential tax savings from a successful appeal.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model in which this tax assessment\'s OPEX (Operating Expenditure) is incorporated. Enables traceability between the assessed tax expense and the DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuation model.']
  assessed_improvement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the building and structural improvements on the property, exclusive of land. Drives depreciation analysis and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) planning in Argus Enterprise models.']
  assessed_land_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value assigned by the taxing authority to the land component of the property, exclusive of improvements. Used in land vs. improvement allocation analysis and depreciation studies. Sourced from Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and Argus Enterprise.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned unique identifier issued by the taxing authority for this assessment record. Used for correspondence, appeals, and cross-referencing with the assessor\'s office. Sourced from Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets module.']
  assessment_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'The ratio of assessed value to estimated market value as applied by the taxing jurisdiction (e.g., 0.80 = 80% of market value). Varies by jurisdiction and property class. Used to back-calculate implied market value from assessed value for investment analysis.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tax assessment record. Tracks progression from preliminary notice through certification, appeal, and final determination. Drives workflow in Yardi Voyager property accounting.']
  assessment_year varchar [note: 'The tax year (calendar year) for which this assessment applies. Drives annual property tax expense calculations and year-over-year valuation trend analysis. Critical for NOI (Net Operating Income) OPEX reporting.']
  assessor_contact varchar [note: 'Phone number or contact detail for the assessor\'s office. Used for inquiry and appeal coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government assessor or assessment office responsible for this assessment. Used for correspondence, appeal filings, and audit documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax assessment record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial controls.']
  effective_tax_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective property tax rate expressed as a decimal percentage of the total assessed value (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%). Accounts for all applicable levies across overlapping jurisdictions. Used in NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling and CAP Rate (Capitalization Rate) analysis in Argus Enterprise.']
  exemption_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total dollar value of tax exemptions applied to this assessment (e.g., homestead exemption, REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) exemption, historic preservation, enterprise zone). Reduces the taxable assessed value or gross tax liability.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary tax exemption applied to this assessment. Determines eligibility criteria and renewal requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homestead|senior|veteran|nonprofit|historic|enterprise_zone|reit — promote to reference product]']
  gross_annual_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross annual property tax liability before application of any exemptions, abatements, or credits. Computed from assessed value and millage rate. Key OPEX (Operating Expenditure) component in NOI (Net Operating Income) calculations and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.']
  implied_market_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated market value implied by the taxing authority\'s assessment, calculated as total assessed value divided by the assessment ratio. Provides a reference point for comparing assessed value against independent appraisals and Argus Enterprise DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) valuations.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Number of installment payments into which the annual tax liability is divided (e.g., 1 = lump sum, 2 = semi-annual, 4 = quarterly). Determines AP (Accounts Payable) payment scheduling and cash flow timing in Yardi Voyager.']
  is_nnn_tenant_recoverable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property tax expense is recoverable from tenants under NNN (Triple Net Lease) or modified gross lease structures. True = tax is passed through to tenants as a CAM (Common Area Maintenance) or operating expense recovery; False = landlord bears the tax expense. Critical for EGI (Effective Gross Income) and NOI (Net Operating Income) modeling.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the taxing authority by governmental level. Properties may have multiple overlapping jurisdictions; this field identifies the type for each assessment record.']
  millage_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The tax rate expressed in mills (dollars of tax per $1,000 of assessed value) levied by the taxing jurisdiction. Multiplied by the assessed value to compute the gross annual tax liability before exemptions.']
  net_annual_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net annual property tax liability after deducting all exemptions, abatements, and credits from the gross tax. This is the actual cash tax expense recorded in Yardi Voyager GL (General Ledger) and reported as a property OPEX (Operating Expenditure) line item in NOI (Net Operating Income) statements.']
  notice_date date [note: 'Date the taxing authority issued the official assessment notice to the property owner. Establishes the start of the appeal window and triggers internal review processes.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the property tax payment must be remitted to the taxing authority to avoid penalties and interest. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) scheduling in Yardi Voyager and cash flow planning.']
  prior_year_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assessed value from the immediately preceding tax year. Enables year-over-year change analysis and supports appeal arguments based on disproportionate value increases. Used in Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling.']
  property_class_code varchar [note: 'Assessor\'s classification code for the property type (e.g., residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural). Determines applicable assessment methodology, exemption eligibility, and tax rate schedule.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the internal reviewer documenting assessment analysis, appeal rationale, comparable evidence, or instructions for the AP (Accounts Payable) team. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act).']
  tax_bill_number varchar [note: 'Official tax bill number issued by the taxing authority for the annual tax payment. Used for AP (Accounts Payable) invoice matching in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA to ensure accurate tax remittance.']
  tax_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Annual net property tax liability expressed on a per-square-foot (PSF) basis. Enables benchmarking across properties of different sizes and is a standard metric in NNN (Triple Net Lease) lease analysis and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliations.']
  total_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assessed value of the property as determined by the taxing authority, representing the sum of assessed land value and assessed improvement value. This is the ad valorem tax base and is typically distinct from market value. Key input to NOI (Net Operating Income) OPEX calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax assessment record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks revisions from appeal outcomes, value corrections, or payment status updates. Required for SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audit trail.']
  yardi_property_code varchar [note: 'Property code as assigned in Yardi Voyager, the system of record for property accounting and tax expense tracking. Enables direct reconciliation between this Silver Layer record and the source system\'s tax ledger entries.']

  Note: 'Property tax assessment record capturing the assessed value assigned by the taxing authority for ad valorem tax purposes. Stores assessment year, assessed land value, assessed improvement value, total assessed value, assessment ratio, effective tax rate, annual tax liability, taxing jurisdiction, assessment notice date, appeal deadline, and appeal status. Distinct from market value appraisals — assessed value often differs materially from market value and drives property tax expense (a key OPEX component in NOI calculations).'
}

Table valuation.tax_appeal {
  tax_appeal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the property tax appeal record. Primary key for the tax_appeal data product.']
  appraiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified appraiser or valuation professional who provided the opinion of value supporting the appeal. Links to the appraiser master record.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property against which the tax appeal is filed. Links to the property master record.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Property tax management programs (annual tax appeal programs) are compliance programs in real estate organizations. Individual tax appeals belong to the governing compliance program; linking enables p']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Tax appeal financial figures (original_assessed_value, appealed_value, estimated_tax_savings, legal_fees_incurred) are currency-denominated. Normalizing the plain currency_code column to a FK supports']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Tax appeals filed during active development use the project\'s cost basis and projected stabilized value as grounds for appeal. Linking tax_appeal to dev_project allows the tax team to associate appeal']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax appeal coordination: the internal employee (property manager, asset manager, tax analyst) who manages the tax appeal process — coordinating legal counsel, tracking deadlines, and reporting savings']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Tax appeals are filed in specific jurisdictions; the jurisdiction_code plain column is a denormalized geographic hierarchy reference. Normalizing to a FK enables jurisdiction-level appeal success rate']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Tax appeals are filed with specific jurisdictions; the appeal process, statutory deadlines, and regulatory requirements are jurisdiction-governed. jurisdiction_code on tax_appeal is a denormalized rep']
  maintenance_capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.capex_project. Business justification: During tax appeal proceedings, attorneys and appraisers reference specific capex improvements to argue assessed value. Capital improvements that added measurable value are key evidence in tax appeal h']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: Tax appeal attorneys and appraisers cite market research to support value opinions in assessment hearings. Market research on cap rates, vacancy, and comparable transactions is submitted as evidence i']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Tax appeals are filed against a specific parcel\'s assessment with the taxing authority. tax_appeal.parcel_number is denormalized from parcel. The parcel record provides the authoritative APN, jurisdic']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Tax appeals are governed by state tax codes and administrative procedures that define filing deadlines, hearing processes, and settlement rules. Linking to regulatory_framework enables jurisdiction-sp']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Tax appeals are filed under specific regulatory frameworks (state tax codes, administrative appeal procedures). Compliance teams track which regulatory obligation governs the appeal process, statutory']
  valuation_tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: A tax appeal is a formal challenge to a specific tax assessment. tax_appeal has asset_id and tax_year but no direct FK to the tax_assessment record being challenged. Adding valuation_tax_assessment_id']
  value_history_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.value_history. Business justification: A tax appeal\'s opinion of value is often supported by a formal valuation record in value_history. Adding valuation_history_id (nullable) to tax_appeal links the appeal to the specific historical valua']
  actual_tax_savings decimal(18,2) [note: 'The realized annual property tax reduction achieved upon settlement or adjudication, calculated from the difference between the original assessed value and the settled value at the applicable tax rate. Key metric for OPEX management and NOI reporting.']
  appeal_grounds varchar [note: 'The legal or factual basis on which the appeal is filed. Common grounds include overassessment (value exceeds market value), unequal assessment (assessed at higher ratio than comparable properties), exemption eligibility, improper property classification, or clerical error. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overassessment|unequal_assessment|exemption|improper_classification|clerical_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  appeal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional narrative, strategic commentary, or case-specific details relevant to the tax appeal. May include assessor communications, negotiation notes, or special circumstances affecting the appeal strategy.']
  appeal_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned or internally generated reference number for the tax appeal, used for correspondence with the taxing authority or assessment appeal board. Serves as the business identifier for tracking and reporting.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'The final outcome of the resolved appeal: value reduced (appellant prevailed), value upheld (taxing authority prevailed), partially reduced (compromise), withdrawn (appellant withdrew), or dismissed (procedural dismissal). Populated upon closure.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tax appeal, tracking progression from initial filing through resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|filed|pending_hearing|settled|adjudicated|withdrawn|closed — promote to reference product]']
  appeal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the appeal by procedural level: informal (administrative review), formal (board of equalization or assessment appeal board), or judicial (court proceeding). Determines the legal process and cost implications.']
  appealed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The property value asserted by the appellant in the appeal filing, representing the owner\'s or advisor\'s opinion of the correct assessed value. Typically supported by an independent appraisal or BPO.']
  appraisal_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee paid to the appraiser or valuation professional for preparing the independent opinion of value submitted as evidence in the appeal. Included in total appeal cost for net savings analysis.']
  argus_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the Argus Enterprise DCF or cash flow model used to support the valuation opinion submitted in the appeal. Enables traceability between the appeal record and the underlying valuation model.']
  assessment_ratio decimal(10,6) [note: 'The ratio of the assessed value to the estimated market value as applied by the taxing authority (e.g., 0.80 = 80% of market value). Used in unequal assessment appeals to demonstrate inequitable treatment relative to comparable properties.']
  cap_rate_used decimal(10,6) [note: 'The Capitalization Rate (CAP rate) applied in the income capitalization approach to derive the opinion of value submitted in the appeal. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.065 = 6.5%). Sourced from market evidence and appraiser analysis.']
  comparable_sales_count varchar [note: 'Number of comparable property sales used as evidence to support the opinion of value in the appeal. Indicates the depth of market evidence presented to the assessment authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the tax appeal record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'The statutory deadline by which the appeal must be filed with the taxing authority. Critical for compliance tracking to avoid forfeiture of appeal rights due to missed deadlines.']
  estimated_tax_savings decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected annual property tax reduction if the appeal is successful, calculated based on the difference between the original assessed value and the appealed value multiplied by the applicable tax rate. Supports OPEX forecasting and NOI optimization analysis.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'The type of property tax exemption being claimed as grounds for the appeal, if applicable (e.g., nonprofit, government, historic preservation, agricultural, REIT-related). Null if the appeal is not exemption-based. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nonprofit|government|historic_preservation|agricultural|reit|low_income_housing|other — promote to reference product]']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal tax appeal was filed with the taxing authority or assessment appeal board. This is the principal business event date for the appeal lifecycle.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual date of the formal hearing before the assessment appeal board, board of equalization, or court. Null if the appeal is resolved prior to hearing through informal settlement.']
  is_multi_year_appeal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appeal covers multiple tax years simultaneously (True) or a single tax year (False). Multi-year appeals have greater financial impact and require additional disclosure under FASB ASC 450.']
  legal_counsel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the law firm or attorney representing the property owner in the tax appeal proceeding. Required for judicial appeals and formal hearings. Supports legal cost tracking and matter management.']
  legal_counsel_reference varchar [note: 'The matter number, case reference, or engagement code assigned by the legal counsel or law firm managing the tax appeal. Used for cross-referencing with the legal matter management system.']
  legal_fees_incurred decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total legal fees and professional costs incurred in prosecuting the tax appeal, including attorney fees, expert witness fees, and filing costs. Used for net tax savings calculation and OPEX reporting.']
  noi_used decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Net Operating Income (NOI) figure submitted as evidence in income-capitalization-based appeals. Represents the property\'s stabilized annual income less operating expenses, used to derive value via the CAP rate approach.']
  opinion_of_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The independent market value opinion submitted as evidence in support of the appeal, typically derived from a formal appraisal, BPO, or CMA. May differ from the appealed value if a range or alternative methodology is used.']
  original_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assessed value of the property as determined by the taxing authority prior to the appeal. This is the baseline value being challenged and is used to calculate potential tax savings.']
  property_class varchar [note: 'The property classification as recognized by the taxing authority for assessment purposes. Determines the applicable assessment ratio, tax rate, and exemption eligibility. May differ from the internal property type classification.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of property tax refund or credit due to the property owner as a result of a successful appeal, covering overpaid taxes for the appealed tax year(s). Impacts AR and cash flow reporting.']
  refund_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the tax refund or credit was received from the taxing authority following a successful appeal. Used for cash flow reconciliation and AR tracking.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the appeal was fully resolved, whether by settlement, adjudication, withdrawal, or administrative closure. Marks the end of the appeal lifecycle.']
  settled_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final assessed value agreed upon through settlement negotiations or determined by adjudication. Populated upon resolution of the appeal. Used to calculate actual tax savings achieved.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which a negotiated settlement was reached between the appellant and the taxing authority, resolving the appeal without full adjudication. Null for appeals resolved by formal adjudication or still pending.']
  tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The applicable property tax mill rate or effective tax rate (expressed as a decimal) used to convert assessed value to annual tax liability. Required for tax savings calculations and OPEX budgeting.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal or calendar tax year for which the assessed value is being appealed. Critical for matching the appeal to the correct assessment roll and tax bill.']
  taxing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental body or assessment authority against which the appeal is filed (e.g., county assessor\'s office, municipal tax board). Identifies the jurisdiction responsible for the assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the tax appeal record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  valuation_methodology varchar [note: 'The primary valuation approach used to support the opinion of value submitted in the appeal. Common approaches include sales comparison, income capitalization (using CAP rate and NOI), cost approach, DCF (Discounted Cash Flow), BPO (Broker Price Opinion), or CMA (Comparative Market Analysis).']
  yardi_property_code varchar [note: 'The property code as assigned in Yardi Voyager, the system of record for property management and financial reporting. Used to reconcile tax appeal records with property-level OPEX and GL entries in Yardi.']

  Note: 'Property tax appeal record tracking formal challenges to assessed values filed with taxing authorities or assessment appeal boards. Stores appeal filing date, appeal type (informal, formal, judicial), grounds for appeal (overassessment, unequal assessment, exemption), opinion of value submitted, hearing date, settlement date, original assessed value, appealed value, settled/adjudicated value, tax savings achieved, legal counsel reference, and appeal status. Supports OPEX management and NOI optimization.'
}

Table valuation.nav_appraisal_input {
  nav_appraisal_input_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each NAV appraisal input record.']
  appraisal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the appraisal report used as an input to the NAV calculation.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the analyst or asset manager who linked this appraisal to the NAV calculation.']
  valuation_nav_calculation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the NAV calculation that consumed this appraisal as an input.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation for any value adjustment applied, supporting audit trail and investor reporting transparency.']
  appraisal_weight_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage weight assigned to this appraisal in the portfolio-level GAV aggregation. Used when multiple appraisals for the same property or when blending appraisal and internal valuations.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NAV appraisal input record was created.']
  effective_date_used date [note: 'The value date (effective date) of the appraisal that was used in this NAV calculation. Typically matches appraisal.value_date but captured here to support audit trail and temporal alignment.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Status of this appraisal input in the NAV calculation workflow. Included = fully incorporated, Excluded = not used, Partial = used with adjustments, Under Review = pending valuation committee approval.']
  is_most_recent_appraisal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this appraisal was the most recent available appraisal for the subject property at the time of the NAV calculation. Supports ASC 820 Level 2/3 input recency requirements.']
  value_adjustment_applied decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment (positive or negative) applied to the appraisal\'s concluded value before inclusion in the NAV. Common adjustments include time decay, market condition changes, or reconciliation to internal models.']
  value_used_in_nav decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific concluded value from the appraisal report that was incorporated into the NAV calculation. May differ from the appraisal\'s concluded_market_value if adjustments or a value range was used.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion of a specific appraisal report in a Net Asset Value (NAV) calculation. It captures which appraisals were used as valuation inputs for portfolio-level NAV calculations, the specific value drawn from each appraisal, the weight assigned to that appraisal in the aggregation, and whether adjustments were applied. Each record links one NAV calculation to one appraisal with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, supporting REIT financial reporting, investor transparency, and ASC 820 fair value hierarchy disclosures.. Existence Justification: In real estate investment management, NAV calculations are portfolio-level aggregations that draw on multiple individual property appraisals as valuation inputs. One NAV calculation incorporates many appraisals (across multiple properties in the portfolio), and one appraisal can be used in multiple NAV calculations over time (e.g., quarterly NAV runs for the same fund, or NAV calculations for multiple funds that own the same property). The business actively manages this relationship through valuation committees that select which appraisals to include, assign weights, and apply adjustments.'
}

  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the development or capital improvement project (e.g., \'Riverside Tower Ground-Up\', \'500 Main St Lobby Renovation\'). Used in all stakeholder communications, board reporting, and portfolio dashboards.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric project code assigned by the development team or project management office (PMO). Used on contracts, RFIs, submittals, permits, and all project correspondence. Serves as the business-facing identifier across Procore, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, and internal reporting.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the development project from pipeline identification through final closeout and handoff to asset management. Drives milestone tracking, CAPEX draw schedule activation, and portfolio reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pipeline|entitlement|design|construction|closeout|complete|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  soft_cost_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total project budget allocated to soft costs — indirect project costs including architecture and engineering fees, legal and entitlement costs, permitting fees, environmental studies, financing costs, and project management fees. Tracked separately for CAPEX classification and tax treatment.']
  target_gla_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Gross Leasable Area (GLA) for the completed development, measured in square feet (SQF). Represents the total floor area designed for tenant occupancy and lease revenue generation. Used in investment underwriting, Argus Enterprise cash flow modeling, and portfolio reporting.']
  target_nla_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Net Leasable Area (NLA) for the completed development, measured in square feet (SQF). Represents usable tenant space after deducting common areas, mechanical rooms, and circulation. Used for PSF (Per Square Foot) rent underwriting and lease absorption modeling.']
  total_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved project budget in US dollars (USD), encompassing all hard costs (construction), soft costs (design, legal, permitting), land costs, financing costs, and contingency reserves. The primary CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) authorization amount. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and Procore budget module.']
  total_stories varchar [note: 'Total number of above-grade floors in the planned development. Used for structural engineering classification, fire code compliance, elevator planning, and building permit applications.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of residential apartments, condominiums, or commercial suites planned for the development. Applicable to residential and mixed-use projects. Used for absorption modeling, HOA planning, and regulatory density compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the development project record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance per SOX financial controls.']
  warranty_registration_date date [note: 'Date on which contractor and equipment warranties were formally registered with the owner (yyyy-MM-dd). Initiates warranty tracking periods for building systems, roofing, and major equipment. Supports maintenance planning in Building Engines and CAPEX reserve scheduling.']
  zoning_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which final zoning approval or variance was granted by the local planning authority (yyyy-MM-dd). Marks completion of the entitlement phase and enables progression to design and permitting. Critical milestone for project schedule and investment commitment decisions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each real estate development or major capital improvement project — ground-up construction, redevelopment, or large-scale CAPEX programs. Captures project name, type (ground-up, renovation, mixed-use), current phase (entitlement, design, construction, closeout), total GLA/NLA targets, zoning classification, project address, estimated and actual start/completion dates, total project budget, and project manager assignment. Includes closeout lifecycle attributes: COO number and issuance date, as-built document delivery date, warranty registration, final retainage release date, O&M manual submission status, and closeout package completion. Also records Procore project ID for source-system traceability. SSOT for all development project identity and full lifecycle from inception through closeout and handoff to asset management.'
}

Table development.entitlement {
  entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each entitlement record within the development domain. Primary key for the entitlement data product.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Entitlements may be subject to specific compliance programs (affordable housing programs, environmental mitigation programs). Linking enables compliance program managers to track entitlement condition']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development project to which this entitlement belongs. Links the entitlement record to its governing project in the development domain.']
  environmental_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.environmental_review. Business justification: entitlement currently stores environmental_review_type and environmental_review_status as STRING fields describing the environmental review requirements for the entitlement. However, there is no FK to']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Entitlements are filed within specific jurisdictions mapped to the geographic hierarchy for market-level entitlement pipeline reporting, regulatory jurisdiction tracking, and submarket development act']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Entitlements are issued by specific regulatory jurisdictions governing zoning codes, FAR allowances, hearing processes, and appeal deadlines. The existing \'jurisdiction\' text field is a denormalized r']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Entitlements are granted at the parcel (APN) level by jurisdictions. Zoning approvals, FAR allowances, density limits, and conditions of approval are parcel-specific regulatory records. The existing p']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Entitlements are processed under specific regulatory frameworks (CEQA, NEPA, local zoning ordinances). Compliance tracking, appeal deadline management, and environmental review classification all requ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Entitlements are granted under specific regulatory obligations (zoning laws, land use regulations, environmental statutes). Linking enables compliance tracking of entitlement conditions against govern']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Entitlements are fundamentally about obtaining approval under or changing a specific zoning code. FAR compliance, density calculations, and conditional use permits all reference the zoning code. zonin']
  affordable_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of dwelling units designated as affordable housing within the proposed development. Often a condition of approval for bonus FAR or density bonuses under inclusionary zoning requirements.']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Date by which an appeal of the entitlement decision must be filed with the appropriate appellate body. After this date, the entitlement decision becomes final and vested. Critical for project risk management.']
  appeal_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appeal of the entitlement decision has been filed by a third party or the applicant. A value of True signals that the entitlement is subject to appellate review and the project timeline may be impacted.']
  applicant_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact information for the entitlement applicant, including name, phone, or email of the responsible representative. Used for correspondence with the regulatory authority.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal entity or individual who submitted the entitlement application to the governing jurisdiction. Typically the property owner, developer, or their authorized representative.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date on which the entitlement application was formally submitted to the governing jurisdiction. Marks the start of the regulatory review clock and is used for tracking processing timelines.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the entitlement was formally approved by the governing regulatory authority or planning commission. Null if the entitlement has not yet been approved.']
  as_built_far decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual FAR of the completed development as measured upon construction completion. Compared against total allowable FAR to confirm final FAR compliance. Populated post-construction.']
  base_far_allowance decimal(10,4) [note: 'The baseline Floor Area Ratio (FAR) permitted by the zoning district without any bonus mechanisms. Represents the ratio of total building floor area to the gross site area as established by the applicable zoning ordinance.']
  bim_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL pointing to the Building Information Modeling (BIM) model associated with this entitlement. Links the regulatory entitlement record to the design model used for FAR calculations and compliance documentation.']
  bonus_far decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additional FAR allowance earned through bonus mechanisms such as affordable housing inclusion, open space dedication, transit proximity, or public amenity contributions. Added to the base FAR to derive total allowable FAR.']
  bonus_far_mechanism varchar [note: 'The specific incentive mechanism through which bonus FAR was earned (e.g., affordable housing set-aside, open space dedication, transit proximity, public amenity). Determines the conditions and obligations attached to the bonus FAR grant.']
  conditions_of_approval varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the conditions, mitigation measures, or obligations imposed by the regulatory authority as part of the entitlement approval. May include infrastructure dedications, design requirements, operational restrictions, or monitoring obligations.']
  counsel varchar [note: 'Name of the law firm or attorney representing the applicant in the entitlement process. Entitlement counsel manages regulatory submissions, hearing appearances, and conditions negotiations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this entitlement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  denial_date date [note: 'Date on which the entitlement application was formally denied by the governing authority. Null if not denied. Used for tracking appeal windows and regulatory timelines.']
  entitlement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or title for the entitlement (e.g., \'Mixed-Use Conditional Use Permit — Block 42\', \'Height Variance — Tower A\'). Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known reference number or case number assigned by the governing jurisdiction or planning authority for this entitlement application (e.g., CUP-2024-0042, VAR-2024-0017). Used for cross-referencing with municipal records.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the entitlement application. Tracks progression from initial draft through submission, regulatory review, planning commission decision, and any appeal or withdrawal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|denied|appealed|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entitlement by its regulatory category. Identifies whether the entitlement is a zoning approval, variance, conditional use permit (CUP), environmental impact review, subdivision map, planned unit development (PUD), or design review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: zoning_approval|variance|conditional_use_permit|environmental_impact_review|subdivision_map|planned_unit_development|design_review — promote to reference product]']
  environmental_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the environmental impact review process associated with this entitlement. Tracks progression from initiation through certification, which is typically a prerequisite for entitlement approval.']
  environmental_review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental review conducted for this entitlement under applicable environmental law (e.g., CEQA, NEPA). Determines the level of environmental analysis required and the associated public review process.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved entitlement expires if construction or required actions have not commenced. Entitlements typically carry a vesting period; expiration triggers re-application requirements.']
  far_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the proposed or as-built development is in compliance with the total allowable FAR. Drives regulatory sign-off and Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance eligibility.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the planning commission or board hearing at which this entitlement application is reviewed and decided. Critical for project scheduling and stakeholder notification.']
  land_use_designation varchar [note: 'General plan or comprehensive plan land use designation for the subject parcel (e.g., \'High Density Residential\', \'Regional Commercial\', \'Mixed Use — Urban Center\'). Establishes the long-range planning framework within which zoning and entitlements must be consistent.']
  max_building_height_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted building height in feet as established by the applicable zoning ordinance or as approved through the entitlement process. A key development constraint tracked for compliance.']
  max_density_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of residential dwelling units permitted on the subject parcel under the applicable zoning ordinance or as approved through the entitlement. Applicable to residential and mixed-use developments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or supplementary information related to the entitlement that does not fit into structured fields. Used by project managers and entitlement teams for operational notes.']
  proposed_building_height_ft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Proposed building height in feet as submitted in the entitlement application. Must not exceed the maximum permitted height unless a height variance has been approved.']
  proposed_far decimal(10,4) [note: 'The FAR proposed by the development project as submitted in the entitlement application. Calculated as proposed gross floor area divided by gross site area. Must not exceed total allowable FAR for compliance.']
  proposed_gross_floor_area_sqf decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total proposed gross floor area of the development in square feet (SQF) as submitted in the entitlement application. Used to calculate proposed FAR and assess compliance with density limits.']
  proposed_units varchar [note: 'Number of residential dwelling units proposed in the development application. Compared against maximum density units to assess density compliance.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory body or planning commission responsible for adjudicating this entitlement (e.g., \'City Planning Commission\', \'Board of Zoning Appeals\', \'Environmental Review Board\'). May differ from the broader jurisdiction.']
  site_area_sqf decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross area of the subject development parcel in square feet (SQF). Used as the denominator in FAR calculations and for density limit assessments.']
  total_allowable_far decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum permitted FAR for the subject parcel, calculated as the sum of base FAR allowance and any approved bonus FAR. Represents the regulatory ceiling for building density on the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this entitlement record was last modified. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Tracks all entitlement activities and FAR compliance calculations for a development project — zoning approvals, variances, conditional use permits, environmental impact reviews, and planning commission decisions. Captures entitlement type, jurisdiction, application date, approval date, conditions of approval, and entitlement status. Includes FAR compliance detail: zoning district, base FAR allowance, bonus FAR mechanisms (affordable housing, open space, transit), total allowable FAR, proposed gross floor area, proposed FAR, as-built FAR, and FAR compliance status. Also records density limits, height restrictions, and the governing regulatory authority. Links to the parent dev_project. SSOT for all entitlement and zoning compliance data within the development domain.'
}

Building permits are issued only after zoning entitlements are approved — the entitlement is a prerequisite for the permit. ']

The existing \'jurisdiction_code\' ']

Business justification: Construction permits are issued under specific regulatory frameworks (IBC, local building codes, ADA).
