  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision counter indicating how many times this RFI has been revised or resubmitted. A value of 0 indicates the original submission. Supports version control and audit trail for design clarification history.']
  rfi_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known sequential alphanumeric identifier for the RFI as assigned by the project management system (e.g., RFI-000123). Used for cross-referencing in construction documentation, submittals, and dispute resolution packages.']
  rfi_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the RFI. \'draft\' indicates not yet submitted; \'open\' indicates submitted and awaiting response; \'answered\' indicates a response has been provided; \'closed\' indicates accepted and resolved; \'void\' indicates cancelled or superseded.']
  rfi_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFI by the nature of the information requested. Supports analytics on root causes of design gaps and construction documentation quality. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design_clarification|site_condition|specification|drawing_conflict|substitution_request|other — promote to reference product]']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated or confirmed number of calendar days of schedule delay attributable to this RFI. Populated when schedule_impact_flag is True. Used for time extension claims and critical path analysis.']
  schedule_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFI has a potential or confirmed impact on the construction schedule or critical path. When True, triggers schedule review and potential time extension claim evaluation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this RFI record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration audits.']
  spec_section varchar [note: 'CSI MasterFormat specification section number referenced by this RFI (e.g., \'03 30 00\' for Cast-in-Place Concrete). Enables traceability between RFIs and project specifications.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the information being requested (e.g., \'Clarification on structural beam connection detail at Grid Line B-4\'). Used for quick identification and search.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the RFI was formally submitted by the originating contractor to the design team. The principal business event date for the RFI lifecycle; used as the baseline for response time SLA calculations.']
  suggested_solution varchar [note: 'Optional proposed resolution or interpretation submitted by the originating contractor alongside the question. Provides the design team with a starting point for their official response and may expedite resolution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this RFI record in the system of record. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks all Requests for Information (RFIs) raised during construction — contractor questions to the design team regarding drawings, specifications, or site conditions. Captures RFI number, subject, originating contractor, assigned design professional, question description, response text, response date, cost impact flag, schedule impact flag, status (open/answered/closed), and days to respond. SSOT for construction RFI tracking within the development domain. Critical for construction documentation, design clarification audit trail, and dispute resolution.'
}

Table development.submittal {
  submittal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each construction submittal record within the development domain. Primary key for the submittal data product.']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contractor or subcontractor entity responsible for preparing and submitting this submittal package.']
  design_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.design_document. Business justification: submittal currently stores bim_model_reference as a STRING. Submittals (shop drawings, product data) are directly tied to specific design documents and BIM models. Replacing the string reference with ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development project to which this submittal belongs. Links the submittal to the parent construction or capital improvement project.']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Submittals are classified by document type (shop drawings, product data, samples, mock-ups). Document type drives review cycle requirements, retention periods, and approval workflows. submittal_type i']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design team member (architect, engineer, or owner representative) assigned to review and respond to this submittal.']
  previous_submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior revision of this submittal when the current record is a resubmission. Enables reconstruction of the full revision chain for a given submittal item. Null for initial submissions (revision_number = 0).']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Submittals for materials/systems must meet specific regulatory standards (fire ratings, energy codes, ADA requirements). Compliance teams review submittals against regulatory obligations; this link en']
  actual_response_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the design team returned the reviewed submittal with a disposition to the contractor. Compared against required_response_date to measure review cycle performance and identify schedule impacts.']
  ball_in_court varchar [note: 'Indicates which party currently holds responsibility for the next action on this submittal. Procore-native field used to track accountability and prevent submittals from stalling. Drives workflow notifications and overdue escalations.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Reviewer\'s written notes documenting specification compliance findings, conditions of approval, required corrections, or deviations from contract documents. Captured as part of the formal review disposition and retained for quality assurance and dispute resolution.']
  contractor_comments varchar [note: 'Comments or cover letter narrative provided by the submitting contractor accompanying the submittal, including any deviations from contract documents, special instructions, or requests for expedited review.']
  cost_code varchar [note: 'Project cost code or work breakdown structure (WBS) code associated with the scope of work covered by this submittal. Links the submittal to the Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) budget line for cost tracking and financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this submittal record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the primary submittal document stored in the project document management system (e.g., Procore Documents, SharePoint, or cloud storage). Provides direct access to the submittal package for review and audit purposes.']
  drawing_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of contract drawing numbers referenced by this submittal (e.g., \'S-101, S-102, S-201\'). Establishes traceability between the submittal and the specific contract drawings it addresses.']
  is_overdue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submittal review response is past the required_response_date without a final disposition having been issued. True when actual_response_date is null and the current date exceeds required_response_date, or when actual_response_date exceeded required_response_date.']
  is_substitution_request boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submittal constitutes a formal substitution request for a product or material not specified in the contract documents. True when the contractor is proposing an alternative to the specified product. Triggers additional review scrutiny and owner approval requirements.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Description of the physical location or building area within the project where the submitted product, material, or system will be installed (e.g., \'Level 3 — East Wing\', \'Parking Structure — Grid Lines A-D\'). Supports spatial tracking of approved materials.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the product or material manufacturer whose product data, shop drawings, or samples are the subject of this submittal. Critical for specification compliance verification and substitution request tracking.']
  number_of_copies varchar [note: 'Number of physical or electronic copies of the submittal package submitted by the contractor, as required by the project specifications or general conditions. Relevant for physical sample and shop drawing submissions.']
  package_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the submittal package or bundle when multiple individual submittals are grouped and transmitted together in a single transmittal. Enables grouping of related submittals submitted simultaneously.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level indicating the urgency of the submittal review relative to the construction schedule. Critical submittals are on the critical path; high-priority submittals have near-term fabrication or long-lead procurement deadlines.']
  procore_submittal_reference varchar [note: 'The native submittal identifier assigned by Procore Construction and Development Project Management system. Used for cross-referencing and data lineage tracing back to the operational system of record.']
  product_model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model or catalog number for the product or material being submitted. Used to verify conformance with specified products and to track approved product substitutions.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the submittal was physically or electronically received and logged by the owner\'s project management team or construction manager. May differ from submission_date if there is a transit or processing delay.']
  required_response_date date [note: 'The contractually or schedule-driven date by which the design team must return a reviewed disposition to the contractor. Used to monitor reviewer compliance with turnaround commitments and to flag overdue reviews that may impact the construction schedule.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'The contractually stipulated number of calendar or business days allowed for the design team to review and respond to this submittal, as defined in the project specifications or general conditions. Drives the calculation of required_response_date from submission_date.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the submittal reflecting the design team\'s disposition. Standard construction industry statuses: approved (proceed with fabrication/installation), approved_as_noted (proceed with corrections noted), revise_and_resubmit (corrections required before approval), rejected (does not conform to contract documents), under_review (in active review), void (cancelled or superseded).']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed technical comments from the design team reviewer beyond the formal compliance notes. May include mark-up references, clarification requests, or guidance for the contractor on how to address deficiencies in a resubmission.']
  reviewer_discipline varchar [note: 'The engineering or design discipline of the assigned reviewer, indicating which professional specialty is responsible for the technical review. Enables workload analysis by discipline and routing of submittals to the correct reviewer queue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architecture|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|landscape|owner_rep|other — promote to reference product]']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the design team professional (architect, structural engineer, MEP engineer, or owner representative) assigned as the primary reviewer for this submittal. Captured as a denormalized field for reporting and correspondence.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer indicating the revision iteration of this submittal. Starts at 0 for the initial submission; increments by 1 for each resubmission following a revise_and_resubmit or rejected disposition. Enables tracking of the full revision history.']
  spec_section varchar [note: 'CSI MasterFormat specification section number governing the work covered by this submittal (e.g., \'03 30 00\' for Cast-in-Place Concrete, \'09 90 00\' for Paints and Coatings). Enables traceability from submittal to contract specification requirements.']
  spec_section_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the CSI MasterFormat specification section (e.g., \'Cast-in-Place Concrete\', \'Structural Steel Framing\'). Provides context alongside the numeric spec_section code for reporting and communication.']
  subcontractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the subcontractor or specialty trade contractor who prepared the submittal when the submitting party is the general contractor acting on behalf of a subcontractor. Nullable when the general contractor is the direct preparer.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the contractor formally submitted the submittal package to the design team or owner representative for review. This is the principal business event date for the submittal lifecycle.']
  submittal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope, content, and purpose of the submittal package. May include the specific materials, products, systems, or assemblies covered and any special instructions from the contractor.']
  submittal_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the submittal, typically formatted as spec-section prefix followed by a sequential number and optional revision suffix (e.g., 03300-001-R2). Used for cross-referencing in RFIs, meeting minutes, and contract documents.']
  substitution_basis varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the contractor justifying the proposed product substitution, including equivalency claims, cost savings, schedule benefits, or unavailability of the specified product. Populated only when is_substitution_request is True.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the submittal package as provided by the submitting contractor (e.g., \'Structural Steel Shop Drawings — Level 3 Framing\', \'Curtain Wall System Product Data\'). Used for identification in logs, reports, and correspondence.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'The formal transmittal document number assigned when the submittal is forwarded from the contractor to the design team or from the design team back to the contractor. Used for document control and chain-of-custody tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this submittal record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Records all construction submittals — shop drawings, product data, samples, and mock-ups submitted by contractors for design team review and approval. Captures submittal number, spec section, submittal type, submitting contractor, assigned reviewer, submission date, required response date, actual response date, review status (approved/approved as noted/revise and resubmit/rejected), revision number, and compliance notes. SSOT for construction submittal tracking within the development domain. Supports specification compliance verification and construction quality assurance.'
}

Table development.inspection_event {
  inspection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each construction inspection event record in the development domain.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Construction inspection events for renovation and TI projects occur at specific existing buildings. Linking inspection_event to property.building enables inspection scheduling, compliance tracking, an']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Construction inspections resulting in building code or safety violations generate compliance violation records. Linking inspection_event to compliance.violation enables tracking of violation resolutio']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the building permit under which this inspection is conducted. Required for municipal and regulatory inspections.']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary contractor or subcontractor whose work scope is being inspected during this event.']
  design_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.design_document. Business justification: inspection_event currently stores bim_model_reference as a STRING. Inspections reference specific BIM models or design documents to verify as-built conditions against approved designs. Replacing the s']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development project against which this inspection event is recorded. Links to the Procore project record.']
  draw_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.draw_request. Business justification: inspection_event currently stores draw_request_number as a STRING. Lender draw inspections are a critical workflow: an inspector must certify work completion before a draw is disbursed. The lender_dra']
  osha_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.osha_incident. Business justification: Construction site inspections that identify OSHA violations or safety incidents must link to the OSHA incident record. Safety managers use this to track regulatory reporting obligations (OSHA 300 log)']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Construction inspections are conducted under specific regulatory frameworks (IBC, OSHA, local building codes, ADA). Inspection compliance tracking, violation reporting, and certificate of occupancy si']
  reinspection_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this failed or partial-pass inspection to its subsequent re-inspection event record. Enables inspection chain traceability for COO documentation.']
  signed_off_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: COO-related and lender-draw inspections require a documented internal employee sign-off for regulatory compliance and lender disbursement approval. signed_off_by is currently a plain text denormalizat']
  areas_inspected varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific building areas, floors, zones, or grid locations covered during this inspection event (e.g., \'Level 3 east wing structural framing, columns C4 through C8\').']
  building_level varchar [note: 'Specific floor or level of the building structure where the inspection was conducted (e.g., \'B1\', \'L1\', \'Roof\'). Supports spatial tracking of inspection coverage across the development.']
  coo_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event is on the critical path for Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. True for all mandatory municipal inspections required before occupancy is permitted.']
  coo_sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspector or authority having jurisdiction (AHJ) provided final sign-off on this inspection, confirming it satisfies COO requirements. Null until formal sign-off is granted.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required to remediate deficiencies identified during this inspection. Drives contractor work orders and re-inspection scheduling.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the contractor must complete all corrective actions identified in this inspection event. Used for milestone tracking and contractor compliance monitoring.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action must be completed before the next inspection or before work may proceed. True when inspection_result is fail or partial_pass.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct deficiencies or non-conformances identified during this inspection event. Used for quality trending and contractor performance analytics.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of all deficiencies, non-conformances, or code violations identified during the inspection. Serves as the official record for corrective action planning and regulatory documentation.']
  deficiency_severity varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the most severe deficiency identified during this inspection. Critical deficiencies may trigger stop-work orders. Supports prioritization of corrective action and OSHA compliance reporting.']
  inspection_category varchar [note: 'Specific trade or discipline category being inspected (e.g., foundation, framing, electrical rough-in, plumbing rough-in, mechanical/HVAC, fire suppression, roofing, concrete, soils, waterproofing, final). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: foundation|framing|electrical|plumbing|mechanical_hvac|fire_suppression|roofing|concrete|soils|waterproofing|insulation|final — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inspection was physically conducted at the construction site. This is the principal real-world event date for the inspection record.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection concluded on site. Combined with inspection_start_time to derive inspection duration for resource planning.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'General narrative notes recorded by the inspector or project manager during or after the inspection event. Captures observations not covered by structured fields.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this inspection event, used for cross-referencing with municipal records, Procore, and lender inspection reports. Format: INSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  inspection_report_number varchar [note: 'Official report number assigned to the inspection report document generated from this event. Used for document control and cross-referencing with Procore document management.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the inspection as recorded by the inspector. Pass results advance the project toward Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. Fail and partial_pass results require corrective action and re-inspection.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection commenced on site. Used for OSHA compliance documentation and inspector time-on-site tracking.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection event. Passed and partial_pass statuses contribute to Certificate of Occupancy (COO) milestone tracking. Failed status triggers corrective action workflow.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by the authority or party conducting it. Municipal inspections are required for Certificate of Occupancy (COO) issuance. Third-party special inspections cover structural, soils, and materials testing. Lender inspections support Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw schedule approvals. Owner quality control (QC) inspections are internal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: municipal|third_party_special|lender|owner_qc|fire_marshal|environmental|utility|accessibility — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency the inspector represents (e.g., municipal building department, third-party inspection firm, lender, owner). Distinguishes authority of inspection for COO and compliance purposes.']
  inspector_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the inspector, required for third-party special inspectors and municipal building officials. Supports regulatory compliance documentation.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the inspection. May be a municipal building official, third-party special inspector, lender representative, or owner quality control (QC) inspector.']
  lender_draw_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event is associated with a Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw schedule request. Lender inspections must pass before draw funds are released.']
  osha_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection event includes OSHA safety compliance verification. Required for all inspections conducted on active construction sites under OSHA 29 CFR 1926 standards.']
  osha_violation_noted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any OSHA safety violations were identified during this inspection event. True triggers mandatory OSHA compliance reporting and corrective action documentation.']
  photo_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to photographic evidence captured during the inspection. Photos document both compliant work and deficiencies for legal and regulatory purposes.']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date for the follow-up re-inspection after corrective actions have been completed. Null if reinspection_required is False.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up re-inspection is required after corrective actions are completed. Automatically set to True when inspection_result is fail or partial_pass.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to the official inspection report document stored in the document management system (Procore or DocuSign CLM). Supports audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled date for the inspection. Compared against inspection_date to track scheduling adherence and delays impacting the construction milestone schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection event record was last modified. Tracks changes to inspection results, corrective actions, or sign-off status for audit compliance.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Prevailing weather conditions at the time of the inspection. Relevant for outdoor inspections where weather may affect work quality assessment or inspector access. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|snow|high_wind|extreme_heat|fog — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records all construction inspections conducted on a development project — municipal building inspections, third-party special inspections, lender inspections, and owner quality control inspections. Captures inspection type, inspection date, inspector name and affiliation, areas inspected, pass/fail result, deficiencies noted, corrective action required, re-inspection date, and final sign-off. Supports COO issuance tracking and OSHA compliance documentation.'
}

Table development.design_document {
  design_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each design document or Building Information Model (BIM) record within the development domain. Primary key for the design_document product.']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: design_document currently stores permit_number as a STRING. Design documents (drawings, specifications) are submitted as part of permit applications and are directly associated with specific permits. ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development project to which this design document belongs. Links the document to its associated ground-up development or capital improvement program.']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Design documents are classified by document type (architectural drawings, structural calculations, specifications, BIM models). Document type drives retention requirements, approval workflows, and reg']
  entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.entitlement. Business justification: design_document currently stores zoning_approval_reference as a STRING. Design documents must comply with entitlement/zoning approvals (FAR compliance, height limits, setbacks). Replacing the string r']
  superseded_by_document_design_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design_document record that supersedes this document when a new revision is issued. Enables navigation of the document revision chain and ensures construction teams are using the current approved revision.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from review to approved status and triggers downstream construction or permitting activities.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the document in the review and approval lifecycle. Controls whether the document may be used for construction, permitting, or record purposes. Drives document control workflows in Procore.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (project manager, lead architect, or owner representative) who granted final approval for this document revision. Establishes accountability for the approval decision.']
  author_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the design firm, engineering consultancy, or contractor organization responsible for producing this document (e.g., \'Gensler\', \'Arup\', \'Turner Construction\'). Used for accountability, transmittal routing, and professional liability tracking.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual professional (architect, engineer, or designer) who prepared or is responsible for this document. Used for professional accountability and document control records.']
  bep_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BIM model has been verified as compliant with the project\'s BIM Execution Plan (BEP), which defines modeling standards, naming conventions, LOD requirements, and coordination protocols. Non-compliant models may not be used for construction or permitting.']
  bim_authoring_software varchar [note: 'Name of the BIM authoring application used to create the model (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD, Tekla Structures). Applicable only when is_bim_model = True. Drives interoperability requirements and IFC export compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Revit|ArchiCAD|Bentley_AECOsim|Vectorworks|Tekla|Navisworks|Civil3D|Rhino — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bim_model_type varchar [note: 'Discipline-specific classification of the BIM model (e.g., architectural, structural, MEP, civil, or federated coordination model). The federated model aggregates all discipline models for clash detection and coordination. Applicable when is_bim_model = True.']
  bim_software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the BIM authoring software used to create the model (e.g., \'Revit 2024\', \'ArchiCAD 27\'). Critical for file compatibility when opening or federating models across project team members.']
  clash_detection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of automated clash detection analysis performed on this BIM model using coordination tools such as Autodesk Navisworks. Tracks whether interdisciplinary conflicts have been identified and resolved prior to construction. Applicable when is_bim_model = True.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this document governing access and distribution controls. Restricted documents (e.g., security plans, structural vulnerability assessments) require elevated access controls. Aligns with enterprise data classification policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this design document record was first created in the data platform. Provides the audit trail entry point for the record lifecycle in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  csi_division_code varchar [note: 'Two-digit CSI MasterFormat division code classifying the technical scope of the document (e.g., \'03\' for Concrete, \'05\' for Metals, \'23\' for HVAC). Enables structured cost coding, specification cross-referencing, and procurement alignment.']
  design_phase varchar [note: 'Project design phase during which this document was produced or is applicable. Aligns with standard AIA project delivery phases from schematic design through as-built documentation. Supports phase-gated milestone tracking.']
  digital_twin_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this design document or BIM model has been linked to the project\'s digital twin platform for ongoing facility management and asset lifecycle tracking post-construction. Supports the transition from construction BIM to operations BIM.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Technical discipline or trade responsible for producing the document. Used to route documents to the correct reviewer and to organize the document register by engineering specialty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|civil|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|geotechnical|landscape|interior|fire_protection|survey|environmental — promote to reference product]']
  dms_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference identifier or URL path to the document as stored in the project document management system (e.g., Procore document ID, SharePoint path, or Autodesk Construction Cloud URN). Enables direct retrieval of the source file.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the document per the project document numbering convention (e.g., A-101, S-201, MEP-301). Used for cross-referencing in RFIs, submittals, and construction sets.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the design document or BIM model (e.g., \'Ground Floor Architectural Plan\', \'Structural Foundation Details\', \'Federated Coordination Model — Level 3\').']
  far_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the design document has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with the applicable Floor Area Ratio (FAR) limits established by local zoning regulations. FAR compliance is a critical entitlement and permitting requirement for development projects.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the document as stored in the document management system (e.g., PDF for issued drawings, RVT for Revit BIM models, IFC for open BIM exchange, DWG for AutoCAD). Drives viewer and interoperability requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DWG|RVT|IFC|DGN|DOCX|XLSX|NWD|NWC — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_kb varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in kilobytes as stored in the document management system. Used for storage capacity planning, upload validation, and document management system performance monitoring.']
  is_bim_model boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a Building Information Model (BIM) file rather than a traditional 2D drawing or document. When True, BIM-specific attributes (authoring software, LOD, clash detection status, etc.) are applicable.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this revision of the document was formally issued or transmitted to the project team, contractor, or authority having jurisdiction. Used for document control tracking and contractual milestone verification.']
  issue_purpose varchar [note: 'Stated purpose for which the document was issued. Determines the legal and contractual standing of the document set. \'For Construction\' documents are the contractual basis for contractor work; \'For Permit\' sets are submitted to authorities having jurisdiction (AHJ); \'For Record\' are as-built documents.']
  last_coordination_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent BIM coordination session or clash detection run for this model. Used to assess model currency and ensure coordination is current before construction activities proceed in the relevant area.']
  level_of_development varchar [note: 'BIM Level of Development designation per the AIA/BIMForum LOD Specification, indicating the degree of completeness and reliability of model elements (LOD 100 = conceptual through LOD 500 = as-built). Governs what information can be extracted from the model for design, fabrication, and facility management.']
  model_manager varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual responsible for managing, maintaining, and coordinating this BIM model. The model manager is accountable for model quality, clash resolution, and compliance with the BIM Execution Plan (BEP).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for document control comments, review notes, approval conditions, or other contextual information not captured in structured fields. Used by project managers and document controllers to record exceptions, clarifications, or action items.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was received by the project team or document control function from the originating party (architect, engineer, or consultant). Relevant for tracking transmittal lead times and review SLA compliance.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision identifier for the document (e.g., \'0\', \'A\', \'B\', \'1\', \'2\'). Tracks the version history of the document through design development, permitting, and construction phases. Each new revision supersedes the prior.']
  scale varchar [note: 'Nominal drawing scale expressed as a ratio or architectural notation (e.g., \'1:100\', \'1/8"=1\\'0"\'). Applicable to 2D drawings; null for BIM models and non-graphical documents. Used for print verification and field measurement reference.']
  sheet_size varchar [note: 'Standard paper or digital sheet size designation for the drawing (e.g., A1, A0, ARCH D). Relevant for print management, document reproduction, and compliance with local authority submission requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A0|A1|A2|A3|A4|ARCH_D|ARCH_E|ANSI_D — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  specification_section varchar [note: 'CSI MasterFormat specification section number associated with this document (e.g., \'03 30 00\' for Cast-in-Place Concrete). Links drawings and models to the corresponding written specification sections for procurement and quality control.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the formal document transmittal package through which this document was distributed to the project team or contractor. Supports document control audit trails and confirms receipt of issued documents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this design document record in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Tracks all design documents and Building Information Models (BIM) associated with a development project — architectural drawings, engineering plans, specifications, geotechnical reports, survey documents, and BIM models (architectural, structural, MEP, civil, federated coordination). Core attributes: document number, document type, discipline, revision number, issue date, issue purpose (for construction, for permit, for record), author, approval status, and document management system reference. For BIM models, additionally captures authoring software (Revit, ArchiCAD), Level of Development (LOD), clash detection status, last coordination date, model manager, and BIM execution plan compliance. SSOT for all design documentation and BIM model registry within the development domain. Supports document control, digital twin linkage, and as-built record management.'
}

Table development.project_schedule {
  project_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the project schedule record in the development domain. Primary key for the master schedule of a real estate development project.']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: project_schedule currently stores permit_reference as a STRING. Permit issuance and inspections are key schedule milestones in CPM scheduling. Replacing the string reference with a proper FK project_s']
  design_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.design_document. Business justification: project_schedule currently stores bim_model_ref as a STRING. Project schedules in BIM-enabled projects are linked to specific BIM models (4D BIM scheduling). Replacing the string reference with a prop']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development project this schedule belongs to. Links the schedule to the project entity in the development domain.']
  entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.entitlement. Business justification: project_schedule currently stores zoning_approval_ref as a STRING. The project schedule is directly dependent on entitlement milestones — construction cannot begin until entitlements are approved. Rep']
  schedule_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Schedule baseline approvals are a named project governance requirement in real estate development — lenders and investors require documented internal employee authorization of project schedules. sched']
  baseline_finish_date date [note: 'The originally approved project completion date from the baseline schedule. Serves as the contractual reference for substantial completion and COO issuance milestones.']
  baseline_start_date date [note: 'The originally approved project start date from the baseline schedule. Used as the reference point for schedule variance analysis and SPI calculation throughout the project lifecycle.']
  capex_draw_schedule_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this project schedule to the associated CAPEX draw schedule in the finance system (Yardi or SAP S/4HANA). Enables alignment of construction milestone completion with capital draw requests to lenders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project schedule record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  critical_path_activity_count varchar [note: 'Number of activities on the critical path (total float = 0 or negative) in the current schedule. Used to assess schedule complexity and focus project management attention on critical activities.']
  current_finish_date date [note: 'The current forecast or actual project completion date from the latest schedule update. Used for investor milestone reporting and CAPEX draw schedule alignment.']
  current_start_date date [note: 'The current forecast or actual project start date reflecting the latest approved schedule update. May differ from baseline_start_date due to approved schedule changes.']
  data_date date [note: 'The as-of date through which actual progress has been recorded in this schedule update (also called the status date or time-now date in CPM scheduling). All forecast dates are projected forward from this date.']
  external_schedule_file_ref varchar [note: 'Path or URI to the native scheduling software file (e.g., Primavera P6 .xer or MS Project .mpp) stored in the document management system or cloud storage. Enables drill-back to the source schedule file.']
  far_approved decimal(6,4) [note: 'The approved Floor Area Ratio for the development project as granted by the zoning authority. Used to verify that the construction schedule aligns with the approved development envelope and FAR compliance requirements.']
  last_updated_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule was last updated with progress data or revised logic. Indicates the currency of the schedule information for reporting and audit purposes.']
  milestone_actual_date date [note: 'The date on which the milestone was actually achieved. Null if the milestone has not yet been completed. Populated upon milestone completion and used for as-built schedule records and regulatory filings.']
  milestone_forecast_date date [note: 'Current forecast date for milestone achievement based on the latest schedule update. Reflects schedule changes, delays, or acceleration. Used for investor milestone updates and CAPEX draw schedule alignment.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the key project milestone tracked in this schedule record (e.g., \'Entitlement Approval\', \'Groundbreaking\', \'Foundation Pour\', \'Topping Out\', \'Substantial Completion\', \'COO Issuance\'). Supports milestone-level reporting to investors and lenders.']
  milestone_planned_date date [note: 'The baseline-approved planned date for this milestone as established in the original project schedule. Used as the reference for milestone variance calculation and investor commitment tracking.']
  milestone_predecessor_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of schedule activity or milestone IDs that must be completed before this milestone can begin, representing predecessor relationships in the CPM network. Supports critical path analysis and dependency visualization.']
  milestone_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the party accountable for achieving this milestone (e.g., General Contractor, Owner\'s Representative, Architect, Civil Engineer, Municipality). Used for accountability tracking and escalation management.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the milestone: not_started, in_progress, completed, delayed (past forecast date without completion), or at_risk (forecast date within risk threshold). Drives milestone dashboard RAG status reporting.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the milestone per real estate development lifecycle: entitlement_approval, groundbreaking, foundation_pour, topping_out, substantial_completion, or coo_issuance (Certificate of Occupancy). Enables cross-project milestone benchmarking.']
  milestone_variance_days varchar [note: 'Variance in calendar days between the milestone forecast (or actual) date and the milestone planned date. Positive value indicates delay; negative indicates early completion. Reported to investors and lenders as a key development performance indicator.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall schedule percent complete as of the data date, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Derived from the CPM schedule and reported to investors and lenders as a key construction progress indicator.']
  procore_project_reference varchar [note: 'The native project identifier from Procore Construction Management system. Used as the system-of-record cross-reference key for reconciling schedule data ingested from Procore into the lakehouse silver layer.']
  schedule_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this schedule version was formally approved by the authorized party. Establishes the effective date of the baseline or revised schedule for contractual and reporting purposes.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the project schedule (e.g., \'Phase 1 Baseline Schedule Rev 3\'). Used for display in dashboards and investor milestone reports.']
  schedule_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative accompanying the schedule update, describing key changes, delays, recovery actions, and risk items. Submitted with each schedule update to the owner and lender as part of the monthly construction report.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the schedule document, used in Procore and cross-referenced in investor reports and construction contracts. Follows the pattern SCH-{ProjectCode}.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule record: draft (under preparation), approved (formally accepted), active (in use for progress tracking), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or archived (historical record only).']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule version: baseline (original approved plan), current (live working schedule), recovery (corrective re-plan), what_if (scenario analysis), or as_built (post-completion record). Drives which schedule is used for SPI and variance reporting.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in calendar days between the current finish date and the baseline finish date (current_finish_date minus baseline_finish_date). Positive value indicates delay; negative indicates ahead of schedule. Key metric for construction oversight reporting.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the schedule (e.g., \'B0\' for baseline, \'R1\', \'R2\' for revisions). Tracks schedule evolution through the project lifecycle per CPM scheduling best practices.']
  scheduling_software varchar [note: 'CPM scheduling application used to produce this schedule (e.g., Primavera P6, MS Project). Required for interoperability and file format management when importing schedule data into Procore or the lakehouse.']
  spi decimal(6,4) [note: 'Schedule Performance Index from Earned Value Management: ratio of earned value to planned value (EV/PV). SPI < 1.0 indicates the project is behind schedule; SPI > 1.0 indicates ahead of schedule. Key metric for lender and investor reporting.']
  total_activity_count varchar [note: 'Total number of schedule activities (tasks) in this schedule version. Used to assess schedule density and compliance with owner-required schedule specifications.']
  total_float_days varchar [note: 'Total float (slack) of the project\'s critical path in calendar days as of the data date. Negative total float indicates the project is behind the contractual completion date. Critical for schedule risk assessment.']
  update_frequency varchar [note: 'Contractually required or agreed frequency at which the schedule is updated with progress data: weekly, bi_weekly, monthly, or milestone_driven. Governs the cadence of schedule submissions to the owner and lender.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this project schedule record was last modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master schedule record for a development project — the baseline and current CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule with integrated milestone tracking. Schedule-level data: schedule version, baseline and current start/finish dates, total float, critical path activities count, schedule performance index (SPI), percent complete, last update date, and scheduling software reference (Primavera P6, MS Project). Milestone-level data: milestone name and type (entitlement approval, groundbreaking, foundation pour, topping out, substantial completion, COO issuance), planned date, forecast date, actual date, days variance, responsible party, predecessor relationships, and completion status. SSOT for project scheduling and milestone tracking within the development domain. Enables critical path analysis, schedule performance reporting, and investor milestone updates.'
}

Table development.environmental_review {
  environmental_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each environmental due diligence or compliance review record within the development domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset subject to the environmental review. Supports property-level environmental compliance tracking.']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Environmental reviews (Phase I/II ESA findings) trigger formal compliance assessments. Compliance teams use this link to track remediation requirements and regulatory findings arising from development']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Environmental reviews are conducted under specific compliance programs (environmental management programs, ESG programs). Linking enables program-level tracking of environmental review outcomes across']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the development or construction project for which this environmental review was conducted. Links to the development project master record in Procore.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Environmental reviews (NEPA, CEQA, Phase I/II ESA) are conducted under specific jurisdictional regulatory frameworks. The jurisdiction determines which environmental laws apply and which agency has ov']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Phase I/II ESAs and environmental reviews are conducted at the parcel level per ASTM standards. Lenders require parcel-level environmental clearance before loan closing. The parcel is the legal unit f']
  prior_review_environmental_review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a preceding environmental review record (e.g., the Phase I ESA that triggered a Phase II ESA). Supports assessment chain-of-custody and audit trail.']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Environmental reviews are conducted under specific regulatory frameworks (NEPA, CEQA, state environmental laws, ASTM Phase I/II standards). Regulatory clearance tracking, lender approval, and remediat']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Environmental reviews are triggered by and must satisfy specific regulatory obligations (NEPA, CEQA, state environmental statutes). The regulatory_obligation defines the legal requirement the review f']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the environmental site assessment or review was conducted in the field or initiated by the consultant. Principal real-world event date for this review.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which the property received full environmental clearance, confirming no further remediation or regulatory action is required. Critical milestone for lender draw schedules and COO issuance.']
  consultant_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the environmental consulting firm engaged to conduct the assessment or review. Used for vendor management, qualification tracking, and report attribution.']
  consultant_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the environmental professional who conducted the review. Validates regulatory qualification requirements.']
  consultant_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed environmental professional (EP) who conducted and signed the assessment. Required by ASTM and EPA to confirm qualified professional oversight.']
  contaminant_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of contaminant types identified during the assessment (e.g., Asbestos, Lead Paint, PCBs, UST, Petroleum Hydrocarbons, Heavy Metals). Supports risk classification and remediation scoping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental review record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  esg_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this environmental review is material to ESG reporting obligations (e.g., LEED certification, BREEAM assessment, SEC ESG disclosure for REITs). Supports portfolio-level ESG analytics.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the environmental review is no longer considered valid per applicable standards (e.g., ASTM 180-day rule for Phase I ESA). Triggers renewal workflow.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the key findings from the environmental review, including identified conditions, contaminants, or concerns. Supports executive reporting and lender due diligence packages.']
  hazardous_materials_found boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous materials (e.g., asbestos, lead-based paint, PCBs, underground storage tanks) were identified during the assessment. Triggers abatement planning and OSHA compliance requirements.']
  lender_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lender has formally accepted and approved the environmental review findings as satisfactory for loan closing or draw release purposes.']
  lender_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the environmental review was required by a lender as part of loan underwriting or draw conditions. Aligns with ASTM All Appropriate Inquiries (AAI) standards for lender due diligence.']
  nepa_review_level varchar [note: 'Level of NEPA environmental review required for the project: Categorical Exclusion (CE), Environmental Assessment (EA), or full Environmental Impact Statement (EIS). Determines federal permitting timeline.']
  phase_ii_triggered_by_phase_i boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this Phase II Environmental Site Assessment (ESA) was triggered by REC findings from a prior Phase I ESA. Establishes the assessment chain of custody for regulatory and lender reporting.']
  rec_classification varchar [note: 'Highest-severity REC classification identified: No RECs (no conditions), REC (current concern), CREC (Controlled REC — subject to regulatory controls), HREC (Historical REC — addressed), PCREC (Potential Controlled REC). Drives risk rating and remediation decisions.']
  rec_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Recognized Environmental Conditions (RECs) identified during the assessment. A REC indicates the presence or likely presence of hazardous substances or petroleum products. Key risk metric for acquisition and lending decisions.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory agency overseeing or receiving the environmental review (e.g., EPA, State DEP, U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, HUD). Determines applicable compliance framework.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the relevant regulatory agency formally approved or cleared the environmental review findings. Null if approval has not yet been obtained.']
  regulatory_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case or docket number assigned by the regulatory agency for tracking this environmental review or remediation action in their system of record.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date for all remediation activities. Critical milestone for COO issuance eligibility and lender draw release.']
  remediation_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the remediation cost estimate (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting.']
  remediation_cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost in USD to complete required environmental remediation activities. Used in CAPEX budgeting, lender underwriting, and acquisition pricing adjustments.']
  remediation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether environmental remediation is required based on the assessment findings. True if remediation action is mandated by regulatory findings or lender requirements.']
  remediation_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual start date for remediation activities. Used in construction milestone scheduling and CAPEX draw schedule planning.']
  remediation_type varchar [note: 'Type of remediation approach required or recommended based on Phase II ESA findings. Determines CAPEX budget requirements and project timeline impacts.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date the final environmental review report was issued by the environmental consultant. Used for report validity tracking per ASTM 180-day validity rule.']
  report_document_ref varchar [note: 'Document management system reference or file path to the full environmental review report stored in Procore or DocuSign CLM. Enables direct access to source documentation.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the environmental review, used in regulatory submissions, lender packages, and consultant correspondence. Format: ENV-YYYY-NNNNN.']
  review_scope_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope limitations, data gaps, or deviations from standard assessment methodology noted by the environmental professional. Required disclosure per ASTM E1527-21.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the environmental review, from initiation through regulatory clearance. Drives workflow routing and lender reporting.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the environmental review or assessment conducted. Determines the scope, methodology, and regulatory framework applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I ESA|Phase II ESA|NEPA Review|Wetlands Delineation|Remediation Plan|Environmental Impact Statement|Regulatory Compliance Audit — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this environmental review record originated (e.g., Procore for construction projects, Yardi Voyager for property management). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental review record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  ust_count varchar [note: 'Number of underground storage tanks (USTs) identified on the property. USTs are a primary source of petroleum contamination and trigger EPA UST program compliance requirements.']
  wetlands_area_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total area of jurisdictional wetlands identified on the subject property, measured in square feet (SQF). Used for Section 404 permit applications and mitigation planning.']
  wetlands_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether jurisdictional wetlands were identified on or adjacent to the subject property during delineation. Triggers U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Section 404 permit requirements.']

  Note: 'Tracks environmental due diligence and compliance reviews for development projects — Phase I and Phase II Environmental Site Assessments (ESAs), NEPA reviews, wetlands delineations, and remediation plans. Captures review type, environmental consultant, assessment date, findings summary, recognized environmental conditions (RECs), remediation required flag, remediation cost estimate, regulatory agency, approval date, and clearance status. Supports EPA compliance and lender environmental requirements.'
}

Table development.proforma {
  proforma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the development pro forma underwriting record. Primary key for the proforma entity within the development domain.']
  cap_rate_benchmark_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.cap_rate_benchmark. Business justification: Development proformas use exit cap rate assumptions that must be supported by market cap rate benchmarks for lender and investor credibility. Linking proforma to cap_rate_benchmark allows the investme']
  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: Proformas model financial returns from green certifications (energy savings, premium rents, tax incentives). Linking proforma to the target green_certification record enables tracking of certification']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Proforma financial models are denominated in a specific currency. Multi-currency investment analysis, cross-border portfolio reporting, and investment committee presentations require a normalized curr']
  dcf_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.valuation_dcf_model. Business justification: Development proformas are reconciled against third-party DCF valuations during investment committee review and lender underwriting. Linking proforma to valuation_dcf_model enables direct comparison of']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent development project record in dev_project. Links the pro forma to its associated ground-up development or capital improvement program.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Proformas are prepared within the context of a specific investment fund whose target IRR, equity multiple, and preferred return hurdle directly drive proforma assumptions. Fund-level return reporting ']
  ground_lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.ground_lease. Business justification: Development underwriting: a proforma\'s land cost, annual ground rent expense, and NOI calculations are directly driven by ground lease terms. Investment committees and lenders require the proforma to ']
  investment_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.investment_deal. Business justification: A proforma is the primary financial model prepared for an investment deal in the pipeline. IC memos reference the proforma tied to the deal. Deal-stage tracking (LOI, contract, IC submission) requires']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Development proformas are prepared for a specific legal entity (investment vehicle/SPE) to model waterfall distributions, REIT compliance, and investor returns. Investment committee approvals and tax ']
  market_research_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_research. Business justification: Development proformas use market research data (cap rates, asking rents, absorption rates, vacancy) as direct inputs for underwriting assumptions. Linking proforma to market_research creates an audit ']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Proformas are modeled against specific market segments (core, value-add, opportunistic) for IRR benchmarking, NCREIF/INREV reporting, and investment committee approval. Market segment classification d']
  ownership_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.ownership_structure. Business justification: The development proforma models the equity waterfall, preferred return, and promote structure defined by the ownership structure. Investment committee approvals and LP/GP reporting require the proform']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio. Business justification: A development proforma is prepared for a project being added to a specific portfolio. Portfolio-level strategy (target cap rate, hold period, IRR) shapes proforma inputs. Portfolio performance reporti']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Investment committee approvals and deal underwriting require knowing which internal employee authored the proforma. prepared_by is currently plain text; normalizing to employee FK enables authorship a']
  prior_proforma_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on proforma (prior_proforma_id)']
  reference_investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.investment_vehicle. Business justification: Proformas model returns for a specific investment vehicle structure (REIT, opportunity fund, JV, separate account). Waterfall structure, preferred return, carried interest, and SEC/regulatory reportin']
  waterfall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.waterfall. Business justification: The proforma models investor return distributions using a specific waterfall structure (preferred return, GP promote, carried interest tiers). IC memos require proforma return projections tied to the ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the investment committee formally approved this pro forma. Marks the go/no-go decision point for project execution and capital commitment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Investment committee approval disposition for this pro forma. Drives go/no-go decision for project commencement, lender engagement, and equity capital deployment.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the investment committee member or executive authority who granted final approval for this pro forma.']
  argus_file_reference varchar [note: 'Reference path or document management system identifier for the Argus Enterprise cash flow model file associated with this pro forma. Enables traceability between the pro forma record and the detailed DCF model.']
  construction_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date for substantial construction completion as modeled in the pro forma. Marks the end of the construction period and the beginning of the lease-up or stabilization phase.']
  construction_loan_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected construction loan facility amount in USD. Used to model the Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio, interest carry, and CAPEX draw schedule assumptions in the pro forma.']
  construction_loan_ltv_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Projected Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio for the construction loan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 = 65%). Key lender underwriting metric determining maximum loan proceeds relative to project value.']
  construction_loan_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Projected interest rate on the construction loan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0650 = 6.50%). Drives the interest carry calculation within the pro forma financing cost model.']
  construction_start_date date [note: 'Projected date for construction commencement as modeled in the pro forma. Anchors the construction period interest carry calculation and CAPEX draw schedule timeline.']
  contingency_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted contingency reserve in USD, typically expressed as a percentage of hard and soft costs. Provides a financial buffer for unforeseen construction costs and scope changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pro forma record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls and investment committee governance.']
  development_spread_bps decimal(8,2) [note: 'Development spread in basis points, calculated as yield on cost minus exit cap rate. Quantifies the value creation premium of development over acquisition and is a key investment committee decision metric.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this pro forma version is considered the active financial underwriting basis for the project. Aligns with the start of the modeled investment period.']
  equity_contribution_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total equity capital required for the project in USD, calculated as total project cost less construction loan proceeds. Drives capital call schedules and waterfall distribution modeling.']
  exit_cap_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Projected exit Capitalization Rate (CAP Rate) applied to stabilized NOI to derive the terminal value at disposition. Key assumption in Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) and IRR modeling.']
  financing_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected financing costs in USD, including construction loan interest carry, origination fees, and lender costs. Captured as a distinct cost line in the pro forma for lender underwriting.']
  gp_promote_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'GP sponsor promote percentage above the preferred return hurdle, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20% carried interest). Key term in joint venture and fund waterfall structures.']
  hard_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected hard construction costs (direct costs) in USD, including site work, structural, MEP, and finishes. Primary driver of Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) draw schedules and construction loan sizing.']
  hold_period_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected investment hold period in years from project commencement to disposition. Drives the IRR calculation horizon and Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model period in Argus Enterprise.']
  land_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total land acquisition cost included in the pro forma, expressed in USD. Represents the basis for the site and is a primary input to total project cost and yield-on-cost calculations.']
  perm_loan_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected permanent loan takeout amount in USD upon project stabilization. Represents the refinancing of the construction loan into long-term debt and is a key assumption in the exit/hold pro forma.']
  perm_loan_dscr decimal(6,4) [note: 'Projected Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) for the permanent loan takeout, calculated as stabilized Net Operating Income (NOI) divided by annual debt service. Minimum threshold typically required by lenders.']
  preferred_return_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Preferred return rate for LP investors in the waterfall distribution model, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.08 = 8%). LP investors receive this return before GP promote participation.']
  proforma_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the pro forma model, typically including the project name and scenario label (e.g., \'Riverside Tower — Base Case v3\').']
  proforma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the pro forma, used in investment committee packages, lender underwriting submissions, and investor reporting. Typically formatted as PF-[ProjectCode]-[Version].']
  proforma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pro forma record. \'approved\' indicates investment committee sign-off; \'superseded\' means a newer version has replaced this one.']
  proforma_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the development pro forma by project strategy. Drives the applicable financial model structure and underwriting assumptions used in Argus Enterprise. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground_up_development|major_capex|value_add|mixed_use|land_acquisition|adaptive_reuse|condo_conversion — promote to reference product]']
  projected_disposition_date date [note: 'Projected date of asset sale or recapitalization at the end of the hold period. Defines the terminal cash flow event in the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and IRR calculation.']
  projected_sale_proceeds_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected gross sale proceeds in USD upon project disposition. Used in for-sale residential or merchant build pro formas as the primary revenue assumption driving IRR and equity multiple calculations.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Financial sensitivity scenario represented by this pro forma version. Investment committees typically require base case, upside, and downside scenarios for go/no-go decisions.']
  soft_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected soft costs in USD, including architecture, engineering, permits, legal, financing fees, and developer overhead. Complements hard costs to form total development cost.']
  stabilization_date date [note: 'Projected date on which the project reaches stabilized occupancy (typically 90–95% leased). Triggers the permanent loan takeout, NOI-based valuation, and exit cap rate application in the pro forma.']
  stabilized_noi_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected stabilized annual Net Operating Income (NOI) in USD upon project completion and lease-up. Primary income metric used to derive cap rate valuation, yield on cost, and permanent loan sizing.']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target equity multiple (e.g., 2.0x) representing total equity distributions divided by total equity invested. Companion return metric to IRR used in waterfall distribution modeling and investor reporting.']
  target_irr_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Target unlevered or levered Internal Rate of Return (IRR) for the project, expressed as a decimal. Primary return hurdle used by the investment committee for go/no-go decisions and investor reporting.']
  total_project_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total all-in development cost in USD, comprising land, hard costs, soft costs, financing costs, and contingency. The denominator for yield-on-cost and the basis for lender underwriting packages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pro forma record was most recently modified. Tracks the evolution of financial assumptions through the project lifecycle and supports version control governance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version label for this pro forma iteration (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Tracks the evolution of financial assumptions through the project lifecycle as actuals replace estimates.']
  waterfall_structure varchar [note: 'Type of equity waterfall distribution structure modeled in the pro forma. Defines how cash flows are allocated between LP investors and GP sponsor across return hurdles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preferred_return|pari_passu|tiered_promote|straight_equity|co_invest|jv_promote — promote to reference product]']
  yield_on_cost_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Projected yield on cost, calculated as stabilized NOI divided by total project cost. Measures the development spread over market cap rates and is a primary go/no-go metric for investment committee review.']

  Note: 'Development pro forma and financial underwriting record for each real estate development project — the financial feasibility model that drives go/no-go investment decisions. Captures land acquisition cost, hard costs, soft costs, financing assumptions (construction loan terms, permanent loan takeout), projected revenue (sale proceeds or stabilized NOI), target IRR, target equity multiple, target yield on cost, development spread, sensitivity scenarios, waterfall distribution assumptions, and approval status. Updated through project lifecycle as actuals replace assumptions. Links to dev_project. SSOT for development financial underwriting within the development domain. Critical for investment committee approvals, lender underwriting packages, and investor reporting.'
}

Table development.project_assignment {
  project_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each project-employee assignment record']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the development project master record']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee master record']
  allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the employee\'s working time allocated to this specific development project, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50% allocation). Used for resource capacity planning, labor cost allocation to project budgets, and workload balancing across multiple concurrent projects.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this project assignment. Values: Active (currently staffed), On Hold (temporarily suspended), Completed (project phase or assignment ended normally), Terminated (assignment ended prematurely). Governs active resource counts and capacity planning.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee\'s assignment to this development project ended or is planned to end. Null indicates an active ongoing assignment. Used for historical staffing records, project closeout documentation, and resource forecasting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee began their assignment to this development project in this specific role. Used for tenure tracking, historical staffing analysis, and project timeline correlation.']
