  accredited_investor_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offering is restricted to accredited investors only as defined by SEC regulations. True = accredited only; False = open to non-accredited.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Primary real estate asset class focus of the offering.']
  carried_interest_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of profits allocated to the general partner after preferred return is met, expressed as a percentage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offering record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which cash distributions are made to investors.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date the offering was filed with the SEC or relevant regulatory authority.']
  final_close_date date [note: 'Date the offering stopped accepting new investor commitments and closed to new capital.']
  geographic_focus varchar [note: 'Primary geographic markets or regions where the offering invests (e.g., US West Coast, Southeast Asia, European Union). Free-text field for flexibility.']
  initial_close_date date [note: 'Date of the first capital close when the offering began accepting investor commitments.']
  investment_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary investment strategy classification defining risk-return profile and asset approach.']
  investment_term_months varchar [note: 'Expected duration of the investment holding period in months from initial close to final liquidation.']
  leverage_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target loan-to-value or debt-to-equity ratio for the portfolio, expressed as a percentage of total capitalization.']
  management_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual management fee charged to the fund as a percentage of committed capital or net asset value.']
  maximum_raise_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum capital the offering is authorized to accept, in USD. May be subject to regulatory caps.']
  minimum_investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum capital commitment required from a single investor to participate in the offering, in USD.']
  minimum_raise_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum capital required to close the offering and commence operations, in USD.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offering record was last modified in the system.']
  offering_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the offerings investment thesis, strategy, and objectives as presented to investors.']
  offering_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the investment offering as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  offering_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the offering, used in investor communications and regulatory filings.']
  offering_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offering. Draft = under preparation; Filed = submitted to regulators; Open = accepting investments; Closed = no longer accepting new capital; Suspended = temporarily halted; Terminated = cancelled before close; Liquidated = assets distributed and dissolved.']
  offering_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investment vehicle structure. REIT = Real Estate Investment Trust.']
  preferred_return_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annualized preferred return rate (hurdle rate) that limited partners receive before general partner participates in profits, expressed as a percentage.']
  qualified_purchaser_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offering is restricted to qualified purchasers as defined under Investment Company Act Section 3(c)(7). True = qualified purchaser only; False = not restricted.']
  redemption_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether investors are permitted to redeem their interests before the end of the investment term. True = redemptions allowed; False = locked until liquidation.']
  redemption_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for an investor to request redemption of their interest.']
  regulation_exemption varchar [note: 'SEC registration exemption under which the offering is conducted.']
  sec_file_number varchar [note: 'Official SEC file number assigned to the Form D filing for this offering.']
  target_equity_multiple decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected total return multiple on invested equity over the investment period (e.g., 2.0x means investors receive twice their capital).']
  target_irr_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected annualized internal rate of return target communicated to investors, expressed as a percentage.']
  target_raise_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital amount the offering aims to raise from investors, in USD.']
  tax_treatment varchar [note: 'Tax classification of the offering entity determining how income and gains are taxed to investors.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the offering was formally terminated or liquidated and all assets distributed to investors.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for offering. Referenced by offering_id.'
}

  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_contract. Business justification: Warranty work orders executed under construction contract warranty provisions must reference the originating contract to enforce contractor recall obligations, track warranty claim costs against the c']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: Work orders for permitted maintenance activities (electrical panel replacement, structural repairs) must reference the governing construction permit for regulatory compliance and inspection sign-off. ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Work orders are charged to cost centers for property-level OpEx/CapEx tracking, NOI reporting, and budget variance analysis. RE property managers require cost center assignment on every work order to ']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Post-construction warranty work orders and punch-list items are tracked against the originating development project. This link enables warranty period cost tracking, contractor performance reporting, ']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Work orders consume approved maintenance budgets. RE property managers track budget vs. actual spend at the work order level for monthly variance reporting and year-end CAM reconciliation. This link e']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Owner approval threshold enforcement: work orders exceeding the opex_approval_threshold or capex_approval_threshold in the governing owner agreement require owner sign-off. Property management systems']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Land/parcel maintenance work orders (drainage, environmental remediation, fence repair on vacant land) require parcel-level tracking for development-stage assets and land cost basis reporting.']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking facility maintenance (gate repair, EV charger service, lighting) generates work orders that must link to the parking facility for parking-level cost tracking and operating expense reporting.']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial real estate work orders are scoped to specific leasable spaces (suites) for CAM cost allocation and tenant billing. Space-level maintenance cost tracking is required for CAM reconciliation ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Work orders triggered by regulatory mandates (OSHA-required remediation, fire code compliance, ADA upgrades) must be traceable to the governing obligation. Compliance officers use this link to demonst']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which this work order is executed. Enables contract utilization tracking, SLA enforcement, and vendor performance measurement.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.service_request. Business justification: A work order is frequently generated in response to a tenant service request. This FK normalizes the originating service request relationship on the work order, enabling traceability from request subm']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.sla_policy. Business justification: work_order carries denormalized sla_response_target_hours and sla_completion_target_hours. These SLA targets are governed by sla_policy records. A FK to sla_policy normalizes the policy reference and ']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant who submitted or is associated with the service request that generated this work order. Null for landlord-initiated or preventive maintenance work orders.']
  tenant_occupancy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.occupancy. Business justification: Tenant-billable work orders, TI completion verification, and move-out damage assessments must be linked to the specific occupancy. Property managers use this to track maintenance costs per occupancy p']
  termination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.termination. Business justification: Lease termination restoration process: when a lease terminates, multiple work orders are created for space restoration, remediation, and condition assessment. Property managers track termination-spawn']
  triggering_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.inspection. Business justification: Property inspections trigger deficiency-remediation work orders. Linking work orders back to the triggering inspection supports inspection close-out tracking, regulatory compliance reporting, and audi']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential and commercial property management requires unit-level work order history for tenant billing, move-out inspections, and warranty tracking. Unit-level maintenance history is a standard prop']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external contractor or service vendor dispatched to fulfill the work order. Null when work is performed by in-house maintenance staff.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the work order was fully completed and signed off. Used for SLA breach analysis, mean time to repair (MTTR) calculation, and vendor performance reporting.']
  actual_labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'The actual cost of labor incurred to complete the work order, including regular and overtime technician hours multiplied by applicable trade rates. Sourced from time-tracking entries in the CMMS or payroll system.']
  actual_material_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'The actual cost of parts, supplies, and materials consumed to complete the work order. Sourced from inventory consumption records or vendor invoices.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when work physically commenced on site. Compared against scheduled_start_date to measure dispatch punctuality and SLA adherence.']
  actual_total_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total actual cost of the work order combining labor and materials costs. Used for CAPEX/OPEX reporting, GL posting, and budget variance analysis. Equals actual_labor_cost + actual_material_cost plus any additional charges.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the work order as either Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) — costs that extend asset life or add value — or Operating Expenditure (OPEX) — routine maintenance and repair costs. Critical for financial reporting, GL coding, and FASB/IFRS compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was first created in the CMMS. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp and the baseline for SLA response time calculations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this work order (estimated_cost, actual_labor_cost, actual_material_cost, actual_total_cost). Supports multi-currency portfolio operations.']
  estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'The pre-work estimated total cost of the work order including labor and materials, in the local operating currency. Used for budget approval workflows, CAPEX/OPEX forecasting, and purchase order generation.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional work, a follow-up inspection, or a new work order is required after this work order is closed. True = a subsequent work order or inspection should be scheduled.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account code to which the work order costs are posted in the financial system. Enables direct integration with Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA for CAPEX/OPEX cost allocation and financial reporting.']
  labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of technician labor hours expended on the work order, including regular and overtime hours. Used for workforce utilization analysis, trade cost benchmarking, and O&M reporting.']
  location_detail varchar [note: 'Specific location within the property where the work is to be performed (e.g., floor number, suite, room, mechanical room, rooftop). Supplements the property-level reference for precise dispatch and asset tracking.']
  osha_safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order involves activities subject to OSHA safety regulations (e.g., confined space entry, lockout/tagout, working at heights, hazardous materials). True = OSHA-regulated activity requiring safety documentation and compliance sign-off.']
  overtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of overtime labor hours charged to the work order. Separated from regular hours for payroll cost allocation, budget variance analysis, and emergency response cost tracking.']
  permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a building permit, hot work permit, or other regulatory permit is required before work can commence. True = permit must be obtained and documented prior to execution.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance schedule that generated this work order, if applicable. Null for reactive or ad-hoc work orders. Links the work order to the PM program for compliance tracking and schedule adherence reporting.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification assigned to the work order at creation or triage. Drives SLA target times, dispatch sequencing, and escalation rules. Critical typically indicates life-safety or building-critical system failure.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number issued to the vendor for this work order. Links the maintenance activity to the procurement and accounts payable workflow for invoice matching and payment processing.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real-world date and time when the maintenance issue or service request was first reported by the tenant or building staff. May differ from created_timestamp if there is a lag in CMMS entry. The principal business event time for SLA measurement.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who submitted the service request or initiated the work order — may be a tenant contact, property manager, or building engineer. Captured for accountability and tenant relationship management.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing the technician\'s or vendor\'s description of the work performed, root cause identified, parts replaced, and any follow-up actions recommended. Critical for asset maintenance history and recurring issue analysis.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'The planned date by which the work order is expected to be completed. Used as the SLA target date for on-time completion measurement and tenant communication.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned date on which work is scheduled to begin. Used for resource planning, vendor scheduling, and preventive maintenance calendar management.']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order exceeded its SLA completion target. True = SLA was breached; False = completed within SLA. Used for vendor performance scorecards, tenant reporting, and contract penalty triggers.']
  sla_completion_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contractually or operationally defined maximum number of hours within which the work order must be fully completed from the time of reporting. Used for SLA breach detection and vendor penalty calculations.']
  sla_response_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contractually or operationally defined maximum number of hours within which a technician or vendor must respond to the work order, based on priority level and service contract terms.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating CMMS or operational system from which this work order record was sourced. Supports data lineage, multi-system integration reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  tenant_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this work order is billable to the tenant under the terms of their lease (e.g., tenant-caused damage, above-standard services). True = costs to be included in tenant billing or CAM reconciliation.']
  trade_category varchar [note: 'The skilled trade or building systems discipline required to execute the work order (e.g., HVAC, electrical, plumbing). Used for vendor dispatch routing, workforce planning, and trade-level cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|electrical|plumbing|structural|elevator|fire_life_safety|janitorial|landscaping|general — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was most recently modified in the CMMS. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  warranty_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order is being executed under an active equipment or construction warranty. True = costs may be recoverable from the manufacturer or contractor; affects CAPEX/OPEX classification and cost recovery tracking.']
  work_order_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the nature of the maintenance issue, the scope of work required, or the service request details as entered by the requestor or dispatcher.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the work order as assigned by the CMMS (e.g., Building Engines). Used in tenant communications, vendor dispatch, and audit trails.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order. Tracks progression from creation through assignment, active execution, hold, completion, and final closure. Used for SLA breach analysis and operational dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|assigned|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order by its operational nature. Corrective = reactive repair; Preventive = scheduled PM; Emergency = life-safety or critical system failure; Inspection = condition assessment; Capital Improvement = CAPEX project work; Tenant Improvement (TI) = tenant-specific fit-out or modification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrective|preventive|emergency|inspection|capital_improvement|tenant_improvement — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a maintenance work order issued for a property or building asset. Captures the full lifecycle from creation through completion — including work type (corrective, preventive, emergency), priority level, CAPEX vs. OPEX classification, assigned trade/vendor, scheduled and actual completion dates, and safety compliance flags (OSHA, HSE, or equivalent jurisdiction-specific standards). Includes embedded cost line items for labor (technician hours, trade type, hourly rates, overtime) and materials (parts consumed, quantities, unit costs, supplier references). Tracks status transitions as an immutable audit trail for SLA breach analysis and compliance. Sourced from CMMS platforms (e.g., Building Engines, Corrigo, Angus). The primary operational record for all facility maintenance activity.'
}

Table maintenance.service_request {
  service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the tenant service request record. Primary key for the service_request data product in the maintenance domain.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Tenants submit service requests for amenity issues (broken gym equipment, pool closure). Amenity-level request tracking supports amenity operations management and tenant satisfaction KPIs.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Service requests are submitted for a specific building. Building-level request volume, response-time, and tenant satisfaction reporting are standard property management KPIs requiring this link.']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Service requests for common area issues (broken lobby door, elevator malfunction) must link to the specific common area for CAM cost allocation and common area maintenance performance reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal maintenance technician or engineer assigned to fulfill the service request. May be null if the request is dispatched to an external vendor.']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active lease associated with the tenant and suite at the time of the service request. Used for CAM charge-back allocation and lease-level reporting.']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Tenants submit service requests for parking issues (broken gate, damaged space markings). Parking-level request tracking supports parking operations management and tenant satisfaction reporting.']
  primary_service_property_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property (building or complex) where the service request originated. Links to the property master record.']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial tenants submit service requests referencing their specific suite/space. Space-level request tracking supports CAM billing, tenant satisfaction reporting, and lease administration.']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.sla_policy. Business justification: service_request carries denormalized sla_response_target_hours and sla_resolution_target_hours. These SLA targets are governed by sla_policy records. A FK to sla_policy normalizes the policy reference']
  tenant_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant_contact. Business justification: service_request stores tenant_contact_name, tenant_contact_phone, tenant_contact_email as denormalized plain columns. A proper FK to tenant_contact enforces referential integrity, eliminates data dupl']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tenant who submitted the service request. Supports tenant relationship management, SLA tracking, and CAM charge-back attribution.']
  tenant_occupancy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.occupancy. Business justification: Service requests must be tied to an active occupancy for move-in/move-out condition reporting, SLA compliance tracking by occupancy type, and tenant satisfaction scoring. Property managers use this li']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific suite or unit within the property from which the service request was submitted. Enables location-level maintenance tracking and CAM cost allocation.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the property management team or assigned technician formally acknowledged receipt of the service request. Used to measure SLA response time compliance.']
  actual_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred to fulfill the service request upon completion, in the local operating currency. Populated from work order actuals or vendor invoice. Used for CAM reconciliation, OPEX/CAPEX reporting, and budget variance analysis.']
  cam_chargeable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of fulfilling this service request is eligible for CAM charge-back to the tenant under the terms of their lease (True = CAM chargeable). Drives accounts receivable and CAM reconciliation processes.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the maintenance activity as either capital expenditure (CAPEX — asset improvement or replacement) or operating expenditure (OPEX — routine repair and maintenance). Critical for financial reporting, fixed asset accounting, and tax treatment.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally closed after tenant confirmation or automatic closure policy. May differ from resolved_timestamp if a tenant sign-off or satisfaction confirmation period applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request record was first created in the system. May differ from submitted_timestamp if the record was entered retrospectively by property management staff.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the estimated_cost and actual_cost fields (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio operations.']
  estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to fulfill the service request, in the local operating currency. Used for budget pre-approval workflows, CAPEX vs OPEX classification, and CAM charge-back estimation.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Floor or level within the building where the service request issue is located. Used for technician dispatch routing and building-level maintenance analytics.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  location_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific location within the suite or building where the issue is occurring (e.g., \'northwest corner office\', \'men\'s restroom 3rd floor\', \'server room\'). Supplements the suite_id with precise on-site location context.']
  osha_safety_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service request involves a safety hazard or OSHA-reportable condition (True = safety concern present). Triggers mandatory safety protocols, escalation procedures, and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request is a recurrence of a previously reported issue at the same location or for the same asset (True = recurring issue). Supports root cause analysis, asset lifecycle decisions, and preventive maintenance program adjustments.']
  request_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the service request by building system or trade (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical, janitorial, access control). Drives routing to the appropriate technician or vendor and supports CAM charge-back categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|plumbing|electrical|janitorial|access|structural|elevator|fire_safety|pest_control|general — promote to reference product]']
  request_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the tenant describing the issue, fault, or service needed. Captures the tenant\'s own words for context and is used by technicians to prepare for the job.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the service request at submission (e.g., SR-2024-000123). Used by tenants and property management staff to track and reference the request across communications and portals.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service request workflow. Tracks progression from tenant submission through resolution and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|acknowledged|in_progress|on_hold|resolved|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  request_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular detail within the request category (e.g., within HVAC: thermostat, air handler, ductwork; within plumbing: leak, drain, fixture). Supports detailed maintenance analytics and vendor specialization routing.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the technician or property manager describing the work performed, root cause identified, and resolution achieved. Supports maintenance history and recurring issue analysis.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request issue was fully resolved and the work was completed. Used to calculate actual resolution time against SLA resolution target.']
  sla_resolution_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA resolution target was breached for this service request (True = breach occurred). Populated at closure. Supports SLA compliance reporting and potential lease penalty calculations.']
  sla_resolution_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the service request must be fully resolved, based on urgency level and lease SLA terms. Distinct from response target — this measures full closure.']
  sla_response_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA response target was breached for this service request (True = breach occurred). Populated at acknowledgement or closure. Supports SLA compliance reporting and tenant relationship management.']
  sla_response_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the property management team must acknowledge or respond to the service request, based on urgency level and lease SLA terms.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this service request record in the originating operational system (Building Engines CMMS). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system of record.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the tenant submitted the service request (e.g., tenant portal, mobile app, help desk call, email). Supports channel analytics and tenant engagement reporting.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tenant submitted the service request through the portal, help desk, or mobile app. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor for all SLA calculations.']
  tenant_feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the tenant in the post-resolution satisfaction survey. Captures qualitative feedback for property management quality improvement and tenant retention analysis.']
  tenant_satisfaction_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric satisfaction rating provided by the tenant upon closure of the service request, typically on a 1–5 or 1–10 scale. Supports tenant relationship management KPIs and property management performance reviews.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Tenant-declared or property-manager-assessed urgency of the service request. Determines SLA tier assignment and dispatch prioritization. Emergency requests may trigger immediate vendor dispatch and OSHA safety protocols.']
  work_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of work orders spawned from this service request. A single service request may generate multiple work orders for different trades or vendors. Supports complexity analysis and operational reporting.']
  work_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical work or active remediation on the service request commenced. Marks the transition from dispatched/acknowledged to in-progress status.']

  Note: 'Tenant-initiated service request submitted through a tenant portal, help desk, or mobile app. Captures the originating tenant, property, suite, request category (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, janitorial, access), description, urgency, submission timestamp, SLA target response and resolution times, and current status. Distinct from a work order — a service request is the tenant-facing intake record that may spawn one or more work orders. Supports tenant relationship management and CAM charge-back tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.pm_schedule {
  pm_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule record. Primary key for the pm_schedule data product in the maintenance domain.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity PM schedules (pool chemical checks, gym equipment inspection, seasonal opening/closing) are organized by amenity. Amenity-level PM compliance supports liability management and tenant satisfact']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: PM schedules are often defined for specific building equipment assets (HVAC units, elevators, fire suppression systems, etc.) in addition to property-level assets. The pm_schedule table currently only']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules are organized by building (e.g., HVAC PM for Building A). Building-level PM compliance reporting and certificate tracking are standard property management KPIs.']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Common area PM schedules (cleaning, fire safety checks, HVAC) are organized by common area. Common area-level PM compliance reporting supports CAM cost justification and regulatory compliance.']
  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: LEED O+M and BREEAM In-Use certifications require specific maintenance protocols (filter changes, commissioning, water treatment). Sustainability managers link PM schedules to the green certification ']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contracted vendor or service provider assigned to execute this PM schedule. Used for vendor dispatch, service contract linkage, and AP processing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PM schedules carry estimated costs budgeted by cost center for annual maintenance planning. RE property managers use cost center assignment on PM schedules for maintenance budget preparation, CAM expe']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Preventive maintenance schedules are established during project handover from development to operations. Linking pm_schedule to dev_project enables warranty-period PM compliance tracking and ensures m']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal facilities technician or engineer assigned as the primary responsible party for executing this PM schedule. Used for workforce scheduling and accountability tracking.']
  last_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recently generated and closed work order for this PM schedule. Provides a direct link to the last execution record for audit and compliance verification.']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking facility PM schedules (sweeping, lighting checks, gate maintenance) are organized by parking facility. Parking-level PM compliance is standard in property operations management.']
  primary_pm_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the building asset or equipment item this PM schedule governs (e.g., HVAC unit, elevator, fire panel, generator). Links to the asset master in the Fixed Assets or CMMS system.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: PM schedules for regulated equipment (elevators, boilers, fire suppression) are mandated by specific regulatory obligations. Compliance officers link PM schedules to obligations to verify maintenance ']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor service contract under which this PM schedule is executed. Links PM activities to contract terms, SLAs, and AP payment obligations.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential PM schedules (annual smoke detector checks, HVAC filter replacements per unit) are scoped to specific units. Unit-level PM compliance tracking is required for habitability regulations and ']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the next_due_date that the CMMS should generate the work order or send a notification alert. Allows time for parts procurement, vendor scheduling, and tenant notification.']
  asset_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the building system or equipment category this PM schedule covers (e.g., HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, Fire & Life Safety, Elevator, BMS, Generator, Roofing). Supports portfolio-level maintenance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|Electrical|Plumbing|Fire & Life Safety|Elevator|BMS|Generator|Roofing|Structural — promote to reference product]']
  breeam_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule contributes to BREEAM certification maintenance for the property. Supports international green building compliance tracking alongside LEED.']
  building_floor_level varchar [note: 'Floor or level within the building where the asset subject to this PM schedule is located (e.g., \'B1\', \'L3\', \'Roof\'). Supports technician navigation and spatial maintenance reporting.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM schedule is mandated by a regulatory, safety, or warranty compliance requirement (OSHA, HSE, LEED, manufacturer warranty). True = compliance-driven; False = discretionary maintenance.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the maintenance activity as CAPEX (capital expenditure — extends asset life or adds value) or OPEX (operating expenditure — routine maintenance). Critical for financial reporting, GL coding, and REIT compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PM schedule record was first created in the CMMS. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this PM schedule expires or is decommissioned. Null for open-ended schedules. Used to sunset PM programs when assets are retired or service contracts expire.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this PM schedule becomes active and begins generating work orders. Typically aligned with asset commissioning date, lease commencement, or service contract start.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG classification of the PM activity indicating its environmental impact category. Used for sustainability reporting, LEED compliance, and GRESB (Global Real Estate Sustainability Benchmark) submissions.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of labor per PM execution in the local currency. Used for OPEX budgeting, CAPEX vs. OPEX classification, and service contract benchmarking.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete one execution of this PM task. Used for workforce scheduling, OPEX budgeting, and service contract cost estimation.']
  estimated_parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of materials and replacement parts required per PM execution. Used for OPEX/CAPEX budgeting and procurement planning.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric multiplier applied to the frequency_type to define the exact recurrence (e.g., frequency_type=\'weekly\' with frequency_interval=2 means every 2 weeks). Allows sub-type granularity beyond standard frequency buckets.']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'Recurrence interval for the PM schedule defining how often the maintenance task must be performed. Drives automated work order generation cadence in the CMMS.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which PM-related costs are posted. Ensures correct financial classification and supports OPEX/CAPEX reporting in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal inspection report or third-party certification is required upon completion of this PM task (e.g., elevator annual inspection certificate, fire suppression test report).']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the PM task was fully executed and closed. Used to calculate next_due_date and to assess compliance with the maintenance schedule.']
  leed_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM task is required to maintain LEED certification for the property. Supports ESG reporting and green building compliance tracking.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed physical location description of the asset within the building (e.g., \'Mechanical Room 3B, East Wall\', \'Rooftop AHU-7 adjacent to cooling tower\'). Aids technician dispatch and asset identification.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The next calendar date on which a work order must be generated and the PM task executed. Recalculated automatically after each completion. Critical for scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information relevant to this PM schedule (e.g., \'Coordinate with tenant 48 hours in advance\', \'Requires roof access key from security\').']
  osha_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM task is specifically required under OSHA regulations (e.g., lockout/tagout, confined space, fire suppression). Drives safety compliance reporting and audit trails.']
  permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a municipal or regulatory permit is required before executing this PM task (e.g., hot work permit, confined space entry permit, electrical work permit). Supports OSHA and local code compliance.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this PM schedule, used to triage scheduling conflicts and resource allocation. \'critical\' typically applies to life-safety systems (fire suppression, elevators, emergency generators).']
  required_parts_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of parts, materials, and consumables required to execute this PM task (e.g., \'MERV-13 filter 20x20x2, belt drive V-belt, lubricant grease\'). Used for inventory pre-staging and procurement.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the PM schedule (e.g., \'Annual HVAC Filter Replacement – Tower A\', \'Monthly Fire Suppression Inspection\'). Used for display in CMMS dashboards and reporting.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the PM schedule, used in work order generation, vendor communications, and CMMS references. Sourced from Building Engines PM module.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the PM schedule. \'active\' means the schedule is generating work orders; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused (e.g., asset offline); \'archived\' means decommissioned.']
  task_checklist_template varchar [note: 'Name or code reference to the standardized task checklist template used for this PM schedule. The template defines the step-by-step inspection and maintenance procedures to be followed during execution.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the maintenance work to be performed under this PM schedule. Includes scope of work, inspection points, and any special instructions for the assigned technician or vendor.']
  trade_category varchar [note: 'The skilled trade or discipline responsible for executing this PM (e.g., HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, Fire & Life Safety, Elevator, Roofing, Landscaping, Janitorial). Drives technician assignment and vendor dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|Electrical|Plumbing|Fire & Life Safety|Elevator|Roofing|Landscaping|Janitorial|General — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PM schedule record. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  warranty_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM task is required to maintain the manufacturer\'s equipment warranty. Failure to execute may void warranty coverage and expose the organization to uninsured repair costs.']
  work_order_auto_generate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CMMS should automatically generate a work order when the next_due_date is reached. If False, a facilities manager must manually trigger work order creation.']

  Note: 'Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule master record defining the recurring maintenance program for a building asset or equipment item. Stores the PM frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, annual), task checklist template, assigned trade category, estimated labor hours, required parts, next due date, last completed date, and compliance flag (OSHA/HSE, LEED, manufacturer warranty). Drives automated work order generation in the CMMS. Distinct from a work order — this is the standing schedule, not the execution record.'
}

Table maintenance.building_asset {
  building_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical building equipment or infrastructure asset record in the maintenance master registry. Primary key for the building_asset data product.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity-specific assets (pool pump, gym equipment, AV system in conference room) are tracked against specific amenities. Amenity asset register supports replacement planning and amenity operating cost']
  as_built_design_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.design_document. Business justification: Building assets must be linked to their as-built design documents for O&M manual compliance, equipment replacement specifications, and regulatory inspections. Real estate facility managers routinely r']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or building where this asset is physically installed and maintained. Links the asset to its parent facility in the property master.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Building assets (HVAC units, elevators, fire panels) are physically located in a specific building. Building-level asset register, replacement cost reporting, and capital planning require this link. b']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: building_asset.gl_asset_account_code is a denormalized reference to the chart_of_accounts GL asset account. A validated FK supports fixed asset capitalization entries, depreciation posting, and asset ']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Common area assets (lobby HVAC, corridor lighting, elevator) are tracked against specific common areas. CAM cost allocation and asset replacement planning require linking building assets to common are']
  construction_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_contract. Business justification: Building assets carry labor warranties tied to the construction contract under which they were installed. Linking building_asset to construction_contract enables warranty claim processing, contractor ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Building assets are assigned to cost centers for depreciation allocation and maintenance cost tracking. RE controllers require cost center on building assets for depreciation posting, capital expendit']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Building assets (HVAC, elevators, fire suppression) are installed during development projects. Linking building_asset to dev_project enables warranty period tracking, as-built documentation retrieval,']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Every capitalized building asset has a corresponding fixed asset record for depreciation and book value tracking. RE asset managers require the physical-to-financial asset link for depreciation schedu']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking-specific assets (gate motors, EV chargers, lighting systems) are tracked against the parking facility. Parking asset register and maintenance cost reporting require this link for capital plann']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial spaces may have dedicated assets (tenant-specific HVAC zone, dedicated electrical panel). Space-level asset tracking supports TI cost allocation, CAM reconciliation, and lease-end asset dis']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Building assets (elevators, boilers, fire suppression systems, pressure vessels) are subject to specific regulatory obligations (annual certifications, operating permits). Compliance officers maintain']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-specific assets (in-unit HVAC, water heater, appliances in residential) are tracked at the unit level. Unit-level asset register is required for tenant move-out inspections, warranty tracking, an']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping of the asset type used for portfolio-level O&M reporting and CAPEX planning (e.g., HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, Vertical Transportation, Fire Life Safety, Roofing, Structural, Generator, Building Envelope). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|Electrical|Plumbing|Vertical Transportation|Fire Life Safety|Roofing|Structural|Generator|Building Envelope|Other — promote to reference product]']
  asset_criticality varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the asset based on its impact on building operations, tenant services, and life safety if it fails. \'critical\' = life safety or total building operations impact; \'high\' = major tenant disruption; \'medium\' = partial service impact; \'low\' = minimal operational impact. Drives work order priority and PM frequency.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the asset. \'active\' = in normal service; \'inactive\' = temporarily out of service; \'decommissioned\' = permanently removed; \'under_repair\' = currently being serviced; \'pending_replacement\' = approved for capital replacement.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Externally-visible physical label or barcode affixed to the asset for field identification during inspections, work orders, and preventive maintenance rounds. Unique within a property. Sourced from Building Engines asset registry.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific equipment type within the asset category (e.g., \'Air Handling Unit\', \'Chiller\', \'Boiler\', \'Fire Suppression Sprinkler System\', \'Hydraulic Elevator\', \'Standby Generator\'). Drives preventive maintenance task templates in Building Engines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product given high cardinality]']
  bim_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique object identifier linking this asset to its corresponding component in the Building Information Modeling (BIM) model (e.g., Revit GUID or IFC GlobalId). Enables spatial visualization, clash detection, and digital twin integration.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification indicating whether maintenance and replacement activities for this asset are capitalized (CAPEX) or expensed (OPEX) per FASB ASC 360 and internal accounting policy. \'mixed\' applies to assets with both capital and operating components (e.g., partial HVAC overhaul).']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical condition rating of the asset as assessed during the most recent condition assessment. Drives prioritization of CAPEX replacement and preventive maintenance frequency. Scale: excellent=new/like-new; good=minor wear; fair=moderate wear; poor=significant deterioration; critical=imminent failure risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset record was first created in the Building Engines CMMS and ingested into the maintenance domain data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method applied to this asset for financial reporting purposes. \'straight_line\' is most common for real estate equipment. Aligns with Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA asset accounting configuration.']
  energy_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-specified energy efficiency rating or metric for the asset (e.g., SEER rating for HVAC, EER, COP, kW/ton). Null if not applicable. Used for ESG reporting, LEED documentation, and energy benchmarking under EPA ENERGY STAR.']
  energy_rated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset has an energy efficiency rating or certification (e.g., ENERGY STAR, LEED-qualifying equipment). Supports ESG reporting, LEED certification tracking, and sustainability benchmarking.']
  extended_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which any purchased extended warranty coverage expires. Null if no extended warranty is in place. Used alongside manufacturer warranty to determine warranty claim eligibility for repair work orders.']
  floor_location varchar [note: 'Floor or level within the building where the asset is physically located (e.g., \'B1\', \'Ground\', \'3\', \'Rooftop\', \'Mechanical Room 2\'). Used for field technician dispatch and Building Information Modeling (BIM) spatial reference.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the asset was physically installed and commissioned at the property. Anchors useful life calculations, warranty start dates, and CAPEX replacement year projections.']
  is_tenant_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is owned by a tenant rather than the property owner. Tenant-owned assets are tracked for maintenance coordination but excluded from owner CAPEX planning and depreciation schedules. Relevant for Tenant Improvement (TI) assets.']
  labor_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the installation contractor\'s labor warranty expires. Covers defects in workmanship separate from equipment manufacturer warranty. Null if no labor warranty applies.']
  last_condition_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal property condition assessment (PCA) or engineering inspection that produced the current condition rating. Indicates data freshness for capital planning purposes.']
  last_pm_date date [note: 'Date the most recent preventive maintenance (PM) service was completed on this asset. Used to calculate next scheduled PM date and identify overdue maintenance items.']
  last_regulatory_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent mandatory regulatory inspection or certification for this asset. Null if regulatory_inspection_required is false. Used for compliance reporting and COO maintenance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this asset record in the source system or data product. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  last_warranty_claim_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim submitted for this asset. Null if no warranty claims have been filed. Used to track warranty utilization and vendor responsiveness.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the asset. Used for warranty claims, spare parts procurement, and vendor dispatch coordination.']
  manufacturer_warranty_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) warranty expires. After this date, repair costs are no longer recoverable from the manufacturer. Critical for work order cost routing decisions.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned model or part number for the asset. Required for ordering replacement parts, scheduling OEM-certified service, and cross-referencing BIM component libraries.']
  next_pm_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the next preventive maintenance (PM) service on this asset, calculated from last PM date and PM schedule frequency. Drives work order scheduling and compliance reporting in Building Engines.']
  pm_schedule_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of preventive maintenance (PM) inspections and servicing for this asset as defined in the O&M program. Drives automated PM work order generation in Building Engines CMMS.']
  purchase_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original acquisition cost of the asset in USD at time of purchase or installation. Used as the cost basis for fixed asset depreciation schedules in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA Fixed Assets module.']
  recommended_replacement_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the asset is recommended for capital replacement based on condition assessment findings and remaining useful life estimate. Used to populate CAPEX schedules in Argus Enterprise and SAP S/4HANA project systems.']
  refrigerant_type varchar [note: 'Type of refrigerant used in HVAC or cooling equipment (e.g., R-410A, R-22, R-134a, R-32). Null for non-refrigerant assets. Required for EPA Section 608 refrigerant management compliance and ESG greenhouse gas reporting.']
  regulatory_inspection_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the current regulatory inspection certificate or permit expires. Null if not applicable. Expiry triggers mandatory re-inspection scheduling to maintain compliance and avoid operational shutdowns.']
  regulatory_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset is subject to mandatory regulatory inspection and certification (e.g., elevators, boilers, fire suppression systems, pressure vessels). True triggers compliance tracking and certificate of occupancy (COO) dependencies.']
  remaining_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of years remaining before the asset requires capital replacement, as determined by the most recent condition assessment. Decimal precision supports mid-year estimates. Key input for CAPEX reserve modeling and Argus Enterprise cash flow projections.']
  replacement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current estimated cost in USD to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit, inclusive of equipment, labor, and installation. Used for CAPEX budgeting, insurance valuation, and reserve fund analysis. Expressed in base currency (USD).']
  room_or_zone varchar [note: 'Specific room number, mechanical room designation, or building zone where the asset is installed (e.g., \'MER-301\', \'Penthouse Mechanical\', \'Zone A West Wing\'). Supports precise technician navigation and BIM spatial coordination.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the specific asset unit. Required for warranty registration, insurance claims, and regulatory compliance inspections (e.g., elevator certificates, boiler permits).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native asset identifier from the originating source system (Building Engines CMMS). Preserves the upstream system key for reconciliation, lineage tracing, and re-ingestion deduplication in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or engineering-estimated useful life of the asset in years from installation date. Used for depreciation schedules (FASB ASC 360), CAPEX reserve planning, and remaining useful life calculations.']
  warranty_claim_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed against any warranty (manufacturer, extended, or labor) for this asset since installation. Indicates reliability history and supports vendor performance evaluation.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all physical building equipment and infrastructure assets subject to maintenance management — HVAC units, elevators, boilers, generators, fire suppression systems, electrical panels, plumbing systems, roofing, and other O&M-tracked components. Captures asset tag, manufacturer, model, serial number, installation date, expected useful life, replacement cost, current condition rating, and BIM reference ID. Includes warranty records (manufacturer, extended, labor warranties with coverage dates and claim history) and periodic condition assessment history (condition ratings, remaining useful life estimates, recommended capital replacement year). SSOT for maintainable building equipment within the maintenance domain.'
}

Table maintenance.vendor_dispatch {
  vendor_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each vendor dispatch record. Primary key for the vendor_dispatch data product in the maintenance domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the vendor dispatch is performed. Used for CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation and property-level maintenance cost allocation.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Vendor dispatches carry a gl_account_code for expense classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for AP invoice processing, CAM recoverability determination, and OpE']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor dispatches generate costs allocated to cost centers for property-level expense tracking and CAM pool management. RE property accountants require cost center on vendor dispatches for AP invoice ']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active service contract under which this dispatch is executed. Enables SLA validation and contract compliance tracking.']
  signoff_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Vendor work sign-off by a named employee (property manager, chief engineer) is required for invoice approval, vendor performance audits, and liability documentation in real estate operations. \'signoff']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.sla_policy. Business justification: vendor_dispatch carries denormalized sla_response_target_hours and sla_resolution_target_hours. These SLA targets are governed by sla_policy records. A FK to sla_policy normalizes the policy reference']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order that triggered this vendor dispatch. Multiple dispatches may exist per work order (e.g., return visits, multi-trade coordination).']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the vendor technician physically arrived on-site at the property. Compared against estimated arrival window to measure SLA on-time arrival performance.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual invoiced or confirmed cost of the vendor dispatch upon completion. Compared against estimated cost for budget variance reporting and vendor cost performance analysis.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the vendor technician departed the property after completing or suspending work. Used to calculate on-site duration and supports OSHA site safety records.']
  building_access_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor requires escorted or key-controlled building access to perform the work. Drives property operations coordination and tenant notification requirements.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the maintenance expenditure as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operating Expenditure (OPEX). Drives GL (General Ledger) coding, asset capitalization, and financial reporting under FASB ASC 360.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the vendor confirmed all work was completed and the dispatch was closed. Used for SLA resolution-time measurement and work order closure workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this dispatch record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting for international real estate portfolios.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable dispatch reference number used in vendor communications, purchase orders, and invoice matching. Format: DSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  dispatch_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this dispatch within the parent work order (e.g., 1 for initial visit, 2 for return visit). Supports multi-trade coordination tracking and return visit analysis.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor dispatch. Tracks progression from initial dispatch through on-site work completion or cancellation. Used for SLA monitoring and vendor performance reporting.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the vendor or contractor was formally dispatched to the property. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA response-time measurement.']
  estimated_arrival_end timestamp [note: 'End of the estimated arrival window communicated to the property manager and tenant at time of dispatch. Together with estimated_arrival_start defines the committed arrival window for SLA tracking.']
  estimated_arrival_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the estimated arrival window communicated to the property manager and tenant at time of dispatch. Used for SLA compliance measurement and tenant communication.']
  estimated_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of the vendor dispatch at time of scheduling, in the property\'s operating currency. Used for budget variance analysis and OPEX/CAPEX forecasting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL (General Ledger) account code to which the dispatch cost is posted. Drives financial reporting, CAPEX/OPEX segregation, and property-level P&L allocation in Yardi Voyager.']
  is_cam_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this dispatch is billable to tenants as part of CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation under NNN (Triple Net Lease) or gross lease structures.']
  is_return_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch is a return visit to address incomplete or unsatisfactory work from a prior dispatch on the same work order. Used for vendor quality and rework rate analysis.']
  parts_used_description varchar [note: 'Description of parts, materials, or consumables used during the dispatch. Supports CAPEX vs. OPEX classification, warranty tracking, and invoice reconciliation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Urgency classification assigned to the dispatch at the time of creation. Determines SLA response and resolution time targets and drives vendor dispatch sequencing.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number issued to the vendor for this dispatch. Enables three-way matching in AP and ensures procurement compliance for maintenance expenditures.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the vendor dispatch is scheduled to occur. Used for day-level planning, preventive maintenance scheduling, and property operations calendaring.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the maintenance service type. Drives CAPEX vs. OPEX accounting treatment and CAM (Common Area Maintenance) billability determination. Preventive and corrective map to OPEX; capital improvement maps to CAPEX.']
  signoff_status varchar [note: 'Status of the work completion sign-off by the property manager or authorized representative. Required for invoice approval and work order closure. Disputed status triggers a return visit or escalation.']
  signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the authorized representative signed off on the completed dispatch work. Triggers invoice approval workflow and work order closure in Building Engines.']
  sla_resolution_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor met the contracted SLA resolution time target (work completed within sla_resolution_target_hours of dispatch). Populated upon dispatch completion. Drives vendor performance scorecarding.']
  sla_resolution_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted SLA (Service Level Agreement) target for total resolution time in hours from dispatch to work completion, as defined in the service contract. Supports vendor performance scorecarding.']
  sla_response_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor met the contracted SLA response time target (arrival within sla_response_target_hours of dispatch). Populated upon actual arrival. Drives vendor performance KPI reporting.']
  sla_response_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted SLA (Service Level Agreement) target for vendor response time in hours from dispatch to on-site arrival, as defined in the service contract. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (Building Engines CMMS). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  tenant_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant must be notified prior to the vendor accessing the leased premises. Driven by lease terms, building rules, and tenant service request origin.']
  trade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trade or technical discipline of the dispatched vendor (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical, elevator, janitorial, roofing, fire safety). Used for multi-trade coordination and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|plumbing|electrical|elevator|fire_safety|roofing|janitorial|structural|landscaping|pest_control — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispatch record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual technician or field representative dispatched on behalf of the vendor. Used for on-site sign-off and OSHA safety compliance records.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or direct phone number of the dispatched technician or vendor representative for on-site coordination and emergency contact.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the vendor or contractor dispatched. Captured at dispatch time for historical accuracy independent of vendor master updates.']
  work_performed_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the work performed by the vendor during this dispatch. Captured by the technician or property manager at completion. Supports maintenance history, asset condition tracking, and audit documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a vendor or contractor dispatched to a property to fulfill a work order. Captures the dispatched vendor, dispatch timestamp, estimated arrival window, actual arrival and departure times, work performed summary, sign-off status, and invoice reference. Tracks vendor performance against SLA commitments and supports CAM reconciliation for billable maintenance costs. Multiple dispatches may occur per work order (e.g., return visits, multi-trade coordination).'
}

Table maintenance.service_contract {
  service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the maintenance service contract record. Primary key for the service_contract data product in the maintenance domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary property covered by this service contract. For multi-property contracts, this represents the lead or billing property; additional covered properties are tracked in a separate association table.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Service contracts (elevator maintenance, HVAC service, janitorial) are scoped to specific buildings. Building-level contract management, cost reporting, and vendor performance tracking are standard pr']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Service contracts carry a gl_account_code for expense classification and CAM recoverability determination. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts supports AP invoice coding, CAM reconciliation, and FASB ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service contracts represent committed annual costs allocated to cost centers for property-level expense budgeting and CAM pool management. RE controllers require cost center assignment on service cont']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Service contracts are approved against annual maintenance budgets. RE asset managers track contracted spend vs. budget for each property. This link supports budget commitment tracking, annual budget p']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Service contracts for regulated equipment (elevators, boilers, fire suppression) are required by regulatory obligations. Compliance officers verifying that mandatory service agreements are in place li']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or service provider party responsible for delivering services under this contract (e.g., HVAC contractor, janitorial company, elevator service firm).']
  annual_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annualized monetary value of the service contract in the operating currency. Used for OPEX/CAPEX budgeting, vendor spend analytics, and portfolio-level O&M cost reporting. Expressed in the property\'s functional currency.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at expiration unless notice of termination is provided within the required notice period. Critical for contract management and avoiding unintended renewals.']
  building_engines_contract_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this contract record in Building Engines CMMS. Used for data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and bi-directional sync between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification of the contract spend as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or Mixed. Drives GL account coding in Yardi and SAP S/4HANA. Critical for NOI calculation, asset capitalization, and REIT financial reporting under FASB and IFRS.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'The date on which the service contract becomes effective and the vendor is authorized to begin providing services. Aligns with FASB ASC 842 and IFRS 16 contract start date concepts for service agreement accounting.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL or document management system path to the fully executed contract document. Provides direct access to the contract for legal review, audit, and compliance purposes.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the service contract (e.g., \'Annual HVAC Maintenance Agreement — Tower A\', \'Janitorial Services Contract — Portfolio Wide\'). Used for display and search in Building Engines and Yardi.']
  contract_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, exceptions, or amendments not captured in structured fields. Used by property managers and asset managers for contract administration context.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned at execution. Used for vendor correspondence, purchase orders, and audit trails. Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Building Engines contract module.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service contract. Drives operational workflows: active contracts authorize vendor dispatch; expired/terminated contracts block new work orders against this contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service contract by the type of facility service covered. Drives routing to the appropriate operations team and CAPEX/OPEX classification rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|elevator|janitorial|landscaping|pest_control|security|fire_life_safety|plumbing|electrical|roofing|general_maintenance|other — promote to reference product]']
  covered_equipment_description varchar [note: 'Description of specific building equipment, systems, or assets covered under this contract (e.g., \'All rooftop HVAC units — Building A and B\', \'Passenger elevators 1-4\'). Supports asset lifecycle tracking and BIM integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service contract record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this contract (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting for international real estate portfolios.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'Unique envelope identifier from DocuSign CLM for the executed contract document. Enables direct retrieval of the signed contract document and audit trail of digital signatures.']
  esg_sustainability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract includes ESG or green building sustainability requirements (e.g., use of eco-friendly cleaning products, LEED-compliant HVAC maintenance practices, EPA-compliant pest control methods).']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties signed and the contract was fully executed. May differ from commencement date. Sourced from DocuSign CLM digital signature completion timestamp.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the service contract term ends and the vendor\'s obligation to provide services ceases, absent renewal. Nullable for evergreen/open-ended contracts. Critical for renewal management and WALE (Weighted Average Lease Expiry) analogous tracking.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which contract costs are posted in the financial system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Ensures correct cost allocation for NOI reporting, budget variance analysis, and REIT financial statements.']
  insurance_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s current certificate of insurance (COI) on file. Triggers compliance alerts when insurance is approaching expiration to prevent lapsed coverage during active contract period.']
