  insurance_liability_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount (in contract currency) that the vendor must maintain throughout the contract term. Used for risk management and compliance verification.']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is contractually required to provide a certificate of insurance (COI) as a condition of performing services. Supports OSHA compliance and risk management workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this service contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer pipeline and SOX audit trail.']
  lien_waiver_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide a lien waiver upon payment as a condition of this contract. Relevant for CAPEX-classified contracts and construction-adjacent maintenance work to protect property title.']
  multi_property_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service contract covers multiple properties in the portfolio (e.g., a portfolio-wide janitorial contract). When true, additional covered properties are tracked in a separate association table.']
  osha_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must comply with specific OSHA safety standards as a condition of this contract (e.g., confined space entry, lockout/tagout, fall protection). Drives safety documentation requirements in Building Engines.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the vendor invoices and payment is due under the contract terms. Drives AP (Accounts Payable) accrual schedules in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  performance_bond_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is required to provide a performance bond guaranteeing contract completion. Typically required for high-value CAPEX contracts or critical building systems.']
  preventive_maintenance_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of preventive maintenance visits or inspections required under this contract. Drives PM schedule generation in Building Engines CMMS and supports OSHA and equipment warranty compliance.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic or optional renewal period. Used to calculate the extended contract end date upon renewal and for WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) analogous vendor contract duration analytics.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the services covered under this contract, including specific equipment, systems, buildings, or areas included. Defines the scope of work (SOW) for vendor dispatch and work order validation in Building Engines.']
  sla_resolution_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed maximum time in hours from service request submission to full resolution and sign-off. Distinct from response time — measures end-to-end resolution commitment for SLA compliance reporting.']
  sla_response_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractually committed maximum response time in hours from service request submission to vendor on-site arrival for emergency or priority calls. Key SLA metric tracked in Building Engines for vendor performance management.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that run to natural expiration. Distinct from expiration_date — captures unscheduled early termination events.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract or prevent auto-renewal. Drives contract expiry alerts and renewal decision workflows.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early contract termination. Used for vendor performance analytics, procurement strategy, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the service contract over its full term, including all renewal options if exercised. Used for capital planning, vendor spend management, and CAPEX vs. OPEX threshold analysis.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary vendor contact person responsible for this contract — typically the account manager or service coordinator. Used for operational communication and escalation.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct phone number for the vendor\'s primary contract contact. Used for emergency dispatch and SLA escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.']
  vendor_contract_reference varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s own internal contract or purchase order reference number for this agreement. Enables cross-referencing between the property manager\'s system and the vendor\'s billing system.']

  Note: 'Master record for a maintenance service contract or vendor agreement governing recurring facility services — HVAC maintenance contracts, elevator service agreements, janitorial contracts, landscaping, pest control, and security. Captures contract number, vendor, covered properties, service scope, contract start/end dates, renewal terms, annual contract value, SLA terms, insurance certificate requirements, and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification. Distinct from individual work orders — this is the standing contractual framework.'
}

Table maintenance.ops_inspection {
  ops_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal property or equipment inspection event record in the maintenance domain. Primary key for the ops_inspection data product.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity inspections (pool safety certification, gym equipment inspection, playground safety) are a distinct inspection category required for liability management and regulatory compliance.']
  brokerage_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.deal. Business justification: Due diligence property inspections are a named phase of commercial real estate transactions. Inspection findings directly affect deal pricing, closing conditions, and remediation obligations. Domain e']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: Formal property inspections (fire safety, elevator certification, HVAC compliance) are performed on specific physical building assets tracked in building_asset. While ops_inspection already links to p']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Operational inspections (fire safety, elevator, boiler) are conducted at the building level. Building-level compliance certificate tracking and regulatory reporting (fire marshal, building department)']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Common area inspections (fire egress, ADA compliance, elevator lobbies) are a distinct inspection category. Linking to common_area supports compliance reporting and CAM cost allocation for remediation']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Operational inspections (elevator, boiler, fire suppression) validate or are required by specific operating permits. A property compliance officer links inspection outcomes to the permit being validat']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Operational inspections with critical deficiencies result in compliance violation citations from regulators. A compliance officer tracking violation abatement needs to link the inspection that trigger']
  construction_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.construction_permit. Business justification: Post-renovation operational inspections (COO compliance, fire safety sign-off) are required by permit conditions. Linking ops_inspection to construction_permit enables permit closeout tracking and reg']
  contractor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party inspection firm or licensed inspector vendor who conducted the inspection. Null when the inspection is performed by internal staff.']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: COO-related and post-construction operational inspections (ops_inspection.coo_compliance_flag) must be traceable to the development project for regulatory closeout. Real estate developers require this']
  environmental_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.environmental_review. Business justification: Operational inspections for properties with known environmental conditions (Phase I/II findings, remediation sites) must reference the environmental review to ensure ongoing monitoring compliance. Rea']
  internal_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal inspection review sign-off by a specific employee is required for regulatory compliance tracking, COO issuance, and LEED/BREEAM certification workflows. \'internal_reviewer_name\' is a denormal']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to brokerage.listing. Business justification: Pre-listing property condition inspections are conducted before a property is listed for sale or lease. Inspection results are disclosed to prospects and affect listing terms and marketing. Domain exp']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking structure inspections (structural integrity, fire safety, ADA compliance) are a distinct inspection category. Compliance certificate tracking for parking facilities requires linking inspection']
  primary_ops_property_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the inspection was conducted. Links to the property master record in the property domain.']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial space inspections (pre-lease condition, TI completion sign-off, post-lease restoration) are conducted at the space level. Space-level inspection records support lease administration and TI ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Operational inspections (fire suppression, elevator, HVAC) are mandated by specific regulatory obligations. Compliance officers need to link each inspection to the governing obligation to track fulfil']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Insurance underwriters require operational inspection records (fire safety, elevator, structural) as inputs to property risk assessments at renewal. Real estate risk managers submit inspection finding']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.service_contract. Business justification: Many formal inspections (elevator certifications, fire suppression checks, HVAC compliance) are performed under the terms of a service contract. Linking ops_inspection to service_contract enables trac']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Unit-level inspections (move-in/move-out, habitability, smoke detector compliance) are standard in residential property management. Housing code regulations require unit-level inspection records.']
  actual_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred to remediate all deficiencies identified in this inspection, recorded upon completion of corrective work orders. Compared against estimated_remediation_cost for budget variance analysis.']
  building_area varchar [note: 'Specific floor, zone, wing, or common area of the building where the inspection was performed (e.g., \'Floor 3 East Wing\', \'Basement Mechanical Room\', \'Rooftop\'). Supports spatial analysis of deficiency patterns.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the remediation costs associated with deficiencies found in this inspection as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or a mix of both. Critical for financial reporting under FASB ASC 360 and internal budgeting.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the compliance certificate or permit issued as a result of a passed inspection (e.g., elevator operating certificate, fire safety certificate). Null if no certificate is issued.']
  coo_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is required to maintain or renew the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) for the property. True when the inspection is a COO-mandated compliance event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the source system (Building Engines). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  critical_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as critical severity — those posing immediate life-safety risk or requiring emergency corrective action. Drives escalation workflows and OSHA reporting obligations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this inspection record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting.']
  deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual deficiency findings identified during the inspection across all severity levels. Provides a summary count for compliance dashboards and trend analysis.']
  esg_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection has ESG reporting relevance (e.g., LEED commissioning, BREEAM assessment, EPA environmental compliance inspection). Supports ESG portfolio reporting for REITs and institutional investors.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in the base currency to remediate all deficiencies identified in this inspection. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting and asset lifecycle planning.']
  inspecting_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection was conducted by internal facility staff, an external third-party inspection firm, or a government/regulatory authority (e.g., fire marshal, building department).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the inspection was conducted. This is the principal real-world business event date for the inspection record.']
  inspection_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection was concluded on-site. Combined with inspection_start_time to derive inspection duration for O&M analytics.']
  inspection_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party inspection company or regulatory agency that conducted the inspection. Null for internal inspections.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes recorded by the inspector or internal reviewer providing additional context, observations, or recommendations not captured in structured fields. Sourced from Building Engines work order comments.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the inspection event, sourced from Building Engines or the inspecting authority. Used for cross-referencing with regulatory filings, certificates, and work orders.']
  inspection_report_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL path to the formal inspection report issued by the inspecting party. Supports document management integration with DocuSign CLM or Building Engines document store.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail outcome of the inspection as determined by the inspecting party. \'Conditional pass\' indicates the property passed subject to minor corrective actions within a defined timeframe.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the areas, systems, or equipment covered by the inspection (e.g., \'All fire suppression systems on floors 1-10\', \'Rooftop HVAC units RTU-01 through RTU-06\'). Sourced from Building Engines work order scope.']
  inspection_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection commenced on-site. Used for duration tracking and scheduling conflict analysis.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection event workflow within Building Engines. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  inspection_subtype varchar [note: 'Further granular classification within the inspection type (e.g., \'Annual Fire Sprinkler Test\', \'Five-Year Elevator Full Load Test\', \'Pre-Occupancy HVAC Commissioning\'). Supports detailed regulatory citation mapping.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection category. Covers fire safety inspections, elevator certifications, HVAC commissioning, roof inspections, ADA/accessibility compliance walkthroughs, and workplace safety audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fire_safety|elevator_certification|hvac_commissioning|roof|ada_accessibility|workplace_safety|environmental|plumbing|electrical|general_property — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_license_number varchar [note: 'Professional license or certification number of the inspector as issued by the relevant regulatory or licensing authority (e.g., state elevator inspector license, fire protection engineer PE number).']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual inspector or lead inspector who conducted the inspection. May be an internal employee or a licensed third-party inspector.']
  leed_breeam_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or credit identifier linking this inspection to a specific LEED or BREEAM certification credit or prerequisite (e.g., \'LEED EA Prerequisite 1 — Fundamental Commissioning\'). Null when not ESG-relevant.']
  major_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as major severity — significant non-conformances requiring corrective action within a defined regulatory or contractual timeframe.']
  minor_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings classified as minor severity — low-risk observations that should be addressed in routine maintenance cycles but do not pose immediate safety or compliance risk.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next recurring inspection of this type must be completed to maintain regulatory compliance and Certificate of Occupancy (COO) obligations.']
  open_deficiency_count varchar [note: 'Number of deficiency findings from this inspection that remain unresolved (not yet remediated or closed). Key metric for tracking open compliance obligations and COO maintenance.']
  osha_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any findings from this inspection trigger mandatory OSHA reporting obligations (e.g., imminent danger, serious violations under 29 CFR 1910). Drives compliance escalation workflows.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal reviewer acknowledged and accepted the inspection report and findings. Marks the start of the internal remediation planning cycle.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the inspection was originally scheduled to occur. Used for maintenance planning and regulatory compliance scheduling.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating Building Engines CMMS system for this inspection event. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection record was most recently modified in the source system (Building Engines). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Record of a formal property or equipment inspection event — fire safety inspections, elevator certifications, HVAC commissioning, roof inspections, ADA/accessibility compliance walkthroughs, and workplace safety audits (OSHA, HSE, or equivalent). Captures inspection type, inspecting party (internal or third-party), inspection date, pass/fail result, and regulatory citation reference. Includes individual deficiency findings with severity classification (critical, major, minor), affected asset or area, recommended corrective action, responsible party, target and actual remediation dates, and remediation work order references. Supports regulatory compliance, certificate of occupancy maintenance obligations, and tracking of open deficiencies through to resolution.'
}

  actual_spend_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenditure incurred on the project to date, sourced from SAP S/4HANA cost collection or Yardi AP postings. Used for budget variance analysis, cash flow forecasting, and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification reporting.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date on which project construction or installation activities actually commenced. Compared against planned start date for schedule variance analysis and contractor performance evaluation.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Level of organizational authority that approved this capital project. Reflects the delegation of authority matrix for CAPEX approvals. Used for SOX controls documentation and audit trail. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_manager|asset_manager|vp_operations|cfo|investment_committee|board — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the capital project received formal investment committee or board approval. Required for SOX financial controls documentation and CAPEX authorization audit trail.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Board or investment committee approved budget for the capital project in the operating currency. Represents the authorized CAPEX spend ceiling. Used for variance analysis against actual spend and DSCR/LTV covenant compliance reporting.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset being created or improved by the capital project. Determines the depreciation method and useful life schedule applied upon capitalization. Aligns with the fixed asset register taxonomy in Yardi and SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building_structure|mechanical_systems|electrical_systems|plumbing_systems|hvac|roof|elevators|parking|it_infrastructure|fit_out — promote to reference product]']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date on which the completed project costs were capitalized and transferred to the fixed asset register in the GL. Marks the start of the depreciation schedule. Critical for FASB ASC 360 compliance and balance sheet accuracy.']
  change_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative value of all approved change orders against the primary construction contract. Added to the original contract value to determine the revised contract sum. Used for budget variance analysis.']
  change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of approved change orders issued against the primary construction contract. Indicates project scope stability and contractor performance. High change order counts may signal design deficiency or scope creep.']
  committed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of purchase orders and contracts committed but not yet invoiced or paid for this project. Represents financial obligations already incurred. Used in cash flow forecasting and budget-to-complete analysis.']
  contract_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted value with the primary general contractor for execution of the capital project. May differ from approved budget due to contingency reserves or owner-direct procurement. Used for commitment tracking and lien exposure management.']
  coo_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Occupancy (COO) was issued by the relevant authority, confirming the completed improvement meets all building code and safety requirements. Required before tenant occupancy and asset capitalization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capital project record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls documentation. Sourced from Building Engines or Procore at project initiation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this project record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio reporting for international real estate portfolios and REIT financial consolidation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting depreciation method to be applied to the capitalized asset upon project completion. Determined by asset category and accounting policy. Straight-line is most common for real estate assets under FASB ASC 360.']
  erp_project_code varchar [note: 'Project code assigned in SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS module) for cost collection, procurement integration, and financial consolidation. Used to reconcile CAPEX spend between the maintenance CMMS and the ERP general ledger.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funds financing the capital project. Determines accounting treatment, loan covenant implications, and investor reporting requirements. Insurance proceeds and tenant reimbursements have distinct revenue recognition rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating_reserves|debt_financing|equity_contribution|insurance_proceeds|tenant_reimbursement|government_grant|refinancing_proceeds — promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA to which CAPEX project costs are posted. Ensures correct balance sheet classification (asset vs. expense) and supports financial consolidation and audit.']
  is_esg_initiative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project is classified as an ESG initiative (e.g., LEED certification upgrade, BREEAM improvement, energy efficiency retrofit, EV charging installation). Supports ESG portfolio reporting and green financing eligibility.']
  is_tenant_improvement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project constitutes a Tenant Improvement (TI) as defined under ASC 842 or IFRS 16. TI projects have distinct lease accounting treatment, amortization rules, and may be partially reimbursed by tenants.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the capital project record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  leed_certification_target varchar [note: 'Target LEED certification level sought for this capital project, if applicable. Relevant for green building upgrades and ESG-linked financing. Null or \'none\' if LEED certification is not a project objective.']
  osha_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capital project has mandatory OSHA safety compliance requirements (e.g., asbestos abatement, fall protection systems, electrical safety upgrades). Triggers safety inspection and documentation workflows.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for substantial completion of the capital project. Used for tenant coordination, COO applications, and fixed asset capitalization planning.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled start date for project construction or installation activities. Used for project scheduling, contractor mobilization planning, and tenant disruption coordination.']
  primary_contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary general contractor or construction firm engaged to execute the capital project. Used for vendor performance tracking, insurance certificate management, and lien waiver documentation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification of the capital project, reflecting urgency, safety risk, regulatory compliance obligation, or strategic importance. Critical projects may involve OSHA safety compliance or COO requirements.']
  procore_project_code varchar [note: 'External project identifier from Procore construction management system. Enables cross-system traceability for RFIs, submittals, change orders, and document control records associated with this CAPEX project.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope of work, business justification, and expected outcome of the capital project. Supports investment committee review, LOI documentation, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive short name for the capital project (e.g., \'HVAC Chiller Replacement — Tower A\', \'Roof Membrane Renewal — Building 3\'). Used in reporting dashboards, budget approvals, and fixed asset registers.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable project identifier assigned at project initiation. Used across Procore, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, and Yardi Fixed Assets for cross-system reference and audit trail. Format: CAPEX-{YYYY}-{NNNNN}.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capital project workflow. Drives approval routing, budget release, and fixed asset capitalization triggers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital project by nature of work. Determines accounting treatment, depreciation schedule, and regulatory reporting category. Tenant Improvement (TI) projects may have separate amortization rules under ASC 842. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_construction|renovation|equipment_replacement|infrastructure_upgrade|tenant_improvement|environmental_remediation|safety_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recent approved revision to the original project budget, reflecting change orders, scope changes, or cost escalations. Null if no revision has been approved. Supports change order management and budget variance reporting.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years, used to calculate the annual depreciation charge upon capitalization. Set at project approval based on asset category and engineering assessment. Aligns with FASB ASC 360 and ISO 55000 asset lifecycle standards.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure project record for major building improvements, equipment replacements, or infrastructure upgrades classified as CAPEX rather than OPEX. Captures project name, description, property reference, asset reference, approved budget, actual spend to date, project start and end dates, funding source, approval authority, ERP project system reference, and capitalization date. Distinct from routine work orders — CAPEX projects involve asset capitalization and fixed asset creation in the GL.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_inventory {
  parts_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each parts inventory record in the maintenance domain. Primary key for the parts_inventory data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property or building at which this parts inventory is stocked. Links the inventory record to a specific facility in the real estate portfolio. A null value may indicate a central warehouse not tied to a single property.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: Parts inventory records are often stocked specifically for particular building assets — critical spare parts for a specific HVAC unit, elevator components, or generator parts. Linking parts_inventory ']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Parts inventory carries a gl_account_code for inventory asset and expense classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for parts issuance journal entries, inventory va']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Parts inventory is tracked by cost center for inventory valuation and maintenance expense allocation. RE property managers require cost center on parts inventory for inventory accounting, CAM expense ']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the central or regional warehouse where this part is stocked, when inventory is managed at a warehouse level rather than at an individual property. Supports multi-site inventory pooling and centralized procurement strategies.']
  annual_usage_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this part consumed across all work orders in the trailing 12-month period. Used for demand forecasting, economic order quantity (EOQ) calculations, and procurement budget planning. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure. Updated periodically from work order issuance records.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, rack, or slot identifier within the storage location where the part is physically placed. Provides granular pick-location detail for technicians and warehouse staff. Complements storage_location for precise part retrieval.']
  compatible_asset_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or descriptive text identifying the building equipment asset types or systems this part is compatible with (e.g., \'Carrier RTU-20, Trane AHU-5, York Chiller\'). Supports technician part selection during work order execution and preventive maintenance scheduling. Sourced from Building Engines asset compatibility records.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification indicating whether the part\'s use is capitalized as a Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) or expensed as an Operating Expenditure (OPEX). CAPEX parts extend asset life or add value; OPEX parts are routine maintenance consumables. Critical for financial reporting, GL coding, and compliance with FASB ASC 360 and IFRS 16.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts inventory record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SOX internal control requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost is denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for portfolios spanning multiple countries.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the current batch or lot of the part expires and should no longer be used. Applicable to chemicals, lubricants, sealants, and other time-sensitive consumables. Supports compliance with EPA and OSHA chemical storage regulations and FIFO inventory rotation.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which the cost of this part is posted when issued to a work order. Enables accurate financial reporting, cost center allocation, and CAPEX/OPEX segregation in the GL. Sourced from the chart of accounts in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parts inventory record: \'active\' (available for use), \'inactive\' (temporarily not stocked), \'discontinued\' (no longer procured or used), \'on_order\' (replenishment order placed, awaiting receipt), \'quarantined\' (held pending inspection or quality review). Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  is_critical_spare boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this part is designated as a critical spare — a part whose unavailability would cause significant building system downtime, tenant service disruption, or safety risk. Critical spares are subject to stricter minimum stock requirements and expedited procurement. True = critical spare; False = standard part.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this part or supply is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, or disposal procedures (e.g., refrigerants, solvents, asbestos-containing materials). Triggers OSHA and EPA compliance workflows for storage, handling, and disposal. True = hazardous; False = non-hazardous.']
  last_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which this part was most recently issued from inventory to a work order or technician. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory items and to support FIFO rotation policies.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was conducted for this part at its storage location. Used to assess inventory record accuracy, schedule cycle counts, and support audit compliance. Discrepancies between physical count and system quantity trigger adjustment transactions.']
  last_physical_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count for this part. Compared against quantity_on_hand to identify shrinkage, loss, or data entry errors. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent replenishment receipt was recorded for this part. Used to track procurement cycle times, validate lead_time_days accuracy, and support inventory aging analysis.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days from placement of a purchase order to receipt of the part at the storage location. Used in reorder point calculations to ensure parts are available before stock is depleted. Sourced from supplier agreements or historical procurement data.']
  leed_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this part or material meets LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) sustainability criteria, such as low-VOC content, recycled material content, or regional sourcing requirements. Supports ESG reporting and green building certification maintenance. True = LEED compliant; False = not LEED compliant.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) or brand that produces this part. Used for warranty validation, OEM compliance requirements, and sourcing alternative suppliers when the preferred supplier is unavailable.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the manufacturer. Used for cross-referencing with OEM documentation, warranty claims, and sourcing equivalent parts from alternative suppliers.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the desired inventory quantity for this part at the storage location. Prevents over-stocking and excess carrying costs. Used in conjunction with reorder_point and reorder_quantity for min/max inventory management. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, substitution guidance, or procurement remarks related to this parts inventory record. Used by facility managers and procurement staff to capture context not covered by structured fields.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping the part into a maintenance category (e.g., HVAC, Plumbing, Electrical, Structural, Landscaping, Fire Safety, Elevator, General Supplies). Supports inventory reporting, spend analysis by trade, and CAPEX vs. OPEX classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|Plumbing|Electrical|Structural|Fire Safety|Elevator|Landscaping|General Supplies|Mechanical|Roofing — promote to reference product]']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the part including specifications, dimensions, material composition, compatibility notes, and any distinguishing technical characteristics. Supports accurate procurement and technician identification during work order execution.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or short title of the maintenance part, supply, or consumable (e.g., \'HVAC Air Filter 20x20x1\', \'Ballast 4-Lamp T8\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in work order fulfillment, procurement requests, and inventory reports.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known manufacturer or supplier part number (catalogue number) that uniquely identifies the part across procurement and vendor systems. Used for purchase orders, vendor quotes, and cross-referencing with supplier catalogues. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification distinguishing the nature of the inventory item: \'part\' (durable replacement component), \'supply\' (non-durable maintenance supply), \'consumable\' (single-use item), \'tool\' (reusable tool tracked in inventory), \'chemical\' (cleaning or treatment chemical), \'equipment\' (small equipment unit). Drives handling, storage, and procurement workflows. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the part available in the storage location at the time of the last inventory update. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure. Core operational metric for work order fulfillment and procurement planning. Updated upon part issuance, receipt, and physical count adjustments.']
  quantity_on_order decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the part currently on open purchase orders placed with suppliers but not yet received into inventory. Used in min/max replenishment logic to avoid duplicate ordering and to project future stock levels.']
  quantity_reserved decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the part currently reserved or allocated to open work orders but not yet physically issued. Reduces the effective available quantity for new work order assignments. Supports accurate available-to-promise calculations for maintenance scheduling.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this part. When quantity_on_hand falls at or below this level, a procurement request is initiated. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure. Core parameter for min/max inventory management in facility O&M operations.']
  reorder_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard quantity to be ordered when a replenishment is triggered (i.e., when quantity_on_hand reaches the reorder_point). May reflect economic order quantity (EOQ), supplier minimum order quantity (MOQ), or standard pack size. Expressed in the part\'s unit of measure.']
  safety_data_sheet_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this part or chemical. Required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) for all hazardous materials. Enables rapid access to handling, storage, and emergency response information.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the part can be stored before it expires, degrades, or becomes unsuitable for use. Applicable to chemicals, lubricants, adhesives, and other perishable maintenance supplies. Null for non-perishable parts. Supports FIFO inventory management and waste reduction.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the part is stored within the property or central warehouse (e.g., \'Basement Storage Room B-12\', \'Mechanical Room 3F\', \'Central Warehouse Bin A-04\'). Enables technicians to quickly locate parts during work order execution and supports physical inventory counts.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'The part number assigned by the preferred supplier in their own catalogue system. Used when placing purchase orders to ensure the correct item is ordered. May differ from the internal part_number.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or last-purchase cost per unit of measure for this part, expressed in the operating currency. Used for CAPEX vs. OPEX cost classification, work order cost tracking, inventory valuation, and procurement budget planning. Sensitive business financial data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the part is counted, issued, and ordered (e.g., EA = Each, BOX = Box, CASE = Case, ROLL = Roll, GAL = Gallon, LB = Pound, FT = Linear Foot, SQF = Square Foot, PKG = Package, SET = Set). Governs quantity calculations on work orders and purchase orders. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE qualifier for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|BOX|CASE|ROLL|GAL|LB|FT|SQF|PKG|SET — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts inventory record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance. Updated on any field change including quantity adjustments, cost updates, and status changes.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or supplier warranty for this part expires. Applicable to durable parts and equipment components. Enables warranty claim tracking and ensures parts are replaced under warranty before expiration where applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record of maintenance parts, supplies, and consumables stocked at a property or central warehouse for building maintenance operations. Captures part number, description, unit of measure, current on-hand quantity, reorder point, reorder quantity, preferred supplier, unit cost, storage location, and asset compatibility. Supports parts availability for work order fulfillment, procurement planning, and min/max inventory management. SSOT for facility O&M inventory within the maintenance domain.'
}

Table maintenance.sla_policy {
  sla_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA policy record. Primary key for the sla_policy data product in the maintenance domain.']
  policy_owner_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: SLA policy ownership by a defined position (Director of Facilities, Property Manager) is a real estate operations governance requirement for policy accountability, review scheduling, and escalation ro']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract or property management agreement under which this SLA policy is defined. Links the SLA policy to its governing contractual instrument managed in DocuSign CLM or Yardi Voyager.']
  applies_to_all_properties boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA policy applies universally to all properties in the portfolio (True) or is scoped to specific property types, classes, or jurisdictions (False). When True, property_class and property_use_type filters are ignored.']
  auto_escalation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA policy triggers automatic escalation workflows in Building Engines when the escalation_threshold_hours is exceeded without resolution. When True, escalation notifications are sent to the designated PM or AM without manual intervention.']
  business_hours_end varchar [note: 'End time of the standard business day used for SLA measurement when measurement_window is \'business_hours\'. Expressed in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'18:00\'). Varies by property jurisdiction and tenant lease terms.']
  business_hours_start varchar [note: 'Start time of the standard business day used for SLA measurement when measurement_window is \'business_hours\'. Expressed in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'08:00\'). Varies by property jurisdiction and tenant lease terms.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies the maintenance activities governed by this SLA policy as CAPEX (capital improvements), OPEX (routine operating expenditure), or both. Critical for financial reporting, GL coding in Yardi Voyager, and REIT FFO/AFFO calculations under SEC reporting requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to monetary penalty amounts defined in this SLA policy (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Supports multi-jurisdiction property portfolios and international REIT reporting.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this SLA policy becomes binding and enforceable for work orders and tenant service requests. Aligns with lease commencement or property management contract start dates. Supports ASC 842 and IFRS 16 lease accounting compliance.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA policy expires or is superseded. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no defined expiry. Used for WALT/WALE-aligned SLA lifecycle management and multi-jurisdiction compliance reporting.']
  escalation_level_count varchar [note: 'Total number of escalation tiers defined within this SLA policy (e.g., 1 = escalate to PM, 2 = escalate to PM then AM, 3 = escalate to PM, AM, then executive). Governs the depth of the escalation chain for unresolved maintenance issues.']
  escalation_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours after which an unresolved or unacknowledged work order is automatically escalated to the next management tier (e.g., Property Manager, Asset Manager). Supports automated escalation workflows in Building Engines and tenant satisfaction management.']
  esg_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities under this SLA policy require ESG impact tracking (e.g., energy consumption, carbon emissions, water usage). Relevant for LEED-certified and BREEAM-rated buildings and REIT ESG disclosure obligations.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction code to which this SLA policy applies (e.g., \'US-NY\', \'US-CA\', \'UK\', \'AU-NSW\'). Supports multi-jurisdiction property management standards and regional regulatory compliance requirements including HUD, EPA, and OSHA.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA policy was most recently formally reviewed and approved by the policy owner. Supports SOX audit trail requirements and ISO 55000 asset management governance documentation.']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'Defines the time basis against which SLA response and resolution targets are measured. \'calendar_hours\' counts all hours including weekends and holidays; \'business_hours\' counts only standard operating hours; \'24_7\' applies continuous measurement for emergency services.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this SLA policy. Calculated based on last_reviewed_date and review_frequency. Triggers review workflow notifications to the policy owner in Building Engines.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Preferred communication channel for tenant notifications triggered by this SLA policy (e.g., email, SMS, tenant portal, phone). Applicable when tenant_notification_required is True. Configured in Building Engines and Salesforce CRM tenant profiles.']
  osha_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders governed by this SLA policy require OSHA safety compliance documentation and sign-off. True for life-safety systems (fire suppression, electrical, structural), hazardous materials handling, and confined space entry work.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary penalty amount applied per SLA breach event, expressed in the policy currency. Applicable when penalty_type is \'fixed_fee\'. Used in Yardi Voyager AR for tenant credit processing and financial reporting under FASB ASC 842.']
  penalty_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial or contractual penalties apply when this SLA policy is breached. True when penalty terms are defined in the property management contract or lease agreement. Drives penalty calculation workflows and tenant credit processing.']
  penalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of monthly rent or service charge applied as a penalty per SLA breach event. Applicable when penalty_type is \'percentage_of_rent\'. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.50 = 2.5%). Used in Yardi Voyager for automated credit calculations.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty applied when this SLA policy is breached. \'rent_credit\' reduces tenant rent obligation; \'service_credit\' credits the tenant\'s service charge account; \'fixed_fee\' applies a fixed monetary penalty; \'percentage_of_rent\' applies a percentage-based deduction. Null or \'none\' when penalty_applicable is False.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the SLA policy across systems and contracts. Used in work order management, tenant service request tracking, and vendor dispatch communications. Sourced from Building Engines.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the SLA policy scope, applicability conditions, and any special terms or exceptions. Provides context for property managers, asset managers, and compliance teams reviewing SLA compliance reports.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA policy (e.g., \'Emergency HVAC Response — Class A Office\', \'Routine Maintenance — Residential\'). Used in tenant communications, reporting dashboards, and compliance documentation.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA policy record. \'active\' indicates the policy is currently enforced; \'superseded\' indicates it has been replaced by a newer version; \'draft\' indicates it is under review prior to activation.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SLA policy document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\'). Supports policy versioning and audit trail for regulatory compliance under OSHA and SOX. Incremented when material changes are made to response or resolution targets.']
  preventive_maintenance_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA policy governs preventive maintenance (PM) schedules in addition to reactive work orders. When True, the policy applies to scheduled PM activities tracked in Building Engines, including HVAC servicing, elevator inspections, and O&M routines.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the maintenance request or work order to which this SLA policy applies. \'emergency\' covers life-safety and critical system failures; \'urgent\' covers significant operational disruptions; \'routine\' covers standard maintenance; \'planned\' covers scheduled preventive maintenance (PM) activities.']
  property_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the property type to which this SLA policy applies. Class A/B/C refers to commercial office grading; residential covers multifamily and single-family; mixed_use and industrial cover respective asset classes. Enables differentiated SLA standards by asset quality tier.']
  property_use_type varchar [note: 'Operational use type of the property covered by this SLA policy. Distinguishes between commercial, residential, industrial, retail, mixed-use, and hospitality assets, enabling use-type-specific SLA frameworks aligned with tenant expectations and lease obligations.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the geographic region or market to which this SLA policy applies (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'Greater London\', \'Sydney Metro\'). Used for regional portfolio reporting and multi-market SLA benchmarking.']
  request_category varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance or facility service request covered by this SLA policy (e.g., HVAC, Plumbing, Electrical, Elevator, Fire Safety, Janitorial, Structural, Security). Drives routing rules and vendor dispatch in Building Engines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|plumbing|electrical|elevator|fire_safety|janitorial|structural|security|general — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_time_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target time in hours within which a maintenance issue must be fully resolved and the work order closed under this SLA policy. Measured from the time the request is logged. Drives OPEX planning and tenant satisfaction KPIs.']
  response_time_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target time in hours within which a maintenance technician or vendor must acknowledge and respond to a work order or tenant service request under this SLA policy. Measured from the time the request is logged in Building Engines. Critical for tenant satisfaction reporting and SLA compliance tracking.']
  response_time_target_mins varchar [note: 'Target response time expressed in minutes for high-precision SLA tracking, particularly for emergency priority tier requests where sub-hour targets apply (e.g., 15-minute emergency response). Complements response_time_target_hours for granular SLA measurement.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this SLA policy is formally reviewed and updated to reflect changes in property management standards, tenant requirements, or regulatory obligations. Supports SOX internal controls and ISO 55000 asset management governance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this SLA policy record was sourced. Primarily Building Engines CMMS for facility operations; Yardi Voyager for property management contract-linked SLAs. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_policy_ref varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this SLA policy record in the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines internal SLA policy ID). Enables reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  tenant_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tenants must be proactively notified of work order status updates under this SLA policy. Supports tenant relationship management obligations in Salesforce CRM and lease compliance requirements under FASB ASC 842.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was most recently modified. Tracks policy amendments, version updates, and status changes for audit compliance under SOX Section 404. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vendor_dispatch_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether work orders under this SLA policy require external vendor dispatch rather than in-house maintenance staff. Drives vendor selection and dispatch workflows in Building Engines and procurement processes in SAP S/4HANA.']

  Note: 'Service Level Agreement policy record defining response and resolution time targets for maintenance work orders and tenant service requests by priority tier, property class, and request category. Captures SLA name, applicable property types, priority level (emergency, urgent, routine, planned), target response time, target resolution time, escalation rules, penalty terms, and effective date range. Supports multi-jurisdiction property management standards and can accommodate varying SLA frameworks across regions. Drives SLA compliance tracking and tenant satisfaction reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.billable_charge {
  billable_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billable_charge']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity operating charges (pool maintenance surcharge, amenity fee) may be billed to tenants. Amenity-level charge tracking supports amenity revenue/expense reporting and lease compliance.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: CAM charge and tenant billable charge approval by a specific employee is required for CAM reconciliation audit trails, tenant dispute resolution, and financial controls in real estate property managem']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Maintenance billable charges (tenant repairs, CAM charges) are invoiced through AR. The billable_charge.invoice_number is a denormalized reference to ar_invoice. This FK enables tenant billing reconci']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which the maintenance activity occurred and from which the billable charge is generated.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Billable maintenance charges are allocated at the building level for multi-building assets. Building-level CAM reconciliation and operating expense reporting require linking charges to specific buildi']
  cam_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAM expense pool to which this charge is assigned for proration and reconciliation. CAM pools group recoverable expenses by building, floor, or expense category per lease terms.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Billable charges carry a gl_account_code for revenue recognition and CAM recovery classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct income coding for AR invoicing, CAM reconciliation']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: CAM charges are allocated to specific common areas. Common area-level charge tracking is the foundation of CAM reconciliation, enabling landlords to justify CAM expenses to tenants.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Billable charges are allocated to cost centers for CAM pool management and property-level revenue tracking. RE controllers require cost center assignment on billable charges to correctly allocate CAM ']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Maintenance billable charges (CAM, tenant repairs) generate GL journal entries for revenue recognition and accrual accounting. RE controllers require the charge-to-journal-entry link for period-close ']
  owner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to owner.owner_agreement. Business justification: Owner NOI and management fee reporting: billable charges (CAM recoveries, maintenance costs) must be attributed to the governing owner agreement to compute net operating income, management fees, and o']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking maintenance charges are billed to tenants or tracked as parking operating expenses. Parking-level charge tracking supports parking revenue/expense reporting and tenant parking lease billing.']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial tenant charges are allocated to specific spaces. Space-level charge tracking is the foundation of CAM reconciliation and tenant billing in commercial real estate.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which this charge was incurred, if the charge originates from a vendor service agreement rather than a discrete work order.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant responsible for or associated with this billable charge. Used for CAM charge-back processing and tenant billing under NNN or modified gross lease structures.']
  tenant_occupancy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.occupancy. Business justification: CAM reconciliation, tenant-billable repair invoicing, and pro-rata share calculations require linking charges to the specific occupancy period. Property accountants need occupancy_id to validate that ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Tenant-billable maintenance charges are allocated to specific units. Unit-level charge history supports lease administration, tenant billing disputes, and move-out cost reconciliation.']
  vendor_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.vendor_dispatch. Business justification: Billable charges to tenants are frequently generated from vendor dispatch events — when a vendor performs work and submits an invoice, the cost may be recoverable from the tenant under CAM provisions ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external vendor or contractor who performed the maintenance service that generated this charge. Used for vendor spend analysis, AP reconciliation, and service contract compliance.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating work order in Building Engines that generated this billable charge. Links the maintenance activity to the recoverable cost.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the billable charge was approved by the property manager or asset manager for tenant invoicing. Part of the internal controls audit trail.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period to which this charge is allocated. Together with billing_period_start_date defines the CAM reconciliation window for this charge.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period to which this charge is allocated. Used in CAM reconciliation to match charges to the correct annual or monthly reconciliation cycle.']
  cam_allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the CAM pool charge to this tenant. Pro-rata NLA/GLA allocates based on the tenant\'s proportionate share of leasable area; fixed percentage uses a lease-defined rate; direct assignment charges the full amount to one tenant.']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity type that generated this billable charge. Used for CAM pool segmentation, tenant billing transparency, and OPEX vs. CAPEX reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HVAC|janitorial|repairs|landscaping|security|electrical|plumbing|pest_control|elevator|general_maintenance — promote to reference product]']
  charge_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the billable maintenance charge was incurred or the service was rendered. Used as the basis for billing period assignment and CAM pool allocation.']
  charge_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable charge reference number used in tenant invoices, CAM reconciliation statements, and property management accounting systems such as Yardi Voyager AR. Format: BC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billable charge from creation through collection or write-off. Drives workflow routing in Yardi Voyager AR and CAM reconciliation processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|invoiced|collected|disputed|written_off|voided — promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes the nature of the billable charge: CAM recoverable (pooled allocation), direct tenant charge (tenant-caused damage or exclusive service), capital improvement (CAPEX), emergency response, or warranty claim. Drives GL coding and lease compliance.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance charge is classified as an Operating Expenditure (OPEX) or Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) per accounting policy. Critical for financial reporting, asset lifecycle tracking, and REIT compliance under FASB and IFRS standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billable charge record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this charge record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio operations for international real estate portfolios.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant formally raised a dispute against this charge. Used to track dispute resolution timelines and SLA compliance for tenant relations.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the tenant\'s stated reason for disputing this charge. Populated when charge_status is \'disputed\'. Used for dispute resolution tracking and lease compliance review.']
  estimated_cam_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated CAM payments already collected from the tenant during the reconciliation period. Used to calculate the reconciliation true-up amount (over- or under-collection) at year-end.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA to which this billable charge is posted. Determines the financial statement classification (revenue, expense recovery, or liability) for the charge.']
  gross_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross cost of the maintenance activity before any tenant allocation, CAM pool proration, or adjustments. Represents the full vendor invoice or internal cost amount in the property\'s operating currency.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on which the tenant invoice containing this charge was issued. Used for payment due date calculation and aging analysis in Accounts Receivable.']
  is_cam_reconciliation_charge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is part of an annual CAM reconciliation adjustment (true) or a current-period direct billing (false). CAM reconciliation charges arise when actual expenses differ from estimated CAM payments collected during the year.']
  lease_structure_type varchar [note: 'The lease structure under which this charge is recoverable from the tenant. NNN (Triple Net) leases pass through all operating expenses; modified gross leases recover specific categories; gross leases are typically non-recoverable. Determines billing eligibility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for property managers to record additional context, special billing instructions, or explanatory notes related to this charge. Visible in tenant-facing CAM reconciliation statements.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the tenant is required to remit payment for this charge per the lease agreement terms. Used for AR aging, late fee assessment, and cash flow forecasting.']
  reconciliation_year varchar [note: 'The calendar or fiscal year for which the CAM reconciliation is being performed. Applicable only when is_cam_reconciliation_charge is true. Used to match reconciliation charges to the correct operating year.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a disputed charge was resolved, either through adjustment, credit, or confirmation of the original amount. Marks the closure of the dispute lifecycle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this billable charge record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Building Engines CMMS and Yardi Voyager AR.']
  source_system_charge_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this charge record in the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines charge ID or Yardi Voyager transaction ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and deduplication in the Silver Layer.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Applicable sales or use tax assessed on the billable maintenance charge, where required by jurisdiction. Included in the tenant invoice and tracked separately for tax remittance compliance.']
  tenant_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the gross charge amount allocated to the specific tenant after applying the CAM pool allocation method (pro-rata share, fixed percentage, or direct assignment). This is the net amount billed to the tenant.']
  tenant_pro_rata_share decimal(8,6) [note: 'The tenant\'s proportionate share of the CAM pool expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.125000 = 12.5%). Derived from the ratio of the tenant\'s Net Leasable Area (NLA) to the total building NLA or applicable pool area as defined in the lease.']
  total_invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total amount invoiced to the tenant, equal to the tenant_allocated_amount plus tax_amount. Represents the final receivable amount recorded in Yardi Voyager AR for this charge.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this billable charge record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail compliance.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s own invoice reference number for the maintenance service. Used to reconcile the billable charge against the corresponding Accounts Payable entry in Yardi Voyager AP.']

  Note: 'Billable maintenance charge record generated when maintenance costs are recoverable from tenants under NNN or modified gross lease structures as part of CAM reconciliation. Captures the originating work order or service contract, tenant reference, charge amount, charge category (HVAC, janitorial, repairs, landscaping), billing period, CAM pool allocation method, and invoice reference in the property management accounting system. Bridges the maintenance domain to the finance and lease domains for CAM charge-back processing.'
}

Table maintenance.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each safety incident or near-miss event recorded at a managed property. Primary key for the safety_incident data product.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenity-related incidents (pool drowning, gym injury, playground accident) are a distinct liability category. Amenity-level incident tracking supports insurance, regulatory reporting, and amenity risk']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the managed property where the safety incident occurred. Links to the property master record in the property domain, sourced from Yardi Voyager or Building Engines property registry.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Safety incidents occur in specific buildings. Building-level incident rate reporting is required for OSHA compliance, insurance underwriting, ESG disclosure, and building safety certification.']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.claim. Business justification: OSHA-recordable safety incidents on property directly generate workers comp or general liability insurance claims. Real estate risk managers track the incident-to-claim lifecycle for loss run analysis']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Slip-and-fall and other common area incidents are a major liability category. Linking incidents to specific common areas supports insurance claims, ADA compliance audits, and litigation defense.']
  compliance_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Safety incidents result in compliance violation citations (OSHA citations, local code violations). Compliance officers tracking penalty exposure and abatement deadlines need to link the incident to th']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person involved in the safety incident (employee, contractor, tenant, or visitor). Links to the workforce or tenant party record. Nullable for near-miss events with no injured party.']
  osha_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.osha_incident. Business justification: A maintenance safety incident generates an OSHA regulatory filing record. Safety managers must link the internal incident record to the compliance OSHA record for OSHA 300 log management, workers\' com']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking incidents (vehicle damage, slip-and-fall in parking garage, carbon monoxide exposure) are a major liability category. Parking-level incident tracking supports insurance claims and safety repor']
  pm_execution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_execution. Business justification: Safety incidents can occur during the execution of preventive maintenance tasks. pm_execution already tracks safety_hazard_identified (BOOLEAN) and osha_incident_report_number (STRING), confirming tha']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Safety incidents in commercial spaces (electrical fire in a suite, chemical spill in a lab space) require space-level tracking for insurance claims, OSHA reporting, and tenant liability determination.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Safety incidents in residential units (gas leak, electrical fire, slip-and-fall) require unit-level tracking for habitability compliance, insurance claims, and regulatory reporting.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order or tenant service request that was in progress at the time of the safety incident, if applicable. Links to the work order record in Building Engines CMMS.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'The specific body part injured or affected in the safety incident, as required for OSHA 300 log recordkeeping. Examples include head, back, hand, foot, eye. Nullable for non-injury incident types such as near-miss or property damage.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the remediation or corrective action costs associated with the safety incident. CAPEX applies when the corrective action results in a capital improvement (e.g., equipment replacement, structural modification); OPEX applies for routine repairs and maintenance. Supports financial reporting and asset management under FASB ASC 360.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded at the time of incident closure, summarizing the final outcome, verification of corrective actions, and any residual risk considerations. Provides a complete audit trail for the incident lifecycle.']
  contributing_factors varchar [note: 'Narrative description of secondary or contributing factors that contributed to the safety incident beyond the primary root cause. May include environmental conditions, time pressure, staffing levels, or equipment age. Supports comprehensive incident investigation.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which all corrective and preventive actions were completed and verified. Null if corrective actions are still pending. Used to measure remediation timeliness and close the incident lifecycle.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date by which all corrective and preventive actions identified in the incident investigation must be implemented. Used for tracking remediation progress and accountability.']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective and preventive actions taken or planned in response to the safety incident to prevent recurrence. May include equipment repair, procedural updates, additional training, or physical modifications to the facility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when the safety incident record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured party was away from work as a result of the safety incident. Required for OSHA 300 log (Column K) and OSHA 300A annual summary. Zero for incidents not resulting in lost time.']
  days_job_transfer_restriction varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured party was on job transfer or work restriction as a result of the safety incident. Required for OSHA 300 log (Column L). Distinct from days_away_from_work.']
  equipment_involved varchar [note: 'Description or identifier of the building equipment, machinery, or system involved in the safety incident, such as HVAC unit, elevator, electrical panel, or scaffolding. Supports equipment lifecycle analysis and O&M safety reviews.']
