  esg_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident is material for ESG reporting purposes, including sustainability disclosures, GRESB assessments, or SEC climate and social risk disclosures for publicly traded REITs. Supports ESG portfolio-level safety metrics.']
  estimated_remediation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of corrective actions and remediation activities required to address the safety incident, expressed in the local currency. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting and insurance claim estimation. Sourced from Building Engines work order cost estimates.']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular categorization of the incident type, such as slip/trip/fall, struck-by object, electrical hazard, chemical exposure, equipment failure, or structural failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: slip_trip_fall|struck_by|electrical|chemical_exposure|equipment_failure|structural|ergonomic|fire_explosion|environmental_release|other — promote to reference product]']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the safety incident or near-miss event, including what happened, the sequence of events, conditions at the time, and any immediate actions taken. Free-text field captured by the reporting party.']
  incident_location_detail varchar [note: 'Specific location within the property where the incident occurred, such as floor number, suite, common area, rooftop, parking garage, mechanical room, or stairwell. Complements the property_id with sub-property granularity.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the safety incident at the time of reporting. Used for cross-referencing with regulatory filings, insurance claims, and Building Engines work order records. Format: INC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the safety incident record, tracking its lifecycle from initial report through investigation, corrective action, and closure. Valid values: open, under_investigation, corrective_action_pending, closed, voided.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the safety incident or near-miss event occurred at the property. This is the principal real-world event timestamp, distinct from the record creation timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the safety incident by nature of the event. Drives regulatory recordability determination and reporting pathway. Valid values: injury, near_miss, property_damage, environmental, fire, security.']
  injured_party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the person involved in the safety incident by their relationship to the property or organization. Determines applicable regulatory reporting obligations and insurance coverage. Valid values: employee, contractor, tenant, visitor, vendor.']
  injury_nature varchar [note: 'Description of the nature of the injury or occupational illness sustained, such as laceration, fracture, sprain, burn, or respiratory condition. Required for OSHA 300 log and workers compensation filings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laceration|fracture|sprain_strain|burn|contusion|respiratory|eye_injury|amputation|fatality|other — promote to reference product]']
  investigating_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the property manager, safety officer, or facility operations lead responsible for conducting the incident investigation. Supports accountability and audit trail for the investigation process.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal incident investigation was completed and findings were documented. Marks the transition from investigation to corrective action phase in the incident lifecycle.']
  is_environmental_release boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident involved a release of hazardous materials, chemicals, or pollutants into the environment. Triggers EPA reporting obligations and environmental compliance review when True.']
  is_osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR Part 1904 and must be entered on the OSHA 300 log. True if the incident resulted in medical treatment beyond first aid, days away from work, restricted work, job transfer, loss of consciousness, or diagnosis of a significant injury by a healthcare professional.']
  is_riddor_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident is reportable under the UK Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations 2013 (RIDDOR). Applicable for properties under UK jurisdiction. Null for non-UK properties.']
  osha_300_log_entry_date date [note: 'The date on which the safety incident was entered onto the OSHA 300 log. OSHA requires recordable incidents to be logged within seven calendar days of receiving information that a recordable case occurred. Nullable for non-OSHA-recordable incidents.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The country jurisdiction governing the regulatory reporting obligations for this safety incident. Determines which reporting framework applies (OSHA 300 for USA, RIDDOR for GBR, etc.). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|SGP|ARE|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who initially reported the safety incident to the facility management team. May be the injured party, a witness, or a property manager. Supports incident reporting accountability.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety incident was formally reported to the facility management team or entered into the Building Engines CMMS system. May differ from incident_timestamp due to delayed reporting.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'The identified root cause of the safety incident as determined through investigation. Describes the underlying systemic or behavioral factor that allowed the incident to occur, such as inadequate PPE, equipment malfunction, procedural non-compliance, or environmental hazard.']
  treatment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the medical treatment provided following the safety incident. Determines OSHA recordability: first aid cases are generally not OSHA recordable, while medical treatment beyond first aid triggers recordability. Valid values: first_aid, medical_treatment, hospitalization, fatality, no_treatment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when the safety incident record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to investigation findings, corrective actions, or regulatory filing status.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Names of individuals who witnessed the safety incident. Recorded as a comma-separated list or narrative. Used during incident investigation and may be required for regulatory filings or legal proceedings. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of witness information.']

  Note: 'Safety incident or near-miss event occurring at a managed property during maintenance or facility operations activities. Captures incident date and time, property location, incident type (injury, near-miss, property damage, environmental), description, injured party details, regulatory recordability flag (OSHA 300 log in US, RIDDOR in UK, or equivalent jurisdiction-specific reporting), first aid vs. medical treatment classification, root cause, corrective actions taken, and regulatory filing reference. Supports workplace safety compliance and building safety governance across jurisdictions.'
}

Table maintenance.pm_execution {
  pm_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each preventive maintenance execution record. Primary key for the pm_execution data product in the maintenance domain.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.building_asset. Business justification: PM executions are performed on specific physical building assets (HVAC units, elevators, fire suppression systems). While pm_execution already links to property.asset (cross-domain), a direct FK to ma']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: PM executions carry a gl_account_code for expense classification. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for maintenance expense posting, CAM recoverability determination, and L']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PM executions record actual labor and parts costs that must be allocated to cost centers for property-level expense posting. RE controllers require cost center on PM executions for GL posting, CAM exp']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the technician or maintenance staff member assigned to and responsible for executing this PM task. Supports workforce productivity and compliance tracking.']
  pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent PM schedule that triggered this execution instance. Links the execution record back to the recurring schedule definition from which it was generated.']
  primary_pm_property_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which this preventive maintenance execution was performed. Enables property-level PM compliance tracking and CAPEX/OPEX reporting.']
  primary_pm_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated work order record in Building Engines that corresponds to this PM execution instance. Provides traceability between the PM execution and the formal work order workflow.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which this PM execution was performed, if applicable. Links the execution to vendor service agreements for contract compliance tracking and invoice reconciliation.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes taken to complete the PM execution, derived from start and end timestamps or manually entered by the technician. Compared against planned_duration_minutes for variance analysis and OPEX labor cost tracking.']
  asset_condition_after varchar [note: 'Technician-assessed condition rating of the asset immediately after the PM execution was completed. Compared against asset_condition_before to measure maintenance effectiveness and inform asset replacement decisions.']
  asset_condition_before varchar [note: 'Technician-assessed condition rating of the asset immediately before the PM execution commenced. Provides a baseline for measuring the impact of preventive maintenance on asset condition and supports equipment lifecycle analysis.']
  checklist_completion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of applicable checklist items (excluding N/A) that were completed (pass or fail) during this PM execution. Calculated as (passed + failed) / (total - na) * 100. Key metric for PM compliance reporting and regulatory audit readiness.']
  checklist_failed_items varchar [note: 'Number of checklist task items that received a \'fail\' result during this PM execution. A non-zero value typically triggers a corrective work order. Key indicator for equipment condition deterioration and OSHA safety compliance.']
  checklist_na_items varchar [note: 'Number of checklist task items marked as not applicable (N/A) during this PM execution, typically due to equipment configuration, seasonal conditions, or scope exclusions. Ensures accurate completion rate calculations.']
  checklist_passed_items varchar [note: 'Number of checklist task items that received a \'pass\' result during this PM execution. Used to calculate pass rate and assess overall equipment condition at time of inspection.']
  checklist_total_items varchar [note: 'Total number of checklist task items defined for this PM execution, as inherited from the parent PM schedule template. Provides the denominator for checklist completion rate calculations.']
  corrective_wo_spawned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective work order was generated as a result of findings identified during this PM execution. True when one or more checklist items failed or findings warranted follow-up corrective action.']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance activity cost as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), or a mixed allocation. Drives general ledger (GL) coding and financial reporting under FASB ASC 360 and IFRS 16 standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this PM execution record was first created in the source system (Building Engines CMMS). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the technician completed the PM task. Used with start_timestamp to derive actual duration for labor reporting and equipment reliability analysis.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'The category of building equipment or system on which the PM was performed (e.g., HVAC, Electrical, Plumbing, Elevator, Fire Safety, Roofing). Enables category-level PM compliance reporting and O&M cost analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|electrical|plumbing|elevator|fire_safety|roofing|structural|mechanical|it_infrastructure|landscaping — promote to reference product]']
  execution_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the preventive maintenance task was performed. Compared against scheduled_date to measure schedule compliance and variance.']
  execution_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number for this PM execution record as assigned by the Building Engines CMMS (e.g., PME-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference, audit trails, and field technician communication.']
  execution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PM execution record. \'completed\' indicates all checklist items were addressed; \'in_progress\' indicates work has started but not finished; \'skipped\' indicates the task was intentionally bypassed with justification; \'cancelled\' indicates the task was voided; \'overdue\' indicates the scheduled date passed without completion.']
  findings_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing observations, anomalies, deficiencies, or notable conditions identified by the technician during the PM execution. Provides qualitative context for failed checklist items and informs corrective action decisions.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account code to which the costs of this PM execution are posted in the financial system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Ensures accurate financial reporting and CAPEX/OPEX segregation.']
  labor_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total labor cost associated with this PM execution, calculated from technician hours and applicable labor rates. Used for OPEX tracking, maintenance budget variance analysis, and total cost of ownership (TCO) reporting.']
  leed_compliance_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM execution contributes to LEED certification maintenance requirements (e.g., HVAC filter changes, energy system inspections). Supports ESG reporting and green building certification compliance tracking.']
  meter_reading_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the meter_reading_value recorded at PM execution (e.g., hours for run-hour meters, cycles for compressor cycle counters, kWh for energy meters). Ensures correct interpretation of meter data for scheduling and MTBF calculations.']
  meter_reading_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The equipment meter or usage counter reading recorded at the time of PM execution (e.g., run hours, cycle count, mileage). Used for meter-based PM scheduling and equipment lifecycle tracking. Null for time-based PM types.']
  osha_incident_report_number varchar [note: 'The OSHA incident report reference number generated when a safety hazard is identified during the PM execution. Populated only when safety_hazard_identified is True. Supports regulatory compliance tracking and audit documentation.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'The overall outcome assessment of the PM execution. \'pass\' indicates all applicable items passed; \'fail\' indicates one or more critical items failed; \'conditional_pass\' indicates minor findings noted but asset is operational; \'incomplete\' indicates the checklist was not fully completed.']
  parts_consumed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct part line items consumed during this PM execution. Provides a summary count for inventory reconciliation and OPEX/CAPEX classification of maintenance activities.']
  parts_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of all parts and materials consumed during this PM execution, in the local operating currency. Used for OPEX vs. CAPEX classification, maintenance budget tracking, and asset lifecycle cost analysis.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The estimated time in minutes allocated for completing this PM task as defined in the parent PM schedule. Used to compare against actual duration for labor efficiency and scheduling accuracy analysis.']
  pm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PM execution by its triggering methodology. \'time_based\' is calendar-driven; \'meter_based\' is triggered by usage readings (e.g., run hours); \'condition_based\' is triggered by sensor thresholds; \'seasonal\' is tied to seasonal cycles; \'regulatory\' is mandated by code or regulation.']
  regulatory_inspection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PM execution fulfills a mandatory regulatory inspection requirement (e.g., fire suppression system inspection, elevator safety inspection, boiler certification). When True, the execution record serves as compliance evidence for regulatory reporting.']
  regulatory_reference_code varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory code, standard, or permit number that this PM execution satisfies (e.g., NFPA 25, OSHA 1910.217, local fire code section). Populated when regulatory_inspection_flag is True. Supports compliance documentation and audit evidence.']
  safety_hazard_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety hazard was identified during the PM execution. When True, triggers mandatory OSHA-compliant incident reporting and escalation workflows. Critical for regulatory compliance and building safety management.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which this PM execution was scheduled to occur, as derived from the parent PM schedule. Used to measure schedule adherence and calculate overdue status.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the technician began executing the PM task. Used in conjunction with end_timestamp to calculate actual duration and support MTBF and labor efficiency analysis.']
  supervisor_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a supervisor approved the PM execution record. Populated when supervisor_approved is True. Provides an audit trail for quality assurance and regulatory compliance sign-off.']
  supervisor_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a supervisor or property manager (PM) has reviewed and approved the completed PM execution record. Required for quality assurance workflows and regulatory compliance sign-off.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this PM execution record was last modified in the source system. Supports data lineage tracking, change auditing, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Execution record of a completed preventive maintenance task instance generated from a PM schedule. Captures the parent PM schedule reference, execution date, technician assigned, actual vs. planned duration, checklist completion status (each task item pass/fail/N/A), findings noted, and parts consumed. Records whether a corrective work order was spawned from findings. Provides the historical audit trail of PM compliance and supports equipment reliability analysis and mean-time-between-failure (MTBF) calculations.'
}

Table maintenance.energy_meter {
  energy_meter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the energy meter master record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  amenity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.amenity. Business justification: Amenities (pools, gyms, common lounges) have dedicated energy meters for amenity cost allocation. Amenity-level energy metering is used in high-end commercial and mixed-use properties for amenity oper']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the managed property at which this meter is installed. Used to associate utility consumption and cost allocation to a specific asset in the portfolio.']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific building asset record (from maintenance.building_asset) representing the physical structure or equipment system to which this meter is attached, enabling asset lifecycle and condition tracking.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Energy meters are installed in specific buildings. Building-level energy consumption reporting for Energy Star benchmarking, LEED certification, and ESG disclosure requires linking meters to buildings']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Energy meters carry a gl_account_code for utility expense posting. A validated FK to chart_of_accounts ensures correct GL coding for utility AP invoices, CAM utility recovery, and ENERGY STAR/ESG util']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Common area meters track energy consumption for CAM cost allocation. Linking meters to specific common areas supports accurate CAM reconciliation and common area energy efficiency reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Energy meters are assigned to cost centers for utility expense allocation and CAM recovery calculations. RE property accountants require cost center on energy meters for utility billing reconciliation']
  parent_meter_energy_meter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent (master) meter in a hierarchical metering configuration. Null for top-level main meters. Enables submeter-to-master reconciliation for tenant utility cost allocation and loss/variance analysis.']
  parking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parking. Business justification: Parking facilities have dedicated meters for lighting and EV charging energy consumption. Parking-level energy cost tracking supports parking revenue/expense reporting and EV infrastructure cost alloc']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial spaces have dedicated sub-meters for tenant utility billing and green lease compliance. Space-level metering data supports CAM reconciliation and LEED/BREEAM energy reporting. Existing suit']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Energy meters are required by mandatory sub-metering laws and energy benchmarking ordinances (NYC Local Law 87, EU Energy Efficiency Directive). Compliance officers link meters to the regulatory oblig']
  replaced_energy_meter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on energy_meter (replaced_energy_meter_id)']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance service contract covering calibration, repair, or monitoring services for this meter. Links to maintenance.service_contract for vendor SLA tracking and OPEX cost classification.']
  suite_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific leasable suite or unit served by this meter, enabling direct tenant utility cost allocation and submeter reconciliation against master meter readings.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Sub-metering at the unit level is standard in multi-family and commercial properties for tenant utility billing. Unit-level energy consumption tracking is required for utility billing, green leases, a']
  building_engines_meter_code varchar [note: 'The native meter identifier in the Building Engines CMMS source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record, and troubleshooting ETL discrepancies.']
  cam_billable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether utility costs recorded by this meter are included in the Common Area Maintenance (CAM) expense pool for tenant pro-rata billing. Critical for NNN and gross lease CAM reconciliation under FASB ASC 842.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The data communication protocol used by the smart meter to transmit readings to the building management system or energy monitoring platform (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, DLMS/COSEM). Relevant only for smart/AMI meters; null or \'none\' for manually read meters. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Modbus|BACnet|DLMS_COSEM|ZigBee|LoRaWAN|pulse|none|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this energy meter master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The date the meter was taken out of service. Null for active meters. Used to close out utility accounts, finalize billing periods, and update ESG reporting boundaries.']
  dial_digits varchar [note: 'Number of digits on the meter register dial. Used to detect meter rollover (when the reading resets to zero after reaching maximum) and ensure accurate consumption calculation between consecutive reads.']
  energy_star_property_code varchar [note: 'The property identifier in the EPA ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager platform to which this meter\'s consumption data is reported. Enables automated data push for energy benchmarking, ENERGY STAR certification, and regulatory benchmarking compliance (e.g., NYC Local Law 84).']
  esg_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether consumption data from this meter is included in the portfolio\'s ESG reporting boundary for greenhouse gas emissions, energy intensity, and sustainability benchmarking (e.g., ENERGY STAR, GRESB, LEED).']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'The floor level within the building where the meter is physically installed. Used for spatial cost allocation, tenant submeter mapping, and building systems analysis.']
  installation_date date [note: 'The date the meter was physically installed at the property. Used to establish the start of the meter\'s data history, warranty tracking, and asset lifecycle management.']
  is_smart_meter boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the meter is an Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter capable of automated, interval-based data transmission. Drives data ingestion pipeline routing and enables real-time energy monitoring and demand response programs.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date the meter was last calibrated or tested for accuracy. Supports preventive maintenance scheduling, regulatory compliance with utility measurement standards, and data quality assurance for energy benchmarking.']
  last_read_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent meter reading recorded for this meter. Used to identify stale or missing reads, trigger alerts for data collection failures, and support billing cycle management.']
  last_read_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The most recent consumption reading recorded for this meter, expressed in the meter\'s unit of measure. Used as the baseline for the next billing period calculation and for detecting anomalous consumption spikes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this energy meter master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the physical installation location of the meter within the building (e.g., \'Electrical Room B2, Panel MDP-1\', \'Roof Level — HVAC Unit 3\'). Supports field technician dispatch and maintenance work orders.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the meter (e.g., \'Landis+Gyr\', \'Itron\', \'Elster\', \'Badger Meter\'). Used for warranty management, spare parts procurement, and vendor performance tracking.']
  meter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive label for the meter (e.g., \'Main Electric Panel — Tower A\', \'Gas Meter — Basement Boiler Room\'). Used in operational dashboards, work orders, and tenant utility billing reports.']
  meter_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identifier assigned to the meter by the utility provider or the property management system. Used for utility billing reconciliation, provider correspondence, and field identification.']
  meter_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the meter. Drives inclusion in active utility billing runs, ESG reporting, and preventive maintenance scheduling.']
  meter_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the meter within its type, distinguishing main utility meters from submeters used for tenant-level cost allocation, check meters for validation, or smart/interval meters for real-time monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: main|submeter|check_meter|interval|smart|pulse|other — promote to reference product]']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the utility commodity measured by this meter. Drives cost allocation methodology, ESG reporting categories, and benchmarking peer groups. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: electric|gas|water|steam|chilled_water|solar|other — promote to reference product]']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model designation for the meter. Used for procurement of compatible parts, firmware updates, and technical support escalation.']
  multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'The demand multiplier or meter constant applied to raw dial readings to calculate actual consumption. Common for high-voltage or high-volume meters where the dial registers a fraction of actual usage. Critical for accurate billing and ESG reporting.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next meter calibration or accuracy test. Drives preventive maintenance work order generation and ensures continuous compliance with utility measurement standards.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or known issues related to this meter (e.g., \'Meter located behind locked panel — contact building engineer\', \'Pending replacement Q3 2025\'). Used by facility operations and maintenance teams.']
  read_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which meter readings are collected, either automatically via AMI/smart meter or manually by a technician. Drives data ingestion scheduling, billing cycle alignment, and energy benchmarking cadence.']
  read_method varchar [note: 'Method by which meter readings are obtained. Automatic reads come from AMI/smart meter telemetry; manual reads are recorded by field technicians; estimated reads are utility-provider projections; remote reads use utility provider remote access.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the physical meter device. Used for warranty claims, regulatory certification tracking, and field identification during maintenance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The standard unit in which consumption is measured and reported for this meter (e.g., kWh for electricity, therms or CCF for gas, gallons for water, lbs or MMBTU for steam). Essential for energy benchmarking, ESG reporting, and utility cost normalization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kWh|therms|CCF|gallons|liters|lbs|MMBTU|kW|other — promote to reference product]']
  utility_account_number varchar [note: 'The account number assigned by the utility provider for billing and service correspondence. Confidential business identifier used to match utility invoices in Accounts Payable and reconcile consumption charges.']
  utility_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the utility company or energy supplier providing the commodity measured by this meter (e.g., \'Con Edison\', \'National Grid\', \'LADWP\'). Used for vendor management, invoice matching, and utility contract administration.']
  utility_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'The tariff or rate schedule code assigned by the utility provider (e.g., \'SC-9\', \'General Service Large\'). Determines the billing rate structure applied to consumption readings and is critical for utility cost forecasting and OPEX budgeting.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer\'s warranty for the meter expires. Used to determine whether repair or replacement costs are recoverable under warranty and to plan capital replacement budgets.']
  yardi_meter_code varchar [note: 'The meter identifier in Yardi Voyager used for utility billing and accounts payable invoice matching. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the CMMS meter record and the property accounting system.']

  Note: 'Master record for utility meters and energy monitoring devices installed at managed properties — electric, gas, water, and steam meters. Captures meter ID, meter type, utility provider account number, installation location (building, floor, unit), read frequency, and current status. Supports utility cost allocation, energy benchmarking, and sustainability reporting.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_service_coverage {
  asset_service_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each asset-contract coverage record']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific building asset covered under this service contract']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service contract providing coverage for this asset']
  added_to_contract_date date [note: 'Date this asset was formally added to the service contract scope. Used for contract amendment tracking and billing reconciliation when assets are added mid-term.']
  asset_specific_sla_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Response time SLA in hours specific to this asset under this contract. May override the contract-level SLA for critical assets (e.g., life safety systems, data center cooling) that require faster response than the standard contract terms.']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date on which coverage for this specific asset under this contract ends. May differ from contract expiration if asset is removed, replaced, or transferred.']
  coverage_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special terms, exclusions, or conditions specific to this asset\'s coverage under this contract (e.g., \'Excludes compressor replacement\', \'Requires 24-hour notice for access\').']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Specific scope of service coverage for this asset under this contract. Defines what types of maintenance activities are covered (full service, PM only, emergency repairs, parts replacement, labor, or custom terms).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific asset became covered under this service contract. May differ from contract commencement if assets are added mid-term.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of coverage for this asset under this contract. Active = currently covered; suspended = temporarily not covered (e.g., asset offline for renovation); expired = coverage period ended; terminated = coverage cancelled before end date.']
  is_primary_contract boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the primary service contract for this asset. Used when an asset has overlapping coverage (e.g., manufacturer warranty + independent maintenance contract) to designate the first-call service provider.']
  removed_from_contract_date date [note: 'Date this asset was formally removed from the service contract scope. Null if asset remains covered. Used for contract amendment tracking and billing adjustments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual coverage relationship between building assets and maintenance service contracts. It captures which specific assets are covered under which service agreements, with coverage-specific terms that exist only in the context of this relationship. Each record links one building asset to one service contract with attributes defining the scope, duration, and service level commitments for that specific asset under that specific contract.. Existence Justification: In real estate facility management, building assets routinely have overlapping service coverage from multiple contracts (manufacturer warranty service, independent maintenance agreements, specialized system contracts), and service contracts routinely cover dozens to hundreds of individual assets across properties. The service_contract.covered_equipment_description STRING field is a denormalized representation that cannot support asset-specific coverage terms, SLAs, or coverage history tracking.'
}

Table maintenance.asset_coverage_schedule {
  asset_coverage_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each asset-coverage schedule record']
  building_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific building equipment or infrastructure asset being scheduled on this coverage line']
  coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the insurance coverage line under which this asset is scheduled']
  asset_specific_deductible decimal(18,2) [note: 'Deductible amount applicable to claims involving this specific asset under this coverage line. May differ from the coverage line\'s standard deductible_amount if the asset has a higher-risk profile or negotiated terms. Null if the coverage line\'s standard deductible applies.']
  asset_specific_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum payout limit for claims involving this specific asset under this coverage line. May differ from the coverage line\'s overall coverage_limit if the asset has a sub-limit or enhanced limit. Null if the coverage line\'s standard limit applies.']
  endorsement_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the policy endorsement that added or modified this asset\'s schedule entry on this coverage line. Null if the asset was included in the original policy schedule.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent insurance carrier inspection or risk assessment conducted specifically for this asset under this coverage line. Used to track compliance with underwriter-required inspection schedules.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next insurance carrier inspection or risk assessment is required for this asset under this coverage line to maintain coverage. Null if no periodic inspection is required.']
  schedule_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset was added to this coverage line schedule and became covered. May differ from the coverage line\'s overall effective_date if the asset was added mid-term via endorsement.']
  schedule_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this asset\'s coverage under this specific coverage line expires. May differ from the coverage line\'s overall expiration_date if the asset was removed mid-term or if coverage was non-renewed for this asset only.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special conditions, underwriter requirements, or risk mitigation measures specific to this asset\'s coverage under this line (e.g., \'Requires annual boiler inspection by licensed engineer\', \'Coverage contingent on sprinkler system maintenance\').']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this asset\'s inclusion on this coverage line schedule. active = currently covered; expired = coverage period ended; pending = endorsement submitted but not yet effective; cancelled = removed mid-term; excluded = explicitly excluded from coverage via endorsement.']
  scheduled_insured_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific insured value assigned to this building asset on this coverage line. May differ from the asset\'s replacement_cost based on agreed value endorsements, depreciation schedules, or coverage-specific valuation methods. This is the amount used for premium calculation and loss settlement for this asset under this coverage.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Basis on which this asset\'s insured value and loss settlement are calculated under this coverage line. May differ from the coverage line\'s overall valuation_basis if the asset has specific endorsements (e.g., agreed value for a historic boiler, ACV for an aging chiller).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the scheduled insurance coverage relationship between building assets and insurance coverage lines. In real estate insurance, major building equipment (boilers, chillers, elevators, HVAC systems) are individually scheduled on equipment breakdown, boiler and machinery, or inland marine coverage lines. Each record captures the insured value, deductible, coverage limits, and effective dates specific to that asset-coverage pairing. A single building asset may appear on multiple coverage lines across different policy years or coverage types, and a single coverage line may schedule multiple high-value assets. This is the operational record used by risk managers to verify adequate coverage per asset and by claims adjusters to determine coverage applicability.. Existence Justification: In real estate insurance operations, high-value building equipment (boilers, chillers, elevators, HVAC systems, generators) are individually scheduled on equipment breakdown, boiler and machinery, or inland marine coverage lines. A single building asset can appear on multiple coverage lines across different policy years, coverage types, or insurers, and a single coverage line typically schedules multiple assets. Risk managers actively manage these schedules, assigning insured values, deductibles, and effective dates specific to each asset-coverage pairing.'
}

Table maintenance.parts_usage {
  parts_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each parts usage transaction record']
  parts_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parts inventory item consumed in this usage transaction']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work order against which parts were issued']
  cost_classification varchar [note: 'Accounting classification indicating whether this specific parts usage is capitalized as a capital expenditure (CAPEX) or expensed as an operating expenditure (OPEX). May differ from the part\'s default classification based on the nature of the work order.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost_at_issuance is denominated. Inherited from the parts_inventory record at time of issuance.']
  issued_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the technician, warehouse staff, or system user who issued the part to the work order. Supports audit trail and accountability for inventory transactions.']
  issued_date date [note: 'The date on which the part was physically issued from inventory and allocated to the work order. Critical for inventory transaction history and work order timeline tracking.']
  quantity_issued decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the part issued from inventory to fulfill the work order, expressed in the part\'s unit of measure. This is relationship-specific data that represents the consumption amount for this specific work order.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the part that was returned to inventory after being issued to the work order (e.g., unused surplus). Enables accurate net consumption calculation and inventory reconciliation.']
  unit_cost_at_issuance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of the part at the time it was issued to the work order. Captures historical cost for accurate work order costing, as the current unit_cost in parts_inventory may change over time.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the consumption of maintenance parts against work orders in facility operations. It captures the operational event of issuing inventory items to fulfill maintenance work. Each record links one parts_inventory item to one work_order with attributes that exist only in the context of this consumption transaction: quantity issued, unit cost at time of issuance, issue date, returned quantity, and cost classification.. Existence Justification: In facility maintenance operations, parts usage represents the operational event of consuming inventory items to fulfill work orders. A single work order routinely requires multiple different parts (e.g., HVAC repair needs filters, belts, refrigerant), and a single part type is consumed across many different work orders over time. CMMS platforms like Building Engines explicitly model this as a parts usage or materials used transaction entity with its own attributes (quantity, cost at issuance, date, returns).'
}

Table maintenance.warehouse {
  warehouse_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for warehouse']
  parent_warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on warehouse (parent_warehouse_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the warehouse facility location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information of the warehouse facility.']
  annual_lease_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual lease cost for the warehouse facility in the operating currency, if leased.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of industry or regulatory certifications held by the warehouse facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the warehouse facility is located.']
  climate_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse has climate control systems for temperature and humidity regulation.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse facility was first commissioned and became operational.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the warehouse is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was first created in the system.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse facility was decommissioned and ceased operations, if applicable.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for warehouse facility communications and operational inquiries.']
  energy_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Energy efficiency rating of the warehouse facility based on building performance assessments.']
  environmental_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse facility meets all applicable environmental regulations and standards.']
  fire_suppression_system varchar [note: 'Type of fire suppression system installed in the warehouse for fire safety compliance.']
  hazmat_storage_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse is approved and equipped for storage of hazardous materials.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the property and liability insurance coverage for the warehouse facility.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety, compliance, or operational inspection conducted at the warehouse facility.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the warehouse facility in decimal degrees.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current lease agreement for the warehouse facility expires, if applicable.']
  lease_ownership_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse facility is owned by the company or leased from a third party.']
  loading_docks_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading dock bays available at the warehouse for inbound and outbound shipments.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the warehouse facility in decimal degrees.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the warehouse manager or facility operations lead responsible for day-to-day operations.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required safety, compliance, or operational inspection of the warehouse facility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the warehouse facility operations or characteristics.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the warehouse facility, including shift schedules and availability windows.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the warehouse facility in its lifecycle.']
  parking_spaces_count varchar [note: 'Total number of parking spaces available at the warehouse facility for employees and visitors.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the warehouse facility operations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the warehouse facility address.']
  property_tax_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual property tax liability for the warehouse facility in the operating currency.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Classification of physical security measures and access controls implemented at the warehouse facility.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the warehouse is located.']
  storage_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Usable storage area within the warehouse measured in square feet, excluding office and common areas.']
  temperature_range_max_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature threshold maintained in the warehouse facility, measured in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  temperature_range_min_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature threshold maintained in the warehouse facility, measured in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  total_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the warehouse facility measured in square feet, including storage, office, and operational spaces.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the warehouse for identification in operational systems and documentation.']
  warehouse_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the warehouse facility.']
  warehouse_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse based on its primary operational function and storage characteristics.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for warehouse. Referenced by warehouse_id.'
}

Table maintenance.hvac_zone {
  hvac_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for hvac_zone']
  parent_hvac_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hvac_zone (parent_hvac_zone_id)']
  air_changes_per_hour decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of times the total air volume in the zone is replaced per hour, critical for ventilation requirements.']
  air_handler_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary air handler unit serving this HVAC zone.']
  area_square_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the HVAC zone measured in square feet.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the building that contains this HVAC zone.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the HVAC zone was originally commissioned and put into service.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or energy efficiency certification status applicable to this HVAC zone.']
  control_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of control system managing the HVAC zone (BMS=Building Management System, DDC=Direct Digital Control).']
  cooling_load_btu_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Design cooling load capacity required for the zone measured in BTU per hour.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost center code for tracking OPEX expenses related to this HVAC zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HVAC zone record was first created in the system.']
  demand_control_ventilation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether demand-controlled ventilation based on occupancy or CO2 levels is enabled.']
  design_temperature_cooling_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target cooling temperature setpoint for the zone in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  design_temperature_heating_f decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target heating temperature setpoint for the zone in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  energy_recovery_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether energy recovery ventilation is enabled for this zone to improve efficiency.']
  fire_smoke_damper_count varchar [note: 'Number of fire and smoke dampers installed in the zone for life safety compliance.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Floor number where the HVAC zone is located. Negative values indicate basement levels.']
  heating_load_btu_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Design heating load capacity required for the zone measured in BTU per hour.']
  humidity_setpoint_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity level for the zone expressed as a percentage.']
  last_balancing_date date [note: 'Date when the HVAC zone last underwent air balancing and commissioning to ensure proper airflow distribution.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when the HVAC zone configuration or parameters were last updated in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HVAC zone record was last updated in the system.']
  maintenance_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for preventive maintenance and service requests for this zone.']
  next_balancing_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next air balancing service for the zone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special requirements, or zone-specific instructions.']
  occupancy_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants the HVAC zone is designed to support for load calculations.']
  operating_schedule varchar [note: 'Standard operating schedule pattern for the HVAC zone defining when conditioning is required.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant occupying this HVAC zone, if applicable for tenant-specific climate control.']
  thermostat_count varchar [note: 'Number of thermostats installed in the zone for temperature monitoring and control.']
  vav_box_count varchar [note: 'Number of VAV boxes installed in the zone for air distribution control.']
  ventilation_rate_cfm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Required outdoor air ventilation rate for the zone measured in cubic feet per minute.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the HVAC zone, used for operational reference and system integration.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the HVAC zone including location details, boundaries, and special characteristics.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the HVAC zone (e.g., \'North Wing Floor 3\', \'Lobby Area\').']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the HVAC zone in the building management system.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the HVAC zone based on its primary use and occupancy type.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for hvac_zone. Referenced by hvac_zone_id.'
}

Table brokerage.listing {

Operational reporting on listing workload and staff productivity requires th']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code.   finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Listings are associated with marketing and leasing budgets.   market_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market_survey. Business justification: Listings are priced against current market survey data (avg asking rent PSF, vacancy rates, concessions). Linking a listing to the market survey used for pricing is standard brokerage practice and sup']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial listings are for specific leasable spaces. CoStar/MLS syndication requires space-level data (rentable area, availability date, condition). Listing must reference the space record to reflect']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: MLS syndication, CoStar reporting, and commission benchmarking all require standardized property type classification. Brokers use property type to determine applicable lease structures, TI benchmarks,']
  reference_lease_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.lease_type. Business justification: Lease type on a listing drives CAM recovery rules, TI allowance benchmarks, ASC 842 classification, and commission structure. Brokers and tenants rely on standardized lease type definitions to price a']
  submarket_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Market and submarket classification drives CoStar reporting, competitive market analysis, broker performance dashboards, and investment benchmarking. Standardized geographic hierarchy is required for ']
  sustainability_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.sustainability_rating. Business justification: ESG reporting, green building premium rent analysis, and GRESB scoring require standardized sustainability certification references. Brokers market LEED/Energy Star certifications to attract ESG-focus']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Transaction type determines regulatory disclosures (HUD, SEC), 1031 eligibility, required document checklist, and commission applicability. Every listing must be classified by a standardized transacti']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential/multifamily listings are for specific units. MLS syndication and leasing platforms require unit-level data (bedroom count, floor, availability date). Listing must reference the unit record']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Zoning classification on a listing is required for permitted-use disclosure, buyer/tenant due diligence, and regulatory compliance. Brokers must reference standardized zoning definitions to confirm pe']
  cam_rate_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated annual Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charge per square foot applicable to the lease listing. Used in total occupancy cost analysis for tenant prospects.']
  close_date date [note: 'The date on which the transaction was completed — sale closed or lease executed. Null for active or pending listings. Used for production reporting and commission trigger.']
  close_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final agreed transaction price at which the property was sold or the lease was executed. Used for comparable sales analysis, commission calculation, and investment performance reporting.']
  co_broker_commission_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The portion of the total commission allocated to the cooperating or buy-side broker in a co-brokerage arrangement. Null if no co-broker is involved.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed brokerage commission rate expressed as a percentage of the transaction price or lease value. Drives commission payable calculations and co-broker split agreements.']
  costar_code varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite-assigned identifier for this listing, used for commercial property syndication, market research, and comparable data alignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the listing record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  days_on_market varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the listing has been active on the market, calculated from listing_start_date to the current date or close date. Key performance metric for brokerage production reporting.']
  deal_stage varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM deal pipeline stage tracking the progression of the listing from initial prospect through close. Aligns with brokerage workflow from Letter of Intent (LOI) submission through contract execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|loi_submitted|under_contract|due_diligence|closed_won|closed_lost — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The contractual expiration date of the listing agreement. After this date, the brokerage\'s exclusive marketing rights lapse unless renewed.']
  gla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total Gross Leasable Area (GLA) of the property or space being listed, measured in square feet. Used for PSF rate calculations, market comparables, and FAR compliance.']
  is_costar_syndicated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing has been published and syndicated to the CoStar Suite commercial property database for market exposure.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brokerage holds an exclusive listing agreement, granting sole marketing rights for the property during the listing term.']
  is_mls_syndicated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing has been syndicated to the Multiple Listing Service (MLS) network. Drives listing visibility and co-broker cooperation eligibility.']
  lease_rate_psf decimal(10,4) [note: 'Annual or monthly asking lease rate expressed on a Per Square Foot (PSF) basis for lease listings. Null for for-sale listings. Used for market comparables and deal underwriting.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the listing. Active indicates available for marketing; pending indicates an accepted offer under contract; expired/withdrawn indicate the listing has ended without a transaction; sold/leased indicate a completed deal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|pending|expired|withdrawn|sold|leased|on_hold|draft — promote to reference product]']
  listing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the listing agreement type governing the brokerage relationship. Exclusive grants sole marketing rights; open allows multiple brokers; co-exclusive is shared between two brokers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exclusive|open|co_exclusive|exclusive_agency|net — promote to reference product]']
  mls_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known Multiple Listing Service (MLS) identifier assigned to this listing upon syndication to the MLS network. Used for cross-brokerage identification and public-facing search.']
  nla_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net Leasable Area (NLA) of the property, excluding common areas and structural elements. Used for tenant space calculations and lease rate benchmarking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for broker remarks, marketing highlights, or special conditions associated with the listing (e.g., owner financing available, sale-leaseback opportunity, as-is condition).']
  number_of_floors varchar [note: 'Total number of above-grade floors in the building. Used for property classification, elevator requirement analysis, and comparable selection.']
  parking_ratio decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of parking spaces per 1,000 square feet of rentable area. A key amenity metric for commercial lease listings and tenant prospect qualification.']
  price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The asking or offering price for a for-sale listing, expressed in the local currency. For lease listings, this field is null and lease_rate_psf is used instead.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding Salesforce CRM opportunity or deal record ID that tracks the brokerage pipeline activity associated with this listing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system of record from which this listing record was sourced (e.g., Salesforce CRM, CoStar Suite). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this listing in the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Opportunity ID, CoStar property listing ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing became active and was authorized for marketing. Used to calculate Days on Market (DOM) and listing agreement compliance.']
  tenant_improvement_allowance decimal(14,2) [note: 'The landlord-offered Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance in absolute dollar terms for lease listings. Represents the capital contribution toward tenant fit-out and is a key deal negotiation lever.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the listing record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit compliance.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'The year in which the property was originally constructed. Used for property age analysis, capital expenditure planning, and market comparable filtering.']

  Note: 'Master record for all property listings managed by the brokerage — commercial and residential, for sale or lease. Captures listing type (exclusive, open, co-exclusive), listing status (active, pending, expired, withdrawn, sold/leased), MLS/CoStar syndication flags, listing price/rate PSF, GLA/NLA, property class, submarket, listing start and expiration dates, days on market, and the originating source system (Salesforce CRM, CoStar). Serves as the SSOT for all brokerage-managed property offerings.'
}

Table brokerage.listing_agreement {
  listing_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the listing agreement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property being listed under this brokerage agreement. Links to the property master record.']
  brokerage_broker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary listing agent or broker responsible for marketing and transacting the property under this agreement.']
  costar_listing_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by CoStar Suite when the property listing is syndicated to the CoStar commercial real estate (CRE) platform for market exposure and comparable tracking.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Commission amounts and financial terms in listing agreements require standardized currency classification for multi-market financial reporting and cross-border transaction compliance. currency_code is']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Listing agreements are governed by specific state/local jurisdictions determining mandatory disclosure requirements, commission rules, dual agency laws, and contract enforceability. governing_law_stat']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Listing agreements are executed by the property-owning legal entity (LLC/SPE). The contracting legal entity must be referenced for contract validity, commission liability attribution, and financial co']
  mls_listing_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by the Multiple Listing Service (MLS) when the property is syndicated to the MLS platform. Enables cross-referencing between the internal listing agreement and the MLS public listing record.']
  owner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property owner or seller who is the principal party authorizing the brokerage engagement.']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.property_sale. Business justification: The listing agreement governs commission terms for the sale. Linking listing_agreement to property_sale enables post-closing commission validation against agreed terms, protection period enforcement, ']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial sublease listing agreements are executed for specific spaces. The agreement grants marketing rights for that suite. Space-level listing agreements are common in commercial sublease transact']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Property type on a listing agreement determines applicable commission benchmarks, required disclosures, and agreement terms. Brokers structure agreements differently for industrial vs. retail vs. offi']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Listing agreements must satisfy specific regulatory obligations (mandatory agency disclosure, lead paint disclosure, environmental disclosure, fair housing posting requirements) that vary by jurisdict']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Listing agreement terms, commission structure, and required regulatory disclosures are governed by transaction type. A sale agreement has different legal requirements than a lease agreement. transacti']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential listing agreements (condos, co-ops, multifamily units) are executed for specific units. The agreement governs marketing rights for that unit. asset_id alone is insufficient; unit-level lis']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the listing agreement, used in DocuSign CLM and Salesforce CRM for cross-system reference and document tracking.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the listing agreement. Draft indicates the agreement is being prepared. Active indicates it is fully executed and in force. Expired indicates the term has lapsed without a transaction. Terminated indicates early cancellation by either party. Withdrawn indicates the property was removed from market. Closed indicates a successful transaction was completed.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the listing agreement defining the scope of brokerage authority and commission entitlement. Exclusive Right to Sell grants the broker commission regardless of who procures the buyer. Exclusive Agency allows the owner to sell without commission if they find the buyer themselves. Open Listing allows multiple brokers with commission only to the procuring broker. Net Listing pays the broker any amount above the owner\'s net price. Exclusive Right to Lease applies to leasing engagements.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of formal amendments executed against this listing agreement since its original execution, tracking changes to price, term, commission, or other material terms.']
  broker_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued real estate broker license number of the brokerage firm executing this listing agreement, required for regulatory compliance and agreement validity under state licensing laws.']
  broker_license_state varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code in which the broker\'s license is issued and under whose laws this listing agreement is governed.']
  brokerage_side varchar [note: 'Identifies the brokerage\'s role in the transaction: sell-side (representing the seller/landlord), buy-side (representing the buyer/tenant), lease listing (marketing a property for lease), tenant representation, or dual agency (representing both parties with disclosed consent).']
  co_broker_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement authorizes the listing broker to cooperate with and compensate a co-broker (buyer\'s agent or tenant\'s representative) from the total commission. True enables MLS co-broker cooperation.']
  co_broker_split_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The portion of the total commission allocated to the cooperating co-broker (buyer\'s agent), expressed as a decimal percentage of the total commission or of the transaction price, as specified in the agreement. Null when co-broker authorization is false.']
  commission_flat_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed fixed brokerage commission amount in the agreement currency, applicable when the commission structure is a flat fee rather than a percentage. Null when a percentage-based commission rate is used.']
  commission_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The agreed brokerage commission expressed as a decimal percentage of the transaction price (e.g., 0.0600 = 6.00%). Applicable when the commission structure is percentage-based. Null when a flat fee structure is used.']
  commission_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the method by which the brokerage commission is calculated: percentage of transaction price, flat fee, tiered based on price thresholds, or net above a reserve price (net listing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this listing agreement record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse, sourced from DocuSign CLM or Salesforce CRM ingestion. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  docusign_envelope_code varchar [note: 'The DocuSign CLM envelope identifier for the executed listing agreement document, enabling retrieval of the signed contract and audit trail of digital signatures.']
  dual_agency_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has provided informed written consent for the broker to act as a dual agent, representing both the seller/landlord and the buyer/tenant in the same transaction, as required by state licensing laws.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing agreement becomes legally binding and the brokerage is authorized to begin marketing the property. Corresponds to the commencement date in DocuSign CLM.']
  esg_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement requires the broker to include Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) disclosures or sustainability certifications (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR) in the property marketing materials.']
  exclusive_territory varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the geographic or market territory within which the broker holds exclusive marketing rights under this agreement, if applicable (e.g., specific zip codes, submarkets, or building floors).']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which all parties signed and fully executed the listing agreement via DocuSign CLM or physical signature. May differ from the effective date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the listing agreement term ends and the brokerage authorization lapses if no transaction has been completed. Nullable for open-ended arrangements.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent formal amendment to the listing agreement. Null if no amendments have been executed.']
  lockbox_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has authorized the broker to install a lockbox on the property to facilitate agent showings without requiring the owner to be present.']
  marketing_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'The agreed marketing expenditure budget allocated for promoting the listed property, covering advertising, photography, virtual tours, signage, and digital syndication costs as specified in the listing agreement.']
  minimum_acceptable_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The lowest transaction price the owner has authorized the broker to accept or present for consideration, as agreed in the listing agreement. Used to guide offer evaluation and negotiation boundaries.']
  owner_disclosure_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property owner has completed and delivered all required property disclosure statements (e.g., seller\'s disclosure, lead paint, environmental hazards) as mandated by applicable state and federal regulations.']
  price_reduction_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the listing agreement grants the broker authority to reduce the listing price within defined parameters without requiring a formal amendment, enabling faster market responsiveness.']
  protection_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after the listing agreement expires during which the broker retains the right to earn a commission if the property is sold or leased to a prospect introduced by the broker during the listing term. Also known as the safety clause or tail period.']
  salesforce_opportunity_code varchar [note: 'The Salesforce CRM Opportunity record ID associated with this listing agreement, enabling traceability from the deal pipeline stage through to agreement execution and transaction close.']
  showing_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions governing how and when the property may be shown to prospective buyers or tenants, including notice requirements, access restrictions, and contact protocols as specified by the owner.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this listing agreement record was ingested: DocuSign CLM (contract execution), Salesforce CRM (deal pipeline), or manual entry for legacy records.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the listing agreement was terminated prior to its scheduled expiration, whether by mutual consent, breach, or unilateral withdrawal. Null if the agreement ran to its natural expiration or closed successfully.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why the listing agreement was terminated before its scheduled expiration. Used for brokerage performance analysis and pipeline reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mutual_consent|owner_withdrawal|broker_default|property_sold_off_market|expired_no_renewal|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this listing agreement record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, capturing any updates from source systems or manual corrections. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Formal contractual agreement between the brokerage and a property owner/seller authorizing the brokerage to market and transact on their behalf. Captures agreement type (exclusive right to sell, exclusive agency, open listing, net listing), term start and end dates, agreed commission rate or flat fee, co-broker split authorization, listing price, and execution status. Sourced from DocuSign CLM and Salesforce CRM. Distinct from a lease agreement (owned by the lease domain) — this governs the brokerage engagement itself.'
}

  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Building class on a deal drives market analytics, broker production reporting by asset class, and investment benchmarking. Deal pipeline reports are routinely segmented by building class for strategic']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Direct campaign-to-deal attribution is required for marketing ROI reporting when deals are sourced directly from campaigns without a lead record (e.g., inbound from a digital campaign). deal.deal_sour']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Deal financial metrics (total consideration, commission amounts) require standardized currency classification for multi-market financial reporting, FX conversion, and investment analytics. currency_co']
