  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this NOI statement, formatted as NOI-YYYY-MM-PropertyCode. Used in investor reports, audit trails, and regulatory submissions.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the NOI statement. Draft indicates work-in-progress; under_review is pending approval; approved is finalized; published is distributed to investors; restated indicates a prior-period correction; voided is cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|published|restated|voided — promote to reference product]']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the statement as actual reported results, approved budget, current forecast, reforecast, or prior-year actual for variance analysis. Drives how the statement is consumed in Argus Enterprise and investor reporting.']
  ti_allowance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tenant Improvement allowance costs incurred or accrued during the reporting period for build-out and fit-out of leased spaces. Impacts AFFO calculations and is tracked separately from operating expenses per FASB ASC 842.']
  total_operating_expenses decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all property-level operating expenditures for the reporting period, including management fees, repairs and maintenance, insurance, property taxes, utilities, and administrative costs. Deducted from EGI to arrive at NOI.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this NOI statement record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks restatements, corrections, and re-approvals for audit and SOX compliance.']
  utilities_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'Landlord-paid utility costs for the reporting period including electricity, gas, water, sewer, and trash removal for common areas and vacant spaces. Component of total operating expenses.']
  vacancy_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue lost due to unoccupied units or spaces during the reporting period. Expressed as a positive deduction from PGI. Includes both physical vacancy and economic vacancy from lease-up periods.']
  vacancy_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Physical vacancy rate for the property during the reporting period, expressed as a decimal percentage of total leasable area that is unoccupied (e.g., 0.0800 = 8.00%). Key driver of PGI-to-EGI waterfall.']

  Note: 'Property-level Net Operating Income statement capturing the full income waterfall — PGI, vacancy and credit loss, concessions, EGI, itemized operating expenses, and NOI — for a given reporting period. Serves as the authoritative financial performance record for each property and the primary input for CAP rate valuation, DSCR calculations, and investor reporting. Attributes include statement period, property reference, PGI, vacancy loss, concessions, EGI, operating expense categories, NOI, management fee, CAP rate applied, and reporting currency.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each fixed asset record in the enterprise fixed asset register. Primary key for the fixed_asset data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the real estate property to which this fixed asset is physically associated or assigned. Enables property-level CAPEX tracking and NOI impact analysis.']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Structural improvements, HVAC systems, elevators, and roofing are capitalized as building-level fixed assets for component depreciation under ASC 360. Real estate accountants performing cost segregati']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account in the chart of accounts to which this asset\'s acquisition cost and depreciation charges are posted.']
  common_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.common_area. Business justification: Common area renovations (lobby upgrades, elevator modernization, corridor improvements) are capitalized as fixed assets at the common_area level for depreciation tracking and CAM cost basis calculatio']
  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: Real estate fixed assets (buildings, major systems) carry green certifications (LEED, ENERGY STAR) affecting depreciation treatment, tax incentive eligibility, and ESG reporting. A real estate sustain']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Real estate fixed assets (building improvements, TI, equipment installations) are created under specific building permits. The permit governs scope of work and must be closed before the asset is place']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: In RE, fixed assets are classified by construction type (steel frame, masonry, wood frame) to determine depreciation method, useful life, insurance replacement cost, and IBC fire resistance classifica']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial cost center responsible for this fixed asset, used for GL allocation, depreciation posting, and CAPEX budget tracking.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE fixed assets (buildings, improvements) in international portfolios require validated currency references for acquisition cost recording, depreciation calculation, and fixed asset register reporting']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to development.dev_project. Business justification: Fixed assets (buildings, improvements) are capitalized from development projects. The fixed asset record references the development project for capitalization basis, depreciation calculation, and audi']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant lease associated with this asset, applicable for Tenant Improvement (TI) assets where the capitalized improvement is tied to a specific lease agreement.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Fixed assets (buildings, land improvements, TI allowances) are owned by specific legal entities in the real estate enterprise. The fixed_asset register must be linked to the owning legal entity for de']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Land is capitalized as a non-depreciable fixed asset at the parcel level for tax basis tracking, disposition gain/loss calculation, and 1031 exchange identification. Real estate accountants require pa']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Fixed assets (TI allowances, building improvements, equipment) correspond to portfolio assets in the investment domain. Linking them enables depreciation schedules to feed into portfolio asset book va']
  property_space_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.space. Business justification: Commercial space TI and leasehold improvements are capitalized at the space level for depreciation tracking and lease accounting. Real estate accountants and auditors expect fixed assets to reference ']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Tenant improvement allowances and unit-specific capital improvements are capitalized as fixed assets at the unit level under ASC 842/IFRS 16. Real estate accountants tracking TI depreciation and lease']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense charged against this asset from the in-service date through the current reporting period. Contra-asset balance used to compute net book value.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized cost of the fixed asset at the time of acquisition, including purchase price, transaction costs, and any costs necessary to bring the asset to its intended use. Represents the gross book value before depreciation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the fixed asset was acquired, purchased, or placed in service. This is the capitalization date used as the start point for depreciation calculations.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the fixed asset into a standardized category that governs depreciation method and useful life defaults. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building|land|land_improvement|tenant_improvement|hvac_system|capital_equipment|furniture_fixtures|leasehold_improvement|infrastructure|intangible — promote to reference product]']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the fixed asset, such as \'HVAC Rooftop Unit — Building A\' or \'Lobby Renovation — Tenant Improvement Suite 400\'. Used for identification in financial reports and audits.']
  asset_location varchar [note: 'Physical location of the asset within the property, such as building name, floor, suite, or room number (e.g., \'Building A — Floor 3 — Mechanical Room\'). Supports physical inventory verification.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset indicating its operational and accounting state. \'under_construction\' applies to assets in the Assets Under Construction (AUC) stage prior to capitalization.']
  asset_sub_class varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity within an asset class, such as \'Mechanical\' or \'Electrical\' within the HVAC class, or \'Structural\' within Building. Supports detailed CAPEX reporting.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Physical barcode or RFID tag number affixed to the asset for inventory tracking and physical verification during annual fixed asset audits.']
  book_tax_difference decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative difference between book depreciation and tax depreciation for this asset, representing the temporary timing difference used in deferred tax calculations under FASB ASC 740.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fixed asset record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in compliance with SOX financial controls.']
  depreciation_convention varchar [note: 'Timing convention applied to the first and last year of depreciation, determining how much depreciation is taken in the year of acquisition and disposal. Relevant for MACRS and book depreciation.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the asset over its useful life. \'macrs\' refers to the Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System used for US tax depreciation. Land assets use \'no_depreciation\'.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date on which the fixed asset was disposed of, sold, scrapped, or retired from service. Triggers the recognition of any gain or loss on disposal in the GL.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the fixed asset was removed from the asset register. Determines the accounting treatment for the disposal transaction.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net cash proceeds received upon disposal or sale of the fixed asset. Used to calculate the gain or loss on disposal (disposal_proceeds minus net_book_value at disposal date).']
  impairment_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment charge recognized against this asset when its carrying amount exceeds its recoverable amount per FASB ASC 360 impairment testing. Reduces net book value below the normal depreciation schedule.']
  impairment_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent FASB ASC 360 impairment test was performed for this asset. Required for SOX compliance documentation and audit trail.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date the asset was placed into active service and depreciation commenced. May differ from acquisition_date for assets under construction that are capitalized upon completion.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or equipment insurance policy covering this fixed asset. Required for insurance claims processing and risk management reporting.']
  insured_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Replacement cost or insured value of the fixed asset as declared on the insurance policy. May differ from net book value and is used for insurance adequacy reviews.']
  leed_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this asset or the improvement it represents has achieved LEED certification from the U.S. Green Building Council. Supports ESG reporting and green building compliance tracking.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the fixed asset on the balance sheet, calculated as acquisition_cost minus accumulated_depreciation minus any impairment charges. Represents the asset\'s accounting value at the reporting date.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the acquisition of this fixed asset. Links the asset to the originating procurement transaction for three-way match and audit purposes.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Used in straight-line depreciation calculations as the floor below which book value is not depreciated.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for equipment and mechanical assets such as HVAC units, elevators, and generators. Used for warranty tracking and maintenance records.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this fixed asset record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA FI-AA.']
  source_system_asset_code varchar [note: 'Native asset identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Yardi asset number or SAP asset master number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  tax_depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method applied for US federal income tax purposes, which may differ from the book depreciation method. MACRS and bonus depreciation are common for real estate capital assets.']
  tax_useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Useful life assigned to the asset for tax depreciation purposes under IRS MACRS or ADS rules. May differ from the book useful_life_years used for GAAP reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fixed asset record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture, incremental loads, and SOX audit trail requirements.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated useful economic life of the asset in years, used to calculate straight-line and other depreciation schedules. Determined per asset class per FASB ASC 360 and IRS guidelines.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor, contractor, or supplier from whom the asset was acquired or who performed the capital improvement. Supports AP reconciliation and vendor spend analysis.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or contractor warranty for this asset expires. Used to trigger preventive maintenance scheduling and warranty claim management before expiry.']
  ytd_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense charged against this asset in the current fiscal year to date. Used for period-end financial reporting and budget-to-actual CAPEX variance analysis.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset register for all capitalized real estate assets including buildings, land improvements, tenant improvements (TI), HVAC systems, and capital equipment. Supports CAPEX tracking, depreciation schedules, and FASB ASC 360 asset impairment testing. Sourced from Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA FA. Attributes include asset number, asset description, asset class, property reference, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method, accumulated depreciation, net book value, disposal date, and disposal proceeds.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bank account record in the master registry. Primary key for the bank_account data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property associated with this bank account. Applicable for property-level operating, escrow, or reserve accounts. Null for corporate-level accounts.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger (GL) cash account in the chart of accounts to which this bank account maps. Enables automated GL posting and bank reconciliation in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center associated with this bank account for financial reporting and expense allocation. Supports property-level and portfolio-level P&L reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: RE bank accounts for international properties are domiciled in specific countries for AML compliance, FATF reporting, foreign ownership regulations, and withholding tax determination. Replaces denorma']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE property management bank accounts (operating, security deposit, reserve) are denominated in specific currencies for bank reconciliation, cash flow reporting, and multi-currency treasury management.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (corporate entity, SPE, or ownership structure) that holds this bank account. Supports multi-entity financial consolidation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio this bank account is associated with. Supports portfolio-level cash management and Assets Under Management (AUM) reporting.']
  primary_signatory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Bank account authorized signatory controls in real estate entities require linking signatories to employee records for SOX treasury controls, termination-triggered access revocation, and dual-signatur']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control associated with this bank account. Links the account to the relevant control in the SOX compliance framework for audit and testing purposes.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the bank account as registered with the financial institution. Typically reflects the legal entity or property name (e.g., \'Acme Realty LLC — Operating Account\').']
  account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Masked bank account number retaining only the last four digits for display and reconciliation purposes. Full account number must not be stored in the lakehouse per PCI-DSS controls. Format: XXXX1234.']
  account_purpose varchar [note: 'Specific operational purpose of the bank account within the real estate business. Provides finer-grained classification beyond account_type for cash management and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rent_collection|vendor_disbursement|payroll|tax_escrow|insurance_escrow|capital_reserve|tenant_security_deposit|loan_proceeds|construction_draw|intercompany — promote to reference product]']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Active accounts are available for transactions; frozen accounts are temporarily restricted by the bank or internal controls; closed accounts are permanently decommissioned; pending_approval accounts are awaiting authorization before activation.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account by its designated business purpose. Operating accounts handle day-to-day receipts and disbursements; escrow accounts hold funds pending transaction close; security deposit accounts hold tenant security deposits per statutory requirements; reserve accounts hold capital reserve funds; payroll accounts fund employee compensation; construction accounts fund development draws.']
  ach_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Automated Clearing House (ACH) electronic payments are enabled for this bank account. Supports automated rent collection and vendor disbursement workflows.']
  balance_as_of_date date [note: 'The effective date of the current_balance figure. Indicates the point in time to which the reported balance applies, enabling accurate cash position reporting.']
  bank_branch_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the bank branch where the account is held. Used for official correspondence and audit documentation.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is domiciled. Used for correspondence and relationship management with the banking institution.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the financial institution where the account is held (e.g., \'JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.\'). Used for bank reconciliation reporting and counterparty risk management.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit American Bankers Association (ABA) routing transit number identifying the financial institution for ACH and wire transfer transactions. Classified confidential as it enables payment initiation.']
  bank_statement_delivery varchar [note: 'Method by which bank statements are delivered for this account. Electronic statements are imported directly into Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA for automated reconciliation; paper statements require manual processing.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank account was closed or decommissioned. Null for active accounts. Required for account lifecycle management and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Most recently confirmed ledger balance of the bank account as of the last reconciliation or bank statement date. Used for cash position monitoring and liquidity management. Not a calculated aggregate — represents the point-in-time balance from the source system.']
  dual_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions on this account require dual authorization (two signatories). True for accounts with high transaction thresholds or escrow accounts per SOX internal controls.']
  house_bank_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA House Bank identifier representing the bank-level grouping under which this account is configured. Used for payment program configuration and bank statement processing.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number (IBAN) for accounts held at international financial institutions. Required for cross-border wire transfers and EU/UK regulatory compliance.']
  interest_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual interest rate applicable to the bank account balance, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045000 for 4.5%). Applicable for interest-bearing accounts such as money market or reserve accounts.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bank account is used for intercompany transactions between related legal entities within the portfolio. Supports intercompany elimination in financial consolidation.']
  last_reconciled_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent completed bank reconciliation for this account. Critical for SOX-compliant financial controls and cash position accuracy monitoring.']
  lockbox_number varchar [note: 'Bank-assigned lockbox number for accounts configured to receive tenant rent payments via lockbox processing. Enables automated AR cash application in Yardi Voyager.']
  minimum_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum balance required to be maintained in the account per bank agreement or internal policy (e.g., compensating balance requirements for credit facilities). Triggers alerts when balance falls below threshold.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank account was officially opened with the financial institution. Used for account lifecycle tracking and audit trail.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft facility approved by the bank for this account. Zero or null if no overdraft facility exists. Used in cash management and liquidity risk monitoring.']
  positive_pay_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in the bank\'s Positive Pay fraud prevention service, which validates issued checks against a transmitted file before clearing. Critical for AP disbursement fraud controls.']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency at which this bank account must be reconciled per internal controls policy. Drives automated reconciliation scheduling in Yardi Voyager.']
  ref varchar [note: 'Internal reference code or short identifier assigned to this bank account within Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA for use in GL postings, bank reconciliation, and reporting. Distinct from the masked account number.']
  secondary_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the secondary authorized signatory on the bank account. Supports dual-control authorization requirements for high-value disbursements.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) Business Identifier Code (BIC) for the bank. Required for international wire transfers. Applicable for accounts at institutions with international operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this bank account record. Used for change data capture, audit trail, and SOX-compliant record management.']
  wire_transfer_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether outgoing wire transfers are enabled for this bank account. Controls payment method availability in AP disbursement workflows.']
  yardi_bank_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this bank account record in Yardi Voyager. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system, and audit traceability.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all corporate and property-level bank accounts used for cash management, rent collection, operating disbursements, and escrow. Supports cash position monitoring and bank reconciliation. Attributes include account number (masked), bank name, bank routing number, account type (operating, escrow, security deposit, reserve), currency, property or entity reference, account status, and signatory details.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each intercompany transaction record within the real estate enterprise. Primary key for the silver layer data product.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Intercompany transaction approval in real estate fund structures (management fee allocations, intercompany loans) requires employee accountability for SOX elimination controls and audit. approved_by i']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property to which this intercompany transaction is attributable, where applicable (e.g., management fee for a specific asset). Enables property-level NOI and OPEX analysis.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account in the chart of accounts to which this intercompany transaction is posted on the sending entity\'s books. Drives the elimination entry pairing during consolidation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center within the sending entity to which the intercompany charge is allocated. Enables property-level and portfolio-level P&L attribution for NOI and EBITDA reporting.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE intercompany transactions between entities in different countries require validated currency references for elimination entries, currency translation, and consolidated financial reporting. Replaces']
  netting_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the netting cycle run in which this transaction was included. Enables traceability of which transactions were settled together in a given netting period.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio or fund to which this intercompany transaction belongs. Supports portfolio-level consolidation, AUM reporting, and REIT distribution compliance.']
  primary_intercompany_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity originating the intercompany charge or payment — the entity recording the intercompany payable or expense. References the enterprise legal entity master.']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.property_sale. Business justification: Related-party property sales between affiliated entities generate intercompany transactions requiring consolidation elimination. Real estate fund accountants must link IC transactions to the triggerin']
  receiving_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity on the receiving end of the intercompany charge or payment — the entity recording the intercompany receivable or income. References the enterprise legal entity master.']
  reversed_transaction_intercompany_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original intercompany_transaction_id that this record reverses. Null if reversal_flag is False. Enables full audit chain tracing for correcting entries.']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: In RE intercompany accounting, transaction types (management fee allocation, intercompany loan, property transfer, cost allocation) drive elimination entries, consolidation rules, and transfer pricing']
  allocation_basis_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantitative driver value used to compute the allocation (e.g., headcount count, square footage, or revenue amount) corresponding to the allocation_method. Supports audit trail for transfer pricing justification.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate shared service costs or management fees across receiving entities. Drives transfer pricing documentation and supports regulatory scrutiny of intercompany arrangements.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the intercompany transaction. SOX-compliant approval workflows require documented authorization before posting. \'Escalated\' indicates the transaction exceeded a threshold requiring senior approval.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the intercompany transaction received final approval. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail for financial controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this intercompany transaction record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for the silver layer.']
  elimination_date date [note: 'The date on which the elimination entry was processed in the consolidation system. Null if elimination_flag is False or elimination has not yet been executed.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this intercompany transaction has been flagged for elimination in the consolidated financial statements. True = transaction is subject to elimination; False = not yet eliminated or not applicable. Critical for FASB ASC 810 consolidation compliance.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to translate the transaction currency amount to the functional currency at the time of posting. Expressed as units of functional currency per one unit of transaction currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month number 1–12 or 1–13 for 4-4-5 calendars) within the fiscal year to which this transaction is assigned. Used for monthly consolidation runs and period-level elimination entries.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this intercompany transaction belongs, as determined by the posting date and the enterprise fiscal calendar. Used for annual financial consolidation and REIT distribution compliance reporting.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net transaction amount translated into the sending entity\'s functional currency using the applicable exchange rate. Required for entities operating in non-USD functional currencies under FASB ASC 830 or IFRS 21.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric GL account code from the chart of accounts for the sending entity\'s posting. Retained for direct reporting without requiring a join to the GL account master.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The full face value of the intercompany transaction before any withholding tax, netting, or adjustments, expressed in the transaction currency. For management fees, this is the fee billed; for loans, this is the principal disbursed.']
  intercompany_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the underlying intercompany agreement (e.g., management services agreement, intercompany loan agreement) that authorizes this transaction. Links to the contract lifecycle management system (DocuSign CLM).']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the nature and purpose of the intercompany transaction (e.g., \'Q3 2024 property management fee — 123 Main Street\', \'Intercompany loan drawdown — Fund III\'). Supports audit review and business user understanding.']
  loan_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number for the intercompany loan facility when transaction_type is \'intercompany_loan\' or \'interest_charge\'. Used to group principal and interest transactions under the same loan instrument for DSCR and LTV analysis.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net value of the intercompany transaction after deducting withholding tax and any approved adjustments (gross_amount minus tax_amount). This is the amount recorded in the GL and used for elimination entries during consolidation.']
  netting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction has been included in an intercompany netting cycle, where offsetting payables and receivables between the same entity pair are settled on a net basis to reduce cash movement.']
  offset_gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The GL account code used for the offsetting (contra) entry on the receiving entity\'s books. Required to confirm the intercompany pair is balanced and to generate elimination journal entries during consolidation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the intercompany transaction was posted to the General Ledger (GL) in the source system. May differ from transaction_date due to period-end cut-off adjustments. Critical for fiscal period assignment.']
  receiving_entity_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the receiving legal entity as defined in the chart of entities. Used for consolidation mapping and elimination journal entries.']
  receiving_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the entity receiving the intercompany transaction. Retained for reporting and audit purposes without requiring a join to the entity master.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal of a previously posted intercompany entry. True = this is a reversal transaction. Used to identify correcting entries during period-end reconciliation.']
  sending_entity_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the sending legal entity as defined in the chart of entities within SAP S/4HANA or Yardi Voyager. Used for consolidation mapping and elimination journal entries.']
  sending_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the entity originating the intercompany transaction. Retained for reporting and audit purposes without requiring a join to the entity master.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this intercompany transaction was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Yardi Voyager (property-level) and SAP S/4HANA (consolidation-level) postings.']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The native transaction identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP document number, Yardi batch reference). Enables reconciliation between the silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Withholding tax or VAT amount applicable to the intercompany transaction, expressed in the transaction currency. Relevant for cross-border intercompany transactions subject to transfer pricing and tax treaty provisions.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The principal real-world business event date on which the intercompany transaction occurred — i.e., the date the management fee was earned, the loan was disbursed, or the allocation was triggered. Used as the primary date for period matching and consolidation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the intercompany transaction, used for cross-entity reconciliation and audit trail. Typically assigned by SAP S/4HANA or Yardi Voyager at the time of posting.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the intercompany transaction. Drives consolidation processing: only \'posted\' and \'eliminated\' records are included in consolidated financial statements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|posted|eliminated|reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this intercompany transaction record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes during incremental loads and to support SCD Type 1 merge operations in the silver layer.']

  Note: 'Records of financial transactions between legal entities within the real estate enterprise — management fee allocations, intercompany loans, shared service cost allocations, equity contributions, and distribution payments. Critical for financial consolidation, elimination entries, and REIT distribution compliance. Attributes include transaction date, sending entity, receiving entity, transaction type, amount, currency, GL account, elimination flag, netting status, and approval status.'
}

Table finance.debt_instrument {
  debt_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each debt instrument record in the master registry. Primary key for the debt_instrument data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collateral property securing this debt instrument. Supports LTV monitoring, property-level DSCR calculations, and collateral reporting to lenders.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger (GL) account used to record the debt liability on the balance sheet. Ensures proper financial consolidation in SAP S/4HANA and SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for this debt instrument for financial reporting and budget allocation purposes within SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE debt instruments (mortgages, mezzanine loans, construction loans) for international properties are denominated in specific currencies for debt service calculation, LTV covenant monitoring, and lend']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Debt instruments (mortgages, construction loans, credit facilities) are obligations of a specific legal entity in the real estate enterprise. The debt_instrument product currently has no FK to legal_e']
  lender_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.lender']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Mortgages and deeds of trust are secured by specific parcels (APN-level collateral). Lenders, servicers, and title officers require structured parcel-level collateral tracking for lien position manage']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio to which this debt instrument belongs. Enables portfolio-level debt aggregation, LTV roll-up, and DSCR covenant tracking across multi-property portfolios.']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: RE debt instruments are governed by specific regulatory frameworks (CMBS pooling agreements, HUD/FHA programs, Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac guidelines) that dictate reporting requirements, covenant structur']
  required_insurance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to insurance.policy. Business justification: Lenders require specific insurance policies as loan covenant conditions (property, liability, flood). Linking debt_instrument to the required insurance policy supports covenant compliance monitoring, ']
  tenure_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.tenure_type. Business justification: In RE debt management, loan terms, LTV limits, and CMBS eligibility differ based on collateral tenure type (fee simple, ground lease, leasehold). Lenders require tenure type classification for underwr']
  title_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.title_record. Business justification: Debt instruments (mortgages, deeds of trust) are recorded as encumbrances on title records with specific recording numbers and priority positions. Title officers and real estate attorneys require this']
  amortization_period_months varchar [note: 'Total number of months over which the loan principal is amortized for payment calculation purposes. May differ from the loan term for balloon loans (e.g., 30-year amortization with 10-year balloon).']
  amortization_type varchar [note: 'Describes the principal repayment structure of the debt instrument. Fully amortizing loans retire principal over the term; interest-only loans defer principal to maturity; balloon loans have a large final payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this debt instrument record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 302/404 and data governance lineage tracking.']
  cross_collateralized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this debt instrument is cross-collateralized with other properties or loans, meaning multiple properties secure the same debt obligation. Impacts disposition and refinancing strategies.']
  debt_issuance_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized costs incurred to obtain the debt financing including legal fees, title insurance, appraisal fees, and origination fees. Presented as a direct deduction from the carrying amount of the debt per FASB ASC 835-30.']
  dscr_covenant_threshold decimal(8,6) [note: 'Minimum Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) required under the loan agreement. DSCR is Net Operating Income (NOI) divided by total debt service. Breach triggers lender notification and potential cash trap provisions.']
  escrow_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lender requires the borrower to maintain escrow accounts for taxes, insurance, and/or capital reserves as a condition of the loan.']
  extended_maturity_date date [note: 'Revised maturity date if the borrower has exercised an extension option under the loan agreement. Null if no extension has been exercised.']
  guarantor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or individual providing a personal or corporate guarantee on the debt instrument. Applicable for recourse and partial-recourse loans. Required for credit risk and exposure reporting.']
  instrument_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the debt instrument. Drives covenant monitoring workflows, lender reporting, and portfolio risk dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|paid_off|in_default|in_foreclosure|refinanced|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the debt instrument by its structural form. Determines applicable accounting treatment, covenant structures, and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mortgage|construction_loan|mezzanine_debt|cmbs|credit_facility|bridge_loan — promote to reference product]']
  interest_only_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the interest-only period expires and principal amortization payments commence. Applicable for hybrid and partial-amortization instruments. Null for fully amortizing loans.']
  interest_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'Annual interest rate applicable to the debt instrument expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.065000 for 6.5%). For variable-rate instruments, reflects the current all-in rate including spread over the index.']
  loan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the lender to this debt instrument. Used for lender correspondence, covenant reporting, and CMBS/RMBS pool identification. Maps to the loan reference number in Yardi Voyager Debt Management and SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  loan_term_months varchar [note: 'Contractual duration of the debt instrument in months from origination to scheduled maturity. Used for debt maturity ladder analysis and refinancing pipeline planning.']
  ltv_covenant_threshold decimal(8,6) [note: 'Maximum Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio permitted under the loan agreement before a covenant breach is triggered. Drives automated covenant monitoring alerts and lender notification workflows.']
  ltv_ratio decimal(8,6) [note: 'Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio calculated as outstanding principal balance divided by the appraised or market value of the collateral property. Key covenant metric monitored against lender thresholds for compliance.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Contractual date on which the outstanding principal balance is due in full. Critical for refinancing planning, balloon payment scheduling, and WALT/WALE analysis in portfolio reporting.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled debt service payment is due. Used for cash management, AP payment scheduling in Yardi Voyager, and covenant compliance monitoring.']
  original_principal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total principal amount of the debt instrument at origination. Serves as the baseline for LTV calculations, amortization schedules, and historical debt capacity analysis.']
  origination_date date [note: 'Date on which the debt instrument was originated and funds were first disbursed. Marks the effective start of the debt obligation and is used for amortization schedule calculations.']
  origination_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upfront fee paid to the lender at loan origination, typically expressed as points (percentage of loan amount). Capitalized as debt issuance cost and amortized over the loan term per FASB ASC 835.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid principal balance of the debt instrument as of the most recent payment date. Used for LTV ratio monitoring, balance sheet reporting, and DSCR covenant compliance.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which scheduled debt service payments (principal and interest) are due under the loan agreement. Drives payment schedule generation and cash flow forecasting.']
  prepayment_lockout_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the borrower is permitted to prepay the loan without triggering a lockout penalty. Null if no lockout period applies. Critical for disposition and refinancing timing decisions.']
  prepayment_penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of prepayment restriction or penalty applicable to early loan payoff. Yield maintenance and defeasance are common in CMBS structures. Drives refinancing cost analysis and disposition planning.']
  rate_index varchar [note: 'Benchmark interest rate index to which a variable-rate instrument is tied (e.g., SOFR, Prime Rate, Treasury). Null for fixed-rate instruments. Required for interest rate risk management and hedging analysis.']
  rate_spread decimal(8,6) [note: 'Contractual spread in basis points expressed as a decimal added to the benchmark index to determine the all-in interest rate for variable-rate instruments (e.g., 0.020000 for 200 bps over SOFR). Null for fixed-rate instruments.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interest rate structure. Fixed Rate Mortgage (FRM) maintains a constant rate; variable/Adjustable Rate Mortgage (ARM) floats against a benchmark index; hybrid combines both periods.']
  recourse_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lender has recourse to the borrower\'s other assets beyond the collateral property in the event of default. Non-recourse is standard for CMBS; full recourse is common for construction loans.']
  scheduled_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total scheduled periodic debt service payment amount including both principal and interest components. Used for cash flow forecasting, NOI impact analysis, and DSCR calculations.']
  servicer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the loan servicer responsible for collecting payments and managing escrow accounts, particularly relevant for CMBS instruments where the servicer differs from the originating lender.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this debt instrument record was sourced (e.g., Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across systems.']
  source_system_loan_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this debt instrument in the originating source system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Enables bi-directional reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this debt instrument record was most recently modified in the data platform. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and SOX audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all property-level and portfolio-level debt instruments — mortgages, construction loans, mezzanine debt, CMBS, and credit facilities — with embedded debt service payment history. Supports LTV monitoring, DSCR covenant tracking, amortization schedules, and lender reporting. Attributes include loan number, lender, loan type, original principal, outstanding balance, interest rate, rate type (fixed/variable), maturity date, LTV ratio, DSCR covenant threshold, collateral property reference, and per-payment: scheduled/actual principal and interest amounts, payment date, payment status, outstanding balance after payment, escrow amount, and late fees.'
}

Table finance.debt_service_payment {
  debt_service_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each debt service payment record. Primary key for the debt_service_payment data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or asset securing or associated with the debt instrument. Enables property-level DSCR calculations and NOI-to-debt-service analysis.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: debt_service_payment has bank_account_reference as a denormalized STRING but lacks a proper FK to finance.bank_account. Debt service payments are disbursed from specific property-level or corporate ba']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account to which this debt service payment is posted. Ensures accurate financial consolidation and SOX-compliant audit trails.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for this debt service obligation. Supports GL allocation and financial consolidation under SAP S/4HANA.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE debt service payments for international properties require validated currency references for payment processing, exchange rate application, and DSCR covenant monitoring in functional currency. Repl']
  debt_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.debt_facility. Business justification: Debt service payments are made against specific investment-domain debt facilities. This link enables covenant compliance monitoring, DSCR calculation, and debt service coverage reporting by connecting']
  debt_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent debt instrument (loan, mortgage, bond, or credit facility) against which this payment is applied. Used to link payments to the originating debt obligation for DSCR and covenant reporting.']
  lender_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lending institution or counterparty to whom the debt service payment is remitted. Used for lender covenant reporting and cash management.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio to which the debt-encumbered property belongs. Supports portfolio-level debt service aggregation and DSCR roll-up reporting.']
  actual_escrow_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual escrow amount remitted to the lender for this payment period. Compared against scheduled_escrow_amount to identify escrow shortfalls or surpluses.']
  actual_interest_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual interest amount remitted to the lender for this payment. Compared against scheduled_interest_amount to identify variances. Feeds into interest expense reporting on the P&L.']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the debt service payment was actually remitted to the lender. Compared against scheduled_payment_date to determine timeliness and trigger late fee calculations.']
  actual_principal_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual principal amount remitted to the lender for this payment. Compared against scheduled_principal_amount to identify shortfalls or prepayments. Used to update the outstanding loan balance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The internal approval workflow status for this debt service payment. Ensures SOX-compliant dual-control authorization before payment disbursement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or user identifier of the individual who authorized this debt service payment in the internal approval workflow. Required for SOX-compliant audit trail documentation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the debt service payment was approved by the authorized approver. Part of the SOX-compliant audit trail for financial disbursements.']
  covenant_breach_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of lender covenant breach triggered by this payment event. Null or \'none\' when no breach occurred. Used for lender reporting and risk management escalation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this debt service payment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX-compliant record-keeping.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days by which the actual payment date exceeded the scheduled payment date. Zero for on-time payments. Used for lender covenant monitoring, late fee calculation, and credit risk reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting fiscal period (YYYY-MM) to which this debt service payment is attributed. Used for period-level P&L reporting, DSCR calculations, and financial consolidation.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days after the scheduled payment date within which payment may be made without triggering a late fee or covenant breach, as defined in the loan agreement.']
  interest_rate_applied decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective annual interest rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.065 for 6.5%) applied to calculate the interest component of this payment. Captures the rate in effect for the period, which may vary for adjustable-rate mortgages (ARM).']
  is_covenant_breach boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment event triggered a lender covenant breach (e.g., missed payment, DSCR threshold violation, LTV breach). Drives escalation workflows and lender notification requirements.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The penalty fee charged by the lender for a payment received after the scheduled due date or grace period. Tracked separately for covenant compliance reporting and expense analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context or commentary on this debt service payment, such as reasons for partial payment, lender waiver details, or manual adjustment explanations.']
  outstanding_balance_after decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining outstanding principal balance of the debt instrument immediately after this payment is applied. Critical for Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio monitoring, DSCR calculations, and lender covenant compliance.']
  outstanding_balance_before decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding principal balance of the debt instrument immediately before this payment is applied. Used to verify interest calculations and track amortization progress.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism used to remit the debt service payment to the lender (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check). Used for cash management, bank reconciliation, and audit trail purposes.']
  payment_period_number varchar [note: 'The sequential payment period number within the loan amortization schedule (e.g., payment 1 of 360 for a 30-year mortgage). Used to track amortization progress and validate payment schedules.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this debt service payment, as assigned by the source system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA) or the lender\'s payment portal. Used for reconciliation and lender confirmation matching.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the debt service payment. Drives DSCR monitoring, lender covenant alerts, and cash flow forecasting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|pending|paid|partial|late|missed|reversed — promote to reference product]']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the debt service payment was posted to the General Ledger. May differ from actual_payment_date due to period-end cut-off rules. Critical for SOX-compliant financial period reporting.']
  prepayment_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The penalty amount charged by the lender for early repayment of principal beyond the scheduled amortization. Relevant for disposition planning and refinancing analysis.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interest rate for this payment period is fixed (FRM), variable/adjustable (ARM), or hybrid. Relevant for interest rate risk analysis and cash flow forecasting.']
  scheduled_escrow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractually scheduled escrow deposit amount due for this payment period, covering property taxes, insurance, and reserves as required by the lender. Included in total debt service obligation.']
  scheduled_interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractually scheduled interest payment amount due for this payment period, calculated based on the outstanding loan balance and the applicable interest rate. Core component of total debt service for DSCR calculations.']
  scheduled_payment_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the debt service payment is due to the lender, as defined in the loan agreement or amortization schedule. Used for cash flow modeling and covenant compliance tracking.']
  scheduled_principal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractually scheduled principal repayment amount due for this payment period, as defined in the loan amortization schedule. Used as the baseline for DSCR numerator calculations and variance analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this debt service payment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  source_system_payment_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this payment record in the originating source system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Silver layer.']
  total_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount actually remitted to the lender for this payment, representing the sum of actual principal, interest, escrow, and any late fees paid. Used for cash management reconciliation and lender statement matching.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this debt service payment record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks changes for audit trail and SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  wire_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The bank-issued confirmation or transaction reference number for wire transfer or ACH payments. Used for payment reconciliation against lender statements and bank records.']

  Note: 'Records of scheduled and actual debt service payments (principal and interest) made against debt instruments. Supports DSCR calculations, cash flow modeling, and lender covenant reporting. Attributes include payment date, scheduled date, payment type (principal, interest, escrow), scheduled principal amount, scheduled interest amount, actual principal paid, actual interest paid, outstanding balance after payment, payment status, and late fee amount.'
}

Table finance.financial_period_close {
  financial_period_close_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each financial period close record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Real estate period-end close is directly tied to audit engagements — external auditors review close packages and the close cannot be finalized without audit sign-off. The financial_period_close has so']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE period close processes for multi-currency portfolios require validated currency references for currency translation completeness checks, bank reconciliation sign-off, and consolidated financial sta']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (company code) for which this fiscal period close is being managed. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA company code and Yardi Voyager entity structure.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the real estate investment portfolio associated with this period close, enabling portfolio-level P&L roll-up and NOI reporting.']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: RE period close procedures are governed by specific regulatory frameworks (SOX Section 302/404, GAAP ASC 842, IFRS 16) that dictate sign-off requirements, control documentation, and reporting deadline']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: Real estate period close procedures are governed by specific SOX controls (period-end close controls, reconciliation sign-off controls). The financial_period_close has sox_sign_off_flag indicating SOX']
  accrual_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of period-end accrual journal entries including CAM expense accruals, property tax accruals, insurance accruals, and deferred rent. Must be \'posted\' before period close gate.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the period was actually closed and locked. Compared against target_close_date to measure close efficiency and identify process bottlenecks.']
  ap_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the AP sub-ledger to GL reconciliation for this period. Must reach \'completed\' before period lock. Covers vendor invoices, CAPEX accruals, and operating expense payables in Yardi Voyager.']
  ar_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the AR sub-ledger to GL reconciliation for this period. Must reach \'completed\' before period lock. Tracks tenant rent roll, CAM charges, and percentage rent AR balances in Yardi Voyager.']
  bank_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank account reconciliation for all operating and escrow accounts associated with the legal entity for this period. Must be \'completed\' before period lock per SOX cash management controls.']
  cfo_sign_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CFO has provided final sign-off on the period close for SEC certification purposes (True = CFO signed off; False = pending). Required for quarterly and annual close cycles under SOX Section 302.']
  cfo_sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the CFO provided final sign-off on the period close. Null if not yet signed. Provides the authoritative executive certification timestamp for SEC filings.']
  close_days_variance varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the actual close date differed from the target close date (positive = late, negative = early, zero = on time). Used for close process performance benchmarking and continuous improvement.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the period status was set to \'closed\' or \'locked\'. Provides the authoritative audit trail timestamp for SOX compliance and financial reporting cutoff.']
  close_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the close cycle: monthly (standard month-end), quarterly (Q-end with additional procedures), annual (year-end with full audit package), or adjustment (out-of-cycle correction period). Drives the task checklist and SOX control scope.']
  completed_task_count varchar [note: 'Number of close checklist tasks that have been completed and signed off as of the last record update. Enables close progress percentage calculation for management dashboards.']
  controller_sign_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Controller or Chief Accounting Officer has formally signed off on the period close, certifying the completeness and accuracy of the financial statements (True = signed off; False = pending).']
  controller_sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the Controller formally signed off on the period close. Null if sign-off has not yet occurred. Used as the authoritative certification timestamp for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this financial period close record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Provides the audit trail for record origination.']
  depreciation_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of fixed asset depreciation journal entry posting for this period. Covers building depreciation, tenant improvement (TI) amortization, and CAPEX asset depreciation as managed in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and SAP S/4HANA.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit fiscal year to which this period close belongs (e.g., 2024). Aligns with the legal entity\'s fiscal calendar as configured in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  gl_posting_completeness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all expected GL journal entries (accruals, prepayments, depreciation, CAM reconciliations, NOI adjustments) have been posted for this period (True = complete; False = postings outstanding).']
  gl_posting_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GL journal entries can currently be posted to this period (True = postings allowed; False = period is closed to new postings). Directly controls transaction entry in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  intercompany_elimination_status varchar [note: 'Status of intercompany transaction elimination processing for this period close. Required for consolidated financial statements across legal entities in SAP S/4HANA. Must be \'completed\' before annual close lock.']
  noi_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of NOI-related GL journal entries (EGI, OPEX, vacancy adjustments) for this period. NOI is the primary performance metric for real estate assets and must be fully posted before period lock.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for Controller or accounting team to document significant period-end events, accounting policy decisions, material adjustments, or exceptions that affected this close cycle (e.g., \'Q3 includes one-time lease termination fee for Tenant ABC\').']
  outstanding_task_count varchar [note: 'Number of close checklist tasks that remain incomplete at the time of record update. Used to track close progress and gate period lock until all tasks are resolved. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Financial Close Cockpit task management.']
  period_close_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this period close record, used in cross-system references between Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA (e.g., \'FY2024-M03-ENT001\').']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the inclusive upper bound for GL postings, rent roll cutoffs, and CAM reconciliation accruals.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the fiscal period (e.g., \'March 2024\', \'Q1 FY2024\', \'Year-End 2024\'). Used in financial reports, NOI statements, and EBITDA roll-ups.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier of the fiscal period within the fiscal year (1–12 for monthly, 1–4 for quarterly, or 13/16 for adjustment periods). Sourced from Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA period definitions.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the inclusive lower bound for GL postings, AR/AP accruals, and revenue recognition within this period.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the GL posting period: open (postings allowed), soft_closed (restricted postings, review in progress), closed (no new postings, pending sign-off), locked (permanently sealed, no changes), or reopened (temporarily unlocked for correction). Controls GL posting eligibility in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  reopen_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation documenting the business justification for reopening a closed or locked period (e.g., \'Missed CAM reconciliation accrual for Property XYZ\', \'Intercompany elimination correction\'). Required for SOX audit trail.']
  reopen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which a previously closed or locked period was reopened for correction. Null if the period has never been reopened. Critical for SOX audit trail and change management documentation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this period close record originates (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager\' for property-level close, \'SAP S/4HANA\' for consolidated entity close).']
  source_system_period_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this period close record in the originating source system (Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the operational system of record.']
  sox_sign_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all SOX internal control checkpoints for this period have been reviewed and signed off (True = SOX sign-off complete; False = pending). Required for publicly traded REITs under SEC reporting obligations.']
  sox_sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the SOX control sign-off was completed for this period. Null if SOX sign-off has not yet occurred. Provides the authoritative audit evidence timestamp for SEC and external auditor review.']
  target_close_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the period close process should be fully completed and the period locked. Used for close calendar scheduling and SLA tracking against the controller\'s close timeline.']
  total_task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of close checklist tasks defined for this period close cycle, including GL reconciliations, AR/AP sub-ledger tie-outs, intercompany eliminations, and SOX control checkpoints.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this financial period close record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks the most recent status change, sign-off update, or task count revision.']

  Note: 'Fiscal calendar periods and month-end/year-end financial close management for each legal entity. Defines when GL postings are open or closed and tracks close process execution — task completion, sign-off milestones, and SOX control checkpoints. Ensures completeness of GL postings, AR/AP reconciliations, and intercompany eliminations before period lock. Attributes include fiscal year, period number, period name, start/end dates, period status (open, closed, locked), close type (monthly, quarterly, annual), target/actual close dates, outstanding task count, SOX sign-off flag, controller sign-off, and close timestamp.'
}

Table finance.tax_provision {
  tax_provision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each tax provision record. Primary key for the tax_provision data product.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tax provision approval in real estate (REIT elections, property tax accruals) requires tracking the tax director or CFO who approved the provision for regulatory compliance and audit. approved_by is a']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property for which a property-level tax provision is recorded. Nullable for entity-level provisions not tied to a single property.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: Real estate tax provisions are subject to external audit review — auditors review deferred tax assets, uncertain tax positions, and REIT distribution deductions as part of the annual audit engagement.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account in the chart of accounts to which this tax provision is posted. Ensures proper financial statement classification and SOX-compliant audit trail in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for this tax provision, enabling property-level and portfolio-level tax expense allocation in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: RE tax provisions for international properties require validated currency references for deferred tax asset/liability calculation, effective tax rate reporting, and multi-currency tax provision consol']
  exchange_1031_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.exchange_1031. Business justification: 1031 exchanges generate tax provisions for deferred gain, boot recognition, and replacement basis (IRS Form 8824). Tax accountants must link the provision to the exchange record to calculate deferred ']
  financial_period_close_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period_close. Business justification: Tax provisions (income tax, property tax accruals, deferred tax) are calculated and recorded as part of the financial close process. Linking tax_provision to financial_period_close establishes the aut']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: RE tax provisions are filed by jurisdiction (state, county, municipality) which maps to the geographic hierarchy for multi-state tax nexus reporting, property tax assessment tracking, and state apport']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Real estate tax provisions are jurisdiction-specific — property tax rates, REIT rules, and filing deadlines vary by state/county/municipality. Linking to jurisdiction enables jurisdiction-specific tax']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (e.g., REIT, LLC, LP, corporation) for which the tax provision is calculated. Supports multi-entity consolidation and REIT distribution compliance.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.parcel. Business justification: Property tax provisions are computed at the APN/parcel level using assessed_land_value, tax_district_code, and zoning data. Real estate tax accountants preparing ASC 740 provisions and property tax ap']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio to which this tax provision belongs. Supports portfolio-level tax expense aggregation and REIT-level tax compliance reporting in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.property_sale. Business justification: Property sales trigger tax provisions for capital gains, depreciation recapture, and transfer taxes. Tax teams calculate provisions directly from sale proceeds and adjusted basis. This link is require']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: RE tax provisions are governed by specific regulatory frameworks (REIT distribution deduction rules, state tax nexus regulations, ASC 740) that determine current vs. deferred tax classification and un']
  tax_appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_appeal. Business justification: When a tax appeal is filed, the provision must reflect the uncertain tax position (UTP) under ASC 740. Finance teams need to link the provision to the active appeal to track contingent tax savings and']
  valuation_tax_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to valuation.tax_assessment. Business justification: Property tax provisions are calculated directly from official tax assessments. Finance teams must trace the assessed value used in the provision to the assessment record for ASC 740 compliance and aud']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tax provision record was formally approved. Provides an audit trail for SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  book_tax_difference decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate difference between book income and taxable income for the provision period, encompassing permanent and temporary differences. Key input for deferred tax calculation and FASB ASC 740 rate reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tax provision record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor required for SOX-compliant financial controls.']
  current_tax_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of total income tax expense attributable to taxes currently payable or recoverable in the current period. Represents the actual tax liability due to taxing authorities for the provision period, per FASB ASC 740.']
  deferred_tax_asset decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deferred tax asset balance arising from temporary differences, net operating loss (NOL) carryforwards, and tax credit carryforwards that will reduce future taxable income. Critical for REIT entities with depreciation timing differences.']
  deferred_tax_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net change in deferred tax assets and liabilities during the provision period. A positive value represents deferred tax expense; a negative value represents a deferred tax benefit. Supports FASB ASC 740 income tax footnote disclosures.']
  deferred_tax_liability decimal(18,2) [note: 'The deferred tax liability balance arising from temporary differences where taxable income will exceed book income in future periods. Common in real estate due to accelerated depreciation (MACRS) versus straight-line book depreciation.']
  effective_tax_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The ratio of total income tax expense to pre-tax income, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2100 for 21%). Used for FASB ASC 740 rate reconciliation disclosures and REIT tax efficiency analysis.']
  filing_due_date date [note: 'The statutory deadline by which the tax return or payment associated with this provision must be filed with the relevant taxing authority. Supports tax compliance calendar management.']
