  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (quarterly or monthly) within the fiscal year for this provision. Supports interim tax provision calculations required under FASB ASC 740-270. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Q1|Q2|Q3|Q4|M01|M02|M03|M04|M05|M06|M07|M08|M09|M10|M11|M12|Annual — promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this tax provision belongs. Used for annual tax provision roll-ups, REIT distribution testing, and SOX-compliant financial reporting.']
  nol_carryforward decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative net operating loss carryforward balance available to offset future taxable income. Tracked per entity and jurisdiction for REIT tax planning and FASB ASC 740 deferred tax asset valuation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for tax preparer or reviewer commentary, including explanations of significant estimates, assumptions, or adjustments made in the provision calculation. Supports audit documentation.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the tax payment associated with this provision is due to the taxing authority. May differ from the filing due date for estimated tax payments and installment obligations.']
  permanent_difference decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the book-to-tax difference that will never reverse, such as non-deductible meals and entertainment, tax-exempt income, or REIT-specific permanent exclusions. Drives the effective tax rate reconciliation.']
  pretax_income decimal(18,2) [note: 'The book income before income taxes for the provision period, used as the base for effective tax rate calculation and FASB ASC 740 rate reconciliation. Sourced from the entity-level P&L in Yardi Voyager or SAP S/4HANA.']
  property_tax_accrual decimal(18,2) [note: 'The accrued property tax expense for the provision period, representing the estimated property tax liability recognized on a straight-line basis over the tax year. Sourced from Yardi Voyager property tax module.']
  property_tax_assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The assessed value of the property as determined by the taxing authority, used as the basis for property tax calculation. Applicable when tax_type is \'property\'. Supports property tax appeal and accrual management.']
  property_tax_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The property tax rate (mill rate) applied to the assessed value to calculate the property tax obligation. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.012500 for 1.25%). Applicable when tax_type is \'property\'.']
  provision_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the tax provision record, used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA GL entries and tax compliance filings.']
  provision_period_end date [note: 'The last day of the fiscal or calendar period covered by this tax provision. Together with provision_period_start, defines the measurement window for current and deferred tax calculations.']
  provision_period_start date [note: 'The first day of the fiscal or calendar period covered by this tax provision. Used to align provisions with reporting periods for FASB ASC 740 interim and annual calculations.']
  provision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tax provision record, tracking progression from initial calculation through review, approval, filing, and closure.']
  provision_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the tax provision entry: current period tax, deferred tax movement, uncertain tax position reserve, periodic accrual, interim estimate, or year-end true-up adjustment.']
  reit_distribution_deduction decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dividends-paid deduction claimed by a REIT entity to reduce taxable income. REITs must distribute at least 90% of taxable income to maintain REIT qualification and avoid corporate-level income tax.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this tax provision data originated (e.g., Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation.']
  source_system_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this tax provision record in the originating source system (e.g., SAP document number, Yardi transaction ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational systems.']
  statutory_tax_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The applicable statutory (legal) tax rate for the filing jurisdiction and tax type in the provision period (e.g., 0.2100 for the US federal corporate rate). Used as the baseline for effective tax rate reconciliation under FASB ASC 740.']
  tax_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax credits applied in the provision period, including Low-Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC), Historic Tax Credits, energy efficiency credits, and other applicable real estate tax incentives.']
  tax_return_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting basis used for the tax return filing associated with this provision (cash, accrual, or modified accrual). Relevant for identifying book-to-tax differences driving deferred tax calculations.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax obligation being provisioned. Income tax applies to REIT taxable income; property tax applies to real estate holdings; transfer tax applies to property dispositions; sales tax applies to taxable services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: income|property|transfer|sales|excise|franchise — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  taxable_income decimal(18,2) [note: 'The income amount subject to tax after applying allowable deductions, exclusions, and REIT distribution deductions. For REITs, this reflects income after the dividends-paid deduction required to maintain REIT qualification.']
  temporary_difference decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the book-to-tax difference that will reverse in future periods, generating deferred tax assets or liabilities. In real estate, primarily driven by depreciation timing differences (MACRS vs. straight-line) and lease accounting.']
  total_tax_expense decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sum of current tax expense and deferred tax expense for the provision period. Represents the total income tax charge recognized in the income statement per FASB ASC 740.']
  uncertain_tax_position_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate amount of unrecognized tax benefits related to uncertain tax positions under FASB ASC 740-10-25 (FIN 48). Represents tax positions where the technical merits do not meet the more-likely-than-not recognition threshold.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tax provision record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  valuation_allowance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The valuation allowance recorded against deferred tax assets when it is more likely than not that some or all of the deferred tax asset will not be realized. Required disclosure under FASB ASC 740-10-30.']

  Note: 'Records of income tax provisions, property tax accruals, and deferred tax calculations at the entity and property level. Supports REIT distribution requirements, tax compliance filings, and FASB ASC 740 income tax accounting. Attributes include provision period, entity reference, property reference, tax type (income, property, transfer, sales), current tax expense, deferred tax asset, deferred tax liability, effective tax rate, provision status, and filing jurisdiction.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cost allocation record']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the cost center receiving the allocated employee cost']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee whose compensation is being allocated']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the employee\'s total compensation and benefits allocated to this cost center. Must sum to 100% across all active allocations for an employee. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  allocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the split allocation (e.g., \'Multi-property portfolio manager\', \'Shared services support\', \'Regional oversight role\'). Required for audit and compliance.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation: active = in effect for GL posting; pending = approved but not yet effective; expired = past effective_end_date; cancelled = revoked before expiry']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this cost allocation was formally approved for financial reporting']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the finance or HR manager who approved this cost allocation arrangement']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this cost allocation ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended allocation. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this cost allocation becomes active for GL posting and financial reporting. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  fte_allocation decimal(4,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation to this cost center, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.50 for half-time). Used for headcount reporting and capacity planning. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']
  primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the employee\'s primary cost center assignment for HR and operational purposes (e.g., physical workspace, manager reporting). Only one allocation per employee can be primary at any time. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation of employee compensation and benefits across multiple cost centers in the real estate portfolio. It captures the financial distribution of workforce costs when employees work across multiple properties, departments, or business units. Each record links one employee to one cost center with allocation percentage, effective dates, and primary assignment flag. This is essential for accurate NOI reporting, OPEX tracking, and multi-property financial consolidation in real estate operations.. Existence Justification: In real estate operations, employees (especially property managers, asset managers, and regional staff) are routinely allocated across multiple cost centers representing different properties or business units, with each cost center having multiple employees. The business actively manages these allocations with specific percentages, effective dates, and primary assignment flags for accurate NOI reporting and OPEX tracking. This is standard split-cost accounting in multi-property real estate firms.'
}

Table finance.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  amended_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on purchase_order (amended_purchase_order_id)']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved this purchase order for issuance, establishing authorization trail.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property for which this purchase order is issued. Links procurement to specific real estate assets.']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or procurement specialist responsible for creating and managing this purchase order.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the general ledger account to which this purchase order will be posted for financial accounting.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master contract or blanket agreement under which this purchase order is issued, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center to which this purchase order expense will be allocated for financial reporting and budgeting.']
  requester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originally requested the goods or services covered by this purchase order.']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase requisition that originated this purchase order, establishing the procurement request trail.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the purchase order was formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address to which invoices should be sent.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address, typically suite or department number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the billing address.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the purchase order was cancelled, if applicable. Supports audit trail and vendor relationship management.']
  cancelled_date date [note: 'The date on which the purchase order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date on which the purchase order was closed after all goods were received and invoices processed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which this purchase order is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied to this purchase order based on vendor agreements or promotional terms.']
  is_capital_expenditure boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this purchase order represents a capital expenditure that will be capitalized on the balance sheet rather than expensed.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this purchase order requires expedited processing and delivery.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this purchase order record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional instructions, special handling requirements, or internal comments related to this purchase order.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms between buyer and vendor specifying when payment is due after invoice receipt.']
  po_date date [note: 'The date on which the purchase order was created and issued to the vendor. Principal business event timestamp.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the purchase order, typically formatted as PO-XXXXXX. Used for vendor communication and invoice matching.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procurement workflow.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order based on procurement strategy and usage pattern.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the vendor has committed to deliver goods or complete services.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods or completion of services.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the physical address where goods should be delivered.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the physical address where goods should be delivered, typically suite or unit number.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The freight or shipping charges associated with delivery of goods for this purchase order.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City name for the shipping address.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipping address.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'The method by which goods will be shipped or delivered to the buyer location.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the shipping address.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the shipping address.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The sum of all line item amounts before taxes, fees, and discounts are applied.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to this purchase order, including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final total amount of the purchase order including subtotal, taxes, shipping, and discounts. Net payable amount.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact for communication regarding this purchase order.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the vendor organization for this purchase order.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary vendor contact for communication regarding this purchase order.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for purchase_order. Referenced by purchase_order_id.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property associated with this payment run, if applicable (e.g., property-specific vendor payments).']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which payments in this run are disbursed.']
  cancelled_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who cancelled this payment run, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for or associated with this payment run.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this payment run.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this payment run for execution.']
  executed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who executed this payment run and initiated the payment processing.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this payment run record.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the portfolio associated with this payment run for portfolio-level payment processing.']
  reprocessed_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reprocessed_payment_run_id)']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment run requires formal approval before execution (True) or can be executed without approval (False).']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run was approved for execution.']
  batch_reference_number varchar [note: 'External batch reference number assigned by the payment processor or banking system for tracking purposes.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this payment run was cancelled.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run was cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all payments in this run (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run was executed and payment processing was initiated.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the payment run was executed and payments were initiated.']
  failed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of payments that failed to process in this run.']
  failed_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payments that failed to process or were rejected during this run.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which this payment run is assigned for financial reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this payment run belongs for financial reporting purposes.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment run transactions are posted to the general ledger for accounting purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was last modified.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used for disbursements in this run (e.g., ACH, wire transfer, check).']
  processing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the processing of this payment run, including any issues or special handling instructions.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment run was reconciled with bank statements.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether this payment run has been reconciled with bank statements and payment confirmations.']
  run_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the payment run for identification purposes (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Vendor Payments\', \'Monthly Lease Payments - March\').']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the payment run, typically formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment run based on the nature of payments being processed.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which payments in this run are scheduled to be disbursed or initiated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this payment run originated (e.g., Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this payment run in the source system of record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run indicating its stage in the approval and execution workflow.']
  successful_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of payments that were successfully processed in this run.']
  successful_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payments that were successfully processed and disbursed in this run.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of all payments included in this payment run.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date for accounting and financial reporting purposes when payments are considered settled.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.lockbox_batch {
  lockbox_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lockbox_batch']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the lockbox batch for posting, enforcing segregation of duties.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this lockbox batch contains tenant payments. Null if batch spans multiple properties.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bank account into which lockbox receipts are deposited.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who processed and posted the lockbox batch in the property management system.']
  reprocessed_lockbox_batch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lockbox_batch (reprocessed_lockbox_batch_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial institution or third-party provider managing the lockbox service.']
  voided_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who voided the lockbox batch.']
  auto_applied_count varchar [note: 'Number of payments in the batch that were automatically applied to tenant accounts without manual intervention.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique batch number for the lockbox processing batch. External identifier used by treasury and accounting teams.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lockbox batch in the cash application workflow.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lockbox batch based on processing method and priority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the lockbox batch record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all amounts in this lockbox batch.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Date the lockbox batch funds were deposited into the designated bank account.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of payments in the batch requiring manual intervention or research due to missing or invalid information.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lockbox batch has been voided and reversed from the general ledger.']
  lockbox_number varchar [note: 'Unique lockbox identifier assigned by the financial institution to the physical or virtual lockbox.']
  manual_applied_count varchar [note: 'Number of payments in the batch that required manual cash application by accounting staff.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the lockbox batch record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes documenting exceptions, variances, or special handling instructions for the lockbox batch.']
  nsf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of checks returned due to non-sufficient funds.']
  nsf_count varchar [note: 'Number of checks in the batch that were returned due to non-sufficient funds.']
  posted_date date [note: 'Date the lockbox batch was posted to the general ledger and accounts receivable subledger.']
  processed_date date [note: 'Date the lockbox batch was processed and payment data was extracted by the lockbox provider.']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the lockbox provider for processing this batch.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the lockbox batch was received by the lockbox provider from the postal service or electronic channel.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the lockbox batch has been reconciled between bank deposits and payment applications.']
  total_applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of payments successfully applied to tenant accounts receivable.']
  total_batch_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all payments in the lockbox batch before adjustments.']
  total_check_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical checks included in this lockbox batch.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual tenant payments included in this lockbox batch.']
  total_rejected_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of payments rejected due to insufficient information, NSF, or other exceptions.']
  total_unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of payments not yet applied to specific tenant invoices or charges.']
  transmission_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the electronic data file transmitted by the lockbox provider containing batch payment details.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the lockbox provider transmitted the batch data file to the property management system.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the bank deposit amount and the total applied payment amount, indicating reconciliation discrepancies.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the lockbox batch was voided.']
  voided_date date [note: 'Date the lockbox batch was voided and reversed.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lockbox_batch. Referenced by lockbox_batch_id.'
}

Table finance.lender {
  lender_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lender']
  parent_lender_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lender (parent_lender_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the lender\'s primary business address, typically street address.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the lender\'s primary business address, typically suite or floor number.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the lender\'s primary business office is located.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the lender\'s primary business location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lender record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Current credit rating of the lending institution as assigned by major rating agencies.']
  credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the credit rating agency that assigned the credit rating.']
  credit_rating_date date [note: 'Date when the current credit rating was assigned or last updated.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the lender\'s primary lending currency.']
  debt_service_coverage_ratio_min decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum debt service coverage ratio required by this lender for loan approval, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.25 for 1.25x coverage).']
  federal_reserve_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing number for the lender, used for wire transfers and ACH transactions.']
  geographic_focus varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or markets where this lender actively provides financing.']
  lender_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique code or identifier for the lender, used in loan documentation and financial systems.']
  lender_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the lending institution or financial entity.']
  lender_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lender by institution type.']
  lending_capacity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum lending capacity or credit line available from this lender for real estate financing.']
  loan_to_value_ratio_max decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum loan-to-value ratio that this lender will accept for real estate financing, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 for 75%).']
  maximum_loan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum loan amount that this lender will underwrite for a single real estate transaction.']
  minimum_loan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum loan amount that this lender will underwrite for real estate transactions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lender record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the lender, including special terms, relationship history, or underwriting preferences.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the lender\'s primary business address.']
  preferred_lender_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lender has preferred status for financing transactions.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the main contact at the lending institution.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the lending institution.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the main contact at the lending institution.']
  property_types_financed varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of property types that this lender will finance (e.g., multifamily, office, retail, industrial, mixed-use).']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this lender was terminated or ended, if applicable.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this lender was established.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the lender\'s primary business office is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the lender relationship in the system lifecycle.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) code for international wire transfers.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number for the lending institution.']
  typical_interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Typical or baseline interest rate offered by this lender, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0525 for 5.25%).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the lending institution.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for lender. Referenced by lender_id.'
}

Table finance.netting_run {
  netting_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for netting_run']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting period for which this netting run was executed, ensuring proper period alignment for financial reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the netting run results, supporting audit trail and segregation of duties requirements.']
  executed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated and executed the netting run, supporting audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary legal entity or consolidation scope for which this netting run was performed, supporting multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  prior_netting_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on netting_run (prior_netting_run_id)']
  reversed_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original netting run that this run reverses, establishing the audit trail for corrections and adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the netting run results have been reviewed and approved by authorized financial personnel per SOX controls.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the netting run was approved, establishing the point at which results became official for financial reporting.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the netting run finished processing and all netting calculations were finalized. Null if run is not yet completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this netting run record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the netting run amounts are denominated.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'The number of transactions that could not be netted due to mismatches, data quality issues, or business rule violations.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the netting run was initiated and began processing intercompany or inter-entity transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number (1-12 or 1-13 for 13-period calendars) within the fiscal year for which this netting run was executed.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this netting run applies, supporting multi-year financial analysis and historical comparisons.']
  gl_batch_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL batch that contains the journal entries generated from this netting run, enabling traceability to posted transactions.']
  gl_posting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the netting run results have been posted to the general ledger and the current state of that posting.']
  gl_posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the netting run results were posted to the general ledger. Null if not yet posted.']
  netted_transaction_count varchar [note: 'The number of transactions that were successfully netted and offset against counterparty positions in this run.']
  netting_method varchar [note: 'The netting methodology applied in this run, determining how offsetting positions are calculated and settled across entities.']
  netting_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the organizational scope of the netting run, indicating which entities or properties are included in the netting calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, explanations, or special circumstances related to this netting run execution.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this netting run represents a reversal of a previously posted netting run, supporting error correction and period adjustments.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the netting run, typically formatted as NR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the netting run based on its purpose and timing within the financial close cycle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system from which the netting run data was sourced, supporting data lineage and reconciliation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the netting run indicating its processing state and readiness for downstream consumption.']
  total_gross_payables decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of payables identified for netting before any offsetting adjustments are applied.']
  total_gross_receivables decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of receivables identified for netting before any offsetting adjustments are applied.']
  total_netted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount after offsetting receivables and payables, representing the final settlement obligation or entitlement.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual transactions processed and included in this netting run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this netting run record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for netting_run. Referenced by netting_run_id.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ledger']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property associated with this ledger, if applicable (for property-level ledgers).']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts structure used by this ledger.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this ledger record.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this ledger record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity that owns or operates this ledger.']
  parent_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ledger in a hierarchical ledger structure, if applicable.']
  accounting_basis varchar [note: 'The accounting method applied to this ledger (accrual, cash, or modified accrual basis).']
  audit_trail_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed audit trail logging is enabled for all transactions posted to this ledger.']
  auto_reversal_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic reversal of accrual entries is enabled for this ledger.']
  budget_tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether budget versus actual tracking and variance analysis is enabled for this ledger.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is included in consolidated financial reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Default cost center code associated with this ledger for expense allocation and tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency of the ledger.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the ledger\'s purpose, scope, and usage within the financial system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ledger ceases to be active for transaction posting. Null for open-ended ledgers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this ledger becomes active and available for transaction posting.']
  encumbrance_accounting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether encumbrance accounting (commitment tracking) is enabled for this ledger.']
  external_ledger_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier or code used by upstream source systems to identify this ledger.']
  fiscal_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) indicating the start of the fiscal year for this ledger.']
  intercompany_elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany transactions in this ledger are subject to elimination during consolidation.']
  journal_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether journal entries posted to this ledger require managerial approval before posting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was last updated or modified.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ledger, used for external reference and reporting.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ledger (e.g., General Ledger, Property Ledger, Subsidiary Ledger).']
  ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ledger in the financial system lifecycle.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger by its functional purpose within the financial system.']
  multi_currency_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger supports transactions in multiple currencies with automatic conversion.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this ledger.']
  period_close_day varchar [note: 'The day of the month (1-31) on which accounting periods are typically closed for this ledger.']
  reporting_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment or division classification for financial reporting and analysis purposes.']
  retained_earnings_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code used for retained earnings in this ledger\'s chart of accounts.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system of record for this ledger (e.g., YARDI, SAP_S4HANA).']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance controls and audit requirements.']
  suspense_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code used for suspense or unallocated transactions in this ledger.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this ledger record, incremented with each modification for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for profit_center']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset associated with this profit center, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated cost center for expense allocation and tracking.']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent profit center in the organizational hierarchy for roll-up reporting.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Real estate asset class category for this profit center if property-based.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budgeted amount allocated to this profit center for the current fiscal year.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or division name to which this profit center is assigned.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code to which this profit center belongs for statutory reporting.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is included in corporate financial consolidation reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the profit center is domiciled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial reporting of this profit center.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the profit center purpose, scope, and operational focus.']
  ebitda_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target EBITDA for this profit center for the current fiscal period.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this profit center was closed or became inactive. Null for currently active profit centers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this profit center became active and began recording financial transactions.']
  gl_account_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending account number in the chart of accounts range assigned to this profit center.']
  gl_account_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting account number in the chart of accounts range assigned to this profit center.']
  investment_strategy varchar [note: 'Investment strategy classification for portfolio management and risk assessment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was last modified or updated.']
  noi_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target Net Operating Income for this profit center for the current fiscal period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this profit center.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the profit center in financial systems and reports.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the profit center as used in financial reporting and management communications.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center by organizational function or asset type.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area where the profit center operates.']
  reporting_segment varchar [note: 'Financial reporting segment classification for external investor and regulatory reporting.']
  responsible_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the manager responsible for this profit center.']
  responsible_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager or executive responsible for the profit center performance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this profit center record originated.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this profit center in the originating source system.']
  sox_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is subject to SOX compliance controls and audit requirements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the profit center in the financial system lifecycle.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary tax jurisdiction or authority governing this profit center for tax reporting purposes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for profit_center. Referenced by profit_center_id.'
}

Table finance.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property associated with this segment. Used for property-level financial reporting and analysis.']
  parent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent segment in a hierarchical segment structure, enabling roll-up reporting and consolidation. Null for top-level segments.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio this segment belongs to. Used for portfolio-level performance measurement and investor reporting.']
  allows_posting boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether financial transactions can be directly posted to this segment. Parent/summary segments typically do not allow posting.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Real estate asset class classification for this segment. Used for asset class performance benchmarking and investment strategy analysis.']
  budget_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether budgeting and forecasting are enabled for this segment. Controls availability in budget planning modules.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to aggregate child segment values into this segment for financial consolidation and roll-up reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated cost center code for expense allocation and management reporting. Links segment to operational cost tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the segment\'s purpose, scope, and usage guidelines for financial reporting and analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment is no longer active for new transactions. Null for currently active segments. Used for historical reporting and audit trails.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment becomes active and available for use in financial transactions and reporting. Used for temporal validity.']
  external_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this segment\'s financial data is included in external financial statements and regulatory filings.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market designation for this segment. Used for regional performance analysis and market trend reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of the segment\'s depth in the organizational hierarchy, with 1 representing the top level. Used for reporting aggregation and drill-down navigation.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path from root to this segment, typically represented as a delimited string of segment codes or IDs, enabling efficient ancestor queries.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this segment is used for intercompany transactions requiring elimination during consolidation.']
  is_leaf_node boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this segment is a leaf node in the hierarchy (has no children). Leaf nodes typically receive direct transaction postings.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this segment record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last updated in the system. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the segment manager for financial reporting notifications and budget variance alerts.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for managing this segment\'s financial performance and budget accountability.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated profit center code for revenue and profitability analysis. Used in Net Operating Income (NOI) and EBITDA calculations.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this segment\'s financial results are reported. Used for multi-currency consolidation.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'Broad category grouping segments by their financial purpose and treatment in financial statements.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the segment in the chart of accounts structure. Used as the business identifier for external reporting and system integration.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the financial segment for human-readable identification and reporting purposes.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment indicating its role in the financial hierarchy. Determines how the segment is used in financial consolidation and reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system of record for this segment (Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA, or manual entry). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this segment is subject to SOX financial controls and audit requirements. Determines additional validation and approval workflows.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment indicating whether it is available for use in financial transactions and reporting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this segment record. Used for audit and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for segment. Referenced by segment_id.'
}

Table finance.source_document {
  source_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for source_document']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property or real estate asset to which this source document is associated for property-level financial tracking.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account to which this source document is posted for financial statement classification.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center or organizational unit to which this source document is allocated for budgeting and expense tracking.']
  reversed_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original source document that this document reverses or corrects, if applicable.']
  superseded_source_document_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on source_document (superseded_source_document_id)']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tenant party associated with this source document for accounts receivable and lease revenue tracking.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier party associated with this source document for accounts payable tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the source document within the financial approval workflow and authorization hierarchy.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the source document for posting and payment processing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the source document was approved by the authorized approver. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the source document record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the source document amounts are denominated.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or narrative providing additional context and details about the source document and its business purpose.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total discount or reduction applied to the source document amount.']
  document_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the source document before any adjustments, taxes, or discounts.']
  document_date date [note: 'The official date assigned to the source document, representing when the business transaction or event occurred. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier or reference number assigned to the source document (e.g., invoice number, receipt number, contract reference).']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the source document within the financial workflow and approval process.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source document indicating its business purpose and nature within the financial system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy or scanned image of the physical source document stored in the document management system.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment for the source document is due, calculated based on payment terms. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the document currency to the reporting currency, if applicable.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-01) to which this source document is assigned for financial reporting purposes.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this source document belongs for annual financial reporting and consolidation.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this source document is part of a recurring transaction series (e.g., monthly rent invoice).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the source document record was last modified or updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net monetary value of the source document after applying all taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or annotations related to the source document for internal reference and audit purposes.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the source document indicating whether payment has been received or made.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for the source document, specifying due date calculation rules (e.g., Net 30, Due on Receipt).']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the source document was posted to the general ledger and became part of the official financial records. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier linking this source document to related documents, purchase orders, or contracts.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this source document represents a reversal or correction of a previously posted document.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the originating system from which the source document was created or imported (e.g., Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or key of the source document in the originating system, used for reconciliation and traceability.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount associated with the source document, including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for source_document. Referenced by source_document_id.'
}

Table finance.reporting_period {
  reporting_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reporting_period']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who closed the reporting period. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  locked_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who locked the reporting period. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  parent_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent reporting period for hierarchical period structures (e.g., monthly period rolling up to quarterly period). Null for top-level periods.']
  prior_reporting_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reporting_period (prior_reporting_period_id)']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget associated with this reporting period (e.g., \'Original\', \'Revised Q2\', \'Final\'). Used to track budget revisions and forecasts.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month within the calendar year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter within the calendar year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Null for non-quarterly periods.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year to which this reporting period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when the reporting period was officially closed for new transactions. Null if period is still open. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reporting period record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes about the reporting period, including any special circumstances or adjustments.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last date of the reporting period (inclusive). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Null for non-quarterly periods.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this reporting period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is designated for year-end or period-end adjustments (e.g., 13th period in some accounting systems).']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the current active reporting period for financial transactions and reporting.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reporting period falls within a leap year (366 days). Relevant for daily and annual period calculations.']
  locked_date date [note: 'The date when the reporting period was locked and finalized. Null if period is not locked. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reporting period record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  number_of_business_days varchar [note: 'Total number of business days (excluding weekends and holidays) in the reporting period.']
  number_of_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in the reporting period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reporting period, used for system integration and reporting (e.g., \'2024Q1\', \'202401\', \'FY2024\').']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the reporting period (e.g., \'Q1 2024\', \'January 2024\', \'FY2024\').']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reporting period granularity (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual).']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the primary reporting currency used in this period (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number for the reporting period within its fiscal year or parent period hierarchy.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this reporting period was created or synchronized (e.g., \'YARDI\', \'SAP\', \'MANUAL\').']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date of the reporting period (inclusive). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reporting period. Open: transactions can be posted; Closed: period is closed for new transactions; Locked: period is finalized and cannot be reopened; Archived: historical period retained for compliance.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for reporting_period. Referenced by reporting_period_id.'
}

Table finance.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for requisition']
  amended_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on requisition (amended_requisition_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the requisition was formally approved by the designated approver.']
  approval_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approval workflow template or routing rule applied to this requisition based on amount thresholds and organizational policies.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the requisition was approved by the authorized approver.']
  approver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager who approved this requisition for processing.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'Budget period (quarterly or annual) to which this requisition is allocated for financial planning purposes.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year against which this requisition is being charged for budget tracking and variance analysis.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation for why a requisition was cancelled after submission or approval.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this requisition was closed after fulfillment or cancellation, marking the end of its lifecycle.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master contract or service agreement under which this requisition is being processed, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center to which this requisition expense will be charged for budgeting and financial reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the requisition amount is denominated.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Physical address where goods or services should be delivered or performed for this requisition.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department or business unit that initiated this requisition for organizational reporting purposes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the goods or services being requisitioned, including specifications and business justification.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the general ledger account to which this requisition will be posted for financial accounting purposes.']
  is_capital_expenditure boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this requisition represents a capital expenditure that will be capitalized on the balance sheet rather than expensed immediately.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this requisition is part of a recurring expense pattern such as monthly service contracts or subscriptions.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and justification for the requisition explaining why the expense is necessary and how it supports business objectives.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this requisition record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or comments related to the requisition for communication between requestor, approver, and procurement team.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms and conditions negotiated with the vendor for this requisition, such as net 30, net 60, or payment upon delivery.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this requisition indicating urgency of fulfillment.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capital project or development initiative to which this requisition is allocated, if applicable.']
  property_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property for which this requisition is being raised. Links requisition to specific real estate asset.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number generated from this requisition once approved and converted to a formal purchase order.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Textual explanation provided by the approver when a requisition is rejected, documenting the rationale for denial.']
  requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or user who initiated this requisition request.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Target date by which the goods or services must be delivered or completed to meet business needs.']
  requisition_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the requisition representing the estimated or quoted cost of goods or services being requested.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the requisition was created or submitted by the requestor.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique requisition number used for tracking and reference in financial systems and approval workflows.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition in the approval and procurement workflow.']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requisition based on the nature of the expense: purchase of goods, service contract, capital expenditure, maintenance, repair, or property improvement.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method of shipment or delivery requested for goods associated with this requisition.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the requisition was submitted for approval.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this requisition based on jurisdiction and tax rules.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount including base requisition amount and all applicable taxes and fees.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom goods or services are being requisitioned.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for requisition. Referenced by requisition_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory obligation record in the master registry. Primary key for the regulatory_obligation data product.']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Regulatory obligations specify required evidence document types (e.g., inspection certificate, audit report, HUD filing). Real estate compliance managers configure document workflows and retention sch']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: regulatory_obligation has jurisdiction_country, jurisdiction_state, and jurisdiction_municipality as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference m']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Obligations are scoped to property types (e.g., residential fire codes vs. commercial ADA requirements). Real estate compliance teams filter applicable obligations by property type during asset onboar']
  reference_investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.investment_vehicle. Business justification: Certain obligations (SEC Form requirements, REIT distribution rules, ERISA compliance) apply only to specific investment vehicle types. Real estate fund compliance officers scope obligations to vehicl']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Regulatory obligations are grounded in specific frameworks (SOX, GDPR, Fair Housing Act). Real estate compliance officers scope obligations by framework for audit planning and regulatory reporting. co']
  applicable_asset_class varchar [note: 'Specific real estate asset class to which this obligation applies (e.g., \'Office\', \'Retail\', \'Multifamily\', \'Industrial\', \'Hotel\', \'Data Center\'). Narrows applicability scope beyond property type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: office|retail|multifamily|industrial|hotel|data_center|healthcare|self_storage|all — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory obligation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of the specific documentation, reports, or filings required to demonstrate compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Phase I ESA Report\', \'Annual 10-K Filing\', \'LEED Scorecard\', \'GDPR Data Processing Agreement\').']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation becomes legally binding and enforceable. Marks the start of the compliance window.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification for this obligation. Supports ESG reporting and portfolio sustainability analytics. \'None\' for obligations not related to ESG.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation ceases to be enforceable or is superseded. Null for open-ended obligations with no defined sunset date.']
  external_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the official regulatory body\'s online portal or filing system where compliance submissions for this obligation are made (e.g., EPA\'s CDX portal, SEC EDGAR, HUD IDIS).']
  filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the renewal date by which the compliance filing or documentation must be submitted to the governing body. Used to calculate advance preparation timelines.']
  gdpr_data_category varchar [note: 'Classification of personal data category under GDPR for privacy-related obligations. \'special_category\' refers to sensitive data per GDPR Article 9 (e.g., health, biometric). \'not_applicable\' for non-GDPR obligations.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or standards body that issued and enforces this obligation (e.g., \'Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)\', \'Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)\', \'U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)\').']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is legally mandatory (True) or voluntary/best-practice (False). Mandatory obligations carry legal enforcement risk; voluntary ones (e.g., LEED certification) are pursued for strategic or ESG reasons.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires periodic renewal or re-filing (True) or is a one-time requirement (False). Drives automated renewal scheduling in the compliance calendar.']
  leed_breeam_level varchar [note: 'Target or required certification level for LEED or BREEAM obligations. Applicable only when compliance_framework is \'LEED\' or \'BREEAM\'. \'not_applicable\' for non-certification obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certified|silver|gold|platinum|pass|good|very_good|excellent|outstanding|not_applicable — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty or fine that can be imposed for non-compliance with this obligation, expressed in the obligation currency. Supports risk quantification and materiality assessment.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the compliance filing, certification, or permit renewal must be completed for this obligation. Drives compliance calendar and deadline management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, interpretive guidance, legal counsel annotations, or operational notes related to this regulatory obligation. Not used for structured data.']
  obligation_category varchar [note: 'Jurisdictional tier of the regulatory obligation, indicating whether it originates from a federal, state, local, or international governing body.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this regulatory obligation (e.g., EPA-PHASE1-001, OSHA-1910-001). Used as the business identifier across systems and filings.']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the regulatory obligation, including its purpose, scope, and key compliance requirements as defined by the governing body.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive name of the regulatory obligation (e.g., \'Phase I Environmental Site Assessment\', \'OSHA Hazard Communication Standard\'). Used as the primary identity label in reports and dashboards.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the regulatory obligation by subject matter domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|privacy|financial_reporting|zoning|building_code|esg|certification|other — promote to reference product]']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the maximum penalty amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance with this obligation (e.g., \'Civil penalty up to $25,000 per day per violation under 40 CFR Part 312\').']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Official citation or reference number for the regulation, statute, or standard (e.g., \'40 CFR Part 312\', \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200\', \'ASC 842\', \'IFRS 16\', \'GDPR Article 5\'). Enables direct traceability to the source regulatory text.']
  regulation_version varchar [note: 'Version or amendment number of the regulation or standard to which this obligation corresponds. Tracks regulatory changes over time (e.g., \'2023 Amendment\', \'Rev. 4\', \'LEED v4.1\').']
  regulatory_obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory obligation in the master registry. \'Active\' means currently enforceable; \'superseded\' means replaced by a newer regulation; \'under_review\' means applicability is being assessed.']
  renewal_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which this regulatory obligation must be renewed, re-filed, or re-certified (e.g., \'annual\' for SEC REIT filings, \'triennial\' for LEED recertification, \'one_time\' for Phase I ESA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|biennial|triennial|quarterly|monthly|one_time|as_needed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requires_third_party_audit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this obligation must be verified by an independent third-party auditor or certifying body (e.g., LEED certification requires USGBC-approved verifier, Phase I ESA requires licensed environmental professional).']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Internal business department or team accountable for managing and fulfilling this regulatory obligation (e.g., \'Legal & Compliance\', \'Asset Management\', \'Property Management\', \'Finance\', \'ESG & Sustainability\').']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Job title or role designation of the individual accountable for this obligation (e.g., \'Chief Compliance Officer\', \'Asset Manager (AM)\', \'Property Manager (PM)\', \'Environmental Health & Safety Manager\').']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of non-compliance with this obligation, reflecting the severity of potential penalties, reputational impact, and likelihood of regulatory scrutiny. Used to prioritize compliance activities.']
  sec_form_type varchar [note: 'SEC filing form type associated with this obligation for publicly traded REITs (e.g., \'10-K\', \'10-Q\', \'8-K\', \'S-11\', \'DEF 14A\'). Null for non-SEC obligations.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding record in the originating source system (e.g., Yardi Voyager compliance module record ID, SAP compliance item number). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this regulatory obligation record originates or is primarily managed (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager\' for lease compliance, \'SAP S/4HANA\' for financial regulatory obligations, \'Procore\' for construction permits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Yardi Voyager|SAP S/4HANA|Procore|Building Engines|DocuSign CLM|Manual|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory obligation record. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all regulatory obligations applicable to the portfolio — federal, state, and local requirements including EPA environmental mandates, OSHA building safety codes, HUD fair housing rules, GDPR tenant data privacy, SEC REIT reporting, and municipal zoning compliance. Each obligation captures the governing body, regulation reference, jurisdiction, applicability scope (property type, asset class), effective dates, and renewal cadence. Serves as the authoritative catalog against which compliance activities are tracked.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_program {
  compliance_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the enterprise compliance program record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: program has jurisdiction_level, jurisdiction_country, and jurisdiction_state as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consi']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance programs in real estate (AML, fair housing, environmental) are owned by specific employees accountable for program performance. Linking enables program ownership succession planning, accoun']
  reference_investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.investment_vehicle. Business justification: Compliance programs (REIT compliance, SEC reporting, ERISA) are specific to investment vehicle types. Real estate fund managers need to link compliance programs to vehicle types to determine which pro']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Compliance programs are structured around regulatory frameworks (SOX program, GDPR program, LEED certification program). Real estate compliance managers link programs to frameworks for audit scoping, ']
