  accommodation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of reasonable accommodation or modification requested under the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) or Fair Housing Act (FHA). Applicable when record_type is \'accommodation_request\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical_modification|policy_exception|service_animal|accessible_unit|parking|communication_aid|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ada_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) is the primary governing statute for this fair housing record (True), particularly for accommodation requests and accessibility compliance.']
  assigned_investigator varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal compliance officer or external investigator assigned to handle the fair housing complaint or investigation.']
  complaint_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the specific nature of a discrimination complaint received. Applicable when record_type is \'complaint\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: discrimination|harassment|retaliation|failure_to_accommodate|steering|redlining|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fair housing compliance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and regulatory record-keeping obligations.']
  ecoa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) applies to this fair housing record (True), such as cases involving credit decisions in real estate transactions.']
  hud_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case reference number assigned by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) for a formal complaint, audit, or investigation. Used to cross-reference HUD records and track regulatory correspondence.']
  hud_filing_date date [note: 'Date on which the complaint or response was formally filed with HUD. Used to track regulatory submission deadlines and audit timelines.']
  hud_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the organization must submit a formal response to HUD in connection with an audit, complaint, or investigation. Supports regulatory deadline tracking.']
  hud_response_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the organization submitted its formal response to HUD. Used to verify timely compliance with regulatory deadlines.']
  incident_date date [note: 'Date on which the alleged fair housing violation, accommodation request, or compliance event occurred. Represents the real-world event date, distinct from the record filing date.']
  internal_case_number varchar [note: 'Organization\'s internal reference number assigned to this fair housing compliance matter, used for internal tracking, audit trails, and cross-referencing with legal and compliance management systems.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal or external investigation into the fair housing matter was formally initiated.']
  legal_counsel_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether legal counsel has been engaged to represent the organization in connection with this fair housing matter (True) or not (False).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to document additional context, investigative findings, correspondence summaries, or case-specific observations not captured in structured fields. Classified as confidential.']
  property_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the property associated with this fair housing record is residential, commercial, or mixed-use. Determines applicable fair housing statutes and HUD reporting requirements.']
  protected_class_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of protected class categories involved in this fair housing record (e.g., race, color, national_origin, religion, sex, familial_status, disability, source_of_income). Captures all applicable protected classes per the Fair Housing Act and applicable state/local laws.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date on which the complaint, accommodation request, or compliance event was formally received and logged by the compliance team. Used to track response deadlines and regulatory timelines.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of this fair housing compliance record. Values include: complaint (discrimination allegation received), accommodation_request (ADA/FHA reasonable accommodation), training_completion (staff fair housing training), hud_audit_response (response to HUD audit), investigation (internal or external investigation), self_audit (proactive internal compliance review).']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the fair housing matter was formally resolved, closed, or concluded — whether through dismissal, settlement, conciliation, or administrative closure.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination or outcome of the fair housing investigation or complaint. Captures the substantive result upon closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_cause_found|cause_found|conciliation_agreement|settlement|administrative_closure|referred_to_doj|other — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fair housing record. Tracks the workflow state from initial receipt through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|resolved|dismissed|settled|withdrawn|referred_to_hud — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any financial settlement reached in connection with a fair housing complaint or investigation. Expressed in USD. Classified as confidential business-sensitive data.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the settlement amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Supports multi-jurisdictional portfolio operations.']
  settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the terms agreed upon in a conciliation agreement or settlement, including any non-monetary remedies, policy changes, or training requirements imposed. Classified as confidential.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this fair housing record originated (e.g., Yardi Voyager, Salesforce CRM, MRI Software, HUD portal, or manual entry by compliance staff).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fair housing compliance record. Used for change tracking, audit trails, and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Tracks HUD fair housing compliance activities across residential and commercial leasing operations — fair housing training completions, accommodation requests (ADA/FHA), discrimination complaints received, investigation outcomes, and HUD audit responses. Captures protected class categories involved, complaint type, resolution status, settlement terms if applicable, and HUD case reference numbers. Supports compliance with the Fair Housing Act, Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), and Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA).'
}

Table compliance.privacy_request {
  privacy_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each data subject rights request record. Primary key for the privacy_request data product.']
  assigned_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA privacy requests from tenants/investors in real estate are assigned to specific employees (DPO, privacy officers) for response within statutory deadlines. Linking enables SLA breach tracking']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Privacy requests are managed under enterprise data privacy compliance programs (GDPR program, CCPA program). Linking privacy_request to program enables program-level privacy request tracking and regul']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Privacy requests relate to specific compliance requirements derived from privacy regulations (e.g., 30-day response requirement under GDPR, 45-day response requirement under CCPA). Linking privacy_req']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: privacy_request has a jurisdiction string field. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes this to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdictional privacy request management and re']
  lead_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.lead. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA privacy requests (erasure, access, portability) are most commonly submitted by marketing leads requesting deletion of their contact data. Linking privacy_request to the specific lead record ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Privacy requests are governed by specific regulatory obligations (GDPR Article 17 erasure obligation, CCPA right-to-know obligation). The existing applicable_regulation string is a denormalized repres']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tenant.tenant. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA data subject access, erasure, and portability requests are submitted by tenants. Property managers must respond within statutory SLA windows. Linking privacy_request to the tenant profile en']
  agent_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorised agent or legal representative submitting the request on behalf of the data subject. Populated only when authorised_agent is True. Classified as restricted PII.']
  authorised_agent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was submitted by an authorised agent acting on behalf of the data subject rather than the data subject themselves. Triggers additional verification requirements under CCPA.']
  consent_record_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the original consent record being withdrawn, where request_type is consent_withdrawal. Links to the consent management register to ensure the withdrawal is actioned against the correct consent instance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this privacy request record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  cross_border_transfer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the data subject\'s personal data has been transferred to a country outside the EEA or the requestor\'s home jurisdiction. Triggers disclosure of transfer safeguards in the SAR response.']
  data_categories_in_scope varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the categories of personal data in scope for this request (e.g., lease contact details, payment history, maintenance service request records, employment records). Aligns with GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) data category taxonomy.']
  dpo_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request requires formal review and sign-off by the Data Protection Officer (DPO) before a response is issued. Typically triggered for complex, sensitive, or high-risk requests.']
  erasure_scope varchar [note: 'For right-to-erasure requests, describes the specific personal data records and systems targeted for deletion. Documents what was erased, partially erased, or retained (with legal justification). Null for non-erasure request types.']
  extended_deadline date [note: 'The revised response deadline when a one-time extension has been granted (e.g., GDPR allows up to 2 additional months for complex requests; CCPA allows a 45-day extension). Null if no extension was invoked.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the requestor\'s identity before processing the request. Supports audit evidence for regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: government_id|account_login|email_confirmation|knowledge_based|notarised_id|agent_authorisation — promote to reference product]']
  identity_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requestor\'s identity has been successfully verified prior to processing the request. Organisations must verify identity before fulfilling access or erasure requests to prevent unauthorised disclosure.']
  intake_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the privacy request was received (e.g., web_portal, email, phone, postal mail, in-person at a leasing office, or via Salesforce CRM). Supports channel analytics and process improvement.']
  legal_basis_for_processing varchar [note: 'The GDPR Article 6 lawful basis under which the organisation originally processed the data subject\'s personal data. Critical for evaluating erasure and objection requests — e.g., legitimate_interests processing may be overridden by the data subject\'s objection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes recorded by the privacy team during request processing. May include identity verification observations, legal counsel input, or escalation history. Not shared with the data subject.']
  objection_grounds varchar [note: 'The data subject\'s stated grounds for objecting to processing of their personal data (e.g., direct marketing, profiling, legitimate interests). Populated for objection_to_processing request type under GDPR Article 21.']
  outcome_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the final outcome and actions taken to fulfil the request. Provides a human-readable audit record of what data was accessed, erased, ported, or restricted, and any exceptions applied.']
  portability_format varchar [note: 'The machine-readable format in which personal data was provided to the data subject for data portability requests (e.g., CSV, JSON). Applicable only for data_portability request type under GDPR Article 20.']
  processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Description of the purpose(s) for which the data subject\'s personal data was processed (e.g., lease administration, tenant relationship management, payroll processing). Required for GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and for responding to subject access requests under Article 15.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time the privacy request was received by the organisation. This is the principal business event timestamp that starts the regulatory response clock under GDPR (30-day) and CCPA (45-day) deadlines.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the supervisory authority was notified, where regulatory_notification_required is True. Supports evidence of timely notification within the 72-hour GDPR Article 33 window.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request or its outcome triggers a mandatory notification to a supervisory authority (e.g., a data breach discovered during SAR processing that requires GDPR Article 33 notification to the ICO or relevant DPA).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'The documented legal or factual basis for rejecting or partially fulfilling the request (e.g., data retention required by law, identity verification failed, request is manifestly unfounded). Required for regulatory defensibility when request_status is rejected or partially_fulfilled.']
  request_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-communicated, human-readable reference number assigned to the request at intake (e.g., PRV-2024-000123). Used in all correspondence with the data subject and in regulatory audit trails.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the privacy request. Tracks progression from intake through resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|in_review|pending_verification|fulfilled|partially_fulfilled|rejected|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data subject rights request. subject_access = GDPR Art. 15 / SAR; right_to_erasure = GDPR Art. 17 / CCPA deletion; data_portability = GDPR Art. 20; consent_withdrawal = GDPR Art. 7(3); objection_to_processing = GDPR Art. 21; opt_out_of_sale = CCPA Section 1798.120.']
  requestor_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the data subject or their authorised representative. Primary channel for request acknowledgement, identity verification, and response delivery. Classified as restricted PII.']
  requestor_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual submitting the data subject rights request. Required for identity verification and correspondence. Classified as restricted PII.']
  requestor_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the data subject or their authorised representative. Used as a secondary verification channel. Classified as restricted PII.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data subject\'s relationship to the real estate organisation. Determines which data systems and processing activities are in scope for the request (e.g., tenant data in Yardi Voyager, employee data in ADP Workforce Now).']
  response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the organisation sent its formal response to the data subject. Used to measure compliance with statutory deadlines and to calculate response cycle time.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'The statutory deadline by which the organisation must respond to the request. Calculated from received_timestamp per applicable regulation (e.g., 30 calendar days under GDPR Art. 12(3), 45 days under CCPA). May be extended once with notification.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the data subject\'s personal data is retained under the applicable retention schedule. Required disclosure under GDPR Article 15(1)(d) for subject access requests and documented in the RoPA under Article 30(1)(f).']
  ropa_record_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this privacy request to the corresponding GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) entry that documents the processing activity in scope. Enables traceability between individual requests and the organisation\'s processing register.']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organisation failed to respond within the statutory or internal SLA deadline. Derived operationally from response_date vs response_deadline but stored as a business flag to support compliance reporting and regulatory audit submissions.']
  systems_in_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of operational systems of record that hold personal data relevant to this request (e.g., Yardi Voyager, Salesforce CRM, ADP Workforce Now, Building Engines). Guides the data retrieval or erasure workflow across the technology estate.']
  third_party_processors varchar [note: 'Names of third-party data processors or recipients to whom the data subject\'s personal data has been disclosed (e.g., cloud hosting providers, payroll processors, credit check agencies). Required disclosure under GDPR Article 15(1)(c) for subject access requests.']
  transfer_mechanism varchar [note: 'The legal mechanism used to legitimise any cross-border transfer of the data subject\'s personal data (e.g., EU adequacy decision, Standard Contractual Clauses). Populated only when cross_border_transfer is True.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this privacy request record. Used for change tracking and audit trail integrity.']

  Note: 'Manages data subject rights requests under GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations from tenants, prospects, employees, and other individuals. Covers subject access requests (SARs), right to erasure, data portability, consent withdrawals, objection to processing, and opt-out of sale (CCPA). Also serves as the registry for GDPR Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) — documenting processing purposes, legal bases, data categories, retention periods, third-party processors, and cross-border transfer mechanisms. Captures request type, requestor identity, date received, response deadline, processing status, response date, and outcome. Supports GDPR Articles 15-22 and 30 compliance, CCPA consumer rights, and tenant data privacy obligations.'
}

Table compliance.esg_metric {
  esg_metric_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ESG metric record in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: ESG metric approval in real estate (GRESB, CDP, TCFD reporting) requires an authorized employee sign-off for data integrity and investor reporting credibility. Linking enables approval workflow tracki']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property at which this ESG metric was measured. Null when the metric is captured at portfolio or fund level rather than individual asset level.']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: ESG metrics are often verified or validated through formal compliance assessments (e.g., third-party ESG audits, GRESB assessments). Linking esg_metric to the verifying assessment enables audit trail ']
  compliance_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.green_certification. Business justification: ESG metrics are frequently captured in the context of green building certifications (LEED, BREEAM). The existing leed_certification_level and breeam_rating string fields are denormalized from the gree']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: ESG metrics are tracked and reported under specific compliance programs (e.g., GRESB reporting program, GRI standards program). Linking esg_metric to program enables program-level ESG performance aggr']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: ESG metrics fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., energy intensity reporting requirement, water consumption disclosure requirement). Linking esg_metric to requirement enables requirement-lev']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: ESG metrics are reported at fund level for GRESB submissions, investor ESG reporting, and fund-level sustainability disclosures. esg_metric already links to portfolio_id; fund-level ESG metrics suppor']
  fund_performance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund_performance. Business justification: ESG metrics feed into fund performance reporting (GRESB scores, ESG-adjusted returns, sustainability KPIs). Linking ESG metrics to fund performance periods supports integrated ESG and financial report']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: esg_metric has a jurisdiction string field. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes this to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdictional ESG reporting and benchmarking.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment portfolio or fund to which this ESG metric is attributed. Supports portfolio-level ESG roll-up reporting for REIT investor disclosures.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: ESG metrics are segmented by property type for GRESB reporting (office vs. retail vs. industrial energy intensity benchmarks differ). Real estate sustainability teams must report ESG performance by pr']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: ESG metrics fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., mandatory GHG emissions reporting under EPA regulations, energy disclosure under local benchmarking ordinances). Linking esg_metric to regula']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: ESG metrics (energy kWh, water m³, GHG tCO2e, waste tonnes) require normalized units of measure for cross-portfolio comparisons and GRESB/GRI/TCFD regulatory submissions. Real estate sustainability te']
  affordable_housing_units varchar [note: 'Number of residential units designated as affordable housing within the property or portfolio for the reporting period. Applicable for Social pillar metrics; null for non-residential or non-applicable metrics. Supports HUD fair housing compliance reporting.']
  anti_corruption_training_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of employees who completed mandatory anti-corruption and ethics training during the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar anti-corruption metrics; null otherwise.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was formally approved for disclosure. Null when metric_status is not Approved. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  baseline_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Reference baseline value against which performance targets and progress are measured (e.g., 2019 energy consumption baseline for net-zero pathway). Null when no formal baseline has been established.']
  baseline_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the baseline measurement used for target-setting and progress tracking (e.g., 2019). Null when no formal baseline year has been established.']
  board_diversity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of board members from underrepresented groups (gender, ethnicity, or combined diversity metric as defined by the organization\'s governance policy) for the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar board diversity metrics; null otherwise.']
  community_investment_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of community investment, charitable contributions, and social programs funded by the organization for the reporting period. Applicable for Social pillar community investment metrics; null otherwise.']
  coverage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total portfolio floor area or units for which actual measured data (as opposed to estimates) is available for this metric in the reporting period. A coverage of 100% indicates fully metered data; lower values indicate partial coverage with estimation applied to gaps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Operational system or external source from which the metric data was obtained (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager Utility Tracking\', \'Building Engines Work Order System\', \'Utility Provider Invoice\', \'ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager\', \'Manual Survey\'). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  esg_pillar varchar [note: 'High-level ESG pillar classification: Environmental, Social, or Governance. Drives framework mapping and investor disclosure categorization.']
  estimation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to fill data gaps where actual measured data is unavailable: Actual (fully metered), Extrapolation, Interpolation, Engineering Estimate, or Proxy. Supports GRESB data quality scoring and third-party assurance.']
  ethics_violations_count varchar [note: 'Number of confirmed ethics policy violations or code-of-conduct breaches recorded during the reporting period. Applicable for Governance pillar ethics metrics. Classified confidential due to sensitive governance nature.']
  framework_indicator_code varchar [note: 'Specific indicator or disclosure code within the reporting framework (e.g., \'GRI 302-1\', \'SASB IF-RE-130a.1\', \'GRESB EC1\', \'TCFD Metrics and Targets\'). Enables precise framework alignment mapping for regulatory and investor filings.']
  ghg_scope varchar [note: 'GHG Protocol emission scope classification applicable when the metric category is GHG Emissions: Scope 1 (direct emissions), Scope 2 (purchased energy indirect), Scope 3 (value chain indirect). Set to N/A for non-emissions metrics.']
  intensity_denominator varchar [note: 'The normalization basis used to compute the intensity value: SQM (square meters), SQF (square feet), GLA (Gross Leasable Area), NLA (Net Leasable Area), or revenue_usd. Null or N/A when intensity is not reported.']
  intensity_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Normalized intensity metric expressing the ESG value per unit of floor area or revenue (e.g., kWh per SQM, CO2e per SQF, m3 per SQM). Enables like-for-like benchmarking across properties of different sizes. Null when intensity normalization is not applicable.']
  is_landlord_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the energy, water, or utility consumption represented by this metric is under landlord operational control (True) or tenant-controlled (False). Critical for determining boundary of operational control under GHG Protocol and GRESB data collection scope.']
  is_like_for_like boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this metric record qualifies for like-for-like (LfL) comparison with the prior period — i.e., the property was owned and operational for the full duration of both the current and prior reporting periods with no significant structural changes. Required for GRESB like-for-like performance scoring.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology, protocol, or standard used to measure or calculate this ESG metric (e.g., \'GHG Protocol Corporate Standard Scope 2 market-based\', \'ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager metered data\', \'Utility bill aggregation via Yardi Voyager\'). Critical for data quality and third-party assurance.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'Sub-category within the ESG pillar (e.g., Energy, Water, GHG Emissions, Waste, Biodiversity, Affordable Housing, Community Investment, Tenant Satisfaction, Board Diversity, Ethics, Anti-Corruption). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Energy|Water|GHG Emissions|Waste|Biodiversity|Affordable Housing|Community Investment|Tenant Satisfaction|Board Diversity|Ethics|Anti-Corruption|Renewable Energy|Indoor Air Quality — promote to reference product]']
  metric_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the ESG metric type within the enterprise taxonomy (e.g., ENV-ENERGY-KWH, SOC-AFFORD-UNITS, GOV-BOARD-DIV). Aligns with GRESB indicator codes and internal ESG data dictionary.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ESG metric (e.g., \'Total Energy Consumption\', \'GHG Emissions Scope 1\', \'Affordable Housing Units\', \'Board Gender Diversity Ratio\'). Used in investor reports and regulatory filings.']
  metric_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this ESG metric record: Draft (data entry in progress), Submitted (submitted for review), Under Review (being reviewed by ESG team or auditor), Approved (finalized and approved for disclosure), Restated (previously published value corrected), Withdrawn (removed from disclosure).']
  metric_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Quantitative measured or reported value for the ESG metric for the specified reporting period (e.g., 1,250,000 kWh of energy consumed, 45.2 tonnes CO2e of GHG emissions, 120 affordable housing units). Precision supports both large energy figures and small fractional rates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, qualifications, or explanatory notes about this ESG metric record (e.g., reasons for data gaps, restatement explanations, boundary assumptions, or methodology deviations). Supports narrative disclosure in ESG reports.']
  prior_period_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Metric value for the immediately preceding comparable reporting period. Stored to support year-over-year variance analysis and trend reporting in investor ESG disclosures without requiring a self-join.']
  renewable_energy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewable energy (solar, wind, geothermal, etc.) for the reporting period. Applicable when metric_category is Energy or Renewable Energy; null otherwise. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']
  reporting_framework varchar [note: 'Primary ESG reporting framework under which this metric is disclosed: GRI (Global Reporting Initiative), SASB (Sustainability Accounting Standards Board), TCFD (Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures), GRESB (Global Real Estate Sustainability Benchmark), CDP, UNPRI, SFDR, Multiple, or Other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GRI|SASB|TCFD|GRESB|CDP|UNPRI|SFDR|Multiple|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the measurement or reporting period for this ESG metric (inclusive). Together with reporting_period_start defines the temporal boundary for the metric value.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the measurement or reporting period for this ESG metric (inclusive). Typically aligns with fiscal year, calendar year, or quarterly reporting cycle.']
  target_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Approved internal or externally committed target value for this metric for the reporting period (e.g., a 10% reduction in energy consumption vs. baseline). Supports performance-against-target reporting for ESG scorecards.']
  tenant_satisfaction_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate tenant satisfaction survey score for the property or portfolio for the reporting period, expressed on the survey scale (e.g., 0–100 or 1–5). Applicable for Social pillar tenant satisfaction metrics; null otherwise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ESG metric record was most recently modified. Tracks data revisions and supports restatement audit trails required by SEC and SOX.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification or assurance status of the ESG metric data: Unverified (raw), Internal Review (reviewed by internal ESG team), Third-Party Assured (externally assured), Limited Assurance, or Reasonable Assurance. Drives investor confidence and regulatory disclosure quality.']
  verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external assurance provider or auditor who verified this ESG metric (e.g., \'Deloitte\', \'KPMG\', \'Bureau Veritas\'). Null when verification_status is Unverified or Internal Review.']
  waste_diversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total waste diverted from landfill through recycling, composting, or reuse for the reporting period. Applicable when metric_category is Waste; null otherwise. Expressed as a percentage (0–100).']

  Note: 'Captures ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) performance metrics at the property and portfolio level for investor reporting and regulatory disclosure. Environmental metrics include energy consumption (kWh), water usage, GHG emissions (Scope 1/2/3 in CO2e), waste diversion rates, and renewable energy percentage. Social metrics cover affordable housing units, community investment, and tenant satisfaction. Governance metrics track board diversity, ethics violations, and anti-corruption measures. Each metric record captures the reporting period, measurement methodology, data source, and verification status. Supports ESG reporting frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD, GRESB) and REIT investor ESG disclosure requirements.'
}

Table compliance.green_certification {
  green_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for green_certification']
  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Green certifications record certified_area_sqft requiring a normalized UOM for cross-certification and cross-jurisdiction ESG reporting. Real estate sustainability teams need standardized area UOM for']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property for which this green certification has been obtained or is being pursued. Links the certification record to the master property entity.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Green certifications are pursued as part of enterprise compliance programs (e.g., LEED certification program, BREEAM program). Linking green_certification to program enables program-level certificatio']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Green certifications fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., energy efficiency requirements, water conservation requirements). Linking green_certification to requirement enables requirement-le']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: green_certification has jurisdiction_country and jurisdiction_state as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent mul']
  previous_certification_green_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior certification record for the same property and certification type, enabling certification history chain tracking and renewal lineage. Null for the initial certification.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Green certifications fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., mandatory energy benchmarking, green building code compliance). Linking green_certification to regulatory_obligation enables obligat']
  sustainability_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.sustainability_rating. Business justification: Green certifications (LEED Gold, BREEAM Excellent, ENERGY STAR) map directly to sustainability rating reference data including scoring thresholds and tier rankings. Real estate ESG teams link certific']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal application or registration for the certification was submitted to the certifying body. Marks the start of the certification pursuit lifecycle.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal on-site or documentary assessment was conducted by the certifying body or licensed assessor. Distinct from the application date and the certification award date.']
  assessor_accreditation varchar [note: 'The professional accreditation or credential held by the assessor (e.g., LEED AP BD+C, LEED AP O+M, BREEAM Assessor, WELL AP). Validates the assessor\'s authority to conduct the certification assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the licensed or accredited assessor, consultant, or LEED Accredited Professional (LEED AP) who conducted the certification assessment or managed the certification submission on behalf of the property.']
  audit_trail_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record requires a formal audit trail of supporting documentation for regulatory or certifying body verification purposes. True for certifications subject to third-party verification audits (e.g., ENERGY STAR verification, LEED documentation review).']
  breeam_category_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured summary of scores across BREEAM assessment categories (e.g., Energy, Water, Materials, Waste, Land Use & Ecology, Pollution, Transport, Health & Wellbeing, Management). Stored as a delimited string for BREEAM-type certifications; null for non-BREEAM certifications.']
  certification_date date [note: 'The date on which the certifying body officially awarded the certification to the property. Represents the commencement of the certified period. Used for ESG reporting timelines and green lease compliance.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'The tier or rating level achieved or targeted under the certification framework (e.g., LEED Platinum, LEED Gold, LEED Silver, LEED Certified; BREEAM Outstanding, Excellent, Very Good, Good, Pass; ENERGY STAR score tier). Varies by certification type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Platinum|Gold|Silver|Certified|Outstanding|Excellent|Very_Good|Good|Pass — promote to reference product]']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-issued unique identifier assigned by the certifying body (e.g., USGBC LEED project ID, ENERGY STAR certification number, BRE BREEAM certificate reference). Used for cross-referencing with the certifying body\'s registry.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification record. \'Pursuing\' indicates the property is in the application or assessment process; \'Certified\' indicates active certification; \'Expired\' indicates the certification period has lapsed; \'Withdrawn\' indicates the application was voluntarily withdrawn; \'Suspended\' indicates certification has been placed on hold by the certifying body.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the green building certification standard being applied. Identifies the framework under which the property is assessed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LEED|BREEAM|ENERGY_STAR|WELL|Fitwel|Living_Building_Challenge — promote to reference product if additional standards are adopted]']
  certification_version varchar [note: 'The specific version or edition of the certification standard under which the property was assessed (e.g., LEED v4.1, LEED v4, LEED 2009, BREEAM 2018, BREEAM In-Use v6). Critical for interpreting scoring criteria and prerequisites.']
  certified_area_sqft decimal(14,2) [note: 'The gross floor area (GFA) in square feet (SQF) covered by this certification. Used for ESG intensity metrics (e.g., energy use per SQF), green lease obligations, and portfolio-level certified area reporting.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'The organization responsible for issuing and administering the certification (e.g., USGBC for LEED, BRE for BREEAM, EPA for ENERGY STAR, IWBI for WELL Building Standard, Fitwel for Fitwel). Used for ESG reporting and audit trail purposes.']
  certifying_body_project_ref varchar [note: 'The project registration or tracking identifier assigned by the certifying body\'s online platform (e.g., USGBC LEED Online project ID, BREEAM Projects portal reference). Used for direct cross-referencing with the certifying body\'s registry and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this green certification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  energy_star_score varchar [note: 'The EPA ENERGY STAR portfolio manager score (1–100) reflecting the property\'s energy performance relative to similar buildings nationally. A score of 75 or above qualifies for ENERGY STAR certification. Applicable when certification_type is ENERGY_STAR or as a supplementary metric for LEED/BREEAM energy credits.']
  esg_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record is included in the organization\'s ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) disclosures and sustainability reporting (e.g., GRESB submission, annual sustainability report, SEC ESG disclosures for REITs). True if reportable; False if excluded.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current certification expires and renewal is required to maintain certified status. Null if the certification does not have a defined expiry (e.g., some LEED versions). Critical for compliance monitoring and renewal workflow triggers.']
  green_lease_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is referenced in or required by one or more green lease agreements for the property. True if the certification underpins green lease obligations (e.g., tenant energy reporting, sustainability covenants); False otherwise.']
  gresb_submission_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this certification data was included in the organization\'s GRESB (Global Real Estate Sustainability Benchmark) submission. Used to track ESG reporting cycles and benchmark performance year-over-year.']
  incentive_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific financial incentives, tax credits, or grants for which the property is eligible as a result of this certification (e.g., IRS Section 179D deduction, state property tax abatement, utility rebate program). Populated when incentive_eligible is True.']
  incentive_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the property qualifies for financial incentives (tax credits, grants, reduced permit fees, expedited permitting) as a result of achieving this green certification. Supports CAPEX planning and investment advisory analysis.']
  leed_category_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured summary of points earned across LEED credit categories (e.g., Sustainable Sites, Water Efficiency, Energy & Atmosphere, Materials & Resources, Indoor Environmental Quality, Innovation). Stored as a delimited string for LEED-type certifications; null for non-LEED certifications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, conditions, exceptions, or observations related to the certification record (e.g., conditional certification notes, outstanding credit appeals, assessor observations, special circumstances affecting the certification outcome).']
  points_available decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum number of credits or points available under the applicable certification version and building type. Used to calculate the percentage score and determine the achievable certification level.']
  points_scored decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total number of credits or points awarded to the property under the certification framework\'s scoring system (e.g., LEED points out of 110, BREEAM percentage score). Represents the principal quantitative outcome of the certification assessment.']
  prerequisites_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all mandatory prerequisites required by the certification framework have been satisfied. A value of True confirms all prerequisites are fulfilled; False indicates outstanding prerequisites that must be resolved before certification can be awarded.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the renewal application must be submitted to the certifying body to avoid lapse of certification. May differ from expiry_date if the certifying body requires advance submission. Supports compliance calendar and deadline management.']
  renewal_in_progress boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal application for this certification is currently in progress with the certifying body. True if the renewal process has been initiated but not yet completed; False otherwise. Supports compliance monitoring and workflow management.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the physical scope of the certification — specifying which building, floor, wing, or portion of the property is covered by this certification record. Relevant for multi-building campuses or partial certifications.']
  score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The certification score expressed as a percentage of total available points (points_scored / points_available × 100). Used for cross-framework ESG benchmarking and portfolio-level green performance reporting.']
  supporting_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the certification documentation package stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM, Procore document repository). Includes the official certificate, assessment report, and supporting evidence submitted to the certifying body.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this green certification record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for green building certifications obtained or pursued for properties — LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design), BREEAM (Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method), ENERGY STAR, WELL Building Standard, and Fitwel certifications. Captures certification type, certifying body (USGBC, BRE, EPA), certification level (LEED Platinum/Gold/Silver/Certified), certification date, expiry date, points scored, prerequisites met, and renewal requirements. Supports ESG reporting and green lease obligations.'
}
