  area_uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.uom. Business justification: Permits specify floor area (floor_area_sqft) which requires a normalized unit of measure for cross-jurisdiction comparisons (sqft vs. sqm). Real estate development teams operating internationally need']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the property asset to which this permit applies. Links the permit to the master property registry for portfolio-level compliance tracking.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Permits are managed under enterprise compliance programs (e.g., building permits under the construction compliance program). Linking permit to program enables program-level permit tracking and complia']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Permits fulfill specific compliance requirements (e.g., building code compliance requirements, fire safety requirements). Linking permit to requirement enables requirement-level permit compliance trac']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Building permits are issued for specific construction types (IBC Type I-A through Type V-B). Real estate development teams and code compliance officers track construction type on permits for fire code']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Permits are specific document types (building permit, certificate of occupancy, environmental permit, zoning variance). Real estate asset managers classify permits by document type for retention manag']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: permit has jurisdiction and jurisdiction_level as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-jurisdictional per']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Permits (building permits, occupancy certificates, environmental permits) are directly tied to portfolio assets. Asset managers track permit status as part of asset hold/disposition strategy, capex pl']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Permits are issued for specific property types (residential building permit vs. commercial certificate of occupancy). Real estate development and asset management teams track permits by property type ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Permits fulfill specific regulatory obligations (e.g., building permit obligation under local zoning code, certificate of occupancy obligation). Linking permit to regulatory_obligation enables obligat']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Building permits and operating licenses in real estate are managed by specific employees (project managers, facilities managers). Linking enables permit expiry alerts routed to responsible employees, ']
  zoning_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.zoning_code. Business justification: Permits are issued under specific zoning codes that govern permitted uses and development standards. Real estate development and land use compliance teams link permits to zoning codes for entitlement ']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit application was submitted to the issuing authority. Used to track application processing lead times and regulatory response windows.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit application was formally approved by the issuing authority, which may differ from the issue date in jurisdictions with staged approval processes.']
  breeam_rating varchar [note: 'BREEAM environmental assessment rating associated with this permit for international properties. Supports ESG compliance reporting and green building certification tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: outstanding|excellent|very_good|good|pass|unclassified|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special conditions, restrictions, or obligations attached to the permit by the issuing authority (e.g., noise restrictions, construction hours, environmental mitigation requirements). Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Procore document records.']
  contractor_license_number varchar [note: 'License number of the contractor or licensed professional (e.g., architect, engineer) named on the permit application. Required for building, electrical, plumbing, and mechanical permits. Sourced from Procore contractor records.']
  coo_number varchar [note: 'Official Certificate of Occupancy (COO) number issued by the local building authority, confirming the structure meets all applicable building codes and is legally habitable or operable. Populated only for COO-type permits.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction in which the permit was issued. Supports international portfolio compliance management (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this permit record was first created in the compliance data product. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX, GDPR, and SEC reporting for publicly traded REITs.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the digitized permit document stored in the document management system (DocuSign CLM, Procore, or SharePoint). Enables direct access to the official permit certificate for audit and due diligence purposes.']
  esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit is relevant to ESG reporting obligations (e.g., environmental permits, LEED/BREEAM certifications, energy efficiency permits). Drives ESG data aggregation for SEC and investor reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit expires and is no longer valid. Null for permits with no defined expiry. Drives renewal workflow triggers and compliance deadline monitoring.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total fee paid to the issuing authority for the permit application and issuance, expressed in the local currency. Used for CAPEX/OPEX tracking and development cost budgeting.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the permit fee amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Supports multi-currency portfolio accounting.']
  floor_area_sqft decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross floor area in square feet (SQF) covered by the scope of this permit. Relevant for building permits, COO, and zoning variance permits. Supports FAR (Floor Area Ratio) compliance calculations.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical inspection by the issuing authority is required as a condition of permit issuance or renewal. Drives inspection scheduling workflows in Building Engines.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit was officially issued by the governing authority. Marks the start of the permit\'s validity period and is the principal business event date for this record.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the governmental body, regulatory agency, or municipality that issued the permit (e.g., \'City of New York Department of Buildings\', \'EPA Region 2\', \'OSHA Area Office\'). Critical for multi-jurisdictional compliance management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent inspection conducted in connection with this permit. Used to assess inspection currency and schedule next required inspection.']
  last_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent inspection associated with this permit. \'conditional_pass\' indicates approval subject to remediation of minor deficiencies within a specified timeframe.']
  leed_certification_level varchar [note: 'LEED certification level associated with this permit, where the permit relates to green building compliance or construction. Supports ESG reporting and USGBC certification tracking.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled or required date for the next inspection associated with this permit. Populated by compliance scheduling workflows and used to drive proactive inspection management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, compliance observations, or internal commentary related to this permit record. Used by property managers and compliance officers for context not captured in structured fields.']
  permit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the permit as assigned by the issuing authority or internally for operational reference (e.g., \'Annual Elevator Operating Permit — Tower A\', \'LEED Construction Waste Management Permit\').']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned permit number issued by the governing authority or jurisdiction. This is the official reference used in regulatory filings, inspections, and correspondence. Sourced from the issuing authority\'s permit management system.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit. Drives compliance dashboards, renewal workflows, and regulatory reporting. \'pending_issuance\' indicates application submitted but not yet approved; \'pending_renewal\' indicates the permit is within the renewal window.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit by its regulatory purpose. Covers the full spectrum of permits required across the real estate portfolio lifecycle — from construction through operations and disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: building|certificate_of_occupancy|environmental|fire_safety|elevator|demolition|zoning_variance|electrical|plumbing|mechanical — promote to reference product]']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the permit renewal application must be submitted to avoid lapse. May precede the expiry date by a jurisdiction-defined lead time. Drives automated renewal reminders in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The number of months between required permit renewals. Null if renewal_required is False. Used to auto-calculate future renewal_due_date values and schedule compliance calendar events.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit requires periodic renewal. True for time-limited permits (e.g., annual elevator permits, fire safety certificates); False for one-time permits (e.g., demolition permits).']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the permit was revoked by the issuing authority, if applicable. Null for permits that have not been revoked. Critical for disposition compliance and regulatory audit trails.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Description of the specific construction, renovation, operational activity, or environmental action authorized by this permit. Aligns with Procore project scope documentation for development permits.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this permit record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation across Yardi Voyager, Procore, Building Engines, and DocuSign CLM.']
  source_system_permit_ref varchar [note: 'The native permit record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Procore permit ID, Yardi Voyager compliance record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province in which the permit was issued. Used for state-level regulatory compliance tracking and reporting (e.g., \'NY\', \'CA\', \'TX\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this permit record was most recently modified in the compliance data product. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.']
  valuation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The declared construction or project valuation amount submitted with the permit application, as required by many jurisdictions to calculate permit fees and assess project scale. Expressed in the local currency.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all permits required and obtained for properties — building permits, certificates of occupancy (COO), environmental permits, fire safety permits, elevator operating permits, demolition permits, and zoning variances. Captures permit type, issuing authority, jurisdiction, permit number, issue date, expiry date, renewal requirements, permit conditions, inspection history, and current status (active, expired, pending renewal, revoked). Critical for development, operations, and disposition compliance. Includes inspection records associated with permit issuance and renewal.'
}

Table compliance.sox_control {
  sox_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SOX internal control record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the sox_control data product.']
  audit_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_engagement. Business justification: SOX controls are tested during formal audit engagements (internal and external SOX audits). Linking sox_control to audit_engagement enables engagement-level SOX control testing tracking and sign-off m']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: SOX controls are part of the enterprise SOX compliance program. Linking sox_control to program enables program-level SOX control inventory management and compliance reporting to the audit committee.']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: SOX controls implement specific compliance requirements derived from SOX regulations. Linking sox_control to requirement enables requirement-level SOX control coverage analysis and gap identification.']
  control_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: SOX control ownership in real estate REITs/public companies is assigned to specific employees accountable for design and operating effectiveness. Linking enables control owner reporting, succession pl']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: SOX controls require specific document types as workpapers and evidence (e.g., reconciliation workpaper, approval memo, system-generated report). Real estate finance and internal audit teams classify ']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: SOX controls in real estate investment management cover fund-level financial reporting processes (NAV calculation, fee computation, distribution calculations). Linking SOX controls to funds supports S']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (REIT, subsidiary, or operating partnership) within whose financial statements this control operates. Supports multi-entity SOX scoping for consolidated SEC filers.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SOX controls fulfill specific regulatory obligations under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act (e.g., SOX Section 302 certification obligation, SOX Section 404 internal control assessment obligation). Linking sox_']
  control_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the control: financial_reporting (direct financial statement controls), itgc (IT General Controls covering access, change management, operations), entity_level (ELC — tone at the top, risk assessment, monitoring), process_level (activity-level controls), or disclosure (controls over SEC/REIT disclosure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_reporting|itgc|entity_level|process_level|disclosure — promote to reference product]']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is expected to operate (e.g., monthly reconciliation of NOI statements, quarterly review of CAP rate valuations, annual entity-level risk assessment). Determines the expected population size for testing sample selection.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the business risk or financial reporting assertion the control is designed to address (e.g., completeness, accuracy, existence, valuation of lease liabilities under ASC 842/IFRS 16). Maps to COSO framework objectives.']
  control_owner_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the control owner (e.g., \'Chief Financial Officer\', \'VP Property Accounting\', \'Director of IT Compliance\'). Supports organizational hierarchy reporting and Section 302 certification mapping.']
  control_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the control within the SOX control framework (e.g., ITGC-00142, ELC-00023, FC-00301). Used in audit workpapers, management assessments, and external auditor reliance packages.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the control within the SOX program: active (in scope and operating), inactive (temporarily suspended), retired (permanently removed from scope), or under_review (being reassessed for design adequacy).']
  control_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name of the internal control (e.g., \'Lease Revenue Recognition Review\', \'User Access Provisioning Approval\'). Used in dashboards, management reports, and Section 404 disclosures.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control by its timing relative to the risk event: preventive (stops errors before they occur), detective (identifies errors after they occur), or corrective (remedies identified errors). Drives audit strategy and reliance decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SOX control record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks silver layer.']
  deficiency_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of any identified control deficiency per PCAOB/SEC definitions: none (no deficiency), control_deficiency (minor gap), significant_deficiency (important enough to merit attention but not material weakness), or material_weakness (reasonable possibility of material misstatement in SEC filings). Material weaknesses require public disclosure in the REIT\'s annual report.']
  design_effectiveness varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the control is suitably designed to prevent or detect a material misstatement if it operates as intended. Evaluated separately from operating effectiveness. A design deficiency may result in a significant deficiency or material weakness.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of exceptions (deviations from the expected control operation) identified during the most recent test execution. A non-zero exception count triggers deficiency evaluation and may require expanded testing or remediation.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of exceptions identified during testing, including the nature of the deviation, root cause analysis, and potential financial statement impact (e.g., \'Lease commencement date not updated in Yardi Voyager within required 5-day window for 3 of 25 samples\'). Null if no exceptions.']
  external_auditor_reliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the external auditor (registered public accounting firm) has placed reliance on this control as part of their integrated audit under PCAOB AS 2201. True means the external auditor is relying on management\'s testing; False means the auditor is performing independent testing.']
  financial_assertion varchar [note: 'The PCAOB/AICPA financial reporting assertion addressed by the control: completeness (all transactions recorded), accuracy (correct amounts), existence (assets/liabilities exist), valuation (proper measurement), rights_obligations (entity owns/owes), or presentation (proper classification and disclosure).']
  financial_statement_line varchar [note: 'The specific financial statement line item or account balance that the control addresses (e.g., \'Rental Revenue\', \'Right-of-Use Asset\', \'Lease Liability\', \'Investment Property at Fair Value\'). Links the control to the SEC/REIT financial reporting assertions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) for which this control record and its testing history apply. Supports year-over-year trend analysis of control effectiveness and deficiency rates across the REIT portfolio.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is fully automated (True) — executed by an application system such as Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA, or MRI Software — or manual/hybrid (False). Automated controls typically require fewer test samples and rely on ITGC effectiveness.']
  is_key_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this control is designated as a key control (True) — i.e., a control whose failure could result in a material misstatement in the REIT\'s SEC financial filings. Key controls receive enhanced testing and are included in the external auditor\'s reliance scope.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent test execution was completed. Used to assess whether the control has been tested within the required testing cycle and to determine if rollforward testing is needed for year-end reliance.']
  operating_effectiveness varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the control operated effectively throughout the testing period — i.e., it was applied consistently and as designed. Determined through test execution (inquiry, observation, inspection, re-performance). Drives the overall test conclusion.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The business process or cycle to which the control belongs (e.g., \'Lease Revenue Recognition\', \'Property Acquisition and Disposition\', \'Accounts Payable — CAM Reconciliation\', \'Financial Close and Reporting\', \'IT Access Management\'). Aligns to the process-level scoping in the SOX risk and control matrix (RCM).']
  remediation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the remediation plan and actions taken to address the identified control deficiency (e.g., \'Updated Yardi Voyager workflow to enforce mandatory lease commencement date entry; retrained property accounting team\'). Null if no deficiency.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which management commits to completing remediation actions for an identified deficiency. Tracked against the fiscal year-end to assess whether the deficiency will be remediated before the Section 404 management assessment sign-off date.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the remediation lifecycle for identified deficiencies: not_applicable (no deficiency), open (deficiency identified, remediation not started), in_progress (remediation actions underway), remediated (management believes deficiency is resolved), or verified (remediation confirmed effective through re-testing).']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual who reviewed and approved the test workpapers and conclusion. Distinct from the tester. Supports the two-person review requirement for SOX documentation integrity.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Inherent risk rating assigned to the control area: high (significant risk of material misstatement), medium (moderate risk), or low (minimal risk). Drives testing frequency, sample size, and management attention. Assessed during the annual SOX scoping exercise.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of transactions, documents, or instances selected for testing in the most recent test execution. Sample size is determined by control frequency and risk rating per PCAOB guidance (e.g., 25 for monthly controls, 60 for daily controls).']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the authorized reviewer (e.g., SOX Manager, VP Internal Audit, CFO) formally approved the test results and conclusion. Required for Section 302 sub-certification and Section 404 management assessment documentation.']
  sign_off_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the test results: pending (awaiting reviewer sign-off), signed_off (approved by authorized reviewer), rejected (returned for rework), or escalated (elevated to senior management or audit committee due to deficiency severity).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record in which the control operates or is documented (e.g., \'Yardi Voyager\', \'SAP S/4HANA\', \'MRI Software\', \'Argus Enterprise\', \'DocuSign CLM\'). Critical for ITGC scoping — automated controls inherit risk from their host application.']
  sox_section varchar [note: 'The SOX section under which this control is evaluated: 302 (CEO/CFO quarterly certification of disclosure controls), 404a (annual management assessment of internal controls over financial reporting), or 404b (external auditor attestation — applicable to large accelerated filers). Determines reporting obligations for the REIT.']
  test_approach varchar [note: 'The audit testing methodology applied to evaluate operating effectiveness: inquiry (interviews with control performers), observation (watching the control execute), inspection (reviewing evidence/documentation), re_performance (independently re-executing the control), or combination (multiple approaches). Per PCAOB AS 2301, re-performance provides the highest level of assurance.']
  test_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall conclusion of the most recent test execution: effective (control operated as designed with no or immaterial exceptions), ineffective (control failed to operate as designed), or inconclusive (insufficient evidence to reach a conclusion, requiring additional testing).']
  tester_name varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual who performed the most recent control test. May be an internal audit team member, management tester, or external auditor representative. Required for audit trail and sign-off accountability.']
  tester_role varchar [note: 'Role of the party who performed the test: internal_audit (in-house IA function), management (first-line or second-line management testing), external_auditor (Big 4 or registered public accounting firm), or co_sourced (third-party firm supporting internal audit). Determines reliance hierarchy for PCAOB purposes.']
  testing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by the most recent test execution. For year-end reliance, this is typically the fiscal year-end date (e.g., December 31). Determines whether rollforward testing is required.']
  testing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by the most recent test execution. For annual controls, this is typically the fiscal year start date. For interim testing, this reflects the rollforward period start.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SOX control record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to control definitions, test results, or remediation status for audit trail and change management purposes.']
  workpaper_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or hyperlink to the test workpaper stored in the audit management system (e.g., AuditBoard, Workiva, or SharePoint). Provides traceability from the control record to the supporting evidence for external auditor review.']

  Note: 'Documents SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) internal controls and their testing history for publicly traded REITs. Captures control definitions (control ID, objective, type — preventive/detective, owner, frequency) and test execution records (tester identity, test approach — inquiry/observation/inspection/re-performance, sample size, exceptions, test conclusion, sign-off status). Covers financial reporting controls, IT general controls (ITGCs), and entity-level controls. Each control record maintains the full testing history including the most recent test date, result (effective/ineffective), and any remediation status for identified deficiencies. Supports SOX Section 302 certifications, Section 404 management assessments, and external auditor reliance testing for SEC-reporting REITs.'
}

Table compliance.violation {
  violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for violation']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_creative. Business justification: Advertising violations (deceptive advertising, fair housing violations in ads) must reference the specific ad creative that caused the violation. Regulators and internal compliance teams require this ']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the property asset where the violation or non-compliance event occurred. Links to the property master record in the portfolio.']
  assigned_to_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Violation resolution tracking in real estate compliance requires assigning violations to specific employees (compliance officers, property managers). Enables violation workload reporting, accountabili']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Violations are frequently identified during compliance assessments. Linking violation to the identifying assessment enables traceability from assessment finding to confirmed violation, supporting enfo']
  compliance_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit violations (e.g., operating without a valid permit, violating permit conditions) are a common compliance violation type in real estate. Linking violation to permit enables traceability from per']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Violations occur within the context of specific compliance programs (e.g., OSHA violation under the workplace safety program, fair housing violation under the fair housing program). Linking violation ']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Violations are breaches of specific compliance requirements. Linking violation to requirement enables requirement-level violation tracking and identifies which requirements have the highest violation ']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Compliance violations at the fund level (SEC violations, AML violations, ERISA violations) affect fund operations, investor reporting, and regulatory standing. Linking violations to funds supports fun']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: violation has jurisdiction_level, jurisdiction_state, and jurisdiction_country as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master and removes t']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (ownership entity, REIT subsidiary, or operating company) against which the violation was issued or that bears regulatory responsibility.']
  osha_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.osha_incident. Business justification: OSHA violations are directly linked to OSHA-reportable incidents. Linking violation to osha_incident enables traceability from incident to citation/violation, supporting OSHA enforcement action tracki']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory obligation that was violated, linking this event to the obligation register for root-cause and gap analysis.']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sox_control. Business justification: SOX control failures can result in confirmed violations (material weaknesses, significant deficiencies escalated to violations). Linking violation to sox_control enables traceability from control fail']
  abatement_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the corrective action or abatement of the cited condition was completed and verified. Used to calculate abatement lead time and confirm regulatory closure.']
  abatement_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the cited hazard or non-compliance condition must be corrected and abated. Failure to meet this date may result in additional penalties or willful reclassification.']
  assigned_to varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the compliance officer, property manager, or responsible party assigned to manage the remediation and regulatory response for this violation.']
  citation_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory body formally issued the citation, notice of violation, or enforcement action. Triggers the statutory response and contest windows.']
  contest_deadline_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which the cited party must file a notice of contest to challenge the citation before the relevant review commission (e.g., OSHRC for OSHA citations). Typically 15 working days from receipt of citation.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the formal corrective action plan (CAP) developed to remediate the cited condition and prevent recurrence. Summarizes the remediation steps, responsible parties, and milestones.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this violation record was first created in the compliance data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX internal control documentation.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the injured or ill worker was away from work as a result of the recordable incident. Required field on the OSHA 300 Log. Zero if not applicable.']
  days_job_transfer_restriction varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the worker was on job transfer or work restriction due to the recordable incident, as required on the OSHA 300 Log. Zero if not applicable.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification for the violation. Environmental violations (EPA, LEED non-compliance) map to E; workplace safety (OSHA) and fair housing (HUD) map to S; financial reporting (SEC, SOX) and data privacy (GDPR) map to G. Supports ESG disclosure and portfolio-level ESG scoring.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or enforcement agency that issued the violation or citation (e.g., EPA, OSHA, HUD, SEC, local building department). Determines the regulatory response protocol and mandatory reporting timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPA|OSHA|HUD|SEC|LOCAL_AUTHORITY|STATE_AGENCY|FIRE_MARSHAL|BUILDING_DEPT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workplace safety incident type for OSHA reporting purposes. Fatalities require separate 8-hour reporting to OSHA; hospitalizations require 24-hour reporting.']
  is_contested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cited party has formally filed a notice of contest challenging the violation citation. When true, the matter is referred to the applicable review commission or administrative tribunal.']
  is_mandatory_report boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation or incident triggers a mandatory regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., OSHA 8-hour fatality report, OSHA 24-hour hospitalization report, SEC material event disclosure, GDPR 72-hour breach notification). When true, drives the mandatory reporting workflow.']
  is_repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat of a substantially similar violation cited at the same establishment within the past five years. Repeat violations carry significantly higher OSHA penalties (up to 10x the base penalty).']
  leed_breeam_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation has a material impact on the property\'s LEED or BREEAM certification status. When true, triggers a review of the certification standing with the U.S. Green Building Council or BRE.']
  mandatory_report_deadline timestamp [note: 'Timestamp by which the mandatory regulatory report must be submitted to the governing body. Calculated from the violation or incident date based on the applicable regulatory timeline (e.g., 8 hours for OSHA fatality, 72 hours for GDPR breach).']
  osha_log_classification varchar [note: 'OSHA 300 Log column classification for recordable workplace incidents. Categorizes the nature of the work-related injury or illness per OSHA 29 CFR 1904 recordkeeping requirements. Applicable only when osha_recordable is true.']
  osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR 1904 and must be entered on the OSHA 300 Log. Applies to work-related injuries and illnesses meeting the recording threshold.']
  penalty_amount_assessed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary penalty amount formally assessed by the regulatory body at the time of citation issuance, in the currency specified by penalty_currency_code. Represents the initial proposed penalty before any informal settlement or reduction.']
  penalty_amount_settled decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final negotiated or settled penalty amount after informal conference, contest resolution, or administrative settlement. May differ from the assessed amount due to reductions for good faith, size, or history.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-jurisdictional portfolio compliance tracking.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known citation or case reference number assigned by the issuing regulatory body (e.g., OSHA citation number, EPA Notice of Violation case ID, building code violation ticket number). Used for correspondence and regulatory tracking.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory code, statute, standard, or rule section that was violated (e.g., \'29 CFR 1926.502(d)(1)\' for OSHA fall protection, \'40 CFR 112\' for EPA Spill Prevention, \'GDPR Article 32\'). Enables precise regulatory mapping.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the person who first reported or logged the violation or safety incident. Supports audit trail and whistleblower protection documentation.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the violation after the regulatory process is complete. Captures whether the matter was abated, dismissed, settled, or resolved through appeal. Used for compliance program effectiveness reporting.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level root cause classification from the post-incident or post-citation root cause analysis (RCA). Drives systemic corrective action programs and recurrence prevention initiatives.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause findings from the formal root cause analysis (RCA) investigation. Captures the specific conditions, actions, or systemic failures that led to the violation.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'OSHA-aligned severity classification of the violation indicating the degree of hazard or non-compliance. Willful and serious violations carry higher penalties and mandatory abatement timelines. Also applied to non-OSHA violations by analogy.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Building Engines work order ID, Procore inspection ID, OSHA citation number from the portal). Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this violation record originated (e.g., Building Engines work order, Procore inspection event, OSHA online portal, manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Yardi|Building_Engines|Procore|SAP|Manual|OSHA_Portal|EPA_Portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the violation type (e.g., \'Hazardous Waste Disposal\' under environmental, \'Fall Protection\' under safety, \'Discriminatory Advertising\' under fair housing). Supports detailed compliance analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this violation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to status, penalty amounts, abatement dates, or resolution outcome for audit and change management purposes.']
  violation_date date [note: 'The date on which the violation, non-compliance event, or workplace safety incident occurred. This is the principal real-world event date, distinct from the citation issuance date or record creation date.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the violation or non-compliance event as documented in the citation or inspection report. Captures the specific condition, act, or omission that constitutes the violation.']
  violation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the violation record. Drives remediation workflow, regulatory response management, and compliance dashboard reporting.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the violation category. Drives routing to the appropriate compliance program and remediation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|building_code|zoning|fair_housing|financial_reporting|data_privacy|fire_code|accessibility|labor — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records confirmed regulatory violations, non-compliance events, enforcement actions, and OSHA-reportable workplace safety incidents across all compliance programs. Covers EPA notices of violation, OSHA citations and recordable incidents (injuries, illnesses, near-misses, fatalities with 300/300A log classification), HUD enforcement actions, SEC comment letters, building code violations, and zoning violations. Captures violation/incident type, regulatory body, citation reference, date, severity (willful, serious, other-than-serious, de minimis), OSHA recordability classification, days away from work, root cause analysis, penalty amount assessed, contest status, and resolution outcome. Drives remediation workflows, regulatory response management, and mandatory incident reporting.'
}

  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Compliance training programs are directly associated with enterprise compliance programs. The existing program_name and program_code string fields are denormalized from the program master. Adding comp']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance requirement record that this training fulfills, linking training completion to the broader compliance program tracked in compliance.requirement.']
  document_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.document_type. Business justification: Training completion records generate specific document types (completion certificate, assessment record, waiver form). Real estate HR and compliance teams classify training documents by type for regul']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee, property manager, leasing agent, or contractor who completed or is enrolled in the training. Sourced from ADP Workforce Now HR records.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: compliance_training has jurisdiction_country and jurisdiction_state as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling jurisdiction-b']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory obligation that mandates this training program (e.g., OSHA 29 CFR 1910, HUD Fair Housing Act, GDPR Article 39). Links to the compliance.regulatory_obligation product.']
  workforce_training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_training. Business justification: Compliance training records must reference the specific workforce training course catalog entry to verify CE credit hours, accreditation, and regulatory framework alignment. Linking enables compliance']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the trainee on the post-training assessment, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to determine pass/fail outcome and document competency for regulatory audits.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued by the training provider upon successful completion. Used as evidence of compliance in regulatory audits and license renewal submissions.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the trainee successfully completed the training program. Null if training is not yet completed. This is the principal business event date for compliance reporting and regulatory audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance training record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX internal control documentation.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education (CE) or professional development credit hours awarded upon successful completion of the training. Relevant for NAR, Appraisal Institute, and state real estate license renewal requirements.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training was delivered: in_person (classroom), online_self_paced (LMS/e-learning), virtual_instructor_led (live webinar), blended (combination), on_the_job (practical/field), third_party_classroom (external provider facility). Relevant for OSHA and HUD compliance documentation.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the training certificate, completion document, or evidence file stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM document ID, SharePoint URL, or DMS reference number). Supports regulatory audit evidence retrieval.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the training must be completed to remain in compliance with the regulatory mandate or internal policy. Used for escalation, reminder workflows, and compliance gap reporting.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the training program in minutes as defined by the program curriculum. Used for workforce planning, CPE/CE credit tracking, and regulatory documentation of training hours.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee or contractor was enrolled or registered for the training program. Used to track lead time to completion and compliance with mandatory enrollment deadlines.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the training certification or completion credit expires and recertification is required. Null for one-time non-recurring training. Critical for tracking OSHA, HUD, and GDPR recurring training mandates.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or industry body that mandates or oversees this training requirement (e.g., \'HUD\', \'OSHA\', \'GDPR Supervisory Authority\', \'FinCEN\', \'SEC\', \'NAR\', \'USGBC\').']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training is mandatory (True) under a regulatory requirement or company policy, or voluntary/elective (False). Mandatory training records are subject to stricter audit and escalation workflows.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training must be repeated on a periodic basis (True) or is a one-time requirement (False). Recurring training records generate renewal obligations tracked via expiry_date and due_date.']
  lms_course_code varchar [note: 'Unique course identifier in the Learning Management System (LMS) or ADP Workforce Now training module. Used for cross-system reconciliation and to retrieve detailed completion records from the source system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, compliance officer observations, or special circumstances related to this training record (e.g., accommodations provided, regulatory exceptions noted, remediation plan details).']
  pass_fail_result varchar [note: 'Assessment outcome for the training: pass (met minimum score threshold), fail (did not meet threshold), not_assessed (no formal assessment required), pending_review (awaiting grading or manual review). Required for OSHA and HUD compliance documentation.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to pass the training assessment (e.g., 80.00 for 80%). Defined at the program level and used to determine pass/fail result. Supports audit documentation for OSHA and HUD mandated training.']
  provider_accreditation varchar [note: 'Accreditation or certification body that has approved the training provider or program (e.g., \'OSHA Authorized\', \'HUD Approved\', \'NAR Accredited\', \'IACET\', \'State Real Estate Commission\'). Validates regulatory acceptance of the training.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or vendor that delivered the training program (e.g., \'NAR Education\', \'OSHA Training Institute\', \'Skillsoft\', \'LRN Corporation\', \'Internal L&D Department\'). Required for regulatory audit documentation.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to this training completion record, used for audit trail references, regulatory submissions, and cross-system reconciliation (e.g., ADP Workforce Now training record ID).']
  recurrence_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the training must be repeated: annual (every 12 months), biennial (every 2 years), triennial (every 3 years), quarterly, monthly, one_time (no recurrence). Drives expiry date calculation and renewal scheduling.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory framework or statute that mandates this training (e.g., \'Fair Housing Act\', \'OSHA 29 CFR 1910\', \'GDPR\', \'Bank Secrecy Act / AML\', \'SOX\', \'SEC Regulation S-K\', \'LEED v4\'). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance tracking.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the source operational system (e.g., ADP Workforce Now training completion ID). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the originating system record for audit and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this training record was sourced (e.g., \'ADP Workforce Now\', \'SAP S/4HANA\', \'Yardi Voyager\', \'Internal LMS\'). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer reconciliation.']
  trainee_role varchar [note: 'Business role of the individual completing the training: property_manager (PM), leasing_agent, broker, contractor, asset_manager (AM), compliance_officer, executive, staff, other. Determines applicable training requirements and regulatory obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: property_manager|leasing_agent|broker|contractor|asset_manager|compliance_officer|facility_manager|executive|staff|other — promote to reference product]']
  trainee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trainee\'s employment relationship: employee (direct hire), contractor (independent contractor), vendor (third-party service provider), intern. Determines applicable regulatory training mandates and documentation requirements.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training record: enrolled (registered but not started), in_progress (started but not finished), completed (successfully finished), failed (did not pass assessment), expired (completion lapsed past expiry date), waived (formally exempted), cancelled (withdrawn from program). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrolled|in_progress|completed|failed|expired|waived|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance training driven by the regulatory mandate: fair_housing (HUD Fair Housing Act), osha_safety (OSHA building and workplace safety), gdpr_privacy (GDPR tenant data privacy), aml (Anti-Money Laundering), ethics_conduct (Code of Conduct and Ethics), esg_awareness (Environmental Social and Governance), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fair_housing|osha_safety|gdpr_privacy|aml|ethics_conduct|esg_awareness|sec_reit|leed_breeam|other — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance training record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer or manager who formally approved the training waiver. Required for SOX and GDPR audit trail documentation when a mandatory training obligation is waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented justification for waiving the training requirement for this individual (e.g., \'Prior equivalent certification\', \'Role change — no longer applicable\', \'Regulatory exemption granted\'). Null if no waiver applies. Required for audit trail when training_status = \'waived\'.']

  Note: 'Tracks mandatory compliance training programs and completion records for employees, property managers, leasing agents, and contractors — fair housing training, OSHA safety training, GDPR data privacy training, anti-money laundering (AML) training, ethics and code of conduct training, and ESG awareness training. Captures training program name, regulatory requirement driving it, delivery method, completion date, expiry date, pass/fail status, and training provider. Supports HUD, OSHA, and GDPR training mandate compliance.'
}

Table compliance.audit_engagement {
  audit_engagement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the audit engagement record. Primary key for the audit_engagement data product.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.program. Business justification: Audit engagements are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness of compliance programs. Linking audit_engagement to program enables program-level audit tracking and aggregation of engagement results by ']
  compliance_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Audit engagements evaluate specific compliance requirements. Linking audit_engagement to requirement enables requirement-level audit coverage tracking and gap analysis.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.fund. Business justification: Audit engagements cover fund-level audits (annual financial audits, GIPS compliance audits, Form PF audits). Linking audit engagements to funds is fundamental for fund administration, investor reporti']
  investment_vehicle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.vehicle. Business justification: Investment vehicles undergo separate audit engagements (REIT qualification audits, SPV financial audits, LP annual audits). Linking audit engagements to vehicles supports vehicle-level audit tracking,']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: audit_engagement has jurisdiction_country and jurisdiction_state as denormalized string fields. Adding jurisdiction_id normalizes these to the jurisdiction reference master, enabling consistent multi-']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal audit engagements in real estate REITs/operators are led by internal employees. Linking enables audit independence checks, engagement assignment reporting, and auditor workload management. le']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Audit engagements in real estate cover specific business units (property management, leasing, finance). Linking to org_unit enables org-unit-level audit coverage reporting, risk-based audit planning, ']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Audit engagements are scoped to specific regulatory frameworks (SOX audit, GDPR audit, environmental compliance audit). Real estate internal audit teams plan and report engagements by framework for re']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Audit engagements are scoped to specific regulatory obligations (e.g., SOX Section 404, EPA Clean Air Act). The existing regulatory_framework string is a denormalized representation. Adding regulatory']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the audit engagement, indicating the subject matter area. Examples include financial reporting, operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) metrics, IT general controls, lease accounting (ASC 842 / IFRS 16), tax, or environmental (EPA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial|operational|compliance|esg|it_general_controls|lease_accounting|tax|environmental — promote to reference product]']
  audit_opinion varchar [note: 'The overall audit opinion issued by the auditor upon completion of the engagement. Unqualified (clean) opinions indicate no material misstatements; qualified, adverse, or disclaimer opinions indicate significant issues. Applies to financial, SOX, and regulatory audits.']
  audit_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period under audit review. Together with audit_period_start_date, defines the temporal scope of the audit engagement.']
  audit_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period under audit review (e.g., the beginning of the fiscal year or quarter being examined). Distinct from fieldwork dates — this defines the scope of transactions and records being audited.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit engagement by type. Distinguishes internal audit reviews, external financial audits, SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audits, regulatory examinations (e.g., SEC, HUD, EPA), and third-party compliance audits (e.g., LEED, ESG). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|external|sox|regulatory_examination|third_party_compliance|forensic — promote to reference product]']
  auditing_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the firm, organization, or internal team conducting the audit engagement. For external audits, this is the audit firm (e.g., Big Four firm). For internal audits, this is the internal audit department or team name.']
  auditing_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity conducting the audit. Distinguishes between the company\'s own internal audit function, an external audit firm (e.g., Big Four), a regulatory body (e.g., SEC, EPA), or a third-party assessor (e.g., LEED certifier, ESG verifier).']
  business_unit_scope varchar [note: 'Name or code of the business unit(s) included in the audit scope (e.g., Commercial Leasing, Residential Management, Investment Advisory, Property Management). Supports organizational-level audit tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit engagement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX record-keeping requirements.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as critical severity — representing material weaknesses or significant deficiencies requiring immediate remediation. Critical for SOX 404 reporting and SEC disclosure obligations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the engagement fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency portfolio operations.']
  engagement_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contracted fee amount payable to the external audit firm or third-party assessor for the audit engagement. Applicable to external, SOX, and third-party compliance audits. Null for internal audit engagements.']
  engagement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the audit engagement. Used for cross-referencing with audit firms, regulators, and internal tracking systems such as SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  engagement_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal engagement owner or audit committee liaison responsible for managing the audit relationship on behalf of the company. Typically a VP of Internal Audit, Chief Compliance Officer, or CFO delegate.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit engagement. Tracks progression from planning through fieldwork, report issuance, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|in_progress|fieldwork_complete|report_issued|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]']
  engagement_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the audit engagement (e.g., \'FY2024 SOX 404 Financial Controls Audit\', \'EPA Environmental Compliance Review — Portfolio Q3\'). Used for identification and reporting.']
  esg_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit engagement includes ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) metrics verification or assessment. Supports ESG reporting obligations and LEED/BREEAM certification tracking.']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date on which active audit fieldwork was completed. Marks the transition from evidence gathering to report drafting and review.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date on which active audit fieldwork commenced — when auditors began on-site or remote testing, interviews, and evidence gathering. Distinct from the audit period start date.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter to which the audit engagement primarily relates (Q1–Q4). Supports quarterly SOX reviews, interim financial audits, and SEC quarterly reporting cycles.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the audit engagement primarily relates. Used for financial reporting alignment, SOX annual audit cycles, and SEC filing period tracking.']
  high_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as high severity — representing significant control deficiencies or compliance gaps requiring prompt remediation within a defined timeframe.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor or engagement partner responsible for conducting and signing off on the audit engagement. For external audits, this is the engagement partner at the audit firm.']
  legal_entity_scope varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the legal entity or entities covered by the audit engagement. Critical for REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) SEC reporting, SOX compliance, and multi-entity portfolio structures.']
  low_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as low severity — representing minor observations or best-practice recommendations that do not pose immediate compliance risk.']
  management_response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which management must provide formal responses to audit findings and remediation action plans. Tracks accountability and follow-up obligations post-report issuance.']
  material_weakness_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more material weaknesses in internal controls were identified during the audit engagement. A material weakness triggers mandatory disclosure under SOX Section 404 and SEC reporting requirements for publicly traded REITs.']
  medium_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings classified as medium severity — representing moderate control weaknesses or compliance gaps that require remediation within a standard timeframe.']
  property_count_in_scope varchar [note: 'Number of individual properties included within the audit scope. Relevant for portfolio-level audits where multiple assets are reviewed. Supports risk-based audit planning and resource allocation.']
  remediation_target_date date [note: 'The target date by which all identified audit findings are expected to be remediated. Used for tracking compliance remediation timelines and escalation management.']
  report_due_date date [note: 'The contractually or regulatorily mandated deadline by which the audit report must be issued. Used to track on-time delivery and manage regulatory filing deadlines.']
  report_issuance_date date [note: 'The date on which the final audit report was formally issued to management, the audit committee, or the regulator. This is the principal business event timestamp for the audit engagement lifecycle.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, including the business processes, systems, properties, legal entities, or geographic areas covered by the engagement. Supports multi-jurisdictional and portfolio-level audit tracking.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit scope dimension. Indicates whether the engagement covers the entire portfolio, specific properties, a business unit, a business process (e.g., lease accounting, CAM reconciliation), a legal entity, or an IT system.']
  significant_deficiency_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more significant deficiencies in internal controls were identified. Significant deficiencies are less severe than material weaknesses but must be communicated to the audit committee under SOX and PCAOB standards.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this audit engagement record in the originating source system. Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this audit engagement record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Yardi Voyager, DocuSign CLM, or a dedicated audit management system). Supports data lineage and silver layer traceability.']
  sox_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit engagement is a SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance audit, including Section 302 or Section 404 internal control assessments. Mandatory for publicly traded REITs.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of audit findings identified across all severity levels during the engagement. Provides a high-level indicator of audit outcome and compliance posture.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit engagement record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail integrity, and silver layer incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Records formal internal and external audit engagements conducted across the portfolio — internal audit reviews, external financial audits, SOX audits, regulatory examinations, and third-party compliance audits. Captures audit type, auditing firm or internal audit team, audit scope (properties, business units, processes), audit period, fieldwork start and end dates, report issuance date, overall audit opinion, and number of findings by severity. Serves as the master log of all formal audit activities.'
}

Table compliance.jurisdiction {
  jurisdiction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory jurisdiction record in the compliance master. Primary key for the jurisdiction reference product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — serves as an authoritative code-list/master for regulatory jurisdictions; per-role minimums are exempt.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Jurisdictions belong to countries and inherit country-level regulatory properties (GDPR applicability, REIT framework, foreign ownership rules). Real estate compliance teams managing cross-border port']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Jurisdictions map to geographic hierarchy nodes (MSA, state, city) enabling real estate portfolio managers to aggregate compliance obligations by market geography for regulatory reporting, market-leve']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Jurisdictions operate under specific regulatory frameworks (e.g., California jurisdiction → CCPA framework, EU jurisdiction → GDPR framework). Real estate compliance teams map jurisdictions to framewo']
  accepted_submission_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of accepted filing submission methods for this jurisdiction (e.g., \'online_portal,mail,email,in_person\'). Informs compliance teams of valid channels for regulatory submissions.']
  applicable_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of real estate property types to which this jurisdiction\'s regulations apply (e.g., \'commercial,residential,industrial,mixed_use\'). Enables targeted compliance obligation assignment based on asset class within the portfolio.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality for city-level and special district jurisdictions. Used to assign municipal compliance obligations (e.g., local building codes, rent control ordinances, municipal ESG reporting mandates) to properties within the portfolio.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Primary compliance domain category governed by this jurisdiction. Drives routing of obligations and filings to the appropriate compliance program. Values: environmental (EPA), safety (OSHA), fair_housing (HUD), financial (SEC/SOX/FASB), privacy (GDPR/CCPA), esg (ESG/LEED/BREEAM), zoning (local planning), tax (IRS/state revenue). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|fair_housing|financial|privacy|esg|zoning|tax — promote to reference product]']
  county_name varchar [note: 'Name of the county or parish within which this jurisdiction operates. Applicable for county-level and municipal jurisdictions. Used for property-level compliance mapping where county authorities govern zoning, permitting, and environmental assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was first created in the compliance master data store. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this jurisdiction record became effective and its regulatory authority became applicable to the portfolio. Used to determine when compliance obligations under this jurisdiction first apply.']
  esg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction mandates Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting for real estate entities operating within its boundaries. Drives ESG compliance program activation for properties in this jurisdiction.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this jurisdiction record expires or ceases to be applicable. Null for jurisdictions with no defined end date. Used to manage superseded or sunset regulatory authorities.']
  filing_portal_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the electronic filing portal or system used to submit compliance filings to this jurisdiction (e.g., \'EPA Central Data Exchange (CDX)\', \'SEC EDGAR\', \'HUD Integrated Disbursement and Information System (IDIS)\').']
  filing_portal_url varchar [note: 'URL of the official online filing portal for this jurisdiction (e.g., EPA\'s CDX portal, SEC EDGAR, HUD IDIS). Used by compliance teams to submit regulatory filings, permits, and disclosures electronically.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to tenant data processing activities within this jurisdiction. Activates GDPR data privacy controls for tenant records associated with properties in this jurisdiction.']
  geographic_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic boundaries or territorial scope of this jurisdiction (e.g., \'All properties within the five boroughs of New York City\', \'Coastal zone within 1,000 feet of mean high tide\'). Supports GIS-based compliance mapping for portfolio properties.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the specific regulatory or governmental body that administers this jurisdiction (e.g., \'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)\', \'Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)\', \'U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)\', \'Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)\'). Referenced by compliance obligations, filings, and permits.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Unique, externally-recognizable alphanumeric code identifying the jurisdiction (e.g., US-EPA, US-CA-DRE, US-NYC-DOB). Used as the business key for cross-system lookups and compliance mapping across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and SAP S/4HANA.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the regulatory jurisdiction within the governmental structure. Drives multi-jurisdictional compliance mapping logic — federal obligations cascade to state and local levels. Values: federal, state, county, city, special_district.']
  jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the regulatory jurisdiction (e.g., \'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency\', \'California Department of Real Estate\', \'New York City Department of Buildings\'). Serves as the human-readable identity label for compliance reporting and filing assignment.']
  jurisdiction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction record. \'active\' indicates the jurisdiction is currently enforcing regulations applicable to the portfolio. \'superseded\' indicates the jurisdiction has been replaced by another record. Used to filter valid jurisdictions for compliance obligation assignment.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the jurisdiction by its governmental or regulatory function. Distinguishes between executive regulatory agencies (EPA, OSHA), legislative bodies, judicial districts, and special-purpose authorities relevant to real estate compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_agency|legislative_body|judicial_district|municipal_authority|special_district|tribal_authority — promote to reference product]']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or BCP 47 language code for the official language of regulatory filings and correspondence required by this jurisdiction (e.g., \'en\', \'fr-CA\', \'es\'). Ensures compliance documents are prepared in the required language.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was most recently modified. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform conventions. Used to detect stale reference data and trigger downstream compliance obligation re-evaluation.']
  leed_breeam_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) or BREEAM (Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method) certification requirements or incentives apply within this jurisdiction. Supports green building compliance tracking.']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Official mailing address of the regulatory jurisdiction\'s governing body office. Used for physical filing submissions, certified mail correspondence, and formal regulatory notices.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum statutory financial penalty that this jurisdiction can impose per violation or per filing period. Expressed in the jurisdiction\'s local currency. Used for compliance risk quantification and penalty exposure reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional compliance context, special instructions, or operational notes about this jurisdiction (e.g., \'Requires wet signature on all permit applications\', \'Annual filing window opens January 1\'). Supports compliance team knowledge management.']
  penalty_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this jurisdiction has statutory authority to impose financial penalties or enforcement actions for non-compliance. True = jurisdiction can levy fines, issue cease-and-desist orders, or pursue legal action. Drives risk prioritization in compliance programs.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which penalties are denominated by this jurisdiction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency portfolio penalty exposure calculations.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address of the primary regulatory contact at the governing body. Used for compliance correspondence and filing submission confirmations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary regulatory contact or liaison officer at the governing body. Used for compliance correspondence, filing inquiries, and audit coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official phone number of the primary regulatory contact at the governing body. Used for urgent compliance inquiries and audit coordination.']
  requires_local_registration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether entities operating within this jurisdiction must register with the governing body before conducting real estate activities (e.g., broker licensing with state DRE, REIT registration with SEC, contractor licensing with local authority). Drives onboarding compliance workflows.']
  sec_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) reporting obligations apply to real estate investment entities operating within this jurisdiction (e.g., publicly traded REITs). Activates SEC filing workflows including 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K submissions.']
  source_record_ref varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Yardi Voyager jurisdiction code, SAP S/4HANA company code). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer back to the source system for audit and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this jurisdiction record was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'yardi\', \'sap\', \'manual\'). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver Layer reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: yardi|mri|argus|salesforce|costar|sap|procore|building_engines|docusign|adp|manual — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  standard_filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Default number of calendar days from the end of the reporting period within which filings must be submitted to this jurisdiction. Used to auto-calculate filing due dates for compliance obligations assigned to this jurisdiction.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state or province in which this jurisdiction operates (e.g., CA, NY, TX). Applicable for state-level and sub-state jurisdictions. Null for federal-level jurisdictions.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'Business code of the jurisdiction that has replaced or superseded this record when status is \'superseded\'. Enables compliance teams to redirect obligations and filings to the successor jurisdiction without data loss.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the jurisdiction (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\', \'Europe/London\'). Used to calculate filing deadline timestamps accurately, particularly for jurisdictions with same-day or end-of-business-day submission requirements.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all regulatory jurisdictions applicable to the real estate portfolio — federal agencies (EPA, OSHA, HUD, SEC, CFPB), state regulatory bodies, county authorities, and municipal/city agencies. Each jurisdiction record captures the jurisdiction level (federal, state, county, city, special district), jurisdiction name, governing body, geographic boundaries, applicable regulatory frameworks, key contact information, and filing portal details.
