[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: completed|reassigned|property_sold|project_closed|employee_terminated|restructure|voluntary_exit — promote to reference product]']
  unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of leasable units or suites under the employee\'s management responsibility within this assignment. Relevant for residential property managers and leasing agents for workload benchmarking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this work assignment record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  yardi_assignment_ref varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier from Yardi Voyager linking this assignment to the property management user-property access configuration. Enables reconciliation between HR assignment records and property system access rights.']

  Note: 'Operational assignments linking employees to specific properties, projects, development sites, or asset management portfolios for a defined period. Captures assignment type (property management, leasing territory, construction site, asset management portfolio, facility operations), assigned property or project reference, start and end dates, FTE allocation percentage, primary vs. secondary indicator, and assignment status. Enables property-level workforce tracking — which PM manages which building, which leasing agent covers which submarket, which construction superintendent is on which development project. Critical for cross-domain joins to property and development domains for operational reporting and cost allocation.'
}

Table workforce.commission_record {
  commission_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each employee-facing commission earnings record. Primary key for the commission_record data product in the workforce domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.asset. Business justification: Brokerage commissions are earned on specific property transactions. Linking commission_record to asset enables commission reporting by property, portfolio-level GCI analytics, and asset disposition co']
  brokerage_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brokerage deal (lease execution, property sale, or acquisition) that triggered this commission event. Sourced from Salesforce CRM deal closure events.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Commission expense GL posting in real estate brokerage requires validated chart of accounts mapping for accurate leasing commission capitalization vs. expensing under ASC 842. gl_account_code is a den']
  compensation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compensation plan governing the commission structure, split percentages, and draw-against-commission terms applicable to this record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commission expense allocation to cost centers in real estate brokerage is required for property-level leasing cost tracking and NOI reporting. cost_center_code is a denormalized string; this FK enable']
  lead_attribution_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.lead_attribution. Business justification: Marketing ROI measurement and commission calculation in real estate require linking each commission payment to the marketing attribution record that generated the deal. This enables campaign-level ROI']
  lease_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to lease.agreement. Business justification: Leasing commissions are triggered by lease execution milestones (signing, rent commencement). Linking commission_record directly to the lease agreement enables commission payment scheduling, clawback ']
  payroll_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ADP payroll run in which this commission was processed and disbursed to the employee. Null if commission is pending payroll processing.']
  portfolio_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to investment.portfolio_asset. Business justification: Broker commissions in real estate are earned on specific property transactions. Linking commission_record to portfolio_asset enables asset-level commission cost tracking, disposition cost reporting, a']
  primary_commission_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (broker, leasing agent, or sales staff) who earned this commission. Links to the workforce employee master record.']
  property_sale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.property_sale. Business justification: Commission payments are triggered by property sale closings. Linking commission_record directly to property_sale enables commission-by-transaction reporting, closing-date-triggered payment processing,']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Real estate commissions vary by transaction type (sale, lease, 1031 exchange). Replaces denormalized deal_type text; enables commission reporting by transaction category, GL classification, tax treatm']
  accrual_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (YYYY-MM format) in which this commission expense is accrued for financial reporting purposes. Used for period-close reconciliation and commission expense accrual journal entries.']
  adp_commission_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by ADP Workforce Now to this commission earnings record, used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the payroll system of record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the commission record was reviewed and approved by the authorized approver (e.g., brokerage manager or finance controller) for payroll processing.']
  clawback_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously paid commission was clawed back due to deal cancellation, lease default, or policy violation. Null if payment_status is not \'clawed_back\'.']
  clawback_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for a commission clawback event. Populated only when payment_status is \'clawed_back\'. Used for audit trail, dispute resolution, and financial restatement.']
  co_broker_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external brokerage firm participating in the co-broker split arrangement. Populated only when co_broker_flag is True. Used for co-broker payment reconciliation and MLS reporting.']
  co_broker_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission record involves a co-broker split arrangement where commission is shared with an external brokerage firm. True = co-broker split applies.']
  commission_event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commission-triggering event. Determines the calculation basis and GL posting treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lease_commission|sale_commission|referral_fee|co_broker_split|override_commission|acquisition_commission — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  commission_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the commission earning period to which this record applies. Paired with commission_period_start_date to define the accrual window.']
  commission_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the commission earning period to which this record applies. Used for accrual accounting, period-over-period reporting, and alignment with payroll pay periods.']
  commission_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The commission rate applied to the deal transaction value to derive the gross commission amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 = 3%). Sourced from the applicable compensation plan or deal-specific commission agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this commission record was first created in the system, typically upon deal closure event ingestion from Salesforce CRM. Audit trail field per SOX controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this commission record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international real estate portfolios.']
  deal_close_date date [note: 'The date the underlying real estate deal (lease execution, property sale, or acquisition) was officially closed and commission was earned. This is the principal business event date that triggers the commission record.']
  deal_transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total value of the underlying real estate transaction (e.g., sale price, total lease value over term) that forms the basis for commission calculation. Used for commission rate validation and analytics.']
  draw_balance_offset decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from the net commission to recover outstanding draw advances previously paid to the employee under a draw-against-commission plan. Zero if the employee is not on a draw plan or has no outstanding draw balance.']
  draw_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is on a draw-against-commission compensation plan. True = draw advances are being issued and recovered against earned commissions.']
  gl_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission expense has been successfully posted to the General Ledger. True = posted; False = pending GL posting. Used for period-close reconciliation.']
  gl_posting_reference varchar [note: 'The document or journal entry reference number from the GL system (SAP S/4HANA or Yardi Voyager) confirming that this commission expense has been posted. Null if GL posting is pending.']
  gross_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total commission amount generated by the deal before any splits, co-broker deductions, or draw offsets are applied. Represents the full commission pool attributable to this event.']
  is_1099_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commission payment must be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC. True for independent contractor brokers; False for W-2 employees whose commissions are included in W-2 wages.']
  net_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The employee\'s net commission earnings after applying the split percentage and any co-broker deductions. Calculated as gross_commission_amount × split_pct minus co-broker deductions. This is the pre-draw-offset amount payable to the employee.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, exceptions, or explanations related to this commission record. Used by payroll administrators and brokerage managers to document non-standard commission arrangements or dispute resolutions.']
  outstanding_draw_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unrecovered draw advance balance owed by the employee after applying the draw_balance_offset from this commission record. Carried forward to subsequent commission records for ongoing recovery.']
  payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount to be disbursed to the employee after applying the split percentage and subtracting the draw balance offset. This is the amount processed through ADP payroll or issued as a 1099 payment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date the commission was actually disbursed to the employee via payroll or direct 1099 payment. Null if payment_status is not \'paid\'.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commission payment. Drives payroll processing eligibility, accrual reversal, and 1099 reporting inclusion. Pending = awaiting approval; Approved = cleared for payroll; Paid = disbursed; Clawed_back = recovered due to deal cancellation or policy violation; Voided = cancelled before payment.']
  salesforce_deal_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known deal or opportunity number from Salesforce CRM that originated this commission event. Used for cross-system traceability between brokerage and payroll domains.']
  split_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the gross commission allocated to this employee under the applicable commission split agreement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6000 = 60%). Owned by the brokerage domain commission_split product; replicated here for payroll calculation audit trail.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year (e.g., 2024) to which this commission record belongs for IRS 1099-NEC or W-2 reporting purposes. Used for year-end tax reporting and accrual cutoff.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission record, including status changes, amount adjustments, or approval actions. Audit trail field per SOX controls.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax classification of the commission recipient. Determines whether commission is processed through W-2 payroll (ADP) or issued as a 1099-NEC for independent contractor brokers. Critical for IRS compliance and 1099 reporting.']

  Note: 'Employee-facing transactional records of commission earnings for brokers, leasing agents, and sales staff. Captures employee reference, deal reference (lease execution, property sale, acquisition), commission event type (lease commission, sale commission, referral fee, co-broker split, override commission), gross commission amount, split percentage, net commission to employee, draw balance offset for draw-against-commission plans, commission period, payment status (pending, approved, paid, clawed-back), and GL posting reference. Sourced from Salesforce CRM deal closure events and processed through ADP Workforce Now payroll. SSOT boundary: deal-level commission structures and split calculations are owned by the brokerage domain; this product owns the employee-facing earnings record for payroll processing, 1099 reporting for independent contractor brokers, and commission expense accrual.'
}

Table workforce.role_training_requirement {
  role_training_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each role training requirement record']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the job profile definition']
  workforce_training_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the training course in the workforce training catalog']
  ce_credit_hours_applicable decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of CE credit hours from this course that count toward license renewal requirements for this job profile. May differ from the course\'s total CE hours if only a subset applies to this role\'s license type.']
  completion_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from hire date (for onboarding) or from effective date (for ongoing requirements) by which employees in this job profile must complete the training']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training requirement record was first created in the system']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this training requirement becomes active for the job profile. Used to manage phased rollouts of new compliance requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this training requirement ceases to apply to the job profile. Null indicates an ongoing requirement.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training course is mandatory for employees in this job profile. Drives compliance tracking and enforcement workflows.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this training requirement record']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training requirement record was last modified']
  recurrence_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required completions for recurring training requirements (e.g., annual compliance training). Null for one-time requirements.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this training requirement. Active requirements are enforced in compliance reporting.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of why this training is required for this role (e.g., Regulatory for state-mandated CE, Compliance for internal policy, Onboarding for new hires, License Renewal for maintaining credentials)']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this job profile can request a waiver or exemption from this training requirement based on prior experience or equivalent credentials']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this training requirement record']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal assignment of mandatory or recommended training courses to job profiles within the real estate enterprise. It captures regulatory CE requirements, compliance mandates, and professional development standards that apply to specific roles. Each record links one training course to one job profile with attributes that define the requirement\'s nature, timing, and applicability. Sourced from ADP Workforce Now LMS and HCM modules, this association supports compliance tracking, license renewal planning, and onboarding curriculum design.. Existence Justification: In real estate workforce management, training requirements are formally assigned to job profiles based on regulatory mandates (state real estate commission CE requirements for brokers, appraisal board requirements for appraisers), compliance policies (Fair Housing, OSHA), and role-specific competencies. A single training course (e.g., Fair Housing Act compliance) is required for multiple job profiles (broker, property manager, leasing agent), and a single job profile requires multiple training courses to maintain licensure and operational competency. This is an actively managed business process in ADP Workforce Now LMS, where HR and compliance teams create, update, and retire training requirements as regulations and business needs evolve.'
}

Table workforce.grade_band {
  grade_band_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for grade_band']
  parent_grade_band_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on grade_band (parent_grade_band_id)']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether executive or board approval is required for compensation decisions within this grade band (typically true for executive-level bands).']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this grade band are eligible for performance-based bonus compensation.']
  career_track varchar [note: 'Career progression track for this grade band (individual contributor, management, executive).']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the grade band grouping similar organizational levels together.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the grade band (e.g., GB01, EXEC, MGR1). Used as a business identifier for payroll and HR systems.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this grade band are eligible for commission-based compensation (typically for brokers and leasing agents).']
  compa_ratio_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target compa-ratio (comparison ratio) for employees in this grade band, expressed as a percentage. A compa-ratio of 100.00 means the employee is paid at the midpoint of the salary range.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grade band record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the grade band, including typical roles, responsibilities, and organizational level.']
  education_requirement varchar [note: 'Typical education requirement for positions in this grade band (e.g., High School Diploma, Bachelor\'s Degree, Master\'s Degree, Professional Certification).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this grade band is no longer effective. Null for currently active grade bands.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this grade band becomes effective and available for use in compensation planning and employee assignments.']
  equity_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this grade band are eligible for equity compensation (stock options, restricted stock units).']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'FLSA classification indicating whether positions in this grade band are exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad job family or functional area associated with this grade band (e.g., Property Management, Asset Management, Brokerage, Construction, Corporate).']
  last_market_review_date date [note: 'Date when the salary ranges for this grade band were last reviewed and updated based on market data.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grade band record was last updated.']
  level varchar [note: 'Numeric level representing the hierarchical position of the grade band within the organization (e.g., 1=entry level, 10=executive). Used for career progression and reporting structure.']
  license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this grade band require professional licenses (e.g., real estate broker license, appraiser certification, property management license).']
  market_pricing_source varchar [note: 'External compensation survey or market data source used to benchmark salary ranges for this grade band (e.g., Mercer, Willis Towers Watson, Radford).']
  maximum_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary amount for positions within this grade band. Represents the ceiling for compensation within this band.']
  midpoint_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target midpoint annual salary for positions within this grade band. Represents the competitive market rate for fully competent performance.']
  minimum_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary amount for positions within this grade band. Used for compensation planning and pay equity analysis.']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience typically required for positions in this grade band.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the grade band (e.g., Executive Leadership, Senior Management, Professional Staff, Administrative Support).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next market review and potential adjustment of salary ranges for this grade band.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this grade band.']
  pay_transparency_disclosure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether salary ranges for this grade band must be disclosed in job postings to comply with pay transparency laws (e.g., California, New York, Colorado).']
  salary_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which salary amounts are expressed (annual, monthly, biweekly, weekly, hourly).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grade band. Active bands are available for employee assignment; inactive bands are no longer used.']
  target_bonus_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target bonus as a percentage of base salary for positions in this grade band (e.g., 15.00 represents 15% target bonus).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for grade_band. Referenced by grade_band_id.'
}

Table workforce.training_session {
  training_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_session']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location, facility, or property where the training session is held (for in-person sessions).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or external trainer who is facilitating this training session.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or business unit sponsoring or organizing this training session.']
  prior_training_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_session (prior_training_session_id)']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent training program or curriculum that this session belongs to.']
  workforce_training_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific course or training module delivered in this session.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the training session concluded, may differ from scheduled time.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the training session commenced, may differ from scheduled time.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must complete a test, quiz, or assessment to pass this training session (True/False).']
  attended_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of participants who attended or completed the training session.']
  average_score_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean assessment score achieved by all participants who completed the training session.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or business reason for cancelling or postponing the training session, if applicable.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type or name of the professional certification, license, or credential awarded upon completion (e.g., broker license, appraiser credential, property manager certification).']
  completion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of enrolled participants who successfully completed the training session.']
  continuing_education_credits decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education credits or professional development hours awarded for completing this training session.']
  cost_per_participant decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost or fee charged per participant for attending this training session, in the organization\'s base currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training session record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this session record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned or actual duration of the training session measured in hours.']
  enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Current number of employees or participants registered for this training session.']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Aggregated or representative participant feedback comments and suggestions for improving the training session.']
  feedback_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average participant satisfaction rating for this training session, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00.']
  is_certification_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion of this session qualifies participants for a professional certification or credential (True/False).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendance at this training session is required for specific employee roles or compliance purposes (True/False).']
  materials_provided varchar [note: 'Description of training materials, handouts, or resources provided to participants (e.g., workbooks, digital files, reference guides).']
  max_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants or seats available for this training session.']
  meeting_url varchar [note: 'Web link or URL for accessing the virtual training session, if applicable.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this training session record was last updated or modified.']
  passing_score_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required on the assessment to successfully complete the training session.']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Required prior training, certifications, or experience that participants must have before enrolling in this session.']
  room_number varchar [note: 'Specific room, conference space, or training area identifier within the location facility.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the training session is scheduled to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the training session is scheduled to begin.']
  session_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the training content area (compliance, technical, leadership, sales, safety, onboarding, professional development).']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the training session, used for scheduling and registration purposes.']
  session_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the training session (e.g., \'Broker License Renewal Course\', \'Property Management Best Practices\').']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the training delivery method (classroom, virtual, hybrid, on-the-job, self-paced, webinar).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the training session (scheduled, in_progress, completed, cancelled, postponed, draft).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended participant group or employee roles for this training session (e.g., \'New Brokers\', \'Property Managers\', \'All Corporate Staff\').']
  total_session_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for delivering this training session, including instructor fees, materials, and facility costs.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the virtual meeting or webinar platform used for remote training delivery (Zoom, Teams, Webex, Google Meet, other).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for training_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table workforce.training_program {
  training_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for training_program']
  prerequisite_training_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on training_program (prerequisite_training_program_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the professional or regulatory body that accredits or recognizes this training program.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Unique accreditation or approval number assigned by the accrediting body.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether participants must complete an assessment or examination to pass the training program.']
  certification_awarded varchar [note: 'Name of the certification or credential awarded upon successful completion of the training program.']
  cost_per_participant decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost in USD per participant for delivering the training program, including materials and instructor fees.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training program record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of continuing education credit hours awarded upon successful completion, applicable for professional certifications.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training program is delivered to participants.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department responsible for administering and maintaining the training program.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training program content, objectives, and learning outcomes.']
  duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total duration of the training program measured in hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the training program is no longer available for new enrollments, nullable for ongoing programs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the training program becomes available for enrollment and delivery.']
  is_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training program is required to meet regulatory or industry compliance obligations.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training program is mandatory for specific roles or all employees.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the training program is delivered, using ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the training program content was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance.']
  learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Specific learning objectives and competencies that participants will achieve upon completion.']
  materials_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the training materials, handouts, and resources for the program.']
  maximum_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of participants that can be enrolled in a single session of the training program.']
  minimum_enrollment varchar [note: 'Minimum number of participants required to proceed with a scheduled training session.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the training program record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training program record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and update of the training program content.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the program owner responsible for content, delivery, and maintenance of the training program.']
  passing_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to successfully pass the training program assessment.']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Required prior training, certifications, or experience needed before enrolling in this program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the training program used in catalogs and scheduling systems.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the training program as displayed to employees and managers.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training program indicating availability for enrollment.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training program by its primary purpose and audience.']
  recertification_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required recertification or renewal training sessions.']
  recertification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether periodic recertification or renewal training is required after initial completion.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience for the training program, including roles, departments, or employee levels.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external vendor or training provider delivering the program, if applicable.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the training program content, incremented when curriculum or materials are updated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the training program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for training_program. Referenced by training_program_id.'
}

Table reference.property_type {
  property_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the property classification taxonomy. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — exempt from per-role minimums; _canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / hierarchical code-list serving as the enterprise-wide property classification taxonomy, not a first-class operational entity with its own lifecycle.]']
  building_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.building_class. Business justification: Property types are associated with a default building class designation (e.g., trophy office properties are Class A, older industrial properties are Class C). Linking to the building_class registry no']
  construction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.construction_type. Business justification: Property types are characterized by their primary construction method (e.g., multifamily residential typically uses wood frame or concrete, industrial uses steel frame). Linking to the construction_ty']
  parent_property_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing foreign key to the parent node in the property classification hierarchy. NULL for Level 1 (asset class) root nodes. Enables traversal of the multi-level taxonomy (L1 asset class → L2 sub-category → L3 quality tier → L4 space use → L5 construction method → L6 amenity classification).']
  space_use_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.space_use_type. Business justification: Property types define the primary space use classification for the asset (e.g., office property type → office space use, retail property type → retail space use). Linking to the space_use_type registr']
  amenity_classification varchar [note: 'Level 6 amenity classification indicating whether amenities associated with this property type are provided at the building level (lobbies, fitness centers, conference rooms), unit level (in-unit washer/dryer, private terrace), or community level (shared outdoor spaces, pools, clubhouses). Used in property marketing, tenant scoring, and lease negotiation.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Top-level (Level 1) asset class classification for this property type node. Denormalized from the root ancestor for fast filtering in analytics and reporting. Aligned with CoStar and NAIOP asset class definitions.']
  bim_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether BIM (Building Information Modeling) digital asset documentation is applicable and required for development and construction projects of this property type. True for complex commercial, mixed-use, and large-scale residential development types managed in Procore. Drives BIM data collection and document control workflows.']
  cam_inclusion_rule varchar [note: 'Standard CAM (Common Area Maintenance) expense inclusion rule applicable to this property type, defining which operating expenses are passed through to tenants. NNN (Triple Net) passes taxes, insurance, and maintenance; NN (Double Net) passes taxes and insurance; Full-Service Gross includes all expenses in base rent. Drives lease structuring defaults in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Full-Service Gross|Modified Gross|NNN|NN|N|Absolute Net|N/A — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cap_rate_benchmark_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Market benchmark CAP Rate (Capitalization Rate) percentage for this property type, representing the expected ratio of Net Operating Income (NOI) to property value. Used as a default underwriting assumption in Argus Enterprise DCF (Discounted Cash Flow) models and investment advisory valuations. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.50 = 5.50%).']
  costar_property_type_code varchar [note: 'CoStar Suite\'s standardized property type code for this classification node. Enables direct mapping and data reconciliation between the enterprise taxonomy and CoStar market research data, comparable sales, and lease analytics. Critical for market benchmarking and CMA (Comparative Market Analysis) workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property type taxonomy node was first created in the enterprise reference data repository. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) compliance for reference data governance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this property type classification node became effective and available for use in the enterprise taxonomy. Supports temporal validity tracking and ensures historical data integrity when taxonomy nodes are introduced or revised.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this property type classification node is no longer valid for new assignments. NULL indicates the node is currently active with no planned expiry. Supports taxonomy versioning and ensures deprecated nodes are not applied to new properties or leases after their retirement date.']
  far_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Floor Area Ratio (FAR) regulations are applicable to development projects of this property type. True for commercial and mixed-use development types subject to FAR zoning restrictions. Used in development feasibility analysis and Procore project planning.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Integer indicating the depth of this node within the six-level property classification hierarchy. Level 1 = Asset Class (commercial, residential, mixed-use, land, special-purpose); Level 2 = Sub-Category (office, retail, industrial, hospitality, single-family, multifamily, condo); Level 3 = Quality Tier / Building Class (Class A/B/C, trophy, institutional-grade, luxury, affordable); Level 4 = Space Use Type (office, retail, restaurant, medical, fitness, storage, parking); Level 5 = Construction Method (wood frame, steel, concrete, masonry, modular); Level 6 = Amenity Classification (building-level, unit-level, community).']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized full ancestry path from root to this node expressed as a delimited string (e.g., \'Commercial / Office / Class A / Office Space Use / Steel Frame / Building-Level Amenity\'). Enables efficient subtree queries and breadcrumb navigation in reporting tools without recursive CTE traversal.']
  hoa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type are subject to HOA (Homeowners Association) governance, fees, and covenants. Applicable to condominium, single-family community, and certain mixed-use residential property types. Drives HOA fee tracking in property management and tenant disclosure requirements.']
  ibc_fire_resistance_type varchar [note: 'International Building Code (IBC) construction type classification indicating the fire resistance rating of the structural system. Types I and II are non-combustible; Types III, IV, and V include combustible elements. Drives insurance underwriting, OSHA compliance, and maintenance safety protocols. Aligned with IBC Chapter 6 construction type definitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type I-A|Type I-B|Type II-A|Type II-B|Type III-A|Type III-B|Type IV|Type V-A|Type V-B|N/A — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_cam_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges are applicable to leases for properties of this type. True for commercial property types (office, retail, industrial) where CAM reconciliation is standard. False for residential or gross lease property types where operating expenses are not passed through to tenants.']
  is_commercial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this property type is classified as CRE (Commercial Real Estate) for regulatory, tax, and accounting purposes. Drives applicability of CMBS (Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities) financing, DSCR (Debt Service Coverage Ratio) underwriting, and commercial lease accounting under FASB ASC 842.']
  is_esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting metrics and sustainability certifications are applicable and tracked for properties of this type. True for property types subject to LEED, BREEAM, or ENERGY STAR certification programs. Drives ESG data collection requirements and SEC sustainability disclosure obligations for publicly traded REITs.']
  is_gla_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Gross Leasable Area (GLA) measurement methodology is applicable to properties of this type. True for retail and commercial property types where GLA is the standard measurement basis per BOMA and ICSC standards. False for property types measured by other methodologies (e.g., residential measured by GFA or NLA).']
  is_investment_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type qualify as institutional investment-grade assets suitable for REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust) portfolios, pension fund investment, and institutional capital allocation. Drives portfolio screening in Argus Enterprise and investment advisory workflows. Typically True for Class A and Trophy commercial assets.']
  is_leaf_node boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this taxonomy node is a terminal leaf node (True) with no child nodes, or an intermediate branch node (False). Leaf nodes represent the most granular assignable property type classifications. Used to enforce that only leaf-level types are assigned to individual properties and leases.']
  is_reit_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether properties of this type qualify as REIT-eligible assets under IRS REIT qualification rules (IRC Section 856). Drives asset classification in financial consolidation (SAP S/4HANA) and SEC regulatory reporting for publicly traded REITs. Distinct from investment_grade as it reflects specific tax and regulatory eligibility.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this property type is classified as residential for regulatory, tax, and accounting purposes. Drives applicability of HUD regulations, residential mortgage underwriting standards (PMI, ARM, FRM, DTI), and RMBS (Residential Mortgage-Backed Securities) eligibility. Distinct from asset_class for use in compliance filtering.']
  leed_certification_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification is applicable and pursued for properties of this type. Drives green building data collection, energy benchmarking, and ESG reporting workflows. Relevant for institutional-grade commercial and multifamily property types.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Standard area measurement methodology applicable to this property type. GLA (Gross Leasable Area) for retail; NLA (Net Leasable Area) for commercial office; GFA (Gross Floor Area) for development and planning; IPMS (International Property Measurement Standards) for cross-border portfolios. Drives how PSF (Per Square Foot) rent and valuation metrics are calculated.']
  naiop_type_code varchar [note: 'NAIOP (National Association for Industrial and Office Properties) standardized property type code for commercial real estate classification. Used for industry benchmarking, market research alignment, and CRE (Commercial Real Estate) portfolio reporting.']
  property_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this property type taxonomy node. \'active\' nodes are available for assignment to properties, leases, and valuations. \'deprecated\' nodes are retained for historical data integrity but no longer assignable. \'draft\' nodes are under review prior to enterprise publication.']
  reso_property_type_code varchar [note: 'RESO (Real Estate Standards Organization) standardized property type code for MLS (Multiple Listing Service) data exchange and industry interoperability. Enables syndication of property listings via MLS platforms and ensures compliance with RESO Data Dictionary standards.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the preferred display sequence of this property type node within its sibling group at the same hierarchy level. Used to control the ordering of property type options in dropdown menus, reports, and dashboards across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Salesforce CRM integrations.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Level 2 sub-category classification denormalized from the taxonomy hierarchy for analytical convenience. Examples: Office, Retail, Industrial, Hospitality, Single-Family, Multifamily, Condo, Mixed-Use Retail/Office. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Office|Retail|Industrial|Hospitality|Single-Family|Multifamily|Condo|Mixed-Use|Self-Storage|Data Center|Life Science|Senior Housing — promote to reference product]']
  ti_allowance_benchmark_psf decimal(10,2) [note: 'Market benchmark for Tenant Improvement (TI) allowance expressed in dollars Per Square Foot (PSF) for this property type. Used as a default starting point in lease negotiations and deal underwriting. Sourced from CoStar market data and internal brokerage transaction history. Supports deal pipeline analysis in Salesforce CRM.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this property type node within the enterprise taxonomy. Used as the stable business key for cross-system integration with Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, CoStar, and Argus Enterprise. Examples: COMM_OFF_A, RES_MF_LUX, IND_WRHSE.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the property classification node, including defining characteristics, typical use cases, and distinguishing criteria from adjacent nodes. Supports business user understanding and onboarding.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for this property classification node as used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces across all operational systems. Examples: \'Class A Office\', \'Luxury Multifamily\', \'Triple Net Retail\'.']
  typical_lease_term_months varchar [note: 'Market-standard typical lease term in months for this property type. Used as a default assumption in lease underwriting, WALT (Weighted Average Lease Term) calculations, and cash flow modeling in Argus Enterprise. Examples: 12 months for residential, 60-120 months for commercial office, 36-60 months for retail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this property type taxonomy node was last modified in the enterprise reference data repository. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and reference data governance workflows.']
  zoning_compatibility_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of standard zoning designation codes compatible with this property type (e.g., \'C-1|C-2|M-1\' for light commercial/industrial). Supports regulatory compliance checks, development feasibility analysis, and GIS (Geographic Information System) integration. Aligned with local municipal zoning ordinances and FAR (Floor Area Ratio) regulations.']

  Note: 'Enterprise-wide property classification taxonomy organized as a self-referencing multi-level hierarchy. Level 1: asset class (commercial, residential, mixed-use, land, special-purpose). Level 2: sub-category (office, retail, industrial, hospitality, single-family, multifamily, condo). Level 3: quality tier / building class (Class A/B/C, trophy, institutional-grade, luxury, affordable). Level 4: space use type (office, retail, restaurant, medical, fitness, storage, parking). Level 5: construction method (wood frame, steel, concrete, masonry, modular). Level 6: amenity classification (building-level, unit-level, community). Each node carries applicable attributes: GLA applicability flags, zoning compatibility codes, fire resistance ratings (IBC Type I–V), ESG/LEED relevance indicators, CAM inclusion rules, TI allowance benchmarks, and measurement methodology (GLA/NLA/GFA). Aligned with RESO, CoStar, BOMA, and NAIOP standards. Referenced by property, lease, valuation, brokerage, development, and maintenance domains as the single source of truth for all property classification.'
}

Table reference.lease_type {
  lease_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lease_type']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Lease types are associated with specific market segments (e.g., NNN leases are typical in Core commercial segments, gross leases in Value-Add residential segments). Linking to the market_segment regis']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Industry-standard abbreviation for the lease type used in market communications, CoStar listings, and brokerage reports (e.g., NNN, MG, FS, PCT, GL). Aligns with CoStar Suite and MLS syndication field conventions.']
  area_measure_standard varchar [note: 'Specifies the standard unit of area measurement used for rent calculation under this lease type: SQF (Square Feet), SQM (Square Meters), GLA (Gross Leasable Area), NLA (Net Leasable Area), or GFA (Gross Floor Area). Ensures consistency in PSF/SQM rent calculations across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and CoStar Suite.']
  argus_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type identifier as used in Argus Enterprise cash flow and valuation models. Enables mapping between this reference record and Argus Enterprise lease structure assumptions for DCF modeling, CAP rate analysis, and portfolio reporting.']
  asc842_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Specifies the default lease classification under FASB ASC 842 for this lease type: \'operating\' (lessee recognizes right-of-use asset and operating lease liability), \'finance\' (lessee recognizes finance lease asset and liability with front-loaded expense), \'short_term_exemption\' (leases ≤12 months exempt from balance sheet recognition), or \'not_applicable\' (lessor-side or non-applicable structures). Drives accounting treatment in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager GL.']
  cam_reconciliation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether annual CAM reconciliation is required for this lease type (True). CAM reconciliation compares estimated CAM charges billed to tenants against actual CAM expenses incurred. Required for NNN and modified gross leases; not applicable for full gross leases. Activates CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.']
  cam_responsibility varchar [note: 'Indicates which party bears responsibility for Common Area Maintenance (CAM) charges under this lease type. \'landlord\' means the landlord absorbs all CAM costs; \'tenant\' means the tenant pays CAM directly (as in NNN); \'shared\' means costs are pro-rated; \'not_applicable\' for lease types where CAM is not a relevant concept (e.g., ground lease). Governs CAM reconciliation workflows in Yardi Voyager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lease type reference record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls, and reference data governance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this lease type classification became or becomes effective for use in new lease agreements. Supports temporal validity tracking for reference data governance and ensures that historical lease records retain the correct classification at the time of execution.']
  escalation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates the standard rent escalation methodology associated with this lease type: fixed_step (predetermined dollar or percentage increases), cpi_indexed (Consumer Price Index-linked adjustments), market_reset (rent reset to prevailing market rate at intervals), none (flat rent for term), or hybrid (combination of methods). Governs rent schedule escalation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise DCF models.']
  esg_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) reporting obligations — such as green lease clauses, LEED/BREEAM compliance requirements, or energy consumption data sharing — are applicable to this lease type (True). Relevant for institutional and REIT portfolios subject to SEC ESG disclosure requirements and USGBC LEED certification programs.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this lease type classification ceases to be valid for use in new lease agreements (nullable for open-ended classifications). Supports reference data lifecycle management and ensures deprecated lease types are not applied to new leases after their retirement date.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard or regulatory body reference code for this lease type, such as RESO standard codes, NCREIF property type codes, or CoStar lease type identifiers. Enables cross-system interoperability and alignment with industry data exchange standards.']
  ifrs16_lease_classification varchar [note: 'Specifies the default lease classification under IASB IFRS 16 for this lease type: \'single_model\' (all leases recognized on balance sheet as right-of-use asset and lease liability), \'short_term_exemption\' (leases ≤12 months), \'low_value_exemption\' (underlying asset of low value), or \'not_applicable\'. Supports international financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA for entities reporting under IFRS.']
  insurance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines which party is responsible for property insurance premiums under this lease type. Relevant for NNN and modified gross lease structures where insurance cost allocation varies. Used in lease administration billing and CAM reconciliation processes.']
  is_ground_lease boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type is a ground lease (True), where the tenant leases land only and typically constructs improvements. Ground leases have distinct legal, accounting, and investment characteristics including long terms (50–99 years), land-only rent, and reversion of improvements to the landlord. Relevant for REIT portfolio classification and Argus Enterprise valuation modeling.']
  is_short_term boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type is classified as short-term (True), typically defined as a lease term of 12 months or less at commencement. Short-term leases qualify for the practical expedient exemption from balance sheet recognition under both FASB ASC 842 and IASB IFRS 16. Drives accounting treatment selection in SAP S/4HANA and Yardi Voyager.']
  is_sublease boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease type represents a sublease arrangement (True), where the original lessee (sublandlord) leases all or part of the demised space to a subtenant. Subleases require distinct accounting treatment under FASB ASC 842 (intermediate lessor classification) and separate tracking in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  lease_category varchar [note: 'Broad property-use category to which this lease type applies — commercial, residential, industrial, mixed_use, or land. Determines which operational workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software are applicable and which regulatory frameworks (ASC 842, IFRS 16) govern accounting treatment.']
  lease_type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lease type (e.g., NNN, GROSS, MOD_GROSS, PCT, GROUND, SUBLEASE, MTM). Used as the canonical lookup key in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and downstream analytical systems to join lease records to their classification.']
  lease_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the lease type, including its defining characteristics, typical use cases, cost responsibility allocation, and distinguishing features from similar lease structures. Used in training materials, lease administration documentation, and business glossary entries.']
  lease_type_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the lease structure classification (e.g., \'Triple Net Lease\', \'Gross Lease\', \'Modified Gross Lease\', \'Percentage Lease\', \'Ground Lease\', \'Sublease\', \'Month-to-Month\'). Displayed in reporting, lease administration screens, and tenant-facing documents.']
  lease_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this lease type reference record: \'active\' (available for use in new lease agreements), \'inactive\' (temporarily disabled but retained for historical reference), or \'deprecated\' (superseded by a newer classification; retained for legacy lease records only). Controls availability in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software lease type selection lists.']
  loi_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Letter of Intent (LOI) is a standard prerequisite in the deal process for this lease type (True). LOIs are standard for commercial leases but typically not required for residential or month-to-month arrangements. Governs deal pipeline workflow in Salesforce CRM and DocuSign CLM.']
  mri_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type code as configured in MRI Software Lease Management module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the MRI source system code for data integration and reconciliation, particularly for residential and investment management portfolios.']
  noi_impact_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies how this lease type impacts Net Operating Income (NOI) calculation: \'gross_revenue\' (all income flows through gross revenue line before expense deductions), \'net_revenue\' (tenant pays expenses directly, reducing gross revenue line), or \'not_applicable\'. Used in Argus Enterprise NOI modeling and MRI Software investment reporting.']
  opex_responsibility varchar [note: 'Defines which party is responsible for property operating expenditures (OpEx) beyond CAM — including insurance, management fees, and administrative costs — under this lease type. Critical for NOI calculation accuracy in Argus Enterprise and financial reporting under FASB ASC 842.']
  percentage_rent_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether percentage rent (a portion of tenant gross sales revenue above a breakpoint) is a feature of this lease type (True). Applicable primarily to retail percentage leases. Activates percentage rent calculation and reporting workflows in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  renewal_option_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether renewal options are a standard feature of this lease type (True) or not applicable (False). For example, ground leases and long-term commercial leases typically include renewal options, while month-to-month leases do not. Informs lease renewal option tracking in Yardi Voyager and WALT/WALE calculations in Argus Enterprise.']
  rent_basis varchar [note: 'Defines the standard unit of measure for rent quotation under this lease type: PSF (Per Square Foot) annual, PSF monthly, flat monthly amount, flat annual amount, or percentage of sales. Aligns with industry PSF/SQF conventions and drives rent schedule generation in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise underwriting models.']
  rent_structure varchar [note: 'Defines the fundamental rent calculation methodology for this lease type: fixed (flat base rent), variable (indexed or market-reset), percentage (percentage of tenant sales revenue), hybrid (base + percentage), or ground_rent (land-only lease payment). Drives rent schedule generation logic in Yardi Voyager and Argus Enterprise cash flow models.']
  security_deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). Security deposits are common in residential and commercial leases but may not apply to ground leases or certain institutional arrangements. Drives security deposit tracking and accounting in Yardi Voyager AR and MRI Software.']
  snda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Subordination Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement is a standard requirement for this lease type (True). SNDAs are typically required for commercial leases where the property is encumbered by a mortgage. Tracked in DocuSign CLM and Yardi Voyager lease document management.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of lease type options in user interface dropdowns, reports, and lease administration screens. Lower values appear first. Enables business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sorting.']
  sublease_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subleasing is a standard permitted feature under this lease type (True). Relevant for distinguishing primary lease structures from sublease structures and for compliance with FASB ASC 842 sublease accounting requirements.']
  tax_responsibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which party is responsible for real estate property taxes under this lease type. In a Triple Net (NNN) lease, the tenant bears tax responsibility; in a gross lease, the landlord absorbs taxes. Drives tax pass-through billing logic in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  ti_allowance_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Tenant Improvement (TI) allowances are a standard feature of this lease type (True). TI allowances are common in commercial gross and modified gross leases but less common in NNN or ground leases. Drives TI allowance tracking and amortization in Yardi Voyager Fixed Assets and FASB ASC 842 right-of-use asset calculations.']
  typical_term_max_months varchar [note: 'The typical maximum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 12 months for short-term, 120 months for standard commercial, 1200 months for ground leases). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling, portfolio duration analytics, and investment underwriting.']
  typical_term_min_months varchar [note: 'The typical minimum lease duration in months associated with this lease type (e.g., 1 month for month-to-month, 12 months for short-term, 60 months for standard commercial NNN). Used in Argus Enterprise for WALT/WALE modeling and portfolio duration analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lease type reference record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under SOX financial controls for reference data governance.']
  utility_responsibility varchar [note: 'Specifies which party is responsible for utility costs (electricity, water, gas, HVAC) under this lease type. Gross leases typically assign utilities to the landlord; NNN leases assign them to the tenant. Drives utility billing configuration in Building Engines and Yardi Voyager.']
  yardi_lease_type_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding lease type code as configured in Yardi Voyager Lease Administration module. Enables direct mapping between this enterprise reference record and the source system code for data integration, reconciliation, and ETL processes from Yardi Voyager.']

  Note: 'Standardized classification of lease structures used across commercial and residential leasing — NNN (Triple Net), gross lease, modified gross, percentage lease, ground lease, sublease, and short-term/month-to-month. Defines rent calculation methodology, CAM/OpEx responsibility allocation, and applicable accounting treatment under ASC 842 / IFRS 16. Includes escalation method indicators (CPI, fixed-step, market reset) and renewal option classification. Serves as the authoritative lookup for lease administration across Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and all operational domains requiring lease structure classification.'
}

Table reference.transaction_type {
  transaction_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each transaction type record in the controlled vocabulary. Primary key for the transaction_type reference product. [Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — _canonical_skip_reason: This is a controlled vocabulary / code-list entity; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_framework. Business justification: Transaction types are subject to specific primary regulatory frameworks (e.g., 1031 exchanges governed by IRS Section 1031, REIT transactions governed by SEC regulations, lease transactions governed b']
  appraisal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal property appraisal or valuation is required for transactions of this type. Triggers valuation workflow initiation in Argus Enterprise and appraisal order management processes.']
  asset_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of real estate asset classes to which this transaction type applies (e.g., \'office,retail,industrial,multifamily,land,hotel,mixed-use\'). Governs which asset classes can be tagged with this transaction type in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software.']
  broker_commission_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether brokerage commissions are applicable to transactions of this type. Drives commission calculation and split workflows in Salesforce CRM and the brokerage domain commission products.']
  capex_or_opex varchar [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type are classified as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), both, or neither for financial reporting and budgeting purposes. Drives budget line categorization in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction type record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance reporting requirements under SOX.']
  due_diligence_period_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days allocated for due diligence for transactions of this type, as defined by industry convention or internal policy. Used as a default in deal timeline planning within Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this transaction type record becomes active and available for use across all operational and analytical systems. Supports temporal validity management of the controlled vocabulary.']
  environmental_review_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether an environmental site assessment (Phase I/II ESA) is required for transactions of this type. Drives due diligence checklist items in Procore and compliance tracking in SAP S/4HANA.']
  esg_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type require ESG-related disclosures (e.g., energy performance, LEED/BREEAM certification status, carbon footprint). Supports ESG reporting obligations for publicly traded REITs and institutional investors.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this transaction type record is retired and no longer available for selection in new transactions. Null indicates no planned expiry. Supports controlled vocabulary lifecycle management.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code used in external systems or industry standards (e.g., RESO transaction type codes, CoStar transaction type identifiers, MLS transaction type codes). Enables cross-system data reconciliation and integration mapping.']
  gl_transaction_class varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) transaction class code used to map this transaction type to the appropriate accounting treatment in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA (e.g., capital event, operating event, financing event). Ensures consistent financial statement classification.']
  is_1031_eligible boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type is eligible for a like-kind exchange under IRS Section 1031, allowing deferral of capital gains taxes. Critical for investment advisory and portfolio management workflows in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  is_distressed_asset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type involves a distressed asset scenario (e.g., foreclosure, REO sale, short sale, deed-in-lieu). Drives specialized due diligence checklists and risk flags in Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  is_fasb_asc842_applicable boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type triggers lease accounting recognition requirements under FASB ASC 842 (e.g., ground lease assignments, sale-leaseback transactions). Used by the finance domain for lease liability and right-of-use asset recognition in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_hud_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type require reporting to HUD (e.g., residential foreclosures, REO sales, FHA-related transactions). Drives compliance workflow routing in DocuSign CLM and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_ifrs16_applicable boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type triggers lease accounting recognition requirements under IFRS 16 for entities reporting under international accounting standards. Supports multi-standard financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_joint_venture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type involves the formation or dissolution of a joint venture (JV) entity. Triggers JV-specific legal entity setup workflows in SAP S/4HANA and investment structuring processes in MRI Software.']
  is_portfolio_trade boolean [note: 'Flags whether this transaction type represents a bulk portfolio trade involving multiple properties transacted as a single deal. Enables portfolio-level aggregation and reporting in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software investment management modules.']
  is_sec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions of this type must be reported to the SEC under applicable regulations (e.g., material acquisitions or dispositions by publicly traded REITs under Form 8-K or 10-K). Drives automated regulatory reporting flags in SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_taxable_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type constitutes a taxable event under applicable U.S. federal tax law (e.g., a standard acquisition/disposition is taxable; a 1031 exchange defers capital gains tax). Used by the finance domain for tax provision calculations in SAP S/4HANA.']
  loi_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether a Letter of Intent (LOI) is a required precursor document for transactions of this type before proceeding to a purchase and sale agreement. Drives deal pipeline stage gating in Salesforce CRM.']
  mls_reportable boolean [note: 'Flags whether transactions of this type are required or eligible to be reported to the Multiple Listing Service (MLS) and syndicated via CoStar Suite. Drives MLS syndication workflow in the brokerage domain.']
  mri_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code as defined in MRI Software, used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the enterprise data lakehouse and the MRI source system.']
  property_sector varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction type applies to Commercial Real Estate (CRE), residential properties, both sectors, or is sector-agnostic. Drives sector-specific workflow routing in Salesforce CRM and Yardi Voyager.']
  reit_qualifying_income boolean [note: 'Flags whether income or proceeds from transactions of this type qualify as REIT qualifying income under IRS Section 856. Critical for REIT compliance monitoring and FFO/AFFO calculations in MRI Software and Argus Enterprise.']
  required_doc_checklist varchar [note: 'Structured list or reference identifier for the documentation checklist required for transactions of this type (e.g., purchase agreement, title commitment, survey, environmental report, estoppel certificates, SNDA, LOI). Drives document management workflows in DocuSign CLM.']
  snda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Subordination, Non-Disturbance and Attornment (SNDA) agreement is required for transactions of this type (e.g., acquisitions of leased properties). Drives document checklist generation in DocuSign CLM.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of transaction types in dropdown lists, reports, and user interfaces across Salesforce CRM, Yardi Voyager, and MRI Software. Lower values appear first.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the parent category providing finer-grained segmentation (e.g., within \'disposition\': voluntary sale, forced sale, REO, short sale; within \'exchange\': 1031 like-kind, 721 UPREIT). Supports detailed analytics in Argus Enterprise and CoStar Suite.']
  title_insurance_required boolean [note: 'Flags whether title insurance (ALTA policy) is required for transactions of this type. Drives documentation checklist requirements in DocuSign CLM and closing process workflows.']
  transaction_type_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the transaction type into a major business category. Supports portfolio-level reporting and analytics segmentation across the transaction and brokerage domains. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|disposition|financing|leasing|development|investment|distressed|exchange — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_type_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the transaction type record within the controlled vocabulary. \'active\' records are available for use across all domains; \'deprecated\' records are retained for historical data integrity but no longer selectable for new transactions.']
  transfer_tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real estate transfer taxes (deed taxes, documentary stamp taxes) are applicable to transactions of this type. Used by the finance domain to trigger transfer tax calculations and GL entries in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-facing alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transaction type (e.g., ACQ, DISP, 1031_EXCH, REO_SALE, FORECLOSURE, SHORT_SALE, GND_LSE_ASSIGN, JV_FORM, PORT_TRADE). Used as the stable business key across all operational systems including Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise.']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the transaction type, including its business purpose, typical use cases, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for business glossary documentation, onboarding materials, and data catalog entries.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable, business-facing name of the transaction type (e.g., \'Acquisition\', \'Disposition\', \'1031 Exchange\', \'REO Sale\', \'Foreclosure Sale\', \'Short Sale\', \'Ground Lease Assignment\', \'Joint Venture Formation\', \'Portfolio Trade\'). Displayed in Salesforce CRM deal pipelines, Argus Enterprise reports, and brokerage dashboards.']
  typical_closing_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of calendar days from executed purchase and sale agreement to closing for transactions of this type. Used for deal timeline forecasting and pipeline velocity reporting in Salesforce CRM and Argus Enterprise.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction type record was most recently modified in the enterprise data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and controlled vocabulary governance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version counter for this transaction type record, incremented each time the record is updated. Supports audit trail and change management for the controlled vocabulary.']
  yardi_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding transaction type code as defined in Yardi Voyager, used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the enterprise data lakehouse and the Yardi source system.']

  Note: 'Controlled vocabulary for all real estate transaction categories — acquisition, disposition, 1031 exchange, REO sale, foreclosure, short sale, ground lease assignment, joint venture formation, and portfolio trade. Includes transaction sub-types, applicable regulatory reporting flags (SEC, HUD), and required documentation checklists per transaction category. Used by the transaction domain and brokerage domain as the authoritative classification source.'
}

Table reference.geographic_hierarchy {
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the enterprise geographic hierarchy. Serves as the primary key for all geographic reference lookups across the real estate portfolio. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP/MASTER_RESOURCE — this is a controlled vocabulary / spatial reference node; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE minimums are applied given the node carries rich business metadata.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Every geographic hierarchy node belongs to a country. Linking to the ISO 3166-1 country registry normalizes the country reference and enables joining to country-level attributes (sovereignty type, pro']
  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Geographic hierarchy nodes (countries, regions, markets) operate in a primary currency used for financial reporting and benchmarking within that geography. Linking to the ISO 4217 currency registry en']
  parent_node_geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the geographic hierarchy (e.g., a submarket node\'s parent is its MSA node). Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for portfolio roll-up and drill-down analytics.']
  area_sqm decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total land area of this geographic node measured in square meters (SQM). Used for density analysis, FAR calculations, and market size benchmarking in portfolio analytics.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality for this geographic node. Used in property address standardization, lease administration in Yardi Voyager, and MLS syndication via Salesforce CRM.']
  costar_submarket_code varchar [note: 'CoStar-assigned submarket identifier code used for market research, comparable lease and sales data alignment, and submarket-level vacancy and absorption analytics. Primary integration key with CoStar Suite.']
  costar_submarket_name varchar [note: 'CoStar-defined submarket name (e.g., \'Midtown Manhattan\', \'Beverly Hills/West Hollywood\'). Used for market benchmarking, CMA preparation, and BPO analysis aligned with CoStar Suite data.']
