  management_fee_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which management fees are calculated for this vehicle type: nav (Net Asset Value), committed_capital (total LP commitments), invested_capital (deployed capital), gross_asset_value (GAV including leverage), other. Used in fee modeling and investor reporting.']
  max_leverage_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum permitted Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio for this investment vehicle type, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65% LTV). Null if leverage is not permitted or no regulatory cap applies. Used in debt structuring, CMBS underwriting, and DSCR compliance.']
  min_investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum capital commitment or investment threshold required for participation in this vehicle type, expressed in the vehicle\'s base currency. Used during investor onboarding and eligibility screening. Null for publicly traded vehicles with no minimum.']
  n1031_exchange_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether interests in this investment vehicle type qualify for like-kind exchange treatment under IRS Section 1031, allowing deferral of capital gains tax upon disposition. Applicable to DSTs and direct property ownership structures; generally not applicable to REIT shares or partnership interests.']
  ncreif_vehicle_type_code varchar [note: 'NCREIF standardized vehicle type code used for performance benchmarking and index inclusion (e.g., ODCE for Open-End Diversified Core Equity funds). Enables comparison against NCREIF NPI, NCREIF ODCE, and NCREIF PREA benchmarks.']
  preferred_return_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Standard preferred return (hurdle rate) percentage applicable to this vehicle type, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0800 = 8%). Represents the minimum return threshold that must be achieved before the general partner or manager participates in carried interest. Null for vehicles without a preferred return structure.']
  property_type_focus varchar [note: 'Primary real estate property type focus or mandate for this investment vehicle type: diversified (multi-sector), office, retail, industrial, multifamily, hospitality, healthcare, mixed_use, other. Used for portfolio construction, sector allocation analytics, and investor mandate matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: diversified|office|retail|industrial|multifamily|hospitality|healthcare|mixed_use|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory classification of the investment vehicle indicating the governing regulatory framework: sec_registered (publicly traded REITs, registered funds), sec_exempt (private placements under Reg D), finra_regulated, state_blue_sky (state-level securities regulation), unregulated (institutional separate accounts), other.']
  reit_distribution_requirement_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of taxable income that must be distributed to shareholders to maintain REIT tax status. Typically 90% per IRS requirements. Null for non-REIT vehicle types. Used in compliance monitoring and investor reporting.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Primary performance and financial reporting standard applicable to this investment vehicle type: NCREIF (National Council of Real Estate Investment Fiduciaries — U.S. institutional), INREV (European non-listed real estate), ANREV (Asia-Pacific non-listed real estate), GIPS (Global Investment Performance Standards), SEC (public company reporting), GAAP (U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCREIF|INREV|ANREV|GIPS|SEC|GAAP|IFRS|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sec_form_type varchar [note: 'Primary SEC filing form type associated with this investment vehicle type (e.g., S-11 for REIT IPO registration, 10-K for annual report, 10-Q for quarterly report, N-2 for closed-end fund registration, D for Regulation D private placement). Null for non-SEC-registered vehicles.']
  sec_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this investment vehicle type requires registration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). True for publicly traded REITs and registered investment companies; False for private placements and institutional separate accounts.']
  sox_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this investment vehicle type is subject to Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) internal control and financial reporting requirements. Applicable to publicly traded REITs and other SEC-reporting entities. Drives audit, internal control, and financial close processes.']
  tax_treatment varchar [note: 'Federal income tax treatment applicable to this investment vehicle type: pass_through (partnerships, LLCs), reit_distribution (REIT must distribute 90%+ of taxable income), c_corp (corporate taxation), ubti_exempt (Unrelated Business Taxable Income exempt for tax-exempt investors), ubti_subject (generates UBTI for tax-exempt investors), other.']
  typical_hold_period_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard or typical investment hold period in years for this vehicle type (e.g., 7.0 for a closed-end value-add fund, 10.0 for an opportunistic fund, null for open-end perpetual vehicles). Used in IRR modeling, DCF analysis, and investor return projections in Argus Enterprise.']
  ubti_generating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this investment vehicle type generates Unrelated Business Taxable Income (UBTI) for tax-exempt investors such as pension funds, endowments, and IRAs. Critical for institutional investor eligibility screening and tax compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this investment vehicle reference record was last modified in the enterprise data platform. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and reference data governance.']
  vehicle_category varchar [note: 'Broad category grouping investment vehicle types for portfolio analytics and regulatory classification. Values: REIT (Real Estate Investment Trust), private_equity_fund, joint_venture, separate_account, debt_vehicle (CMBS/RMBS), tax_exchange_vehicle (1031/DST/TIC), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: REIT|private_equity_fund|joint_venture|separate_account|debt_vehicle|tax_exchange_vehicle|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-unique alphanumeric code identifying the investment vehicle type (e.g., REIT_EQUITY, PE_CLOSED, JV, DST, TIC, CMBS). Used as the canonical lookup key across all consuming domains including investment, transaction, owner, and finance.']
  vehicle_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the investment vehicle type (e.g., \'Equity Real Estate Investment Trust\', \'Closed-End Private Equity Fund\', \'Delaware Statutory Trust\'). Used in reports, investor documents, and regulatory filings.']
  vehicle_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular subtype classification within the vehicle category (e.g., equity_reit, mortgage_reit, hybrid_reit, open_end_fund, closed_end_fund, odce_fund, value_add_fund, opportunistic_fund, core_fund, core_plus_fund, cmbs, rmbs, dst, tic, 1031_exchange). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equity_reit|mortgage_reit|hybrid_reit|open_end_fund|closed_end_fund|odce_fund|value_add_fund|opportunistic_fund|core_fund|core_plus_fund|cmbs|rmbs|dst|tic|1031_exchange — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Reference classification for real estate investment structures and legal vehicles — REIT (equity, mortgage, hybrid), private equity fund (open-end, closed-end), ODCE fund, value-add/opportunistic fund, joint venture (JV), separate account, CMBS, RMBS, DST (Delaware Statutory Trust), TIC (Tenants in Common), and 1031 exchange vehicle. Defines regulatory classification (SEC, FINRA, state blue sky), applicable tax treatment (pass-through, REIT distribution requirements, UBTI), investor eligibility requirements (accredited, qualified purchaser, institutional), minimum investment thresholds, and reporting standards (NCREIF, INREV, ANREV). Referenced by investment, transaction, owner, and finance domains for fund structuring, investor onboarding, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table reference.sustainability_rating {
  sustainability_rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sustainability certification level or rating record in the enterprise reference registry. Role: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — exempt from per-role minimums; this table is a controlled vocabulary of green building certification schemes and their defined rating tiers. _canonical_skip_reason: This is a reference/lookup entity providing standardized code sets for sustainability certifications; no MASTER_RESOURCE or TRANSACTION minimums apply.']
  certifying_body_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Sustainability certification schemes are issued by certifying bodies domiciled in specific countries (e.g., USGBC in USA for LEED, BRE in UK for BREEAM). Linking to the country registry enables joinin']
  superseded_by_sustainability_rating_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sustainability_rating_id of the newer record that supersedes this one when a scheme version is updated (e.g., LEED v3 Platinum superseded by LEED v4.1 Platinum). Enables version lineage tracking and migration mapping for existing certified properties in the portfolio.']
  applicable_building_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of building or asset types to which this certification level applies (e.g., \'Office, Retail, Industrial\' or \'New Construction, Existing Buildings, Interior Design\'). Aligns with RESO property type taxonomy and CoStar property classifications for ESG eligibility screening.']
  applicable_project_phase varchar [note: 'Project lifecycle phase for which this certification level is applicable (e.g., \'NEW_CONSTRUCTION\' for LEED BD+C, \'EXISTING_BUILDING\' for LEED O+M, \'INTERIOR_FIT_OUT\' for LEED ID+C). Used in Procore development project ESG planning and Building Engines facility operations tracking.']
  breeam_assessment_type varchar [note: 'BREEAM-specific assessment type applicable to this record (e.g., \'NEW_CONSTRUCTION\', \'IN_USE\', \'REFURBISHMENT\'). Set to \'NA\' for non-BREEAM schemes. Supports BREEAM portfolio tracking and compliance reporting for UK and European assets.']
  cap_rate_compression_bps varchar [note: 'Indicative benchmark basis points of CAP rate compression (yield reduction) associated with achieving this certification level relative to non-certified comparable assets. Used in Argus Enterprise investment analysis and portfolio valuation models to quantify the sustainability premium on asset value.']
  carbon_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification scheme and level includes or requires carbon emissions measurement and reporting as part of the certification criteria (True). Supports Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions tracking for TCFD and SEC climate disclosure compliance.']
  certifying_body_name varchar [note: 'Name of the official organization or authority that administers, audits, and issues the certification (e.g., \'U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC)\', \'Building Research Establishment (BRE)\', \'U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)\', \'International WELL Building Institute (IWBI)\', \'GRESB B.V.\'). Required for regulatory compliance filings and ESG audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sustainability rating reference record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX-compliant change management for reference data governance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this certification level definition became officially valid and available for use in property certifications and ESG reporting. Supports temporal validity management of the reference data registry and ensures correct scheme version application by certification date.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this certification level definition is no longer valid for new certifications (null for currently active records with no planned expiry). Used in conjunction with effective_from to enforce temporal reference data integrity across the enterprise data lakehouse.']
  energy_performance_focus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether energy efficiency and performance is a primary evaluation criterion for this certification scheme and level (True), as opposed to schemes focused primarily on health/wellness, water, or social factors (False). Used for OPEX reduction analysis and CAPEX planning in Argus Enterprise models.']
  energy_star_eligible_property_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EPA-defined property use types eligible for ENERGY STAR certification under this rating record (e.g., \'Office, K-12 School, Hospital, Retail Store\'). Applicable only to ENERGY STAR scheme records; null for other schemes. Used in Yardi Voyager and MRI Software ESG module property eligibility screening.']
  esg_framework_alignment varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ESG reporting frameworks to which this certification scheme contributes or aligns (e.g., \'GRI, SASB, TCFD, GRESB, UN SDGs, EU Taxonomy\'). Critical for investor-grade ESG reporting, REIT sustainability disclosures, and SEC climate risk reporting compliance.']
  external_reference_url varchar [note: 'Official URL linking to the certifying body\'s published documentation, scoring guide, or technical standard for this certification level (e.g., USGBC LEED scorecard page, BRE BREEAM technical standards). Provides direct access to authoritative source documentation for ESG analysts and compliance teams.']
  gresb_score_contribution boolean [note: 'Indicates whether achieving this certification level contributes to the GRESB Real Estate Assessment score (True) or is not recognized by GRESB (False). Directly impacts REIT investor relations and AUM-weighted ESG benchmarking.']
  indoor_air_quality_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether indoor environmental quality or air quality is a primary criterion for this certification scheme and level (True), as is the case for WELL Building Standard and LEED Indoor Environmental Quality credits. Relevant for tenant health and wellness disclosures and OSHA compliance.']
  is_renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether periodic renewal or recertification is required to maintain this certification level (True) or whether it is a one-time permanent certification (False). Drives automated compliance alerts in Building Engines and ESG portfolio monitoring workflows.']
  leed_category varchar [note: 'LEED-specific rating system category applicable to this record (e.g., \'BD_AND_C\' for Building Design and Construction, \'O_AND_M\' for Operations and Maintenance, \'ID_AND_C\' for Interior Design and Construction). Set to \'NA\' for non-LEED schemes. Enables precise LEED sub-system tracking in ESG portfolios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BD_AND_C|ID_AND_C|O_AND_M|ND|HOMES|CITIES_AND_COMMUNITIES|NA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_possible_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total maximum achievable score under the scheme\'s scoring framework (e.g., 110 points for LEED v4, 100% for BREEAM, 100 for ENERGY STAR). Used to normalize scores across schemes for cross-portfolio ESG benchmarking and GRESB reporting.']
  max_score_threshold decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum numeric score defining the upper boundary of this certification tier (exclusive upper bound for non-top tiers; null or scheme maximum for the highest tier). Enables precise tier-band mapping in ESG scoring engines and Argus Enterprise valuation models.']
  min_score_threshold decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum numeric score required to achieve this certification level within the scheme\'s scoring framework (e.g., 80 points for LEED Platinum, 85% for BREEAM Outstanding). Used in valuation models, ESG gap analysis, and development project target-setting.']
  premium_rent_uplift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Indicative benchmark percentage premium on market rent per square foot (PSF) attributable to achieving this certification level, based on industry research and comparable market data. Used in Argus Enterprise DCF models, lease underwriting, and investment advisory valuations to quantify the green premium.']
  rating_level_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the specific certification tier or rating level within the scheme (e.g., \'CERT\', \'SILVER\', \'GOLD\', \'PLATINUM\' for LEED; \'PASS\', \'GOOD\', \'VERY_GOOD\', \'EXCELLENT\', \'OUTSTANDING\' for BREEAM; \'CERTIFIED\' for ENERGY STAR). Used as a lookup key in property, development, and compliance domain tables.']
  rating_level_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of what the certification tier represents, including the performance expectations, sustainability outcomes, and distinguishing characteristics relative to other tiers within the same scheme. Supports ESG analyst interpretation and investor due diligence.']
  rating_level_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the certification tier or rating level (e.g., \'LEED Platinum\', \'BREEAM Outstanding\', \'ENERGY STAR Certified\', \'WELL Gold\'). Displayed in ESG portfolio dashboards, investor reporting packs, and tenant-facing marketing collateral.']
  recognition_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of recognition for this certification scheme and level (e.g., \'GLOBAL\' for LEED and BREEAM International, \'NATIONAL\' for NABERS Australia, \'REGIONAL\' for jurisdiction-specific schemes). Used for cross-border portfolio ESG comparability and investor reporting.']
  renewal_cycle_years varchar [note: 'Number of years after which the certification must be renewed or recertified to remain valid (e.g., 3 years for ENERGY STAR, 5 years for BREEAM In-Use). Drives compliance calendar management in Building Engines and ESG reporting schedules for REIT investor disclosures.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the green building or sustainability certification scheme (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ENERGY STAR, WELL Building Standard, GRESB, Fitwel, NABERS). Used as the primary grouping key for ESG portfolio reporting and investor disclosures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LEED|BREEAM|ENERGY_STAR|WELL|GRESB|FITWEL|NABERS|EDGE|DGNB|GREEN_STAR — promote to reference product]']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the sustainability or green building certification scheme (e.g., \'Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED)\', \'Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method (BREEAM)\', \'ENERGY STAR\'). Used in investor reports, ESG disclosures, and tenant marketing materials.']
  scheme_version varchar [note: 'Version or edition of the certification scheme under which this rating level is defined (e.g., \'LEED v4.1\', \'BREEAM 2018\', \'WELL v2\', \'ENERGY STAR 2023\'). Critical for tracking certification vintage and ensuring comparability across portfolio assets assessed under different scheme versions.']
  score_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used by the certification scheme\'s scoring system (e.g., \'POINTS\' for LEED, \'PERCENTAGE\' for BREEAM, \'STARS\' for NABERS, \'SCORE\' for ENERGY STAR 1–100 scale). Ensures correct interpretation of min/max score thresholds across heterogeneous schemes.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sort order for this record within its certification scheme grouping, used to control the presentation sequence in ESG reporting dropdowns, investor dashboards, and property management system lookup lists (e.g., Yardi Voyager, MRI Software ESG modules).']
  sustainability_rating_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this sustainability rating reference record (e.g., \'ACTIVE\' for currently valid certification levels, \'DEPRECATED\' for retired tiers, \'SUPERSEDED\' for levels replaced by a newer scheme version, \'DRAFT\' for pending publication). Controls which records are available for selection in downstream property and compliance domain lookups.']
  third_party_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification level requires independent third-party verification or audit by an accredited assessor (True), as opposed to self-reported or self-assessed ratings (False). Critical for investor-grade ESG credibility and SOX-compliant financial reporting for public REITs.']
  tier_rank varchar [note: 'Ordinal rank of this certification level within its scheme, where 1 represents the highest/best tier (e.g., LEED Platinum = 1, LEED Gold = 2, LEED Silver = 3, LEED Certified = 4). Enables ranked sorting and tier-progression analytics in ESG portfolio dashboards.']
  typical_certification_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Indicative benchmark cost in US dollars to achieve this certification level, including registration, assessment, and certification fees (excluding tenant improvement or capital expenditure costs). Used in CAPEX budgeting within Argus Enterprise and SAP S/4HANA project systems for development feasibility analysis.']
  typical_certification_duration_days varchar [note: 'Indicative number of calendar days from application submission to certification award for this scheme and level. Used in Procore construction project scheduling and development timeline planning to account for ESG certification lead times.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this sustainability rating reference record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture in Databricks Lakehouse, and reference data version control in ESG reporting workflows.']
  water_efficiency_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether water efficiency and conservation is a scored or required criterion within this certification scheme and level (True). Relevant for ESG portfolio reporting under GRI 303 (Water and Effluents) and LEED Water Efficiency credits.']
  well_concept varchar [note: 'Primary WELL Building Standard concept category associated with this certification level (e.g., \'AIR\', \'WATER\', \'LIGHT\', \'MIND\'). Set to \'NA\' for non-WELL schemes. Supports tenant wellness program reporting and WELL portfolio tracking for health-focused investors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AIR|WATER|NOURISHMENT|LIGHT|MOVEMENT|THERMAL_COMFORT|SOUND|MATERIALS|MIND|COMMUNITY|INNOVATION|NA — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Registry of green building and sustainability certification schemes — LEED (Certified through Platinum), BREEAM (Pass through Outstanding), ENERGY STAR, WELL Building Standard, GRESB, Fitwel, and NABERS. Stores certification level definitions, scoring thresholds, applicable building types, certifying body, renewal cycle requirements, and ESG reporting framework alignment (GRI, SASB, TCFD). Referenced by property, development, and compliance domains for ESG portfolio tracking and investor reporting.'
}

Table reference.country {
  country_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the country record. Serves as the primary key and top-level node in the geographic hierarchy referenced across all real estate domains. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a controlled vocabulary / code-list table providing ISO country codes and geographic metadata; per-role minimum categories do not apply.]']
  alpha2_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., US, GB, DE). Used as the primary short-form identifier in lease agreements, tenant records, property addresses, and regulatory filings across Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  alpha3_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used in financial consolidation reporting in SAP S/4HANA, CMBS/RMBS documentation, and cross-border investment portfolio reporting in Argus Enterprise.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'FATF-aligned AML risk classification for the country. Used in KYC/AML due diligence for real estate transactions, tenant onboarding, and cross-border investment approvals. High-risk countries require enhanced due diligence per SOX and SEC compliance requirements.']
  area_measurement_body varchar [note: 'Name of the primary standards body governing property area measurement methodology in the country (e.g., BOMA, IPMS, RICS). Ensures consistent GLA/NLA calculations for PSF rent benchmarking and CAM reconciliation.']
  breeam_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether BREEAM certification is officially recognized or incentivized in the country. Relevant for ESG compliance, green building asset classification, and sustainability reporting for European and international portfolios.']
  capital_city varchar [note: 'Name of the country\'s capital city. Used as a geographic reference point for market research, CoStar comparable data alignment, and investment market classification.']
  capital_gains_tax_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether capital gains tax applies to real estate dispositions in the country. Affects IRR and NPV modeling in Argus Enterprise, investment advisory recommendations, and property acquisition/disposition strategy.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Official short-form country name in English as defined by ISO 3166-1 (e.g., United States, United Kingdom, Germany). Used as the display label in property records, lease agreements, tenant profiles, and portfolio reports.']
  country_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the country record within the enterprise reference data registry. \'active\' indicates the country is in use across operational systems; \'inactive\' indicates no current portfolio exposure; \'deprecated\' indicates the country code has been superseded (e.g., post-dissolution or renaming per ISO 3166-1 updates).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country record was first created in the enterprise reference data registry. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial controls for reference data governance.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary legal tender of the country (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used as the default currency for property valuations, lease rent schedules, NOI calculations, and CAP rate analysis in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and Argus Enterprise.']
  dialing_code varchar [note: 'International telephone dialing prefix for the country (e.g., +1 for USA, +44 for UK). Used for formatting tenant and owner contact phone numbers in Salesforce CRM and Yardi Voyager tenant records.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this country record is valid and active within the enterprise reference data registry. Supports temporal data management and ISO 3166-1 change tracking (e.g., country reclassifications, new sovereign states).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this country record is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended active record. Used to manage deprecated or superseded country codes following ISO 3166-1 updates (e.g., dissolution of a country or code reassignment).']
  esg_reporting_framework varchar [note: 'Primary ESG reporting framework mandated or commonly adopted for real estate assets in the country (e.g., GRI, TCFD, SFDR, local regulation). Drives sustainability data collection, LEED/BREEAM certification tracking, and ESG disclosure requirements for REITs and institutional investors.']
  fatf_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country is a member of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF). Relevant for AML/KYC compliance in cross-border real estate investment transactions, REIT capital flows, and CMBS/RMBS structuring.']
  foreign_ownership_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether foreign nationals and entities are permitted to own real property in the country. Critical for investment advisory, portfolio acquisition strategy, and REIT eligibility assessments in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software.']
  foreign_ownership_restriction_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any restrictions, conditions, or limitations on foreign ownership of real property in the country (e.g., maximum ownership percentage, restricted zones, required local partner). Supports investment advisory and compliance review.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to real estate operations conducted in or with residents of this country. Drives data handling policies for tenant PII in Salesforce CRM, Yardi Voyager, and DocuSign CLM.']
  ifrs_adopted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country has adopted International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) for financial reporting. Determines applicable accounting treatment for investment property (IAS 40), lease liabilities (IFRS 16), and financial instrument disclosures in SAP S/4HANA.']
  land_registration_system varchar [note: 'Type of land title registration system in use in the country. Affects title insurance requirements, ALTA policy applicability, due diligence procedures for property acquisition, and LTV calculations for debt instruments.']
  lease_accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The applicable lease accounting standard for real estate leases in the country. Drives right-of-use asset recognition, lease liability classification, and financial statement presentation in Yardi Voyager lease accounting and SAP S/4HANA financial consolidation.']
  leed_recognized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether LEED certification is officially recognized or incentivized by the country\'s regulatory or governmental bodies. Supports ESG reporting, green building compliance tracking, and sustainability-linked financing decisions.']
  measurement_standard varchar [note: 'Predominant unit-of-measure standard used for property area measurement in the country. Determines whether GLA, NLA, and GRI calculations are expressed in SQF (square feet) or SQM (square meters) in Yardi Voyager, CoStar Suite, and lease administration systems.']
  multiple_time_zones boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country spans multiple time zones. Relevant for scheduling lease payment processing, maintenance dispatch, and financial period close operations across geographically dispersed property portfolios.']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit ISO 3166-1 numeric country code (e.g., 840 for USA, 826 for GBR). Used in UN trade statistics, ALTA title insurance documentation, and cross-system integration where alpha codes may conflict.']
  official_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the country as recognized by the United Nations (e.g., United States of America, United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland). Used in legal contracts, SNDA agreements, and regulatory filings requiring formal jurisdiction identification.']
  portfolio_exposure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enterprise currently holds active real estate assets, leases, or investments in this country. Used to filter active jurisdictions in portfolio reporting, AUM dashboards, and regulatory compliance monitoring.']
  primary_valuation_standard varchar [note: 'The predominant property valuation standard applied in the country. Determines the methodology used for appraisals, CAP rate benchmarking, DCF modeling, and NAV/GAV calculations in Argus Enterprise and investment advisory services.']
  property_law_system varchar [note: 'Legal system governing property rights and real estate transactions in the country. Determines applicable title transfer procedures, SNDA enforceability, ground lease structures, and tenure type classifications used in lease administration and investment advisory.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region or continent grouping for the country (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Middle East, Africa, Latin America). Used for portfolio segmentation, AUM reporting, and investment strategy classification in Argus Enterprise and MRI Software. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: North America|Europe|Asia-Pacific|Middle East|Africa|Latin America|Oceania|South Asia — promote to reference product]']
  reit_framework_exists boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country has an established legal and regulatory framework for Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) or equivalent listed property vehicles. Used in investment portfolio structuring and AUM classification in Argus Enterprise.']
  sanctions_status varchar [note: 'Current OFAC/UN sanctions status for the country. Determines whether real estate transactions, lease agreements, and investment activities are permissible. \'sanctioned\' or \'embargoed\' countries are blocked from all transaction processing in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display sequence for ordering country records in dropdown lists, reports, and reference data selectors across Yardi Voyager, Salesforce CRM, and other operational systems. Lower values appear first.']
  sovereignty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the country or territory\'s political sovereignty status. Determines applicable legal frameworks, title insurance requirements (ALTA), and regulatory reporting obligations for cross-border real estate transactions.']
  standard_vat_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard VAT or GST rate applicable to real estate transactions in the country, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%). Used in lease rent schedule calculations, AP invoice processing, and tax provision modeling in SAP S/4HANA.']
  sub_region varchar [note: 'UN M.49 sub-regional grouping for the country (e.g., Northern Europe, South-Eastern Asia, Northern America). Supports granular portfolio analytics and cross-border investment reporting in Argus Enterprise.']
  tax_treaty_with_us boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country has an active bilateral tax treaty with the United States. Affects withholding tax rates on rental income, capital gains treatment for cross-border property dispositions, and REIT distribution taxation for international investors.']
  time_zone_primary varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the country\'s primary or capital time zone (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling lease payment due dates, maintenance work orders in Building Engines, and financial period close timestamps in SAP S/4HANA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this country record in the enterprise reference data registry. Used for change data capture, reference data synchronization across Yardi Voyager, SAP S/4HANA, and MRI Software, and audit trail compliance.']
  vat_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Value Added Tax (VAT) or equivalent goods and services tax applies to real estate transactions and rental income in the country. Affects lease rent invoicing, CAM charge calculations, and AP/AR processing in Yardi Voyager and SAP S/4HANA.']
  withholding_tax_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard withholding tax rate applied to rental income and property-related payments remitted to foreign investors in the country, expressed as a percentage. Used in cross-border investment advisory, REIT distribution modeling, and DSCR calculations.']

  Note: 'ISO 3166-1 country registry supporting international real estate portfolio operations. Stores country code (alpha-2, alpha-3, numeric), country name, region/continent grouping, default currency, applicable regulatory frameworks (GDPR, local property law), and tax treaty status. Serves as the top-level node in the geographic hierarchy and is referenced by all domains requiring country-level jurisdiction identification.'
}

Table reference.regulatory_framework {
  regulatory_framework_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory or compliance framework record in the enterprise registry. Primary key. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP/MASTER_RESOURCE — this entity is a reference registry of regulatory frameworks; per-role minimums for MASTER_RESOURCE apply and are satisfied below.]']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Regulatory frameworks are jurisdiction-specific and governed by a country\'s legal system. Linking to the country registry enables joining to country-level attributes (property law system, REIT framewo']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: State and municipal regulatory frameworks apply to specific geographic jurisdictions represented in the enterprise geographic hierarchy. Linking enables spatial queries (e.g., all regulations applicab']
  penalty_currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Regulatory frameworks specify maximum penalty amounts denominated in a specific currency (max_penalty_amount with penalty_currency_code). Linking to the ISO 4217 currency registry ensures referential ']
  applicable_business_processes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of core business processes governed by this framework (e.g., \'Lease Administration, Financial Reporting, Property Acquisition\'). Maps the framework to operational workflows for compliance impact assessment and process-level control assignment.']
  applicable_data_domains varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of enterprise data domains impacted by this framework (e.g., \'Lease, Finance, Tenant, Property, Compliance\'). Used to route compliance requirements to domain data stewards and enforce data governance controls.']
  asset_class_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of real estate asset classes to which this framework applies (e.g., \'commercial, residential, industrial, retail, multifamily, mixed_use\'). Enables property-type-level compliance filtering and applicability determination in portfolio management.']
  citation_reference varchar [note: 'Formal legal or standards citation for the framework (e.g., \'FASB ASC 842\', \'IFRS 16\', \'15 U.S.C. § 1681 (FCRA)\', \'29 CFR Part 1910\', \'EU Regulation 2016/679\'). Used in legal documents, audit reports, and compliance filings.']
  compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name or title of the internal role, department, or individual responsible for managing compliance with this framework (e.g., \'Chief Compliance Officer\', \'Legal — Lease Accounting\', \'Environmental Health & Safety Manager\'). Drives accountability assignment in compliance workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory framework record was first created in the enterprise registry. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping requirements under SOX and GDPR.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory framework became or becomes legally effective and enforceable. Used to determine applicability windows for properties, leases, and transactions. Aligns with ASC 842 and IFRS 16 adoption date tracking.']
  external_reference_url varchar [note: 'URL linking to the official regulatory text, standards document, or governing body publication for this framework (e.g., FASB codification link, EUR-Lex GDPR URL, OSHA CFR link). Provides direct access to authoritative source material for compliance officers.']
  framework_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the nature of the regulatory framework. Drives compliance domain routing, reporting groupings, and risk classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lease_accounting|financial_reporting|fair_housing|environmental|workplace_safety|data_privacy|accessibility|building_code|local_ordinance|tax|other — promote to reference product]']
  framework_code varchar [note: 'Short, unique alphanumeric code identifying the regulatory framework (e.g., ASC842, IFRS16, SOX, GDPR, ADA, OSHA-29CFR1910, CERCLA, IBC). Used as the business key for cross-system referencing and compliance tagging across all operational and analytical domains.']
  framework_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the regulatory or compliance framework (e.g., \'ASC 842 — Leases\', \'IFRS 16 — Leases\', \'Sarbanes-Oxley Act\', \'General Data Protection Regulation\'). Serves as the primary human-readable label in compliance dashboards and reports.']
  framework_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the framework by its issuing authority level — federal statute, state regulation, local ordinance, international standard, or industry self-regulatory standard. Determines jurisdictional applicability and enforcement hierarchy.']
  internal_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the enterprise\'s internal policy, procedure, or control document that implements this regulatory framework (e.g., \'POL-LEASE-001\', \'POL-PRIVACY-GDPR-002\'). Links the external regulation to internal governance documentation.']
  is_esg_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this framework has direct relevance to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting or sustainability performance (True/False). Flags frameworks such as LEED, BREEAM, EPA environmental regulations, and fair housing laws for ESG portfolio reporting.']
  is_financial_reporting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this framework governs financial statement preparation, disclosure, or audit requirements (True/False). Flags frameworks such as ASC 842, IFRS 16, SOX, and SEC Regulation S-X that directly impact GL, lease accounting, and financial close processes in Yardi Voyager, MRI Software, and SAP S/4HANA.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this framework is legally mandatory (True) or voluntary/best-practice (False) for the enterprise. Mandatory frameworks (e.g., SOX, GDPR, OSHA) require formal compliance programs; voluntary frameworks (e.g., LEED, BREEAM, ESG) are adopted by choice.']
  is_reit_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this framework has specific applicability or implications for Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs), including SEC reporting requirements, FFO/AFFO disclosure standards, and REIT qualification rules (True/False).']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority, standards body, or government agency that issued or administers the framework (e.g., \'FASB\', \'IASB\', \'SEC\', \'HUD\', \'EPA\', \'OSHA\', \'GDPR — EU Commission\', \'USGBC\', \'ISO\'). Used for compliance ownership assignment and regulatory liaison tracking.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of the framework\'s applicability (e.g., \'USA — Federal\', \'EU — All Member States\', \'California\', \'New York City\'). Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance mapping and property-level applicability determination.']
  last_amended_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment, revision, or update to the framework. Used to track regulatory change management and trigger re-assessment of impacted business processes and contracts.']
  lease_classification_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this framework affects operating lease classification, finance lease classification, both, or is not applicable to lease accounting. Directly relevant for ASC 842 and IFRS 16 compliance in lease administration systems (Yardi Voyager, MRI Software).']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty per violation or per reporting period as defined by the framework, expressed in the framework\'s base currency. Used for financial risk modeling, insurance coverage assessment, and compliance investment prioritization.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled internal compliance review or regulatory re-assessment for this framework. Automatically calculated from last review date plus review frequency, but may be manually overridden for ad-hoc reviews triggered by regulatory amendments.']
  penalty_structure varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the penalty regime for non-compliance, including fine ranges, criminal liability, license revocation, or other sanctions (e.g., \'Up to €20M or 4% of global annual turnover — GDPR\', \'Civil penalties up to $25,000/day — EPA CERCLA\'). Informs risk quantification and compliance investment decisions.']
  regulatory_framework_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory framework\'s purpose, scope, and key requirements. Provides compliance officers and business users with a plain-language summary of what the framework mandates and its impact on real estate operations.']
  regulatory_framework_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory framework record. \'active\' — currently in force; \'superseded\' — replaced by a newer version; \'repealed\' — formally withdrawn; \'pending\' — enacted but not yet effective; \'draft\' — proposed but not yet enacted; \'sunset\' — expired per sunset clause.']
  related_framework_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of framework codes that are cross-referenced or interdependent with this framework (e.g., ASC 842 cross-references ASC 840, IFRS 16 cross-references IAS 17, GDPR cross-references ePrivacy Directive). Supports compliance impact analysis across related regulatory requirements.']
  reporting_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which compliance reports or disclosures must be submitted to the governing body or internal stakeholders under this framework (e.g., \'quarterly\' for SEC 10-Q, \'annual\' for SOX, \'event_driven\' for GDPR breach notification).']
  reporting_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the reporting period end (or triggering event) by which the required compliance report or disclosure must be filed. For example, GDPR breach notification requires 72 hours (3 days); SEC 10-K requires 60–90 days.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between scheduled internal compliance reviews for this framework. Drives the compliance calendar and review workflow scheduling (e.g., 12 months for annual SOX review, 6 months for semi-annual OSHA safety audits).']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Enterprise risk classification for non-compliance with this framework, based on penalty severity, reputational impact, and operational disruption potential. \'critical\' — existential risk (e.g., SOX, GDPR); \'high\' — significant financial/legal exposure; \'medium\' — moderate operational impact; \'low\' — minor administrative risk.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used abbreviation or acronym for the framework (e.g., \'ASC 842\', \'IFRS 16\', \'SOX\', \'GDPR\', \'CCPA\', \'ADA\', \'OSHA\'). Used in UI labels, report headers, and compliance matrices where space is limited.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this framework record was sourced or is primarily managed (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA\', \'YARDI\', \'DOCUSIGN_CLM\', \'MANUAL\'). Supports data lineage tracking and master data management.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-to-six character code for the state or province where the framework applies, when jurisdiction is sub-national (e.g., \'CA\' for California, \'NY\' for New York). Null for federal or international frameworks.']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory framework expires, is scheduled for repeal, or requires renewal. Null for frameworks with no defined expiry. Triggers compliance review workflows when approaching.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this regulatory framework record. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and compliance audit trails.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier for the framework (e.g., \'2016\', \'Rev. 3\', \'Amendment 2023-01\'). Supports version-controlled compliance tracking and audit trail for regulatory changes over time.']

  Note: 'Registry of regulatory and compliance frameworks governing real estate operations across all jurisdictions. Covers lease accounting (ASC 842, IFRS 16), financial reporting (SOX, SEC Regulation S-X), fair housing (HUD Fair Housing Act), environmental (EPA CERCLA, state brownfield programs), workplace safety (OSHA 29 CFR 1910), data privacy (GDPR, CCPA), accessibility (ADA), building codes (IBC, NFPA), and local ordinances (rent control, inclusionary zoning). Each framework record defines jurisdiction scope, effective date, sunset/renewal date, applicable business processes, required reporting cadence, penalty structure, responsible compliance owner, and cross-references to related frameworks. Serves as the authoritative framework registry for the compliance domain and all domains requiring regulatory context.'
}

Table reference.program_property_type_coverage {
  program_property_type_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this program property type coverage requirement record. Primary key.']
  insurance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enterprise insurance program within which this property type coverage requirement is defined.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the property classification taxonomy node for which coverage requirements are being defined within this insurance program.']
  coverage_requirement varchar [note: 'Indicates whether coverage for this property type is required, optional, excluded, or conditional within this insurance program. Drives underwriting decisions and policy issuance workflows.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coverage requirement record was created in the system. Audit trail for program structuring process.']
  deductible_override decimal(18,2) [note: 'Property-type-specific deductible amount that overrides the program-level deductible for properties of this type. Null if program-level deductible applies. Used for high-risk property types requiring higher retention.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this property type coverage requirement becomes effective within the insurance program. May differ from program inception date if property types are added mid-term.']
  exclusion_list varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of specific coverage exclusions or perils excluded for this property type within this program. Examples: flood, earthquake, terrorism, mold, asbestos. Drives policy wording and disclosure requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this property type coverage requirement expires or is removed from the insurance program. Null if active through program expiration.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this coverage requirement record. Audit trail.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this coverage requirement record was last modified. Tracks changes during program renewal or mid-term adjustments.']
  mandatory_endorsements varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of mandatory policy endorsement codes that must be attached when insuring properties of this type under this program. Examples: WC000313 (Protective Safeguards), CP1018 (Ordinance or Law), IL0021 (Additional Insured).']
  min_coverage_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum coverage limit amount required for properties of this type within this insurance program, expressed in dollars. Enforced during policy binding and renewal.']
  rate_tier varchar [note: 'Underwriting rate tier classification assigned to this property type within this program. Drives premium calculation and carrier selection. Examples: tier_1 (lowest risk), tier_5 (highest risk), preferred, standard, substandard.']
  underwriter_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the underwriter or risk manager documenting special considerations, carrier requirements, or risk mitigation measures specific to this property type within this program.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this coverage requirement record. Audit trail.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the underwriting coverage configuration between property_type and insurance_program. It captures the specific coverage requirements, limits, endorsements, and exclusions that apply when a particular property type is included in an insurance program. Each record links one property_type to one insurance_program with underwriting parameters that exist only in the context of this relationship. This is an operational underwriting design artifact created during program structuring and renewal processes.. Existence Justification: Insurance programs in real estate cover portfolios containing multiple property types (office, retail, industrial, multifamily, land), and each property type appears across multiple insurance programs over different policy years and portfolio segments. The underwriting process requires risk managers to define specific coverage requirements, minimum limits, mandatory endorsements, exclusions, and rate tiers for each property type within each program. This is an operational underwriting design activity performed during program structuring and renewal.'
}

  currency_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency_code. Business justification: Campaign budget approval and financial reporting requires a normalized currency reference for multi-currency real estate portfolios. Normalizing enables FX conversion for consolidated marketing spend ']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Normalizing enables market-level campaign performance reporting, budget allocation by geography, and comparison against m']
  market_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.market_segment. Business justification: Real estate campaigns are designed for specific investment strategies (core, value-add, opportunistic). Normalizing portfolio_segment to reference.market_segment enables campaign ROI reporting by inve']

Normalizing property_type_focus to reference.property_type enables cross-cam']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date the campaign concluded. Used to calculate true campaign duration and compare against planned timeline for post-campaign performance analysis.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date the campaign went live and began generating impressions or leads. May differ from planned start date due to creative approval delays or market timing adjustments.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the campaign received final budget and compliance approval. Used for SOX audit trails and to measure planning-to-launch lead time.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current state of the campaign\'s internal approval workflow, including budget authorization and compliance sign-off. Distinct from campaign lifecycle status — a campaign may be approved but not yet active.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) / Operating Expenditure (OPEX) budget allocated to the campaign, expressed in the operating currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budget approval workflow and reconciled against finance.budget.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who granted final budget and compliance approval for the campaign. Supports SOX audit trail requirements for marketing spend authorization.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the campaign, used in cross-system references, purchase orders, and vendor invoices. Format: MARKET-YEAR-SEQUENCE (e.g., CRE-2024-QTRQ1).']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign as displayed in Salesforce CRM and internal reporting dashboards (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Downtown Office Leasing Drive\').']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign. Controls visibility in active reporting, spend authorization, and lead attribution routing within Salesforce CRM.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing channel or medium used for the campaign. Drives channel-specific performance benchmarking and spend allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: digital|print|out_of_home|email|social_media|event|direct_mail|broadcast — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing channels deployed within this campaign (e.g., \'google_ads,facebook,email,mls_syndication\'). Provides a high-level channel inventory for multi-channel campaign analysis without requiring a separate channel line table.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for the Silver Layer and supports SOX compliance audit requirements.']
  creative_theme varchar [note: 'The overarching creative concept or messaging theme of the campaign (e.g., \'Urban Living Redefined\', \'Prime Office Space Available\', \'ESG-Certified Buildings\'). Used for brand consistency audits and creative performance analysis.']
  digital_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the primary digital advertising platform used for the campaign (e.g., \'Google Ads\', \'Meta Ads Manager\', \'LinkedIn Campaign Manager\', \'CoStar Advertise\'). Supports platform-level spend and performance reconciliation.']
  esg_aligned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign promotes Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) credentials such as Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) certification, Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method (BREEAM) ratings, or sustainability initiatives.']
  fair_housing_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign has been reviewed and confirmed compliant with the Fair Housing Act and U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) advertising guidelines. Mandatory for all residential marketing campaigns.']
  gdpr_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign\'s audience targeting, data collection, and lead capture processes comply with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) requirements. Required for campaigns targeting EU-based prospects.']
  mls_syndication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign includes syndication of property listings to Multiple Listing Service (MLS) platforms (e.g., Zillow, Realtor.com, CoStar). Drives integration with brokerage.mls_syndication records.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for campaign managers to record strategic context, special instructions, vendor notes, or post-campaign observations not captured in structured fields.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective the campaign is designed to achieve. Used to align campaign KPIs and measure success against strategic marketing goals across commercial and residential portfolios.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The originally approved end date for the campaign. Used for budget period alignment, Weighted Average Lease Term (WALT) correlation analysis, and campaign lifecycle management.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The originally approved start date for the campaign as defined during the planning and budget approval process. Used for campaign scheduling and budget period alignment.']
  primary_channel varchar [note: 'The dominant marketing channel driving the majority of campaign spend and reach. Used for channel-level performance benchmarking and budget allocation optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_search|paid_social|email|display|out_of_home|print|event|mls_syndication|direct_mail|broadcast — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this campaign record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, CoStar Suite, SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_campaign_code varchar [note: 'The native campaign identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Campaign ID). Enables reconciliation between the Silver Layer and source systems during ETL validation.']
  target_impression_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of ad impressions or audience reach exposures targeted for the campaign. Used for brand awareness campaigns and digital media planning benchmarks.']
  target_lead_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of qualified leads the campaign is expected to generate. Set during campaign planning and used as the primary lead generation KPI benchmark in Salesforce CRM pipeline reporting.']
  total_spend_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual spend incurred against the campaign budget as of the last reconciliation date. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable (AP) and reconciled with finance.ap_invoice records. Enables real-time budget utilization monitoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the campaign record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and change data capture (CDC) processing.']

  Note: 'Master record for every marketing campaign executed across commercial and residential portfolios. Captures campaign name, type (digital, print, OOH, email, social, event), objective (lead generation, brand awareness, listing promotion, market penetration), target audience segment, geographic market, property type focus, campaign status (draft, active, paused, completed), planned start and end dates, actual run dates, total approved budget, total spend to date, campaign owner, and associated brand or sub-brand. SSOT for campaign identity across all marketing activities.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_flight {
  campaign_flight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a discrete campaign flight execution record within the marketing domain. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_creative. Business justification: campaign_flight.creative_asset_ref is a STRING reference to the creative asset used in the flight. Normalizing to ad_creative_id FK links the flight to the authoritative creative master, enabling retr']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Media buying approval and budget authorization for campaign flights require a traceable FK to the approving employee. Real estate marketing budget controls mandate employee-level approval accountabili']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property asset being marketed in this flight, if applicable. Enables property-level marketing spend attribution and ROI analysis per asset.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: campaign_flight.target_audience_segment is a STRING. Flights can target different audience segments than the parent campaign (e.g., a retargeting flight targets a different segment than the awareness ']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.building. Business justification: Campaign flights in commercial RE are targeted at specific buildings for building-level leasing campaigns. campaign_flight has asset_id but building-level flight targeting (e.g., a specific tower in a']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign under which this flight is executed. A campaign may contain multiple flights across different channels or time periods.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: campaign_flight.channel_type and channel_subtype are STRING fields. Normalizing to channel_id FK links the flight to the authoritative channel master, enabling retrieval of channel cost models, UTM pa']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Campaign flights represent discrete spend commitments coded to GL accounts for accrual and opex reporting. campaign_flight.gl_account_code is a denormalized string. A FK to chart_of_accounts enables a']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  geographic_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.geographic_hierarchy. Business justification: Campaign flights in real estate are geo-targeted to specific submarkets or MSAs. Normalizing target_geography enables flight performance analysis by geographic hierarchy node and media mix optimizatio']
  listing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property listing being promoted by this flight, if the flight is tied to a single listing (e.g., a Zillow or LoopNet featured listing flight). Null for portfolio-level or brand awareness flights.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investment or property portfolio being marketed, for portfolio-level brand or awareness flights not tied to a single property. Supports AUM-level marketing spend reporting.']
  prior_campaign_flight_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_flight (prior_campaign_flight_id)']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Campaign flights are targeted at specific property types; media buyers and marketing analysts filter flight performance by property type for ROI reporting and budget optimization across asset classes.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to marketing.marketing_vendor']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total media spend actually incurred for this flight to date, in the operating currency (USD). Sourced from ad platform billing or vendor invoices. Used for budget pacing, ROI analysis, and OPEX reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state for this flight prior to launch. Tracks the internal review and sign-off process for creative, budget, compliance, and targeting parameters. Values: pending, approved, rejected, revision_required.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this flight received final approval for execution. Distinct from the flight start date; captures the authorization event for audit and compliance purposes.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Total number of user clicks on the ad creative during this flight. Primary engagement metric for digital channels. Used in CPC (Cost Per Click) calculations and click-through rate (CTR) analysis for lead generation attribution.']
  cpc_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated or platform-set cost per click (CPC) for this flight, in the operating currency. Used for click-based channel cost benchmarking and ROI analysis on lead generation flights.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated or platform-set cost per one thousand impressions (CPM) for this flight, in the operating currency. Used for media buy pricing benchmarking and impression-based channel cost analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign flight record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance tracking.']
  creative_format varchar [note: 'Format type of the creative asset deployed in this flight. Determines rendering requirements and platform compatibility. Values: image, video, carousel, text, html5, direct_mail_piece, email_template, outdoor (billboard/signage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: image|video|carousel|text|html5|direct_mail_piece|email_template|outdoor — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this flight record (flight_budget, actual_spend, cpm_rate, cpc_rate). Defaults to USD for domestic US real estate operations.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this campaign flight is scheduled to stop delivering. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen flights. Used for budget pacing, expiry alerts, and WALT-style weighted average flight term calculations.']
  esg_aligned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight promotes ESG-certified or sustainability-focused properties (e.g., LEED, BREEAM certified assets). Supports ESG marketing reporting and green portfolio brand positioning.']
  fair_housing_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this flight\'s creative, targeting, and placement have been reviewed and confirmed compliant with the Fair Housing Act (FHA) and HUD advertising guidelines. Mandatory for residential property marketing flights. True = compliant, False = pending review or non-compliant.']
  flight_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated to this specific flight in the operating currency (USD). Represents the planned CAPEX/OPEX marketing spend for this channel activation. Used for budget vs. actual spend variance analysis.']
  flight_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for this flight, typically including the channel, property portfolio, and run period (e.g., \'Q3-2024 Google Ads — Downtown Office Portfolio\').']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this flight, used in media buy orders, vendor invoices, and campaign tracking systems. Format: FLT- followed by 4–12 alphanumeric characters.']
  flight_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign flight. Tracks progression from planning through execution to closure. Values: draft (being configured), scheduled (approved, not yet started), active (currently running), paused (temporarily halted), completed (run period ended), cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'Total number of ad impressions served to target audiences during this flight. A key reach metric for brand awareness campaigns across digital channels (Google Ads, Meta, CoStar, Zillow, LoopNet). Used in CPM (Cost Per Mille) performance calculations.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'The destination URL to which users are directed upon clicking the ad in this flight. Typically a property listing page, microsite, or lead capture form. Used for conversion tracking and digital experience analysis.']
  leads_generated varchar [note: 'Number of qualified leads (prospect inquiries, form submissions, or contact requests) directly attributed to this flight. Core lead generation KPI linking marketing spend to brokerage pipeline activity in Salesforce CRM.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for campaign managers to capture contextual notes, special instructions, vendor negotiation details, or performance observations related to this flight. Not used for structured analytics.']
  placement_name varchar [note: 'Specific placement, ad unit, or inventory position within the channel where the flight is served (e.g., \'CoStar Featured Listing — Office\', \'Zillow Premier Agent Slot\', \'Google Search — Brand Keywords\', \'Billboard I-95 Northbound\').']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this flight record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-source reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer. Values: salesforce_crm, costar, google_ads, meta_ads, yardi, manual.']
  start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this campaign flight begins delivering impressions or activating the media buy. Used for scheduling, pacing analysis, and campaign timeline reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this campaign flight record was last modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign name or code for this flight in web analytics. Enables end-to-end attribution from ad impression to website visit to lead conversion.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter identifying the marketing medium for this flight (e.g., \'cpc\', \'display\', \'email\', \'social\', \'direct_mail\'). Used alongside utm_source for multi-touch attribution modeling.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'Urchin Tracking Module (UTM) source parameter appended to landing page URLs for this flight, identifying the originating platform (e.g., \'google\', \'meta\', \'costar\', \'zillow\'). Enables web analytics attribution and lead source tracking in CRM.']
  vendor_order_number varchar [note: 'The vendor-assigned order, insertion order (IO), or contract number for this media buy. Used to reconcile flight spend against AP invoices and vendor billing statements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing a discrete execution flight or burst within a parent campaign — a specific channel activation, media buy, or ad run period. Captures flight name, channel type (Google Ads, Meta, CoStar, Zillow, LoopNet, email, direct mail, billboard), flight start and end dates, flight budget, impressions delivered, clicks, spend, placement details, creative asset reference, and flight status. Enables multi-channel campaign decomposition and channel-level performance tracking.'
}

Table marketing.ad_creative {
  ad_creative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each marketing creative asset record. Primary key for the ad_creative master resource.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Ad creatives must comply with brand guidelines (brand_compliance_status already exists on ad_creative). Normalizing to brand_id FK enables enforcement of brand standards, retrieval of logo assets, col']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this creative asset is assigned to. Links the creative to its parent campaign for performance attribution and spend tracking.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: ad_creative.channel is a STRING denoting the marketing channel (e.g., \'Digital\', \'Social\', \'Print\'). Normalizing to channel_id FK links the creative to the authoritative channel master, enabling chann']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Brand compliance and fair housing audit processes require knowing which employee created each ad creative. Role-prefixed \'created_by_employee_id\' used because a second employee FK (approver) is also n']
  derived_ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_creative (derived_ad_creative_id)']
  primary_ad_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific property this creative asset is associated with. Null if the creative is portfolio-level or brand-level rather than property-specific.']
  property_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.property_type. Business justification: Ad creatives are produced for specific property types (e.g., industrial warehouse vs. luxury residential). Brand compliance and fair housing review processes require knowing which property type a crea']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Ad creatives in real estate must comply with specific regulatory obligations (Fair Housing Act, RESPA, state advertising laws). Linking each creative to its governing obligation enables compliance rev']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to property.unit. Business justification: Residential RE ad creatives are produced for specific units: unit photography, virtual staging, floor plan renders. Linking creatives to units enables unit-level creative asset management and ensures ']
  accessibility_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the creative asset meets web accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA), including alt-text for images, captions for video, and sufficient color contrast. Required for ADA compliance in digital marketing.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external creative agency, photography studio, or freelance vendor responsible for producing this creative asset. Used for vendor performance tracking and procurement reporting.']
  alt_text varchar [note: 'Alternative text description of the creative asset for screen readers and accessibility compliance. Required for all image and banner ad assets per ADA Section 508 and WCAG 2.1 AA standards.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the creative asset received final approval for deployment. Used for compliance audit trails and to calculate time-to-market for creative production workflows.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the creative asset in the brand compliance and legal approval lifecycle. Controls whether the asset may be deployed in active campaigns. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this master resource.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (marketing manager, legal reviewer, or brand officer) who granted final approval for the creative asset. Supports audit trail requirements for SOX-compliant marketing spend controls.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'Aspect ratio of the creative asset expressed as width:height (e.g., \'16:9\', \'1:1\', \'4:3\'). Used for responsive ad placement and social media platform compliance.']
  brand_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the creative asset has been reviewed and confirmed to meet corporate brand guidelines, including logo usage, color palette, typography, and fair housing statement requirements.']
  call_to_action varchar [note: 'The primary call-to-action (CTA) instruction embedded in the creative asset. Used to measure conversion intent alignment and to standardize CTA taxonomy across campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: schedule_tour|contact_agent|view_listing|download_brochure|request_info|apply_now|learn_more|get_quote — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this creative asset record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this master resource. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  creative_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business code assigned to the creative asset for cross-system reference, DAM cataloguing, and agency handoff (e.g., \'MKT-PROP001-001\'). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this master resource.']
  creative_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the creative asset as used by the marketing team (e.g., \'Downtown Tower Q3 Banner 728x90\', \'Luxury Residences Email Header v2\').']
  creative_subtype varchar [note: 'Granular classification of the creative asset within its format type, specific to real estate marketing use cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: listing_photo|virtual_tour|brochure|video_walkthrough|banner_ad|email_template|social_graphic|signage_artwork|floor_plan|aerial_photo|site_plan|renderings — promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the production_cost amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'GBP\', \'EUR\'). Required for multi-currency portfolio operations.']
  duration_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration of the creative asset in seconds. Applicable to video and audio format types. Used to validate platform-specific length requirements (e.g., 15s, 30s, 60s pre-roll) and broadcast standards.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the creative asset is authorized for use in active campaigns. Assets must not be deployed before this date. Aligns with campaign flight dates and property listing go-live schedules.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the creative asset must be retired from active use. Triggered by property sale/lease completion, campaign end, pricing changes, or brand refresh cycles. Prevents use of outdated or non-compliant materials.']
